item: gw_driver_helpers.py:90 — hardcoded sys.path.insert('/pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/scripts/profiling') in _try_import_pf
evidence: Path exists and pf.py is real (/pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/scripts/profiling/pf.py), but the absolute Perlmutter-scratch path is baked into portable LORRAX source with no env override (PF_ARTIFACTS_DIR at line 81 covers only the output dir, not the module path). Failure degrades gracefully to a printed '[pf] profiling hooks unavailable' (lines 93-95); the only call site is src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:352, so on local WSL/other checkouts profiling silently disables rather than crashing. Not a correctness bug; should resolve via env var (e.g. PF_MODULE_DIR) or a relative-to-sandbox lookup.

item: gw_driver_helpers.py:225 — sym_real.transpose(0,2,1) applied only in the aux-WFN branch of build_bgw_v_grid_fn
evidence: Both branches yield the same convention. BGW mf_header/symmetry/mtrx acts on G as G'=mtrx@G; the k-acting matrices are the transpose. src/common/symmetry_maps.py:843 builds SymMaps.sym_mats_k = sym_matrices.transpose(0,2,1) with comment 'these apply to k-points as sym_mats_k[i] @ [kx,ky,kz]' (same at :758 for the identity path). So branch A (raw h5 mtrx from aux WFN) applies the transpose that branch B's sym.sym_mats_k already received inside SymMaps — the asymmetry is data-source format, not convention divergence. Downstream fill_v_grid_for_q (src/file_io/read_bgw_vcoul.py:170-178) consumes it as q_full = S_k@q_table + kg0 and G_full = S_k@G_irr - kg0, matching symmetry_maps.get_gvecs_kfull. Correct today; the duplicated raw-mtrx→k-convention conversion is a mild candidate for routing through SymMaps per the unified-sym-action principle, but it is not a bug.

item: gw_driver_helpers.py:270-272 / 28-30 — ppm_sigma_scale, ppm_sigma_flip_neg, ppm_invalid_mode in PPMSigmaRuntimeOptions
evidence: Confirmed dead config surface — stronger than the first-pass hypothesis. Grep over all src/ and tests/ (including the getattr(ppm_options, ...) dynamic-access block at src/gw/ppm_sigma.py:1526-1531) finds ZERO consumers of any of the three fields; only gw_config.py:322-324,585-587,952-954 (parse) and gw_driver_helpers.py (pack). Git history: sigma_scale/flip_neg consumers deleted in commit 237e787 ('Eliminate ppm_options unpacking and delete obsolete debug blocks'); the last opts.ppm_invalid_mode reads deleted in 87b1d0a ('Clean PPM entry block: kill debug opts'). Meanwhile docs/docs_gwjax/COHSEX_INPUT.md:204-215 and :257-262 still document them as functional — ppm_invalid_mode='fixed_2ry' is now a silent no-op (the fallback_omega machinery in ppm_sigma.py/minimax_screening.py runs unconditionally, not policy-gated). Delete the three fields from gw_config + PPMSigmaRuntimeOptions and fix COHSEX_INPUT.md.

item: gw_driver_helpers.py:252-254 — n_omega = floor((max-min)/step + 0.5) + 1
evidence: Round-half-up guards against binary-FP truncation of the grid count: with defaults (5.0 - (-5.0))/0.25 can evaluate to 39.999999999999996, so bare floor()+1 would drop the omega_max endpoint; +0.5 restores the intended 40+1 points. Matches the documented contract COHSEX_INPUT.md:226 'n_ω = floor((max − min)/step) + 1' (endpoint-inclusive), and inputs are validated just above (step>0, max>=min at gw_driver_helpers.py:245-248). Standard idiom, keep as-is.

item: gw_driver_helpers.py:93-104,117-125,155-173 — pervasive except Exception in profile_section and setup_runtime
evidence: Explicitly documented best-effort design: profile_section docstring lines 62-66 ('Failures in any of (1)-(4) degrade gracefully... the only visible effect is one print_fn line') and setup_runtime docstring lines 130-146 ('All three are best-effort startup optimizations whose absence is not fatal... failures are logged and swallowed'). Every swallow site prints the exception via print_fn except _snapshot's silent pass (lines 103-104), which affects only optional memprof snapshots. The MPI concern is bounded: setup_runtime's phdf5_init_mpi/mpi4py imports only amortize the ~400 ms MPI_Init cost off the timed critical path; if MPI is genuinely broken the first collective H5Fcreate in zeta_fit_chunked still fails loudly, and the skip is logged ('[phdf5 init_mpi] skipped: ...'). No correctness exposure identified.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py: e_ref parameter of compute_ppm_sigma_pipeline
evidence: Sole in-file occurrence is the signature (ppm_pipeline.py:290); body never reads it. Both callers pass it (sigma_dispatch.py:215, gw_jax.py:431) but that proves plumbing, not use. Enumerated mechanisms: no getattr/dict/string dispatch of compute_ppm_sigma_pipeline anywhere (grep: only the 2 call sites + comments); no tests/tools/scripts reference it; pyproject console_scripts route only through gw.gw_jax:main and centroid.kmeans_cli:main; sandbox skills/runs grep hit only runs/Si/B_centroid_sweep_2026-04-27/run_w0_persist.py which uses e_ref for compute_chi0, not this function. Transitive note: in compute_sigma_xc (sigma_dispatch.py:98) e_ref exists only to be forwarded here, so it is dead there too; live e_ref consumption is compute_screening (screening.py:160), a separate call at sc_iteration.py:285 / gw_jax.py:421. Removal touches 3 signatures + 2 call sites.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:320 `from . import w_isdf` inside compute_ppm_sigma_pipeline
evidence: Name `w_isdf` never used after L320 (full-file read). The comment's claimed purpose ('ensure_compilation_cache + cache hit timings') is served in ppm_sigma.py, which calls w_isdf._ensure_compilation_cache() at lines 520/573/622 and imports w_isdf itself at module level (ppm_sigma.py:77) — ppm_pipeline already imports ppm_sigma at top (L36-40), so w_isdf is in sys.modules before this line executes. w_isdf has no import-time side effects: module level is imports, two empty dict caches, and function defs (verified by grep of top-level statements). git log -L 320: line was born verbatim in extraction commit cfbe259 ('gw: extract compute_ppm_sigma_pipeline'), a leftover from the previously-inlined gw_jax code. Safe to delete with the comment.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:92 omega_p^2 = 16*pi*N_e/V_cell (Ry^2) in _fit_head_correction
evidence: Exactly BGW's HL plasma frequency: sources/BerkeleyGW/Common/read_rho_vxc.f90:193 `wpg%wpsq(ispin)=ryd*ryd*16.0d0*PI_D*wpg%nelec(ispin)/crys%celvol` (eV^2; dropping ryd^2 gives 16*pi*n in Ry^2). Unit derivation checks: Hartree AU omega_p^2 = 4*pi*n; Ry energy = 2 Ha so squared quantities pick up factor 4 -> 16*pi*n, matching the in-code comment (L89-91). Downstream fit_head_hl_analytic (head_correction.py:378-428, Omega_h^2 = omega_p^2/I_eps_head with I_eps_head = -W^c/v = 1 - eps^-1, mirroring BGW Sigma/wpeff.f90) takes omega_p_sq_ry as a parameter, so this single construction site is effectively already the centralized location. No duplicate 16*pi constant elsewhere in src (grep). Not worth further extraction.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:126-127 _inject_analytic_head silently skips head in streamed mode
evidence: Confirmed real gap, not handled elsewhere: compute_ppm_head_sigma_kij has exactly one caller (ppm_pipeline.py:139 inside _inject_analytic_head), which returns (None, None) when sigma_c_omega is None (L126-127). ppm_sigma.py contains zero occurrences of 'head' — the streamed _StreamedH5Accumulator writes raw per-tau contributions only. copy_sigma_kij_h5_to_omega_h5 (sigma_output.py:340-393) only unit-converts. _eval_sigma_c_at_dft_energies reads the headless h5 (ppm_pipeline.py:211-215). So kij_stream mode — config-selectable AND auto-selected on large grids per docs_gwjax/COHSEX_INPUT.md:268-273, which does not document the omission — produces sigma_mnk.h5, QP diagonals, and eqp output all missing the analytic q->0 head, magnitude ~1.24 eV/band on Si 4x4x4 60b per gw_jax.py:403-404 history note. Aggravating: _fit_head_correction still runs and prints head diagnostics in streamed mode (ppm_pipeline.py:361-364, format_head_diagnostics at L105), and gw_jax's per-band 'sig_c_head(Edft)' column quietly disappears (gw_jax.py:858 None-gate) — the run looks like it has a head but doesn't. Streaming is single-process-only (ppm_sigma.py:125), which limits but does not eliminate exposure. At minimum needs a hard warning or error; correct fix is adding the head into the h5 copy or the streamed accumulator.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:263-265 vs 274-275 RYD_TO_EV applied in one _write_sigma_omega_h5 branch, raw in the other
evidence: Verified numerically consistent: the in-memory branch pre-converts (RYD_TO_EV * sig_x/sig_h/sigma_c, ppm_pipeline.py:263-265) into write_sigma_omega_h5's *_ev parameters, while copy_sigma_kij_h5_to_omega_h5 converts internally — sigma_output.py:381-386 write RYD_TO_EV*sigma_sx_kij_ev and RYD_TO_EV*hartree_kij_ev, and the omega loop at 388-393 writes RYD_TO_EV*sigma_c_kij_ry — so both backends produce identical eV-unit files; docstring at sigma_output.py:349 ('Convert a Ry-units streamed ... to an eV-units file') states the contract. One genuine wart: copy_sigma_kij_h5_to_omega_h5's kwargs are NAMED sigma_sx_kij_ev/hartree_kij_ev yet receive Ry-valued arrays and get multiplied by RYD_TO_EV inside — the names lie about units at the call boundary (ppm_pipeline.py:274-276 passes raw sig_x/sig_h into *_ev kwargs). Behavior correct today; rename kwargs to *_ry in the refactor to prevent a future double-conversion mistake.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:168/238 sigma_omega 'object' duck-typed param + SimpleNamespace stub in sc_iteration
evidence: Contract verified: the only attribute ever consulted is sigma_kij_h5_path (ppm_pipeline.py:211 in the streamed read, :269-271 in the streamed write guard); the in-memory branches ignore the object entirely. sc_iteration.py:664-674 imports the private helper and stubs SimpleNamespace(sigma_kij_h5_path=None), with the implicit contract documented only in a caller-side comment (sc_iteration.py:669-672: 'only consulted attribute is sigma_kij_h5_path ... Stub it for shape compatibility'). The real type is SigmaOmegaResult-like from ppm_sigma (sigma_c_kij + sigma_kij_h5_path, ppm_sigma.py:149-150). Works today but the contract lives in a comment two modules away from the consumer; refactor should pass sigma_kij_h5_path: str | None directly instead of the object, eliminating both the 'object' forward-ref noqa and the SimpleNamespace stub.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:317 docstring claims step 4 diag interpolation is '(rank-0 only)'
evidence: Stale comment, confirmed contradicted by the implementation and by two nearer comments: _eval_sigma_c_at_dft_energies docstring (ppm_pipeline.py:174-185) says 'on all ranks ... so the result is consistent across ranks — required by the post-Σ flow in gw_jax which now runs on all ranks', and the step-4 call-site comment (L371) says 'replicated across ranks'. Code agrees: in-memory path uses extract_sigma_diag_replicated (L203-206, allgather of the diagonal), streamed path does rank-0 h5 read then broadcast_one_to_all (L208-220). The word 'now' in the helper docstring marks the flow change that orphaned the top-level docstring line. One-line doc fix.

item: src/gw/sc_iteration.py:664 imports private _write_sigma_omega_h5 from ppm_pipeline
evidence: Confirmed sole cross-module consumer of the underscore helper (grep: definition ppm_pipeline.py:236, internal use :387, external import sc_iteration.py:664 inside dump_sigma_omega_h5_final; no tests). It is de facto public API — the SC driver's single end-of-run sigma_mnk.h5 write depends on it (write_sigma_omega_h5=False during iterations, sc_iteration.py:293-303 comment + :655-658 docstring). Promote to a public name (and fold in the sigma_kij_h5_path-only contract from the duck-typed-param finding — the SimpleNamespace stub exists purely because the function is private-shaped). No behavioral issue.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:400-405 history note on analytic head removal/re-add
evidence: Deliberate, self-aware documentation, not live churn: the comment opens 'History note (kept here because it explains a specific decision and is not yet captured anywhere else)' and cites the removal commit (1542342, Apr-10), the re-add date (2026-04-25), the on-shell magnitude ±W^c(0)/(2·V_cell·N_k) ≈ 1.24 eV/band on Si 4x4x4, and a report path (reports/mos2_kgrid_gnppm_head_convergence_2026-4-10/). Current code has exactly one injection site (_inject_analytic_head) with sign internally consistent: R_h = B_h/(2Ω_h), B_h = -W^c(0)·Ω_h² (head_correction.py:416-418), on-shell occ diagnostic -R_h/(Ω_h·V·N_k) printed at ppm_pipeline.py:149-157 matches the comment's formula. No parallel/vestigial head path found. Its main present-day value: it quantifies the magnitude of the streamed-mode head omission flagged above.

item: src/gw/sigma_dispatch.py:163 — W_static = W_by_role.get('static', V_q) silent fallback
evidence: Only caller is sc_iteration.py:294, which always feeds W_by_role from screening_requests_for (screening.py:84-94): X_ONLY -> [] (and the X_ONLY branch returns at sigma_dispatch.py:185 before W_static is ever consumed at L165/L213), COHSEX/GN_PPM/HL_PPM always include role 'static', and compute_screening (screening.py:139-167) solves every requested role unconditionally. So the V_q default exists only to keep line 163 from KeyError-ing in X_ONLY, and no live path today silently substitutes bare V for W. However the asymmetry with the explicit probe check (sigma_dispatch.py:205-208 raises KeyError) is a latent hazard: any future caller reaching the COHSEX branch without 'static' would compute Σ_SX/Σ_COH with W=V and produce wrong-but-plausible numbers. Fix is to move the lookup into the branches with a required-role check mirroring L205-208. Not a live bug; defensive-asymmetry cleanup.

item: src/gw/sigma_dispatch.py:166-177 — Gij=None and do_screened=True hardcoded; no bispinor args
evidence: Three sub-claims verified separately. (a) Gij=None is CORRECT for SC: build_Gij (cohsex_sigma.py:37-51) builds the band-index occupation projector diag(1..1,0..0); sc_iteration rotates wfns to the QP basis (sc_iteration.py:272-277) where eigh-sorted eigenvalues put occupied states in the first n_occ band indices, so the default projector is the right QP-basis density matrix. (b) Bispinor Σ^B drop is REAL and silent: the dispatch never passes wfns_transverse/bispinor_v_q_path (not even fields of SCInputs; grep of sc_iteration.py finds zero bispinor/transverse hits), so compute_v_h_sigma_x's Σ^B branch (cohsex_sigma.py:315-326) never fires in SC, while the one-shot path passes them (gw_jax.py:359-367). Every SC iteration's H_QP therefore omits Σ^B with no warning — this is the known 'SC path silently drops Σ^B' limitation, unguarded in code. (c) 'cannot do V-only screening variants' is mostly WRONG: compute_mode auto-resolution (gw_config.py:755) maps do_screened=False to X_ONLY, which the dispatch handles via compute_v_h_sigma_x; only the explicit compute_mode=cohsex + do_screened=false combination diverges (one-shot: W_q=V_q at gw_jax.py:291-292 with do_screened=False and coh-head zeroed via cohsex_sigma.py:138-139; SC: compute_screening solves real W and dispatch hardcodes do_screened=True at L168). Net verdict driven by (b): silent physics loss for bispinor SC runs; deserves at minimum a hard error when self_consistent and wfns_transverse is not None.

item: src/gw/sigma_dispatch.py:180-181 — zero-placeholder sig_sx/sig_coh unpacked then discarded
evidence: Explicitly documented contract, not cruft: compute_v_h_sigma_x docstring (cohsex_sigma.py:286-288) says 'Returned dict mirrors compute_cohsex_sigma's contract with sig_sx/sig_coh set to zero placeholders so downstream cohsex["sig_sx"] accesses don't have to special-case None.' Implementation at cohsex_sigma.py:328-334 allocates one (nk,nb_sigma,nb_sigma) zeros buffer aliased under both keys — tens of MB max per the replication-cost note at cohsex_sigma.py:192-198. In the dispatch, sig_sx/sig_coh are only consumed by the COHSEX branch (L191-197), which never sees the placeholder (COHSEX takes the compute_cohsex_sigma path at L164-171). X_ONLY and PPM discard them. A None-based contract would be marginally cleaner but this is a deliberate, documented, cheap uniformity choice.

item: src/gw/sigma_dispatch.py:230-231 — sig_x device_put to replicated P(None,None,None) before build_qsgw_sigma_xc
evidence: The kernel genuinely requires replicated sig_x: build_qsgw_sigma_xc._kernel (qsgw_utils.py:262-285) computes M = 0.5*(A+B) + sig_x where A/B inherit ('x','y') sharding on (m,n) from the sharded P(None,None,'x','y') sigma_c_omega gather, then forces M replicated before Hermitisation (L284-285) so the conj-transpose doesn't generate cross-shard comms — documented in its Sharding docstring section (qsgw_utils.py:202-212). Replicating small (nk,nb,nb) Σ matrices is the module-level design contract (cohsex_sigma.py:192-198: 'tens of MB... replication is essentially free; the heavy ω-grid Σ_c tensor stays sharded'). This does NOT violate the zero-replicated-intermediates principle, which targets heavy zeta-fit pipeline buffers. Minor note: the device_put is a redundant no-op re-pin — sig_x from compute_v_h_sigma_x is already constrained replicated (cohsex_sigma.py:303-304 and 311-313), so lines 230-231 could be deleted, but they are harmless defensive pinning.

item: src/gw/sigma_dispatch.py:229 — e_qp_rel_ev = e_qp_ev - float(wfn.efermi)*RYD_TO_EV
evidence: The unit arithmetic itself is CORRECT: wfn.efermi is Ry (wfn_loader.py:201-207 computes midgap from WFN.h5 Ry energies; converted with RYD_TO_EV consistently at gw_jax.py:502 and ppm_pipeline.py:194) and e_qp_ev is eV (sc_iteration.py:297 passes E_qp_ry*RYD_TO_EV). But verification found a Fermi-REFERENCE mismatch beyond the claimed 'hazard': build_qsgw_sigma_xc requires omega_ev and e_qp_kn_ev to share a common reference (qsgw_utils.py:195-200), and the Σ_c(ω) grid's implicit anchor is the Fermi level recomputed inside the sigma kernel from the CURRENT bundle energies — ppm_sigma.py:1502 (enk_full = wfns.enk[:, s.full]) feeding _prepare_sigma_state (ppm_sigma.py:330-340, midgap of occ/unocc enk). In SC iterations >= 2, wfns_qp.enk are QP energies (rotate_wavefunctions with enk_active_new=E_qp_ry, sc_iteration.py:272-277), so the kernel's E_F is the QP midgap, while line 229 subtracts the frozen DFT wfn.efermi. Iteration 1 coincides (bundle enk = DFT, same vbm/cbm midgap); afterwards the QSGW interpolation argument carries an offset Delta = E_F^QP - E_F^DFT (plausibly 0.1-0.5 eV as the gap opens), giving an O((1-Z)*Delta) error in Sigma_xc^QSGW. Fix: subtract the per-iteration efermi_ry (already computed by _diagonalize_and_get_efermi, sc_iteration.py:266-267) instead of wfn.efermi, or pass Fermi-relative energies in from sc_iteration. Affects SC-PPM only (the dispatch's sole caller is the SC loop; one-shot PPM uses the separate gw_jax path).

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py — contributions dict (compute_sigma_x_bispinor L175/L191)
evidence: Function-local dict: declared sigma_x_bispinor.py:175, written L191, never read in the function, not returned (returns sig_x_b only, L199), not stored on any object — unreachable by any dispatch mechanism (getattr/config/CLI cannot reach a local). Repo-wide grep: only other 'contributions' hit in this file is the unrelated error string at L197. All callers ignore it: cohsex_sigma.py:241-251 and :315-326 use only the returned array; tests/test_sigma_x_bispinor.py imports only _wfns_with_lorentz_vertices and _TRANSVERSE_INDICES (L80, L123); no hits in skills/ or runs/*.sh (runs/ hits are XLA dump artifacts, not invocations); no console_scripts reference. Nuance: the tr scalar computed at L188 IS alive via the L193 print (the very diagnostic the bispinor_tt_conditioning report's FINAL correction relied on, report.md L128-130) — only the dict storing it is write-only residue of the commit-69e8863 MoS2 cross-check (commit exists, 2026-05-05).

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py L119-121 — docstring Sigma^B formula ends with stray unmatched bras
evidence: Confirmed docs-only truncation: L119-121 reads 'Σ^B[k,m,n] = -Σ_{i,j∈{1,2,3}} ⟨m,k| γ̃^i V^{i,j}_{q=k-k'} γ̃^j |n,k⟩ ⟨m',k'| ⟨m,k|' — the trailing '⟨m',k'| ⟨m,k|' is a dangling fragment and the formula is missing the internal occupied-band sum Σ_{m',k'} that the G^0 insertion supplies (correct form: -Σ_{ij}Σ_{k',m'∈occ} ⟨m,k|γ̃^i|m',k'⟩ V^{ij}_{k-k'} ⟨m',k'|γ̃^j|n,k⟩). Code unaffected — I verified the executed chain independently: build_G (greens_function_kernel.py:24-26, einsum 'ksxi,kij,kjty->ksxty' with conj(psi_yr)), _convolve (cohsex_sigma.py:83-87), project (wavefunction_bundle.py:377-381) implement exactly the module-docstring formula at L13. Fix the docstring; nothing else.

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py L184-193 — in-loop trace diagnostic (bare except, in-loop import, hardcoded agent-B commit-69e8863 reference)
evidence: Confirmed Milestone-A validation scaffolding running in production: both production call sites (cohsex_sigma.py:243, :318) omit verbose, so default verbose=True; and even with verbose=False the trace einsum + float() host-pull at L188 executes unconditionally per tile (9x per Σ^B call) — only the print at L192-193 is gated. XLA dumps from a real run confirm it compiles/executes (runs/MoS2/.../xla_dump module_1037.jit__einsum source_line=188). Marginal cost is small (L183 block_until_ready already syncs; the pull is one scalar), so 'forces extra host sync' is overstated — but the bare 'except Exception: nan' (L189-190) can mask real device errors, the 'from common import RYD_TO_EV' inside the double loop (L186) belongs at module top, and the dead contributions dict rides along. Caveat for the refactor: DO keep the per-tile 'tr Σ' print itself — reports/bispinor_tt_conditioning_2026-06-16/report.md L128-130 shows it is the gauge-invariant physical diagnostic that overturned two wrong tile-level verdicts. Delete the dict, hoist the import, narrow/remove the except, keep the verbose-gated print.

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py L31-33 docstring ('6 unique kernel calls + 3 Hermitian fills') vs L178-182 (9 kernel launches)
evidence: Doc drift confirmed, and the claimed 33% saving is mathematically valid — verified by per-element index math, not einsum pattern-matching. Code launches sigma_sx_k for all 9 (i,j) pairs (L178-182); only V reads are Hermitian-synthesized (v_q_bispinor.py:857-867: V^{ji} = conj(swapaxes(V^{ij},-1,-2)), and even those re-read the companion dataset from disk per get_tile call — no cache, so neither compute nor I/O is 6-of-9). Hermiticity derivation: tile (i,j) computes Σ^{ij}[k,m,n] = c Σ_{q,x,y,a,b} [ψ*_{m,k}γ̃^i ψ_{a,k-q}](x) G_{ab} [ψ*_{b,k-q}γ̃^j ψ_{n,k}](y) V^{ij}_q(x,y) with c real (build_G greens_function_kernel.py:25 → _convolve cohsex_sigma.py:83-87, prefactor 1.0 and -1/√Nk real → project wavefunction_bundle.py:379-381). Taking (Σ^{ij}[k,n,m])* and using Gij Hermitian (occupation projector), γ̃ Hermitian (already required at L82), V^{ji}(q,y,x)=V^{ij}(q,x,y)*, and relabeling a↔b, x↔y, q-sum over full BZ: (Σ^{ij}[k,n,m])* = Σ^{ji}[k,m,n] exactly. So the 3 lower-triangle launches can be replaced by dagger fills of the upper-triangle contribs (6 kernel calls + 3 transposes), matching the docstring. Today the code is correct but does 9/6 of the necessary kernel work; the docstring describes the optimized version that was never written.

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py L162-172 — _pad_V_to_padded second padding source of truth
evidence: Confirmed redundant layer, a runtime no-op today: reader-side BispinorVqReader._padded_shape_LR (v_q_bispinor.py:828-843) rounds each V axis up to prod(mesh.x, mesh.y); ψ-side load_centroids_band_chunked pads n_rmu via round_up_to_mesh_product (src/common/load_wfns.py:360,418 → runtime/padding.py:128-143) — identical round-up-to-mesh-product formula on the same mesh, so the L167 early-return is always taken and jnp.pad at L169-172 never fires. Worse, v_q_bispinor.py:832-834 explicitly promises 'a single pad here makes Σ^B's V tile broadcast against ψ with no further padding step in sigma_x_bispinor' — the companion module's contract says this function shouldn't exist. Latent hazards if it ever fired: pads both axes to the single scalar psi_yr.shape[-1] (assumes square TT tiles — safe today since the L178-179 loop fetches only transverse pairs, n_L=n_R=n_rmu_T, but wrong for any future mixed charge×transverse tile); negative pad width raises if the reader padded larger than ψ; jnp.pad on the P(None,'x','y')-sharded tile would break shard divisibility. Violates single-source-of-truth (no-redundancy rule); delete it or reduce to a shape assertion.

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py L183/L198 — block_until_ready per tile serializes the 9 convolutions
evidence: Deliberate one-tile-resident memory bound, consistent module-wide: cohsex_sigma.py uses the identical pattern after every kernel (L234, L250, L305-306, L313, L325). Each loop iteration holds one (n_q, n_pad, n_pad) c128 V tile (read via get_tile, v_q_bispinor.py:871-875) plus one contrib; blocking before the next get_tile ensures the previous convolve's temporaries are retired before the next tile's read_slab allocates — directly relevant to the known bispinor memory ceiling (Peak-C pair-density; bispinor band ceiling ~3x lower than scalar, per cri3_gnppm_maxbands). The L198 final block matches the callers' convention. Cost: no read/compute overlap (could double-buffer 2 tiles for pipelining), but that is a conscious peak-memory trade the refactor should re-decide explicitly, not an accident. Note the L183 block also makes the L188 diagnostic trace cheap — remove them together or keep them together.

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py — consumer of v_q_bispinor transverse unfold (external in-plane Σ^B caveat)
evidence: The suspect's hypothesis ('bug lives on the v_q_bispinor unfold side; fix = IBZ charge + full-BZ-direct transverse') is OUTDATED — superseded by the FINAL section of reports/bispinor_tt_conditioning_2026-06-16/report.md: the R⊗R unfold_v_q_bispinor_lorentz is CORRECT (gauge-invariant S(q)=Σ_ij‖V^{ij}_TT(q)‖²_F is orbit-constant to 1e-7 for IBZ-unfolded tiles; even full-BZ-direct 'truth' was itself 6-18% non-covariant in-plane, so it was never a valid reference). The real bug is the per-q indefinite-CCT solve: transverse CCT is Hermitian-indefinite with TRS-paired near-null modes (|λ|~1e-7·σmax) above the fixed LU ridge (1e-12), so LU amplifies sub-floor noise as 1/λ (the 23% in-plane gap + xy sign flip). Validated FIX on lorrax_C 9128728: relative-|λ| truncated-eig pseudoinverse in _ridge_indef_solve (rcond=1e-5, knob LORRAX_RCOND_INDEF) — gap 23%→~3%, xy sign consistent, IBZ-unfold exactly covariant by construction. CRITICAL for this audit: the fix is NOT in lorrax_D — src/common/isdf_fitting.py:1382-1393 still implements the old fixed-ridge LU (LU_RIDGE·tr(L)/n + jnp.linalg.solve) and 'RCOND_INDEF' has zero hits in lorrax_D src. So this checkout's transverse Σ^B is still affected, but the defect (and pending port) is in isdf_fitting._ridge_indef_solve, upstream of both the unfold and this module; sigma_x_bispinor.py itself is a correct consumer.

item: src/gw/qsgw_utils.py: plot_qp_energy_comparison
evidence: Whole-tree grep (all file types) over /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/sources/lorrax_D hits only the def (qsgw_utils.py:306) and __all__ (line 351). gw_jax.py:48-52 imports only build_qsgw_sigma_xc / extract_sigma_diag_replicated / solve_diagonal_sigma_fixed_point; no 'import *', no getattr/dict dispatch, no test/tool/script references (tests/, tools/, scripts/ have zero 'qsgw' hits). pyproject.toml [project.scripts] exposes only gw.gw_jax:main and centroid.kmeans_cli:main. Sandbox-wide grep over skills/, runs/, reports/ finds only identical copies in other checkouts and a refactor-map doc noting zero callers. Git proves deliberate orphaning: commit 8c50c21 (2026-04-11, 'Remove diagnostic plot, sigma_munu streaming, dead imports') deleted the only call site + import in gw_jax.py but left the definition and __all__ entry. Also pulls a lazy matplotlib import used nowhere else in the module. Safe to delete along with its __all__ entry.

item: src/gw/qsgw_utils.py:143-171 extract_sigma_diag_replicated — full-tensor allgather before diagonal
evidence: Claim verified. Input sharding P(None,None,'x','y') for (nω,nk,m,n) confirmed at ppm_sigma.py:962 and :1057, so band axes m,n live on different mesh axes. Implementation (qsgw_utils.py:167) forces with_sharding_constraint to fully-replicated 4-D before einsum('...ii->...i'), materialising nω·nk·nb²·16 B per device — the docstring itself budgets ~270 MB for MoS2 4x4x1/80b/41ω and defers a shard_map specialisation 'for follow-up' (lines 158-160); at the empirical non-bispinor ceiling ~700 bands, 16 k, 41 ω this is ~5.1 GB/device, a real fraction of the 28 GB budget. Two caveats found by verification: (1) the docstring's 'off-diagonal mesh shards silently produce garbage' premise (lines 151-155) describes shard_map per-shard semantics, but the code runs under jax.jit where einsum has global GSPMD semantics and would be correct anyway (with partitioner-inserted comms) — the allgather is a conservative, correct, but self-admittedly non-scalable choice; (2) the sibling docstring at ppm_pipeline.py:177-178 mischaracterises this call as a 'cheap allgather of the diagonal only, ~MB', contradicting the actual full-tensor replication. Three call sites: gw_jax.py:639, gw_jax.py:781, ppm_pipeline.py:205. Explicitly self-labelled deferred work with a stale companion comment: refactor target, not a bug today.

item: src/gw/qsgw_utils.py:128-136 solve_diagonal_sigma_fixed_point — inconsistent convergence metric + latent NameError
evidence: Both defects confirmed by explicit algebra, both unreachable at current call sites. In-loop (line 132): diff = |E_next − E_old| = mix·|E_new − E_old| (mix=0.6 default). Post-loop return (line 136): after E = E_next, |E_new − E| = |E_new − E_next| = (1−mix)·|E_new − E_old| — a different metric, smaller by factor (1−mix)/mix = 2/3, so the exhausted-max_iter flag is systematically more lenient than the in-loop test; neither is the true residual at E_next (that would need Σ re-evaluated at E_next). Second defect: if max_iter=0 the loop body never runs and line 136 raises NameError on unbound E_new. Impact today: the sole caller gw_jax.py:649 discards the converged flag entirely ('E_sc_rel_ry, _, n_iter = ...') and passes max_iter=120; default is 80; no caller passes 0 (only other references are a docstring mention in scissor.py:211). Confirmed copy-slip-class defect — harmless in the current call graph but should be fixed in the refactor (compute diff once, reuse for both return paths).

item: src/gw/qsgw_utils.py:78-136 + gw_jax.py:649/699-705 — *_ev parameter names used with Ry values; false 'kernel internals convert' comment
evidence: Confirmed naming/comment debt; no numerical bug. solve_diagonal_sigma_fixed_point's math is fully unit-agnostic (searchsorted + linear interp + |diff|<tol; every quantity shares whatever unit the caller uses). gw_jax.py:646-652 calls it entirely in Ry: h0_diag_ry − efermi_ry, sigma_xc_diag_w_kn_ry, omega_grid_ry, tol_ev=1.0e-7/RYD_TO_EV — deliberately, per the block comment at gw_jax.py:634-636 ('All quantities below are in Rydberg... mixing that with eV-converted h0/Σ_x is a footgun'). So h0_diag_ev/tol_ev/omega_ev names are stale from an eV-first draft. build_qsgw_sigma_xc: at both call sites (gw_jax.py:703-705 passes omega_grid_ry*RYD_TO_EV + E_sc_rel_ev while sigma_c_omega/sig_x stay Ry; sigma_dispatch.py:232-234 same pattern) the abscissae (omega_ev, e_qp_kn_ev) are consumed ONLY to build dimensionless interp indices/weights (lines 240-251: idx = searchsorted(omega, E); w_hi = (E−ω_lo)/(ω_hi−ω_lo)); the interpolated VALUES remain Ry throughout, so mixed units are consistent because units cancel in the weights. The gw_jax.py:699-700 comment 'kernel internals convert; result is Ry' is factually false — nothing converts; result is Ry because inputs are Ry. Bonus cruft: gw_jax.py:705 converts omega_grid_ry*RYD_TO_EV even though omega_grid_ev already exists at line 618. Fix in refactor: unit-neutral names on the solver, delete/correct the false comment, pass omega_grid_ev directly.

item: src/gw/qsgw_utils.py:259-260 — real interp weights cast to complex128 before device_put
evidence: Confirmed harmless. w_lo/w_hi are real-by-construction (line 249-251: w_hi=(E_clamped−ω_lo)/denom with real inputs, w_lo=1−w_hi; imag part exactly zero after the cast). Inside _kernel they only multiply complex128 Σ_c gathers (lines 273, 279); float64 weights would promote to complex128 with bit-identical results under x64. The complex cast merely keeps all six jit kernel args' value dtypes uniform and avoids convert_element_type ops in the graph — a dtype-promotion convenience exactly as the first-pass reader hypothesised. Cost is 2x storage on a tiny (nk,nb) replicated array (kilobytes). No action required; harmless to change to float64 in a refactor.

item: src/gw/qsgw_utils.py:124 — fixed-point init E0 = h0 + Re Σ(argmin|ω|) assumes ω-grid brackets 0
evidence: The Fermi-centered grid is a documented convention, and violation degrades gracefully rather than failing silently. Grid construction: gw_driver_helpers.py:252-258 builds omega from config ppm.omega_min/max/step; defaults are −5.0..+5.0 eV (gw_config.py:314-316), bracketing 0. COHSEX_INPUT.md:210-221 documents the grid as 'relative to E_F per fermi_reference' and 'the ω grid is centered on E_F'; the caller matches by passing h0_diag_ry − efermi_ry (gw_jax.py:650), so ω=0 corresponds to E_F consistently. If a user configured a non-zero-bracketing grid, argmin|ω| picks the grid point nearest 0 — a merely poorer initial guess; subsequent iterations use interp_along_omega whose eval points are edge-clamped (line 59), and out-of-grid bands are then patched by the scissor/classify_bands_in_grid machinery at gw_jax.py:663-695. So no crash and no silent garbage, just possibly slower/edge-clamped convergence flagged downstream via n_clipped diagnostics. A bracket-0 assertion would be a nice-to-have nit, not a correctness need.

item: src/gw/sc_iteration.py — SCState, gw_iteration_map (externally-unused exports)
evidence: Reachable from production CLI: pyproject.toml:33-34 registers lorrax-gw/gw_jax → gw.gw_jax:main; gw_jax.py:482-486 imports make_initial_state_from_dft + run_self_consistency when config.self_consistent; run_self_consistency calls gw_iteration_map at sc_iteration.py:434 (one-shot), 473 (linear), 548 (rcrop residual_fn), and SCState is constructed at lines 164, 328, 479, 590 and consumed by gw_jax (state_final.last_sigma_result, .H_qp_dft at gw_jax.py:556+). Enumerated mechanisms: direct/from-import (only gw_jax.py), string dispatch (grep for getattr/importlib/quoted names in src/ — only the __all__ literals at sc_iteration.py:748-749), tests/tools/scripts/docs/skills (zero matches), console_scripts (route through gw_jax). Reader's secondary claims CONFIRMED: __all__ over-advertises SCState/gw_iteration_map (no external importer of either name), and test coverage is zero — no file under tests/ mentions sc_iteration or self_consistent.

item: src/gw/sc_iteration.py:89-94 vs 114 — SCInputs docstring claims partition defaults to BandPartition.all_protected, field is required
evidence: Confirmed stale in TWO places: sc_iteration.py:90-93 ('Default BandPartition.all_protected(nb_active) reduces the masking step to the identity') and band_partition.py:74-78 ('Used as the default in :class:`sc_iteration.SCInputs`'). The field at sc_iteration.py:114 has no default, and cannot get one without reordering: it is followed by non-default fields e_dft_active_kn_ry (line 115) and valence_mask_active_kn (line 116), so the field-ordering hypothesis is consistent. No behavioral impact: the sole constructor call (gw_jax.py:520-533) always passes partition explicitly; omitting it would raise TypeError at construction, not silently misbehave. Doc-only rot — fix both docstrings.

item: src/gw/sc_iteration.py:730 — E_qp_nk = enk_qp_ry * 0.5 inline Ry→Hartree
evidence: Matches the consumer contract exactly: file_io/qp_wfn.py:38 docstring 'E_qp_nk: QP eigenvalues (nk, nb) in Hartree atomic units', and the writer stores both E_qp_nk_hartree (line 56) and E_qp_nk_rydberg = E_qp_nk * 2.0 (line 57) with f.attrs['energy_units'] documenting it (line 76). The identical inline conversion exists at the other call site, gw_output.py:363 (results.E_qp_ry / 2.0 with the same '# Ry → Hartree' comment). Correct physics (1 Ha = 2 Ry); the only cosmetic improvement would be a shared RY_TO_HA constant in common.units to deduplicate the two inline conversions.

item: src/gw/sc_iteration.py:209-221 — _KSHARD_EIGH_CACHE module-global jit cache keyed by id(mesh_xy)
evidence: The claimed stale-kernel-via-id-reuse bug CANNOT occur: the cached values are jax.jit wrappers whose closures capture rep_E/rep_U/k_shard_3d = NamedSharding(mesh_xy, ...) (lines 183-185), and NamedSharding holds a strong reference to its mesh — so mesh_xy is kept alive as long as its cache entry exists, and id() cannot be recycled for a different live mesh. What remains is a minor wart: unbounded growth (entries never evicted; every distinct-but-equal Mesh object in a long test session leaks one compiled pair) and a fragile key choice. jax Mesh is hashable, so keying on mesh_xy itself (or a WeakKeyDictionary) would be strictly better; in production a single mesh lives for the process lifetime, so no observed harm.

item: src/gw/sc_iteration.py:539-565 — impure residual_fn mutating closure lists; state.iteration counts pipeline calls not rCROP iterations
evidence: Deliberate and documented adapter around a black-box solver interface: mixing.acceleration.rcrop_nojit accepts only residual_fn(x), so capturing the final SigmaResult (_last_sigma, needed by the output writer per docstring line 133) and progress printing (_e_history/rms_history) must go through closure state. The 2-calls-per-rCROP-iteration semantics are explicitly documented at lines 513-517 ('rCROP makes **two** gw_iteration_map calls per rCROP-iteration; max_iter here is the rCROP iteration count'). Verified no correctness consumer of the counter: grep '\.iteration' in gw_jax.py has zero matches; SCState.iteration is only printed in the linear path (line 492) and rcrop prints len(rms_history) instead (line 560). Consistent with the project's no-new-API-layers style; not worth an abstraction.

item: src/gw/sc_iteration.py:735-739 — try/except Exception: pass around multihost sync_global_devices barrier
evidence: Directly contradicts the function's own contract at lines 709-712: 'a multihost barrier follows so the caller can rely on both files existing on every rank when this function returns.' The write is rank-0-only (line 721 'if jax.process_index() == 0'); if multihost_utils.sync_global_devices raises on a multi-process run (collective timeout, comm error, backend mismatch), except Exception: pass lets ranks 1..N return and print success while rank 0 is still writing WFN_qp.h5 — any downstream reader on those ranks sees a partial/absent file with zero diagnostic. Failure scenario: multihost QSGW run with config.debug.write_wfn_h5, barrier raises → silent race on WFN_qp.h5/qp_wfn_rotations.h5. Fix: narrow to ImportError (the plausible benign case, old JAX) or gate on jax.process_count() > 1 and let real barrier failures propagate.

item: src/gw/sc_iteration.py:40-55 — module docstring: metals NOT robust; TODO diagonal-Sigma for inactive in-grid bands
evidence: Documented, dated deferred design decision, not forgotten cruft: lines 49-55 cite 'per design discussion 2026-05-08' and explicitly flag the straddling-band case 'for a separate design pass'; lines 40-45 state the metals limitation and the exact mitigation ('Add a re-sort + re-occupy step here if/when metals are supported'). The system is partially mitigated in code: out-of-ω-range bands DO get per-iteration scissor treatment (_scissor_E_qp_for_outofrange, lines 339-377, wired at gw_jax.py:497-517), and occ is rebuilt per-band-vs-efermi (line 42-43). The unimplemented part (diagonal Σ for inactive in-grid bands) degrades gracefully to scissor extrapolation. Known-limitation documentation of exactly the kind AGENTS.md conventions favor.

item: src/gw/sc_iteration.py:191/199, 544, 589 — triple Hermitisation
evidence: Each site is load-bearing for a distinct entry path, not blind redundancy. (1) Lines 191/199 inside the jit'd eigh kernels: covers ALL kernel callers — _diagonalize_and_get_efermi on iteration output where apply_band_partition masking + kin_ion_dft addition can introduce float-level asymmetry, and the linear path's per-iteration eigvalsh (line 467/484). (2) Line 544 in residual_fn: rCROP's Anderson linear combinations of past H's do not preserve Hermiticity; this projection changes the residual itself (line 565 returns state_out.H_qp_dft − H using the projected H), not just the eigh input — without it the solver's x-space accumulates anti-Hermitian components invisible to the Hermitising eigh but present in the residual norm. (3) Line 589 on result.x: the solver's final x never passed through line 544 (it is solver state, not a residual input), and H_final feeds state_final.H_qp_dft which gw_jax uses algebraically as sigma_total = H_qp_dft − kin_ion (per gw_jax.py:556-580 comment), so Hermiticity matters beyond eigh there. Cost is O(nk·nb²) — negligible next to one eigh.

item: src/gw/sc_iteration.py:664-681 — dump_sigma_omega_h5_final calls private ppm_pipeline._write_sigma_omega_h5 with SimpleNamespace(sigma_kij_h5_path=None) stub
evidence: Functionally safe today, verified by branch enumeration: _write_sigma_omega_h5 (ppm_pipeline.py:236-278) reads sigma_omega.sigma_kij_h5_path ONLY in the 'elif' streamed-fallback branch (line 269) which requires sigma_c_omega is None; dump_sigma_omega_h5_final early-returns when sigma_result.sigma_c_omega_kij_ry is None (sc_iteration.py:662), so the in-memory branch (line 259) is always taken and the stub attribute is never dereferenced. But the seam is real coupling: sc_iteration must know a private ppm_pipeline function's internal branch structure (the comment at lines 669-672 is literally documenting another module's control flow) and imports build_ppm_sigma_runtime_options for it. If ppm_pipeline ever consults another sigma_omega attribute, this breaks silently. Fix: split _write_sigma_omega_h5 into an in-memory writer taking the tensor directly (both callers then share it), per the single-source-of-truth convention.

item: src/gw/sc_iteration.py:244 — _rotate_to_dft_basis einsum 'kmp,kpq,knq->kmn'; chi_from_dipole.py:37 notes opposite direction
evidence: Index math checked per element, both directions. eigh(H_dft) gives H·U = U·diag(E) with columns of U the eigenvectors in the DFT basis, so U[k,m,n] = ⟨DFT_m|QP_n⟩ (matches docstrings at sc_iteration.py:10 and 629). QP→DFT: O_dft[m,n] = Σ_pq ⟨DFT_m|QP_p⟩·O_qp[p,q]·⟨QP_q|DFT_n⟩ = Σ_pq U[m,p]·O_qp[p,q]·conj(U[n,q]) = (U·O_qp·U†)[m,n] — exactly einsum('kmp,kpq,knq->kmn', U, O_qp, conj(U)) with the docstring formula at line 243 element-for-element. DFT→QP (what chi_from_dipole needs for the velocity operator): V_qp[m,n] = Σ_pq conj(U[p,m])·V_dft[p,q]·U[q,n] = (U†·V_dft·U)[m,n], i.e. 'kpm,akpq,kqn->akmn' with conj(U) first — which is precisely what chi_from_dipole.py:30-40 writes down, explicitly cross-referencing that it is the OPPOSITE of _rotate_to_dft_basis 'which does U·O·U† to bring Σ from QP back to DFT'. Both directions are correct for their respective transports and each site documents the other. This is a documented direction convention, not a trap; usage in gw_jax.py:600-620 (rotating QP-basis SigmaResult fields back to DFT) is the correct direction with an extensive CRITICAL comment.

item: src/gw/cohsex_sigma.py: get_cohsex_kernels (lines 337-344)
evidence: Zero live references by exhaustive mechanism enumeration. Imports: only gw_jax.py:44-47 (build_Gij, compute_cohsex_sigma), sigma_dispatch.py:154 (compute_cohsex_sigma, compute_v_h_sigma_x), sigma_x_bispinor.py:154 (private _make_cohsex_kernels, bypassing the wrapper); no 'import *' in src/gw. String dispatch: no getattr/importlib hit on 'cohsex'. CLI: pyproject.toml [project.scripts] = gw.gw_jax:main, centroid.kmeans_cli:main only. tests/, tools/, scripts/, docs/, sandbox skills/, runs/**/*.{sh,py,in}, templates/: zero hits (only the audit's own report mentions it). Docstring-claimed consumer (SC-COHSEX loop) actually routes through sigma_dispatch.compute_sigma_xc (sc_iteration.py:75) -> compute_cohsex_sigma(Gij=None) (sigma_dispatch.py:165-171), never the raw kernels. CAVEAT for the refactor: delete only the 8-line wrapper; the underlying _make_cohsex_kernels is alive via sigma_x_bispinor.py:154-156.

item: src/gw/cohsex_sigma.py:80 _inv_sqrt_nk = -1.0/sqrt(Nk) (hidden global minus sign)
evidence: Sign algebra verified per call site: _convolve (line 87) returns prefactor*FFT[G*V*(-1/sqrt(Nk))]. sigma_sx (line 98) passes prefactor=1.0 -> net -1/sqrt(Nk)*FFT[G_occ*W], matching module docstring line 6 (Sigma_SX = -project[...]) and the standard Hedin/BGW COHSEX sign (screened exchange negative, like Fock); the same kernel with V_q gives bare Sigma_X, also correctly negative. sigma_coh (line 106) passes prefactor=-0.5 -> (-0.5)*(-1/sqrt(Nk)) = +1/(2 sqrt(Nk)), matching docstring line 7 (Sigma_COH = +G_RI(W-V)/(2 sqrt(Nk)), the +1/2 G(W-V) Coulomb-hole term). NOT a bug -- code, docstring, and physics agree. But the constant's name asserts a positive normalization while holding a negative value, and the visible kernel prefactors (+1.0, -0.5) read as sign-flipped relative to the physics unless the reader knows the constant is negative. Refactor: rename to _neg_inv_sqrt_nk or move the minus into the prefactors ((-1.0, +0.5)) so each kernel's sign is local and self-documenting.

item: src/gw/cohsex_sigma.py:136-139 _add_static_head: do_screened passed to static_head_terms_to_kij AND coh_h zeroed afterwards
evidence: First-pass 'belt-and-braces / redundant double gate' hypothesis is WRONG -- the two gates do different, non-overlapping things and the zeroing is load-bearing. head_correction.py:621-625: do_screened only selects sx_diag = sigma_sx_diag vs sigma_x_diag for the FIRST return value; the SECOND return is unconditionally expand(head.sigma_coh_diag) regardless of the flag. So without coh_h = jnp.zeros_like(coh_h) at cohsex_sigma.py:138-139, the do_screened=False path would add a spurious COH head to sig_coh (which is otherwise exactly zero there, since W_q=V_q makes the coh kernel operand W-V=0). The other two bare-X call sites are consistent: cohsex_sigma.py:230-231 and 309-310 both unpack (x_head, _) and discard the coh return. Do NOT remove the zeroing in the refactor. Optional cleanup (not required for correctness): fold the coh gating into static_head_terms_to_kij itself so the helper's do_screened gates both channels -- all three call sites already discard/zero the coh piece when do_screened=False, so that move is safe.

item: src/gw/cohsex_sigma.py:170-173 unenforced contract: caller must pass V_q as W_q when do_screened=False
evidence: Contract is real and currently enforced only at call sites: gw_jax.py:291-292 ('if not config.do_screened: W_q = V_q' -- 'unscreened: W = V') is the sole production enforcement; sigma_dispatch.py:163 independently defaults W_static = W_by_role.get('static', V_q) and only takes the screened compute_cohsex_sigma path in COHSEX mode (V-only modes go to compute_v_h_sigma_x, which takes no W at all). If a future caller passes a real W with do_screened=False, sig_sx silently becomes screened-SX while the head added to it (static_head_terms_to_kij with do_screened=False -> sigma_x_diag) is the bare-X head, and sig_coh becomes nonzero with its head zeroed -- an inconsistent, hard-to-spot result. One-line internal enforcement in compute_cohsex_sigma ('if not do_screened: W_q = V_q') removes the trap without adding any API layer, and lets gw_jax.py:292 drop its copy (single source of truth).

item: src/gw/cohsex_sigma.py:60-73 kernel cache keyed on id(mesh_xy), never evicted
evidence: Repo-wide pattern, not local weirdness: 25+ id(mesh)/id(mesh_xy) cache keys across src (ppm_sigma.py:513,569; w_isdf.py:53,209,285; v_q_g_flat.py:71; v_q_tile.py:509-844; common/isdf_fitting.py x9; common/symmetry_maps.py:360,583; common/wfn_transforms.py:571; file_io/wfn_loader.py:491; file_io/zeta_reader.py:384; bandstructure/htransform.py:135; gw/sc_iteration.py:213). wfn_loader.py:488-490 documents the safety argument explicitly: 'id(mesh) is fine because the mesh outlives the loader in every production driver (the mesh is built once at top-of-main and threaded through every kernel)' -- and gw_jax.py:77 is indeed the single production Mesh construction. The theoretical stale-id hazard (GC'd mesh, CPython id reuse -> kernels sharded on a dead mesh) exists only for callers that churn Mesh objects in one process; no such caller exists in src. Only v_q_tile.py:236-254 bothers with eviction, because its keys include per-run tile shapes; the cohsex cache holds at most a handful of (mesh, kgrid) entries. Any fix (e.g. weakref-keyed caches) should be a repo-wide sweep, not a one-off in this file.

item: src/gw/cohsex_sigma.py:40-47 build_Gij DO-NOT-REVERT note (host numpy instead of jnp)
evidence: Note is accurate and backed by verifiable history: commit 7781b80 ('gw_jax + wavefunction_bundle: _build_Gij and _build_occ on host (numpy)', 2026-04-18) exists in this checkout and its message documents the measured effect -- each jnp zeros/eye/at[].set/asarray fired a standalone pjit at trace time (~8 cache misses), and the numpy conversion took the wavefunction_setup timing section from 1.79s to 0.09s (20x). The array is tiny host bookkeeping ((nk, nb_sigma, nb_sigma) < 1 MiB) with a single device_put at return (line 51). Keep as-is in the refactor, exactly as the note demands.

item: src/gw/scissor.py:169-170 — denom < 1.0e-30 guard in _ols_line
evidence: denom = sum((x-mean)^2) over eV-scale fit abscissae is ~0 only when all fit energies are (near-)degenerate; two points split by 1 ULP at 1 eV give denom ~6e-33, so 1e-30 catches both exact and ULP-level degeneracy where the slope would be float-noise garbage. Fallback (slope=0, intercept=mean(y), RMSE=std(y)) is the correct degenerate limit and matches the explicit n==1 branch at scissor.py:163-164. Standard numerical guard, not underflow cruft; harmless and load-bearing for degenerate-band fit sets.

item: src/gw/scissor.py:247-254 — _ols_line RMSE discarded, residuals recomputed on DeltaE
evidence: First-pass hypothesis ('the two RMSEs differ') is WRONG — they are algebraically identical. _ols_line residual: E_qp − (α·E_dft + β). Recompute at 249-252: (E_qp − E_dft) − ((α−1)·E_dft + β) = E_qp − α·E_dft − β. Same expression, same mask. Edge branches also match: n=0 both give 0.0; n=1 (α=0, β=y0) both give resid≡0; degenerate-denom (α=0, β=ȳ) both give E_qp − ȳ. The comment at 244-246 claiming ΔE-RMSE is a distinct 'human-readable' quantity is vacuous, and the recompute is pure duplicated work — delete lines 249-254 and use the returned RMSE (violates the sandbox no-redundancy rule). Zero behavioral risk in the change.

item: src/gw/scissor.py:229-241 — independent per-k argsort of E_DFT and E_QP, masks reordered by DFT permutation only
evidence: Index math: pair p at k = (E_dft[k, order_dft[k,p]], E_qp[k, order_qp[k,p]]); vm/fm gathered by order_dft only (237-238), so rank-p QP inherits rank-p DFT band's occupation/in-grid flags. Docstring (183-201) explicitly documents this: band identity dropped for energy-sorted matching ('the right pairing for a scissor fit'), masks 'tied to the DFT band each E_DFT_sorted[k,p] came from'. Callers: gw_jax.py:684 one-shot G0W0 where QE eigenvalues are already ascending so both sorts are no-ops (comment at gw_jax.py:680-683); sc_iteration.py:368 QSGW per-iteration where H_diag reorderings are the case the sort exists for. The reader's caveat is real but bounded: a QP rank crossing the v/c gap or ω-grid boundary mislabels ONE point's fit assignment in an O(nk·nb)-point OLS — graceful degradation requiring a pathological (gap-inverting or grid-edge-crossing) iterate, and the design tradeoff is documented in-code. Not a silent-corruption bug.

item: src/gw/scissor.py:123-151 classify_bands_in_grid *_ev params vs Ry caller at gw_jax.py:665
evidence: Confirmed: gw_jax.py:664-666 passes E_dft_rel_ry and omega_grid_ry[0]/[-1] into E_kn_ev/omega_min_ev/omega_max_ev. Function body (scissor.py:146-148) does only (E >= min) & (E <= max) comparisons plus an all-reduce — fully unit-agnostic when both sides share units, which the docstring already states ('same reference as the ω-grid', line 135). The Ry usage is deliberate per the block comment at gw_jax.py:634-636 ('All quantities below are in Rydberg... mixing that with eV-converted h0/Σ_x is a footgun'); the other caller (gw_jax.py:506-507) passes eV consistently. Misleading parameter names only; no bug. A rename to unit-neutral names would be a trivial cleanup but nothing depends on it.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L19 _maybe_init_jax_distributed
evidence: 12 live consumers under /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/scripts/ do `from gw.gw_jax import _maybe_init_jax_distributed` (debug_zct_ws.py:12, dump_fit_bufasgn.py:19, validate_si10_4gb_dump.py:12, test_iocallback_sharded.py:10, t6_hlo_si10_8gb.py:19, validate_fft_workspaces.py:21, validate_aot_chooser.py:12, t6b_hlo_si10_4gb.py:14, dump_fit_hlo.py:17, bisect_fit_rchunk_compile.py:26, test_remat_effect.py:8, debug_mem_stages.py:10); scripts/profiling/pf.py:144 and docs/ENVIRONMENT_COMPREHENSIVE.md:365 reference it by name. The shim comment at gw_jax.py:17-18 ('a few sandbox scripts import this name') is accurate. The first-pass reader saw these hits but wrongly ruled them out-of-scope; the audit scope explicitly includes lorrax_sandbox scripts as call mechanisms. Removing the alias breaks all 12 scripts at import time.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L21 write_sigma_omega_h5 import
evidence: Grep of the whole repo + sandbox: the ONLY hit inside gw_jax.py is the import line itself (src/gw/gw_jax.py:21). No string-dispatch, no `getattr(gw_jax, ...)`, and no re-export consumer exists — every `from gw.gw_jax import X` anywhere (repo src/tests/tools + sandbox scripts/skills) imports only _maybe_init_jax_distributed, main, or main_phase, never write_sigma_omega_h5. The function itself stays alive through file_io (src/file_io/sigma_output.py:254, exported in file_io/__init__.py:37) via its real callers ppm_pipeline.py:251,261 and sc_iteration.py:664-674; only the gw_jax.py import binding is dead. All other same-named grep hits in gw/ are a bool kwarg `write_sigma_omega_h5=` (ppm_pipeline.py:299, sigma_dispatch.py:106, sc_iteration.py:305), not the function. Safe to delete from the L20-23 import list.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L1-2, L13-15 module-level side effects (set_default_env before import jax; distributed init at import time)
evidence: runtime/__init__.py:47-62 documents set_default_env 'Must be called BEFORE import jax — JAX reads these at import time' and uses os.environ.setdefault so user overrides win; init_jax_distributed (runtime/__init__.py:109-133) is explicitly idempotent via the _LORRAX_JAX_DISTRIBUTED_DONE env sentinel, with a docstring explaining the Perlmutter Cray-MPICH one-GPU-per-rank hang it works around. The same import-order pattern is deliberately replicated across all entry-point drivers (psp/run_nscf.py:24, psp/run_sternheimer.py:49, centroid/kmeans_cli.py:11, ffi/cusolvermp/profile_batched.py:37). It IS import-order load-bearing — a refactor that moves line 2 below `import jax` silently loses JAX_PLATFORMS/JAX_ENABLE_X64 defaults — but that constraint is documented at both ends and is the established LORRAX convention for drivers, and the 12 sandbox scripts importing gw.gw_jax rely on the import-time init.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L224-269 block_until_ready + del chi0 donation choreography (no .watch())
evidence: The donation contract is real and documented on BOTH sides of the seam: w_isdf.py:226-230 has `@partial(jax.jit, donate_argnums=(1,))` with the comment 'chi_flat is donated (position 1): the caller releases chi0 right ... (see gw_jax.main — the del chi0_q inside the W.exec timing block)', matching gw_jax.py:244 (chi0_q.block_until_ready), :255 (del chi0_q after IBZ slice), :264-268 (solve_w then del chi0_q_solve, 'the reference is now invalid'). The ban on _chi_sec.watch() (gw_jax.py:234-236) is correct JAX semantics: a bound `chi0_q_solve.block_until_ready` method stored on the section object holds a strong reference to the array past the solve_w call, so XLA cannot donate the (nq,mu,mu) complex buffer and peak memory grows by one full chi0. Fragile, yes — any refactor extracting this block must preserve reference lifetimes — but it is a deliberate, cross-referenced memory optimization, not an accident.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L276-277 _n_sym_spatial = sym_perm.shape[0] // 2
evidence: The //2 is guaranteed exact on this path: _sym_perm comes from _resolve_ibz_q_list (gw_jax.py:213-220) which builds it via compute_centroid_sym_perm(..., extend_trs=True) (v_q_g_flat.py:176-198), producing shape (2*ntran, n_rmu) — first half spatial ops, second half TRS-composed. v_q_g_flat.py:443-450 performs the identical halving with the comment 'sym_perm came from compute_centroid_sym_perm(..., extend_trs=True) so its shape[0] is 2*ntran'. Consumer semantics: unfold_v_q (symmetry_maps.py:161-237) documents sym_idx in [0, 2*n_sym_spatial) and 'values >= n_sym_spatial' trigger conj. Per-element math: W_full[f,mu',nu'] = exp(2*pi*i q.(L[s,mu']-L[s,nu'])) * W_ibz[irr_idx[f], alpha[s,mu'], alpha[s,nu']], conjugated iff s >= ntran, with alpha/L rows for TRS ops living in the table's second half — so indexing is in-bounds by construction. This IS the fix for the historical TRS-blind bug (spatial-only table indexed with TRS-augmented indices -> silent OOB clip); the halving recovers ntran from the always-TRS-augmented table. Caveat for refactorers: passing a spatial-only (non-extended) table here would silently halve ntran — the invariant lives in the extend_trs=True call site, not in an assert.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L237 quad/e_ref defined only under do_screened but consumed at L421 (dynamic) and L520-522 (SCInputs)
evidence: Confirmed latent NameError by static trace (could not execute — login node). quad, e_ref bind only inside `if config.do_screened:` (gw_jax.py:199, 237). Consumers: L419-421 (compute_screening, gated `mode.is_dynamic and not self_consistent`) and L520-522 (SCInputs(quad=quad, e_ref=e_ref), gated only on `config.self_consistent`, L475). SCInputs has NO defaults for quad/e_ref (sc_iteration.py:103-104). The only validation is gw_config.py:745-748, which raises 'use_ppm_sigma=true requires do_screened=true' — but ONLY on the compute_mode='auto' legacy-inference path. Two unguarded configs crash with NameError instead of a clean config error: (a) explicit compute_mode=gn_ppm/hl_ppm + do_screened=false (bypasses the auto-path check, ComputeMode(raw) at gw_config.py:757 accepts it, mode.is_dynamic=True -> L421); (b) self_consistent=true + do_screened=false (SC Hartree-Fock, a physically legal request; mode resolves to X_ONLY, no check anywhere -> L522). Fails loudly but late (after the full ISDF/V_q build, potentially hours in); fix is a config-level cross-validation or defining quad/e_ref unconditionally.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L534-540 SC hyperparameters from LORRAX_SC_* env vars
evidence: Self-declared temporary scaffolding: gw_jax.py:534-535 reads 'TODO: plumb max_iter / tol_ev through config; env vars for now so the first end-to-end QSGW test can vary them without rebuild.' Five knobs (LORRAX_SC_MAX_ITER=20, LORRAX_SC_TOL_EV=1e-4, LORRAX_SC_ACCEL=rcrop, LORRAX_SC_DEPTH=5, LORRAX_SC_MIXING=1.0) control run_self_consistency (L545-551) — physics-affecting parameters invisible to cohsex.in, the manifest, and docs/docs_gwjax/COHSEX_INPUT.md, so SC runs are not reproducible from their input files alone. The values ARE printed to the log (L541-544), which mitigates but does not fix provenance. Hypothesis confirmed: promote to config keys (an [sc] section) per the in-code TODO.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L600 import of sc_iteration._rotate_to_dft_basis
evidence: The cross-module use is sanctioned, not sneaky: sc_iteration.py:755 lists "_rotate_to_dft_basis" in __all__ with the comment 'used by main() to rotate SC SigmaResult fields', and gw_jax.py:590-599 carries a CRITICAL comment explaining why (SigmaResult fields live in QP basis, kin_ion in DFT basis; skipping the rotation makes the L745-746 eigh 'off by tens of eV per band on MoS2'). So it is a documented, correctness-critical seam deliberately exported — but the underscore name contradicts its __all__/public status and invites a future 'cleanup' that deletes the export and silently... actually loudly breaks main. Minimal fix: rename to rotate_to_dft_basis (public), keeping the single source of truth (definition sc_iteration.py:242, second internal use at :314). Matches the no-parallel-routines rule; classification: naming/API hygiene, not a bug.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L767-771 try/except Exception: pass around multihost_utils.sync_global_devices
evidence: Context (gw_jax.py:757-772): one-shot WFN_qp.h5 is written synchronously by rank 0 only (L760-766), then all ranks attempt sync_global_devices('oneshot_qp_wfn_h5_write'). The barrier's only job is to keep non-zero ranks from racing past the write; nothing later in main() reads WFN_qp.h5 back, and all subsequent output writes are themselves rank-gated, so a swallowed sync failure cannot corrupt results — in a genuinely broken multihost job the next real collective fails loudly anyway. The bare except is a single-host/CPU-compat shim (import + collective can misbehave off-SLURM, e.g. the CPU fallback path from fallback_to_cpu_if_no_gpu_backend). Sloppy (should be narrowed to ImportError/RuntimeError or at least print0 a warning) but I could construct no input/state where the swallow produces wrong output rather than, at worst, a less-tidy failure mode of an already-failing run.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L400-405 history note: analytic q->0 PPM head removed in 1542342, re-added 2026-04-25
evidence: The comment is explicitly self-justifying: 'kept here because it explains a specific decision and is not yet captured anywhere else' (gw_jax.py:400-401), and it is not purely comment-only memory — it cites a durable artifact, reports/mos2_kgrid_gnppm_head_convergence_2026-4-10/ (L405), plus a git commit hash (1542342) recoverable from history. The +-W^c(0)/(2*Vcell*Nk) ~ 1.24 eV/band magnitude documents why a future reader must not 're-simplify' the head injection away — exactly the class of silent-regression this sandbox's TRS/head-bug history shows agents make. Placement at the mode-dispatch decision point is deliberate. Optional improvement (move the physics note into docs/docs_gwjax/ and leave a pointer), but as-is it is working institutional memory, not cruft or a bug.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L649-651 hard-coded max_iter=120, tol_ev=1e-7/RYD_TO_EV, mixing=0.6
evidence: These parameterize solve_diagonal_sigma_fixed_point (qsgw_utils), the inner on-shell diagonal Dyson fixed point E = h0 + Re Sigma(E) per (k,n). Unlike the L534-540 SC env-var knobs (outcome-shaping, iteration-count-limited), these are numerical-safety internals: tol 1e-7 eV is ~4 orders below the meV scale of any reported comparison, mixing 0.6 is a damping factor for a scalar interpolated fixed point, and max_iter=120 is a generous ceiling — the converged answer is insensitive to all three, so they behave like eigh workspace constants, not physics knobs. No TODO marks them, no test or config references them, and per-band non-convergence would surface in the printed 'Diagonal SC: ... n_iter iterations' line (L669-671). Config-exposing them would add input-surface for no observed need; leave hard-coded.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L830 float(efermi_dft_ev or 0.0)
evidence: Enumerated the cases: efermi_dft_ev is either a float from ppm_outputs (L446), float(wfn.efermi)*RYD_TO_EV in SC (L615), or None in static one-shot modes (L446 with ppm_outputs None). `x or 0.0` diverges from `x if x is not None else 0.0` only for falsy-but-not-None x, and the ONLY falsy float is 0.0 itself, which the `or` maps to 0.0 — the correct value. NaN is truthy and passes through. So the claimed footgun has no realizable failure case with THIS fallback constant; E_F exactly 0.0 and None both yield an 'Edft-Ef' column equal to raw E_dft, which for None (static modes, no Fermi computed) is the intended fallback and for 0.0 is exact. It would become a real bug only if the fallback were ever changed to nonzero; an `is not None` check would be more honest but the current code cannot produce a wrong number. This is a debug-table column only (gated on config.debug.sigma_freq_debug_output, L805), not physics.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L187 getattr(isdf, 'wf_bundle_transverse', None)
evidence: The getattr default is unreachable: prepare_isdf_and_wavefunctions has exactly ONE return statement (gw_init.py:1324-1328, verified by grepping all `return SimpleNamespace` in the function span) and it always sets wf_bundle_transverse — to the transverse Wfns bundle in the bispinor fresh path, or explicitly to None in the restart path (gw_init.py:1320-1323, with a comment that bispinor restart is not-yet-supported 'so consumers fail loud'). The result is a fresh in-process SimpleNamespace, never unpickled/persisted, so no stale-object version can lack the field. Plain `isdf.wf_bundle_transverse` is strictly equivalent today; the getattr is leftover defensiveness from when the field was being bolted on. Downstream gating (bispinor_v_q_path at L188-191) keys off None correctly either way. Harmless; simplify opportunistically.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py L96 argparse default input='cohsex_test.in'
evidence: No live invocation relies on the default: the regression test passes -i explicitly (tests/test_gw_jax_regression.py:131, `[-m gw.gw_jax, -i, input_file.name]` with the fixture named cohsex_test.in in tests/regression/cohsex_debug/), and every production entry point passes -i cohsex.in (skills/execute_workflow/SKILL.md:211,238,315; run.sh scripts under runs/). The default's name matches the regression fixture (tests/test_gw_jax_regression.py:14), i.e. a test-flavored default that leaked in, and it mismatches the templates/cohsex.in convention — a bare `python -m gw.gw_jax` in a run dir fails with a file-not-found for cohsex_test.in rather than picking up cohsex.in. Since the failure mode is an immediate loud error on a usage no documented workflow performs, this is cruft, not a bug; changing the default to 'cohsex.in' (or making -i required) is a one-line cleanup.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: fit_head_gn (348-360)
evidence: Enumerated all call mechanisms: from-imports of head_correction occur only in gw_jax.py:53 (HeadResolver, compute_static_head_terms_from_sample, format_head_sample_diagnostics, format_static_head_diagnostics), cohsex_sigma.py:26 (static_head_terms_to_kij), ppm_pipeline.py:35/68-72/128 (HeadResolver, fit_head_hl_analytic_from_sample, fit_head_ppm_from_samples, fit_head_with_fixed_omega_from_sample, format_head_diagnostics, compute_ppm_head_sigma_kij), bse_io.py:502/816 (apply_q0_head_rank1[_sharded]). No wildcard imports; gw/__init__.py re-exports only get_bandranges/read_*_input; no getattr/importlib dispatch (grepped src, tools, scripts); pyproject [project.scripts] targets only gw.gw_jax:main and centroid.kmeans_cli:main; tests/test_head_correction.py:3 imports only compute_static_head_terms, resolve_head_override, static_head_terms_to_kij; grep over /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/{skills,runs,docs,templates,tools} = zero hits. Only mentions: definition, docstring cross-ref head_correction.py:643, stale prose docs/theory/physics.md:540 (says GN-PPM path uses fit_head_gn — actual path is _fit_head_correction -> fit_head_ppm_from_samples, ppm_pipeline.py:102). All three live fit paths go through fit_head_ppm/fit_head_hl_analytic/fit_head_with_fixed_omega directly; the GN wrapper (probe_omega=1j*omega_p) is never invoked.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: fit_head_gn_from_samples (489-494)
evidence: Same exhaustive enumeration as fit_head_gn: zero references outside its own definition anywhere in lorrax_D src/tests/tools/scripts/docs or the sandbox skills/runs trees. ppm_pipeline._fit_head_correction (ppm_pipeline.py:60-107), the sole head-fit dispatcher, selects fit_head_with_fixed_omega_from_sample | fit_head_hl_analytic_from_sample | fit_head_ppm_from_samples; the GN-specific wrapper (which just forwards probe_omega=1j*omega_p_ry to fit_head_ppm_from_samples, lines 492-494) is unreachable. Safe to delete together with fit_head_gn; also fix the stale docstring ref at head_correction.py:643 and docs/theory/physics.md:540.

item: fit_head_ppm:305-315 and fit_head_hl_analytic:402-407 degenerate fallback Omega_h=1.0 placeholder
evidence: Physics effect is nil, verified through the only consumer: compute_ppm_head_sigma_kij early-returns zeros when abs(R_h)<1e-30 (head_correction.py:690-691), and both fallbacks set B_h=0.0, R_h=0.0. The rank-1 BSE injectors never see HeadGNParams (they take raw vhead/whead scalars). But the diagnostic claim is confirmed: format_head_diagnostics (706-728) prints 'Omega_h^2 = 1.000000 Ry^2 / Omega_h = 1.000000 Ry (13.6 eV)' with no degenerate marker — the '(degenerate)' label at line 727 only triggers when abs(omega_h)<=1e-30, which the placeholder omega_h=1.0 deliberately avoids. A log reader sees an apparently fitted 13.6 eV pole. Cheap fix: print a 'degenerate fit, head disabled' line; no numerical consequence.

item: fit_head_ppm:320-332 negative Omega_h^2 branch (omega_h=|Omega_h^2|^0.5, R_h from it)
evidence: Explicit algebra: with omega_h_sq<0, B_h=-w1*omega_h_sq (line 318, signed) and R_h=B_h/(2*|Omega_h^2|^0.5) (line 322). In compute_ppm_head_sigma_kij the on-shell occupied static limit is R_h/(V*Nk*omega_h) = B_h/(2*|omega_h_sq|*V*Nk) = -w1*omega_h_sq/(2*|omega_h_sq|*V*Nk) = +w1/(2*V*Nk) — the SIGN-FLIPPED value of the normal-branch static limit -w1/(2*V*Nk) that the model is anchored to (docstring 652-657: must reduce to -W^c(0)/(2*V*Nk) for occupied). So the fallback pole does not reproduce the very W^c(0) sample it was fitted from. It also matches no BGW convention: BGW handles wtilde2<0 via explicit sig%invalid_gpp_mode cases 0-3 (mtxel_cor.f90:778-791 — skip mode / purely imaginary sqrt(COMPLEXIFY(wtilde2)) / fixed 2 Ry / static-COHSEX 1/TOL_ZERO); 'real pole at |Omega| with signed residue' is none of them. Edge case (normal screening gives omega_h_sq>0: w1,w2<0, |w1|>|w2| => w2*wp^2/(w1-w2)>0), but when triggered it is silent and self-inconsistent. Should either zero the mode (BGW case 0 equivalent, like the degenerate branch) or use B_h=-w1*|omega_h_sq| to preserve the static limit, plus a warning.

item: fit_head_hl_analytic:411-412 I_eps_head<=0 -> 1.0 silent fallback
evidence: Confirmed physics-changing and silent: unlike the degenerate branch, this path continues with B_h=-w1*omega_p_sq != 0 and R_h != 0 (lines 414-417), so a head contribution IS injected with a fabricated bare-plasmon pole, and format_head_diagnostics prints it as fitted. Reachable only when I_eps_head=-w1/vc0<=0 with |w1|>=1e-30 and |vc0|>=1e-30 (line 402 catches the tiny cases), i.e. w1=W(0)-v>=0: an antiscreened static head, which is unphysical input (broken eps0mat/dipole or convention mismatch). Given garbage-in, any pole choice is arbitrary, so not a numerical bug in valid runs — but it converts a should-be-loud data problem into a plausible-looking number. Needs at minimum a print_fn-style warning (the function takes no print_fn — plumb one or raise); note module has no logging, diagnostics go through returned strings.

item: resolve_head_sample:191-195 source_order fallthrough to non-requested source
evidence: Deliberate design: docstring line 106 ('using overrides and configured source order'), want_source validated with printed default at 112-115, and the resolved provenance is carried in HeadSample.source ('epshead' vs 's_tensor', lines 140/183) and printed in every run header via format_head_sample_diagnostics line 263 ('Head source: ...'), so the actual source used is always visible in gw.out. RuntimeError at 197-199 if both fail. Residual sharp edge confirmed though: when the user asks epshead and eps0mat.h5 is simply absent, from_epshead returns None with NO message (lines 123-124 — contrast from_s_tensor which prints at 153), so the fallback happens without an explicit 'requested source unavailable' warning; only the source line reveals it. A one-line print on the missing-file branch would close the gap without changing the (intentional) resilience semantics.

item: resolve_head_sample.from_epshead:147-149 bare 'except Exception' swallow
evidence: head_correction.py:147-149: 'except Exception as exc: print_fn(f"epshead wcoul0 failed: {exc}"); return None'. Intent is clear (pragma: no cover, diagnostic resilience: fall through to s_tensor), and the exception MESSAGE is surfaced via print_fn plus the resulting 'Head source: s_tensor' line, so it is not fully silent. But the catch wraps EPSReader construction AND compute_q0_averages (lines 130-139), so a genuine bug in either (shape mismatch, h5 schema drift, bad S_cart=None path) degrades to a source switch with one log line and no traceback — exactly the class of masking that made the TRS-blind sym bug hard to find. Narrow to (OSError, KeyError, ValueError) or re-raise after printing when want_source=='epshead' was explicitly configured.

item: resolve_head_sample.from_s_tensor:162-163 occ[:, :min(nelec,nb)]=1.0 spin degeneracy
evidence: Factor-of-2 verified clean for THIS code path. wfn.nelec is NOT the electron count: wfn_loader.py:193-198 sets nelec = max(ifmax) = 1-based index of highest occupied band (falls back to count of occs>0.5), i.e. occupied-BAND count. So occ[:, :nelec]=1.0 marks exactly the occupied bands for both nspinor=1 (nelec=N_e/2) and nspinor=2 (nelec=N_e). Spin degeneracy is then carried entirely by compute_S_omega's prefactor, chi_from_dipole.py:153: pref = 4/(V*Nk*nspin*nspinor) — nspinor=1,nspin=1 gives 4/(V*Nk) = 2(spin) x 2(Ry units); nspinor=2 gives 2/(V*Nk) (one electron per band, no degeneracy). Per-state weight ratio nspinor=1 : nspinor=2 = 2:1 as required. compute_S_omega independently recounts nelec from f_nk (line 151), consistent. HOWEVER the verification uncovered a real factor-of-2 in the NEIGHBORING HL path — see separate ppm_pipeline.py:92 verdict.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:92 HL analytic omega_p^2 = 16*pi*meta.nelec/V uses band count, not electron count (NEW finding from item-8 verification)
evidence: The comment (ppm_pipeline.py:90-92) says 'omega_p^2 = 16*pi*N_e/V_cell in Ry^2' — the f-sum plasmon needs the ELECTRON count. But meta.nelec = wfn.nelec = max(ifmax) = highest occupied band index (common/meta.py:49,98; file_io/wfn_loader.py:196). For nspinor=2 (bispinor FR WFNs, the common sandbox case) band count == N_e and the formula is correct. For nspinor=1 scalar WFNs, ifmax = N_e/2, so omega_p^2 is halved and omega_p is low by sqrt(2): Si (8 valence e-, V~270 bohr^3) gives 16*pi*8/270 => omega_p ~ 16.6 eV (correct, matches experiment) vs 16*pi*4/270 => 11.7 eV as coded. Affects only ComputeMode.HL_PPM head-pole fitting (line 92-94) on nspinor=1 runs; GN 2-point fit and COHSEX static head are untouched. Fix: use nelec * (2 if nspinor==1 else 1) (and audit nspin=2 collinear, where max(ifmax) is also not N_e).

item: HeadResolver cache (202-252) no invalidation for eqp self-consistency
evidence: Confirmed as a documented, deliberately deferred limitation, not an oversight: common/chi_from_dipole.py:69-90 spells it out — 'HeadResolver.at(omega) caches S(omega) computed at iteration 1's basis... The SC loop reuses these cached samples for all subsequent iterations, so the analytic head_gn is fitted to DFT-basis transitions and never refreshed', quantifies the error ('few percent per head value, ~10s of meV per band; wrong near gap closure'), and lays out the 4-step fix roadmap (rotate dipoles, recompute deltaE from E_QP, refresh f_nk, invalidate the cache) plus the note that the file is 'intentionally NOT modified'. HeadResolver's own docstring (203-216) states the memoization rationale (3x redundant Voronoi-integral work per run). Known deferred TODO; when SC-GW on the head becomes load-bearing, implement the documented roadmap rather than an ad-hoc cache flush.

item: apply_q0_head_rank1:790 vs apply_q0_head_rank1_sharded:831 — q=0 hardcoded at index 0, k axes on opposite sides of (mu,nu)
evidence: Correctness verified at both call sites: q=0 at mesh index (0,0,0) follows the FFT-ordered k-mesh convention used throughout (compute_vcoul zeroes the G=G'=0 element at that slot, module comment 735-745), and each variant's docstring pins its layout — dense (...,nkx,nky,nkz,n_mu,n_nu) with .at[...,0,0,0,:,:] (773,789-790) matching the GW-side V_qmunu consumed at bse_io.py:816-830; sharded (n_mu,n_nu,nkx,nky,nkz) with .at[:,:,0,0,0] (830-831) matching the BSE dual-sharded W_q at bse_io.py:502-505. Index math: dense adds v*conj(G0)[mu]*G0[nu] to the q=(0,0,0) (mu,nu) block; sharded adds the same rank-1 outer product (conj(g0_X)[:,None]*g0_Y[None,:], line 822) to slice [:,:,0,0,0] — same element set, different axis order. So no bug — but this is exactly the parallel old/new twin-routine pattern the codebase standard forbids (two implicit layout contracts, shared _head_rank1_scalars helper already extracted at 748). Unify on one layout or one routine taking the k-axis position explicitly.

item: HeadGNParams.omega_p semantics drift (299, 426, 472)
evidence: Drift confirmed across all three producers: fit_head_ppm:295-299 stores probe_mag=|probe_omega| with an in-code admission of the drift ('"omega_p" was historically the imaginary-axis magnitude; for HL it's the real frequency itself'); fit_head_hl_analytic:426 stores the true f-sum plasmon sqrt(omega_p_sq_ry); fit_head_with_fixed_omega:472 stores omega_h itself. Sole consumer is diagnostics: format_head_diagnostics:716 prints 'W^c(q->0, omega=iwp) = ... [wp={omega_p} Ry]' — a label that is wrong for two of the three producers (for fixed-omega it prints the user's Omega_h as 'wp'; for HL wc_head_iwp=0.0 is printed against a 'iwp' label that never existed). No numerical consequence (grep confirms omega_p is read nowhere else). Rename to probe_label_ry or split per-model; low risk since diagnostic-only.

item: compute_ppm_head_sigma_kij:637 eta=1e-6 Ry default + module-wide 1e-30/1e-14 magic thresholds
evidence: Confirmed unplumbed: the only caller (ppm_pipeline.py:139-147) omits eta, so 1e-6 Ry (~13.6 microEV) is always used, while the BODY sigma kernel's on-shell regularization IS configurable (ppm_sigma.py:1526: sigma_regularization_ry, default 0.018374661 Ry = 0.25 eV, via ppm_options) — a 4-orders-of-magnitude inconsistency in nominal broadening between head and body. Practically inert: the head poles sit at omega-eps_n = -/+Omega_h with Omega_h ~ 1-2 Ry (plasmon scale), far from any evaluated QP shell, so the denominators are never near-singular in practice and eta only guards exact-degeneracy corner cases. The 1e-30 guards (305, 322, 402, 463, 690, 722) are pure zero-division sentinels and the 1e-14 checks (91, 126, 140, 183, 267) are omega==0 tests on float inputs — standard.
