Harmless as-is; if the head is ever evaluated on-shell near a pole, plumb eta through ppm_options alongside sigma_regularization_ry.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py — e_ref parameter of compute_ppm_sigma_pipeline
evidence: e_ref appears exactly once in ppm_pipeline.py (L290, the parameter); zero body uses, no locals()/**kwargs. Callers gw_jax.py:431 and sigma_dispatch.py:215 pass it, but passing into a sink is not liveness. Checked all mechanisms: no getattr/dict dispatch on the function, no tests/ tools/ pyproject console_scripts/ sandbox skills/ or runs/*.sh reference it (run scripts invoke gw.gw_jax as a module, irrelevant to a kwarg). BONUS: compute_sigma_xc's e_ref param (sigma_dispatch.py:98) is transitively dead too — its only consumption is the L215 pass-through into this dead sink (the live e_ref uses in gw_jax:431 and sc_iteration:285 feed compute_screening, a different call). Removing requires touching 3 signatures: ppm_pipeline:290, sigma_dispatch:98/215, sc_iteration:300 kwarg.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:320 — from . import w_isdf
evidence: Name w_isdf occurs only at L320 in the file; never referenced after. The commented purpose (ensure_compilation_cache) actually happens in ppm_sigma.py — module-level `from . import w_isdf` at ppm_sigma.py:77 plus explicit w_isdf._ensure_compilation_cache() calls at ppm_sigma.py:520/573/622 inside precompile/exec paths. ppm_pipeline imports from .ppm_sigma at module top (L36-40), so w_isdf is already in sys.modules before L320 runs; the local import is a guaranteed no-op. w_isdf has no import-time side effects (w_isdf.py:1-60: only defs and empty cache dicts; _ensure_compilation_cache is defined, not called, at module level). Safe to delete line 320 with zero behavior change.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:126-127 — streamed mode (sigma_c_omega is None) silently skips analytic q->0 head injection
evidence: compute_ppm_head_sigma_kij is called ONLY from _inject_analytic_head (repo-wide grep; head_correction.py:628 is the def), which returns (None, None) on streamed input (L126-127). Downstream: (a) _eval_sigma_c_at_dft_energies reads the raw streamed sigma_c_kij_ry h5 (L211-215) — headless diag(Sigma_c)(E_DFT); (b) _write_sigma_omega_h5 streamed branch copies the h5 verbatim via copy_sigma_kij_h5_to_omega_h5 (sigma_output.py:386-391, RYD_TO_EV scaling only, no head); (c) head_sigma_diag_w_kn_ry=None makes gw_jax.py:858 skip the sig_c_head(Edft) eqp column. ppm_sigma.py contains zero head handling (grep -i head: no hits) and no warning at streamed-mode selection (ppm_sigma.py:97-131). Docs COHSEX_INPUT.md:268-275 describe kij_stream with no head caveat. Aggravating: _fit_head_correction still runs and prints head diagnostics (ppm_pipeline.py:361-364 executes unconditionally before the skip), so logs look like a head was applied. Head magnitude is material: ~1.24 eV/band on Si 4x4x4 per the gw_jax.py:404 history note. Mitigating scope: KIJ_STREAM activates only when single-process AND sigma_kij_h5_file is set (ppm_sigma.py:125-130), so multi-GPU production runs are immune; but a single-process large-grid run with a stream path configured silently loses ~1 eV/band with a misleading log.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:263-265 vs 274-275 — RYD_TO_EV applied in in-memory branch, raw Ry passed in streamed branch
evidence: Numerically consistent, verified: copy_sigma_kij_h5_to_omega_h5 (file_io/sigma_output.py:340) multiplies internally — sigma_sx_kij_ev at L380 (`RYD_TO_EV * np.array(sigma_sx_kij_ev)`), hartree_kij_ev at L384, and the streamed sigma_c_kij_ry chunks at L388-391. So both backends emit eV files. The defect is the contract: copy_...'s parameters are NAMED `sigma_sx_kij_ev`/`hartree_kij_ev` but RECEIVE Ry values and convert inside, while write_sigma_omega_h5's identically-named kwargs receive already-converted eV values (ppm_pipeline.py:263-265). Same kwarg name, opposite unit expectation across the two backends — a future caller pattern-matching the name will double- or zero-convert. Rename copy_...'s params to *_ry or hoist the conversion to the caller.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:92 — omega_p^2 = 16*pi*N_e/V_cell (Ry^2) for HL analytic head
evidence: Physics verified explicitly: in Hartree a.u. omega_p^2 = 4*pi*n (e=m=hbar=1) with n = nelec/V_cell in bohr^-3; converting squared energy Ha^2 -> Ry^2 multiplies by 4 (1 Ha = 2 Ry), giving 16*pi*n in Ry^2 — exactly meta.nelec/meta.cell_volume as coded, and matching BGW's HL-PPM G=0 plasma frequency convention. The in-code comment (L89-91) already documents the factor derivation, and Omega_h^2 = omega_p^2/(1 - eps_head^-1) is the standard HL head pole. Correct as-is; centralizing the constant is optional polish, not a defect.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:168/238 — sigma_omega 'object' duck-typed param, SimpleNamespace stub in sc_iteration
evidence: Confirmed: the only attribute ever consulted is .sigma_kij_h5_path (ppm_pipeline.py:211 in _eval..., :269-271 in _write...), and only on the streamed branch (sigma_c_omega is None). sc_iteration.py:673 stubs SimpleNamespace(sigma_kij_h5_path=None) and always hits the in-memory branch (guarded by sigma_c_omega_kij_ry is None -> return None at sc_iteration.py:662). The real object is SigmaOmegaResult (ppm_sigma.py:147-150) which has that field. Contract works today but is enforced only by comments (sc_iteration.py:669-672); the honest signature is `sigma_kij_h5_path: str | None` instead of an opaque object — also removes the noqa: F821 annotations. Additional latent edge: at ppm_pipeline.py:258-278, if sigma_c_omega is None AND sigma_kij_h5_path is falsy, _write_sigma_omega_h5 returns out_path without writing any file — silent phantom path.

item: src/gw/sc_iteration.py:664 — imports private _write_sigma_omega_h5 from ppm_pipeline
evidence: Confirmed at sc_iteration.py:664 (`from .ppm_pipeline import _write_sigma_omega_h5`) inside dump_sigma_omega_h5_final, called at :674 with the SimpleNamespace stub. Two modules now depend on the underscore-private helper (its own module at ppm_pipeline.py:387 plus this cross-module import), making it de facto public API with a private name. Combined with the duck-typed sigma_omega param above, the clean refactor is: promote to a public function taking `sigma_kij_h5_path: str | None` explicitly. No correctness issue today.

item: src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:317 — docstring says step 4 diag interpolation is '(rank-0 only)'
evidence: Stale comment, confirmed by direct contradiction: the helper it describes, _eval_sigma_c_at_dft_energies, documents and implements all-rank replication — docstring L174-184 ('Interpolate diag(Sigma_c)(omega) at each DFT energy on all ranks ... so the result is consistent across ranks — required by the post-Sigma flow in gw_jax which now runs on all ranks'), implemented via extract_sigma_diag_replicated (L203-206) and, in the streamed fallback, rank-0 h5 read + broadcast_one_to_all (L209-220). The step-list comment at L317 predates the all-rank post-Sigma flow. Fix: change '(rank-0 only)' to '(replicated across ranks)'. Note the sibling comment at L371 already says 'replicated across ranks' — only the docstring line is stale.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:400-405 — history note on analytic head removal/re-add, on-shell magnitude ±W^c(0)/(2 V_cell N_k)
evidence: The comment self-declares intent ('kept here because it explains a specific decision and is not yet captured anywhere else') and cites the report reports/mos2_kgrid_gnppm_head_convergence_2026-4-10/. Formula cross-checked against the implementation: for a PPM pole B/(w^2 - Omega^2), residue R = B/(2*Omega) so W^c(0) = -B/Omega^2 = -2R/Omega, hence W^c(0)/(2*V_cell*N_k) = -R/(Omega*V_cell*N_k) — exactly ppm_pipeline.py:149-153 on_shell_occ = -R_h/(omega_h * cell_volume * nk_tot). Sign convention is internally consistent (occupied bands shift by -R/(Omega V N_k), the +/- in the comment covers occ/unocc branches). Not churn to fix — but it is the documented reason the streamed-mode head omission (suspected-bug above) is material.

item: e_ref parameter of compute_ppm_sigma_pipeline (src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:290)
evidence: Entire function body (ppm_pipeline.py:281-409) read: e_ref appears only in the signature; no locals()/**kwargs/getattr use. Enumerated call mechanisms: only two call sites exist (gw_jax.py:431, sigma_dispatch.py:215, both keyword-passing e_ref=e_ref); zero hits in tests/, tools/, docs/, pyproject.toml entry points, lorrax_sandbox/skills, and runs/*.sh for compute_ppm_sigma_pipeline. Caveat for the refactor: removing it requires editing both call sites since it is keyword-only after '*'; the e_ref VALUE is alive elsewhere (screening.py:109, w_isdf.py:446-459) — only this pipeline's acceptance of it is dead. Likely vestigial from when the probe-frequency chi0+W solve lived inside this pipeline (docstring L302-310 says that was deliberately moved out to gw.screening.compute_screening).

item: from . import w_isdf at src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:320
evidence: Only occurrence of 'w_isdf' in ppm_pipeline.py (grep). The bound name is never used in the function. Import side effects ruled out by reading w_isdf.py top level: stdlib/jax imports, two empty module-level cache dicts (_chi_minimax_kernel_cache, _w_solve_cache), and function defs only; the compilation-cache activation the comment refers to (_ensure_compilation_cache, w_isdf.py:38) runs inside compute_chi0/precompile_* calls (w_isdf.py:584,626,668), not at import time. w_isdf is also already imported by the driver before this pipeline runs (gw_jax.py:237 uses build_static_quadrature), so even module initialization is a no-op here. A function-local unused import cannot be reached by string dispatch/CLI. Comment 'for ensure_compilation_cache + cache hit timings' is a leftover from removed code.

item: omega_p^2 = 16*pi*N_e/V_cell HL analytic head constant (src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:92)
evidence: Unit math checks out: in Hartree AU omega_p^2 = 4*pi*n_e (n_e = nelec/V_cell in bohr^-3); Ry energies are 2x Hartree so omega^2 picks up factor 4 -> 16*pi*n_e in Ry^2, exactly as the L90-91 comment states. Consumed by fit_head_hl_analytic (head_correction.py:378-428) which implements Omega_h^2 = omega_p^2 / I_eps with I_eps = 1 - eps^{-1}(0,0) = -W^c(0)/v(0) (L410-415), explicitly documented as mirroring BGW Sigma/wpeff.f90's f-sum-rule q=g=g'=0 special case. This is the standard bulk-plasmon formula, printed as 'BGW-style' at L96-98. Minor nit only: the 16*pi literal could live next to fit_head_hl_analytic in head_correction.py (single consumer today, so low value).

item: streamed mode silently skips analytic q->0 head injection (src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:126-127)
evidence: Confirmed real gap, not compensated elsewhere: grep 'head' in ppm_sigma.py returns ZERO hits, so the streamed _StreamedH5Accumulator writes Sigma_c with no head; _inject_analytic_head returns (None,None) when sigma_c_omega is None (L126-127) with no warning print; _eval_sigma_c_at_dft_energies then reads the headless sigma_c_kij_ry from the streamed h5 (L211-215), so eqp/sigma_diag 'sigC_EDFT' values are headless too; copy_sigma_kij_h5_to_omega_h5 (sigma_output.py:341-395) only unit-converts, adds no head. Docs (COHSEX_INPUT.md:268-275) describe kij_stream with no accuracy caveat. Magnitude is material: gw_jax.py:403-404 history note quotes ~1.24 eV/band on Si 4x4x4. Mitigating scope: _select_accum_mode (ppm_sigma.py:97-131) only selects KIJ_STREAM when single-process AND sigma_kij_h5_path is set (or auto with >0.5GiB grid + path), so multi-rank production runs fall back to KIJ_HOST and get the head. Still: a single-GPU large-grid run silently loses ~eV-scale head. Minimum fix is a loud warning or head injection during the h5 copy (head is band-diagonal, cheap to add per omega-chunk).

item: RYD_TO_EV applied in in-memory branch but raw Ry passed in streamed branch of _write_sigma_omega_h5 (src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:263-265 vs 274-275)
evidence: Behavior is correct — no unit bug: copy_sigma_kij_h5_to_omega_h5 (file_io/sigma_output.py:341-395) multiplies sigma_sx, hartree (h5 create_dataset data=RYD_TO_EV*...) and each Sigma_c omega-chunk (dset_out[ibeg:iend] = RYD_TO_EV * dset_c[...]) internally, and its docstring says 'Convert a Ry-units streamed ... to an eV-units file'. But the convention split is genuinely confusing: Ry-valued arrays are passed into kwargs NAMED sigma_sx_kij_ev/hartree_kij_ev (named after the output datasets, not the input units). Additional real divergence found: the streamed output file contains only sigma_c/sx/hartree datasets and OMITS sigma_total_kij_ev, which write_sigma_omega_h5 always creates (sigma_output.py:315-318) — no in-repo reader of sigma_total_kij_ev exists outside sigma_output.py, so currently harmless, but the 'one writer, two backends' claim in the L247 docstring is not schema-true. Refactor: rename the copy helper's kwargs to *_ry and emit the same dataset set from both backends.

item: sigma_omega: 'object' duck-typed param + SimpleNamespace stub contract (src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:168,238; sc_iteration.py:664-681)
evidence: Confirmed fragile implicit contract: _eval_sigma_c_at_dft_energies reads sigma_omega.sigma_kij_h5_path at L211 and _write_sigma_omega_h5 at L269; the real object is SigmaOmegaResult (ppm_sigma.py:147-150) but the annotation is the string 'object' with noqa F821. sc_iteration.py:667 does 'from .ppm_pipeline import _write_sigma_omega_h5' and L673 fabricates SimpleNamespace(sigma_kij_h5_path=None), relying on a comment-level guarantee ('only consulted attribute is sigma_kij_h5_path') that nothing enforces — if _write_sigma_omega_h5 ever reads another SigmaOmegaResult field, the SC final-dump path breaks at convergence time (worst moment). It works today because the elif at L269 short-circuits on sigma_kij_h5_path=None. Simplest fix consistent with the no-new-API-layers rule: change both helpers to take sigma_kij_h5_path: str | None directly instead of the object, deleting the stub.

item: docstring says step 4 diag interpolation is '(rank-0 only)' (src/gw/ppm_pipeline.py:317)
evidence: Confirmed stale: the helper it describes, _eval_sigma_c_at_dft_energies, documents the opposite at L174-181 — 'Interpolate diag(Sigma_c)(omega) at each DFT energy on all ranks', using extract_sigma_diag_replicated 'so the result is consistent across ranks — required by the post-Sigma flow in gw_jax which now runs on all ranks', and even the streamed fallback broadcasts rank-0's read to all ranks (L219-220, broadcast_one_to_all). The word 'now' in the helper docstring dates the outer docstring to before the all-rank post-Sigma refactor. One-line doc fix; no behavioral consequence. (Related mild staleness: _inject_analytic_head's own docstring L116 says 'on rank 0' while the head add at L161 also executes on every rank on the replicated tensor.)

item: sc_iteration.py:667 imports private _write_sigma_omega_h5
evidence: Confirmed: sc_iteration.dump_sigma_omega_h5_final (sc_iteration.py:642-685) is a cross-module consumer of the underscore-private helper, making it de facto public API with two callers (ppm_pipeline.py:387 internal, sc_iteration.py:674 external) but a private name and an unannotated duck-typed second parameter (see previous finding). The usage is legitimate — it deliberately reuses the single canonical writer for the end-of-SC dump instead of duplicating it (consistent with the no-redundancy rule), so promotion (drop the underscore, take sigma_kij_h5_path directly) is the right move rather than inlining a second writer.

item: gw_jax.py:400-405 history note on analytic-head removal/re-add churn
evidence: The comment is explicitly self-justifying: 'History note (kept here because it explains a specific decision and is not yet captured anywhere else)' (gw_jax.py:400-401), citing commit 1542342 (removed Apr-10), re-add 2026-04-25, and reports/mos2_kgrid_gnppm_head_convergence_2026-4-10/. I verified its quoted on-shell magnitude against the current code: _inject_analytic_head L149-153 prints on_shell_occ = -R_h/(omega_h*V_cell*N_k); with R_h = B_h/(2*Omega_h) and B_h = -W^c(0)*Omega_h^2 (head_correction.py:416-417) this reduces exactly to +W^c(0)/(2*V_cell*N_k), matching the comment's ±W^c(0)/(2 V_cell N_k). So the note is accurate, deliberate documentation of resolved sign/convention churn, not evidence of a live problem — though it is the reason the streamed-mode head omission (finding above) deserves the suspected-bug rating: the head was already lost once by accident and had to be re-added.

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py — contributions dict (compute_sigma_x_bispinor L175/L191)
evidence: Function-local variable: declared L175, written L191, never read; compute_sigma_x_bispinor returns only sig_x_b (L199). No call mechanism can reach a local — repo-wide grep for 'contributions' in src/ shows only L175/L191/L197 (L197 is an unrelated error-message string). tests/test_sigma_x_bispinor.py imports only _wfns_with_lorentz_vertices (L80) and _TRANSVERSE_INDICES (L123), never the orchestrator; skills/runs invoke the module only through cohsex_sigma.py L241/243 and L316/318, which consume the returned array, not the dict. Nuance: the tr value stored in the dict IS live (printed at L193) — only the dict accumulation itself is write-only residue of the agent-B MoS2 cross-check (commit 69e8863, verified in git log: 'gw: bispinor Σ_X^B over 10 V_q Lorentz tiles').

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py L119-121 — docstring Σ^B formula ends with stray unmatched bras '⟨m', k'| ⟨m, k|'
evidence: Confirmed at L119-120: 'Σ^B[k, m, n] = -Σ_{i, j ∈ {1, 2, 3}} ⟨m, k| γ̃^i V^{i,j}_{q=k-k'} γ̃^j |n, k⟩ ⟨m', k'| ⟨m, k|'. The trailing '⟨m', k'| ⟨m, k|' is a truncated remnant of the internal-band sum Σ_{m' occ, k'} ⟨m,k|...|m',k'⟩⟨m',k'|...|n,k⟩ that the code actually implements via build_G with the Gij occupation projector (cohsex_sigma.py L96). Docs-only; code path (γ̃-dressed ψ → build_G → _convolve → project) is unaffected. Fix the docstring in the refactor.

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py L184-193 — in-loop diagnostic: 'kmm->' trace, bare except → nan, in-loop import, hardcoded agent-B/69e8863 reference
evidence: All confirmed: 'from common import RYD_TO_EV' at L186 executes inside the 3×3 double loop (9 times per call; cheap after first import but stylistically wrong); bare 'except Exception: tr = nan' at L189-190 swallows real errors; float(jnp.einsum('kmm->', contrib).real) at L188 forces a device→host sync per tile and runs UNCONDITIONALLY — only the print at L192-193 is gated by verbose, the trace computation and sync are not. Comment L184-185 hardcodes the validation context ('agent-B's MoS2 reference values (commit 69e8863)'). This is Milestone-A validation scaffolding in the production path (module is live: called from cohsex_sigma.py L243/L318). Refactor: gate the whole diagnostic behind verbose or delete it along with the dead contributions dict.

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py L31-33 docstring vs L178-182 — '6 unique kernel calls + 3 Hermitian fills' claimed, 9 sigma_sx_k launches executed
evidence: Code loops i,j ∈ {1,2,3}² = 9 sigma_sx_k invocations (L178-182); only V reads are deduplicated (BispinorVqReader.get_tile returns conj(swapaxes(V_companion)) for the 3 lower-triangle tiles, v_q_bispinor.py L857-867). Σ-level Hermiticity IS exploitable — index math: Σ^(i,j)[k,m,n] = Σ_{q,sx,ty} conj(ψ_xr)[k,m,s,x]·F_k{G^(ij)·V^(ij)_R[x,y]}·ψ_yn[k,t,y,n] with G^(ij) = Σ_ab (γ̃^i ψ)[s,x,a]·Gij[a,b]·conj(γ̃^j ψ)[b,t,y] (greens_function_kernel.py L25-26, wavefunction_bundle.py L379-381, cohsex_sigma.py L83-98). Conjugate-transposing in (m,n) and using Gij Hermitian, V^(ji)(q) = V^(ij)(q)^† on μ axes (reader L866), γ̃ Hermitian (L81-82), and x/y sharding the SAME transverse centroid set (L128-130 'All 9 transverse tiles share these centroids') gives Σ^(j,i)[k] = (Σ^(i,j)[k])^†. So 6 kernel calls + 3 adds of the (m,n)-dagger would reproduce the sum exactly, ~33% Σ^B convolution saving. Docstring described the intended design; implementation never got it. Caveat: verified under the stated assumptions; the psi_xr≡psi_yn identification (same ψ, transposed layout) is by convention of the flat-k bundle, not re-derived here.

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py L162-172 — _pad_V_to_padded: second padding source of truth on top of BispinorVqReader mesh padding
evidence: Confirmed redundant: reader-side _padded_shape_LR pads both V axes to mesh.shape['x']*mesh.shape['y'] (v_q_bispinor.py L839-843) and its own docstring asserts this 'makes Σ^B's V tile broadcast against ψ with NO further padding step in sigma_x_bispinor' (L828-834); ψ-side pads via round_up_to_mesh_product (runtime/padding.py L128-143, from common/load_wfns.py L418) to the identical mesh product for an (x,y) mesh. So the L167 early-return always fires today — _pad_V_to_padded is a live-but-no-op defensive layer, a second source of truth exactly as suspected. The n_L==n_R assumption (both padded to the single n_rmu_T_padded from psi_yr.shape[-1], L162) is structurally safe on THIS path since only TT tiles (i,j ∈ {1,2,3}) are fetched and _tile_shape gives n_L=n_R=n_rmu_T (v_q_bispinor.py L823-826), but would silently mis-pad if the function ever fetched a CT/TC tile. Unify: delete _pad_V_to_padded and trust the reader contract, or assert shapes instead.

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py L183/L198 — block_until_ready per tile serializes the 9 convolutions
evidence: L183 blocks after every tile; L198 (post-loop) is harmless. No comment justifies L183 as a memory bound — and it is partially redundant: the unconditional diagnostic float(jnp.einsum(...)) at L188 already forces a host sync per tile, so removing L183 alone changes nothing until the diagnostic (L184-193) is also gated/removed. The memory-bound rationale is plausible (each padded TT tile is (n_q, n_T_pad, n_T_pad) c128 read via SlabIO host IO, v_q_bispinor.py L871-875; without syncs several tiles could be resident concurrently), but it also prevents overlapping the next get_tile read with the current convolution. The refactor should decide deliberately: either keep one-tile-resident semantics with an explicit comment, or pipeline read/compute with a 2-tile budget. Couple this with removing the L184-193 diagnostic — they stand or fall together.

item: src/gw/sigma_x_bispinor.py — consumer of v_q_bispinor transverse IBZ-unfold (wrong in-plane Σ^B, CrI3 ~23% off / ~2 eV, z exact)
evidence: Confirmed in-repo, not just agent memory: /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/reports/bispinor_tt_conditioning_2026-06-16/report.md L6-23 — 'in-plane (x,y) Lorentz block loses C3-covariance... z-z and charge are covariant to 1e-8... transverse Σ^B differs ~2 eV in-plane, z-z exact'. The bug lives upstream: the transverse IBZ-unfold + 3×3 Lorentz mixing at v_q_bispinor.py L693-709 (unfold_v_q_bispinor_lorentz, active only when _use_ibz_T; full-BZ path is a no-op per L652-659 comment). sigma_x_bispinor.py itself is convention-correct math over whatever tiles the reader hands it, but has NO guard or warning when consuming IBZ-unfolded transverse tiles (grep for covarian/in-plane in both modules: zero hits). Mark this module as an affected consumer; fix on record per the tt-noncovariance finding is IBZ charge + full-BZ-direct transverse, i.e. force the L652 full-BZ branch for TT until the unfold is fixed.

item: gw_driver_helpers.py:90 hardcoded sys.path.insert('/pscratch/.../scripts/profiling') in _try_import_pf
evidence: Target exists (/pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/scripts/profiling/pf.py confirmed present) and the hardcode is what makes `import pf` work under srun/shifter from any cwd for all four lorrax_{A..D} checkouts, which share the one sandbox path; src/gw/PERFORMANCE.md:63,67,166 hardcodes the same path, so it is the de-facto sandbox convention. But it silently degrades on any other host (local WSL per CLAUDE.md memory-budget table) to a printed '[pf] profiling hooks unavailable' line, the docstring (gw_driver_helpers.py:58) claims a relative 'scripts/profiling' add, and sys.path.insert(0,...) executes on every profile_section.__enter__ (ppm_pipeline.py:352), growing sys.path each sigma call. Not a bug (fully guarded), but should resolve via env var (a PF_ARTIFACTS_DIR-style knob already exists at line 81) or single guarded insert.

item: gw_driver_helpers.py:225 sym_real.transpose(0,2,1) applied only in aux-WFN branch of build_bgw_v_grid_fn
evidence: The asymmetry is correct by construction: the else-branch input sym.sym_mats_k is ALREADY transposed by SymMaps (symmetry_maps.py:843 `self.sym_mats_k = self.sym_matrices[:wfn.ntran].transpose(0,2,1)` with comment 'these apply to k-points as sym_mats_k[i] @ [kx,ky,kz]'; convention block at :835-841: BGW mtrx acts on G column-form G'=mtrx@G, k-action is mtrx.T; also :1162 'mtrx.T (= sym_mats_k)'). The aux branch reads RAW mf_header/symmetry/mtrx from HDF5, so it must apply the identical conversion. Per-element: sym_mats_k[i,a,b]=mtrx[i,b,a] => (sym_mats_k[i]@k)_a = sum_b mtrx[i,b,a] k_b = (mtrx^T k)_a. Downstream use matches the canonical k-space convention: find_q_index does S_k@qf (read_bgw_vcoul.py:77) and fill_v_grid_for_q maps G_full = S_k@G_irr - kg0 (read_bgw_vcoul.py:176), same integer formula as symmetry_maps get_gvecs_kfull (:648, :689-690). One noted (harmless) inconsistency: the aux branch omits the TRS -S augmentation that SymMaps appends (symmetry_maps.py:855-856); harmless because BGW's vcoul q-list is spatial-only IBZ and v(-q-G)=v(q+G), but per the unified-sym-action principle this inline duplicate of the SymMaps conversion is a consolidation candidate.

item: gw_driver_helpers.py:270-272,28-30 ppm_sigma_scale / ppm_sigma_flip_neg / ppm_invalid_mode in PPMSigmaRuntimeOptions
evidence: Confirmed dead plumbing, hypothesis (leftover PPM bring-up debug knobs) verified via git archaeology. Current tree: grep of all src/, tests/, tools/ for 'sigma_scale|flip_neg|invalid_mode' excluding gw_config.py and gw_driver_helpers.py returns ZERO hits — the three fields are parsed/defaulted (gw_config.py:322-324, 585-587, 952-954), packed into the options struct, and never read by any sigma kernel. History: at commit 5470b174 the knobs were live — old src/gw_isdf/ppm_sigma.py:941 `neg_scale = float(sigma_scale) if sigma_flip_neg else -float(sigma_scale)` (sign flip of the omega<E_F branch, marked 'debug only' in old gw_init.py:579); the consumer was deleted in the cleanup series (237e787 'Eliminate ppm_options unpacking and delete obsolete debug blocks', then c785df4/2e5615e/a263dcd config consolidation) while the config plumbing and docs survived. Stale docs too: docs/docs_gwjax/COHSEX_INPUT.md:202 references ppm_invalid_mode='fixed_2ry', a mode that exists nowhere in the code; :257-260 document scale/flip_neg. Safe to delete fields + config keys + doc entries (all runs/*/cohsex.in occurrences set the inert defaults 1.0/false).

item: gw_driver_helpers.py:252-254 n_omega = floor((max-min)/step + 0.5) + 1
evidence: Standard inclusive-endpoint grid-count idiom guarding binary-float truncation: with the documented defaults (COHSEX_INPUT.md:223-226, max=5.0, step=0.25) (omega_max-omega_min)/step can evaluate to N-epsilon (e.g. 39.999999...) in float64, and plain int()/floor would drop the last grid point. Preconditions at gw_driver_helpers.py:245-248 (step>0, max>=min) guarantee the argument is >=0, where floor(x+0.5) is exact round-half-up, so n_omega = round((max-min)/step)+1 as intended. Grid then built as min + step*arange(n_omega) (lines 255-257), inclusive of both endpoints for step-commensurate ranges. No other call sites needed; magic idiom but correct and intentional.

item: gw_driver_helpers.py:93-104,117-125,155-173 pervasive except Exception in profile_section and setup_runtime
evidence: Documented best-effort design, and no failure is fully silent where it matters. profile_section's docstring explicitly promises graceful degradation ('Failures in any of (1)-(4) degrade gracefully... only visible effect is one print_fn line', lines 62-66); _try_import_pf prints the exception (line 94); only the snapshot/region-exit paths (103-104, 121-122) are silent, and those are pure profiling side-effects. setup_runtime: docstring states 'best-effort startup optimizations whose absence is not fatal' and 'failures are logged and swallowed' (lines 130-146); every except prints the exception with a labeled prefix (lines 159, 167, 173). Crucially each wrapped call is a WARMUP whose real initialization is retried on the hot path: ffi_loader.phdf5_init_mpi is 'Collective across all ranks; idempotent after first call' (ffi_loader.py:303-308) and MPI init recurs at the first collective H5Fcreate in zeta_fit_chunked, mpi4py initializes on its first real import by SlabIO, and the compile cache is optional. So a real MPI breakage surfaces loudly at first use, just later and after a logged skip line — the reader's concern is acknowledged in the design (deferred, not hidden). Residual theoretical hazard: divergent per-rank failure before a collective could desync ranks, but that is inherent to any optional eager-init and is mitigated by the log line.

Checked: no from-import (only scripts/checks/sigma_direct_check.py:69 imports the _from_Wc variant), no getattr/dict dispatch on 'extract_gn_ppm' anywhere, not in gw/__init__.py exports, not a console_script (pyproject.toml:32-34), zero hits in tests/ tools/, zero hits in /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/skills and runs/ (runs hits are old XLA dumps of fit_gn_ppm_from_wc_pair, a different live function). Live driver path is ppm_sigma.fit_ppm (ppm_sigma.py:690) -> fit_gn_ppm_from_wc_pair.

The other imports in the same block ARE used (fit_gn_ppm_from_wc_pair @690, solve_laplace_minimax_interval @776,871, solve_phase_minimax_bandwidth @853, MinimaxNodes @156,863). item: _TINY=1e-12 floor on x_min/A_dim (minimax_screening.py:29, applied at 579, 632, 680, 742, 801)
evidence: For a metallic/zero-gap input (cmin<=vmax), line 742 clamps x_min to 1e-12 instead of raising: R=x_max/1e-12~1e13, and per the module's own documented error convention (lines 570-576) the physical absolute error becomes target_error/x_min = 1e-6/1e-12 = 1e6 — a garbage quadrature returned silently (max_error is reported in the dataclass but never checked by any caller). No gap validation exists anywhere upstream: w_isdf.build_static_quadrature (446-459) passes raw enk slices, and w_isdf.py:595/636 separately compute E_gap=cmin-vmax UNclamped, so a negative gap makes the prefold exp(-tau*E_gap) a growing exponential against a quadrature fit on the clamped interval — internally inconsistent. The Laplace representation 1/x=sum(a*exp(-tau*x)) is undefined for x<=0, so the physically correct behavior is a hard error ('metallic system unsupported'), not a clamp. Never triggers for valid gapped inputs, so latent.

item: floats as lru_cache keys with ad-hoc rounding; sha256(JSON) disk keys (minimax_screening.py:599-604, 642-647, 699-705, 192-198)
evidence: Correctness-safe by construction: rounding round(logR,12)/round(target,14) only merges requests whose R differs by <~5e-13 relative — utterly negligible against target_error>=1e-14 (floor at line 581), and the merged result is a valid quadrature for either request since the solver re-derives R=exp(logR_key) (line 475). Boundary-straddling near-identical requests re-solve and store under a second sha256 key (lines 196-198) — a duplicate ~KB npz and one redundant solve, no wrong answer. Deterministic serializable keys are needed because the payload doubles as the on-disk identity (namespace_digest.npz). Minor cruft (cache dir accretes near-duplicate entries) but deliberate and safe.

item: pervasive bare 'except Exception: return None / pass' in catalog/table/disk-cache loaders and store cleanup (minimax_screening.py:36-37, 66-76, 85-94, 205-212, 235-240)
evidence: Verified every fallback path is correctness-preserving: corrupt catalog.json or table npz -> _pick_shipped_table returns None -> exact solver at 599/699 (same absolute-error convention, per docstring 106-113); corrupt disk-cache npz (205-212) -> recompute and overwrite via os.replace (234); failed store (235-240) -> just no caching. So no silent WRONG results — only silent slow results (exact minimax solve can be expensive at large R/max_nodes=500) with zero log line to explain why shipped assets stopped being used. Should warn on non-FileNotFoundError failures; not a bug.

item: shipped-table selection: 1e-18 error slop, exact-1e-12 eps_q match, sort key prefers loosest acceptable error (minimax_screening.py:134, 137, 144, 150)
evidence: All three behaviors are documented and conservative. Line 137 accepts entry_err <= target_error+1e-18 (float-equality slop, negligible vs err floors of 1e-14). Line 144's 1e-12 eps_q match: a nonstandard eps_q silently bypasses shipped tables — but the fallback is the exact solver keyed on the caller's actual eps_q (line 699-705), which is MORE correct than serving a mismatched-eps_q table. Sort key (entry_range, -entry_err, node_count) at 150: within the smallest adequate range it prefers the loosest error bound still <= target — every candidate already satisfies the requested tolerance (gate at 137), and looser bound = fewer nodes, which is the stated goal ('choose the loosest available error bound that is still <= requested target_error', docstring 170-177 and 106-113). Tests exercise exactly this rule (tests/test_minimax_assets.py:36,90). Easy to misread but working as designed.

item: hard-coded 7D squeeze [:, :, :, 0, :, 0, :] (minimax_screening.py:861-863 and 920-921)
evidence: Per-element: for X of shape (nkx,nky,nkz,s1,mu,s2,nu), X[:,:,:,0,:,0,:] keeps only the (s1=0,s2=0) block — for a bispinor ns=2 tensor that silently drops the (0,1),(1,0),(1,1) spin blocks instead of erroring. However I traced every producer of the 7D form: instances at 861-863 are inside the confirmed-dead legacy function; the live one (920-921, extract_gn_ppm_parameters_from_Wc) has exactly ONE caller, scripts/checks/sigma_direct_check.py:502-504, which manufactures the singleton axes itself ('wc0[None,None,None,None,:,None,:]'), so the squeeze is the exact inverse and cannot lose data today. The production driver (ppm_sigma.fit_ppm:690) uses flat-q (nq,mu,mu) and never enters this branch, and per the bispinor roadmap no bispinor W^c exists yet. Latent trap, not an active bug: an assert shape[3]==shape[5]==1 would make it safe; the docstring (908) already declares the singleton contract.

item: GN residue B from different objects: B=-0.5*Wc0*Omega (line 455) vs B=-0.5*pi0*Omega (line 884)
evidence: Each formula is the same GN algebra applied to its path's fitted object. GN single-pole model f(w)=2*B*Omega/(w^2-Omega^2) gives f(0)=-2B/Omega, hence B=-0.5*f(0)*Omega. Line 455 fits W^c directly (consumer: ppm_sigma sigma kernels consume B_q/Omega_q parameterizing W^c, per fit_ppm docstring ppm_sigma.py:678-683 and the omega_sq=-(z^2)*Wc_probe/(Wc0-Wc_probe) ratio at 443-445 which is the same model evaluated at the probe). Line 884 fits the interacting polarizability Pi=chi(I-V*chi)^{-1} (871-873), whose consumer would reconstruct W^c=V*Pi*V. Internally consistent per path — and moot, since the Pi-based path (extract_gn_ppm_parameters) is confirmed-dead above. The unification trap the reader flags is real but only matters if someone resurrects the dead path.

item: two weight conventions: MinimaxWindowPair prefolds w_i=alpha*exp(-tau) (line 752-753) vs MinimaxNodes raw alpha
evidence: Confirmed the WindowPair prefold convention has zero consumers (pair list discarded at w_isdf.py:457; w_i field never read anywhere). The live convention is: LaplaceMinimaxQuadrature carries raw (tau, alpha); w_isdf folds the chi0 prefactor itself as alpha_chi = -2.0*alpha*exp(-tau*E_gap) at w_isdf.py:598-601 and 636-640 (note: exp(-tau*E_gap), not the WindowPair's exp(-tau*1) — the two prefolds aren't even the same convention) before building MinimaxNodes, whose scan body just does chi_R_acc + alpha*chi_tau (w_isdf.py:185-188). The sigma path uses raw-alpha MinimaxNodes with its own external scalings (ppm_sigma.py:863 t/xi, alpha/xi). So MinimaxNodes+caller-side folding is the single live convention; the WindowPair w_i is dead legacy and dies with the dataclass layer.

item: time_axis string enum ('real'/'imag'/'crossing_hgl') with 1/xi rescale applied by caller (minimax_screening.py:308-349, ppm_sigma.py:863)
evidence: The sign conventions are correct per consumer: 'real' t=tau+0j for chi0's Laplace exp(-tau*dE) (w_isdf.py:170-176 takes jnp.real(t)); 'imag' t=-1j*tau for sigma's exp(-t*...) windows integrating on the imaginary-time contour (ppm_sigma.py:877 nodes = q.to_minimax_nodes(time_axis='imag')); 'crossing_hgl' keeps tau real and the caller applies MinimaxNodes(t=raw.t/xi, alpha=raw.alpha/xi) at ppm_sigma.py:862-863 with an inline comment. The split is explicitly documented on both sides (_crossing_to_minimax_nodes docstring 337-339: 'Callers that need to rescale by 1/xi apply that externally') and enables one shared complex128 pytree slot for both pipelines (MinimaxNodes docstring 293-298, sibling-kernel comments w_isdf.py:150 / ppm_sigma.py:1268). Unchecked strings raise ValueError on typo (326, 341), so no silent path. Deliberate design, not an accident.

item: solve_laplace_minimax_imag_interval: no use_shipped_tables, no shipped family, magic defaults (minimax_screening.py:618-659)
evidence: Asset-coverage gap confirmed: tools/generate_minimax_assets.py:37 DEFAULT_FAMILIES=('crossing','noncrossing') and line 89 hard-rejects anything else, so no 'noncrossing_imag' tables can even be generated; the function is LIVE (w_isdf.build_imag_quadrature:467-468 -> screening.py:151, the GN chi0(i*omega_p) probe) so every GN run pays an exact solve, mitigated only by the disk cache (499-501). The '2.0' defaults are NOT magic though: ppm_omega_p=2.0 / ppm_fallback_omega=2.0 are documented config defaults (gw_config.py:298-304, 'ppm_omega_p ~ 2 Ry by default' — the standard GN probe choice), and the |denom|>1e-14 gate (line 443) is the standard degenerate-fit guard whose failures are counted in unfulfilled_fraction and routed to fallback_omega. Verdict: imag path added later without asset coverage — worth adding a family or a use_shipped_tables hook, otherwise consistent.

item: fit_gn_ppm_from_wc_pair host sync (line 456) vs 'no host gathers' docstring (lines 433-435)
evidence: Docstring contradiction is real and verifiable by reading: line 456 unfulfilled_fraction = 1.0 - _scalar_to_host_float(jnp.mean(good...)), and _scalar_to_host_float (lines 40-46) does _mh.process_allgather when process_count>1 (a cross-process collective) and jax.device_get otherwise (a blocking device sync) — directly contradicting 'no host gathers and no communication beyond whatever sharding is already attached to the inputs' (434-435). Consequence: fit_ppm (ppm_sigma.py:690) blocks async dispatch at the fit boundary on every PPM build; in multihost it inserts an allgather that every process must reach, so a caller trusting the docstring and invoking this on a subset of processes would deadlock. The value itself is a correct diagnostic (consumed at ppm_sigma.py:710 print and PPMBuildResult). Classifying suspected-bug on the documentation contract (the safe fixes are: return the mean as a lazy jax scalar and let the printer sync, or amend the docstring); numerics are fine.

item: src/gw/cohsex_sigma.py:337-344 get_cohsex_kernels
evidence: Zero live references by every mechanism enumerated: (1) repo-wide grep over lorrax_D (*.py/*.sh/*.md/*.toml/*.yaml/*.in) hits only the definition at cohsex_sigma.py:337; (2) pyproject.toml [project.scripts] wires only gw.gw_jax:main (lorrax-gw, gw_jax) and centroid.kmeans_cli:main — no path to this symbol; (3) no string dispatch: grep for getattr/importlib/__getattr__ across src finds no cohsex/kernel-name construction (all getattr hits are vcoul.py:191 sys_dim, aot_memory_model attrs); (4) tests/, tools/, scripts/: no hits (covered by repo-wide grep); (5) sandbox-wide grep over /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox skills/, runs/ (run.sh invoke gw.gw_jax module), docs/, templates/, reports/: only identical definitions in sibling checkouts (blitz4-6) and the first-pass audit report reports/gw_refactor_map_2026-07-01/files/cohsex_sigma.md. The docstring's claimed consumer (SC-COHSEX loop) actually routes through sigma_dispatch: sc_iteration.py:73 imports compute_sigma_xc, which calls compute_cohsex_sigma(Gij=None) at sigma_dispatch.py:165-168 — the SC Gij-iteration hook is compute_cohsex_sigma's Gij parameter (cohsex_sigma.py:156, docstring 174-177), not this raw-kernel accessor. Note _make_cohsex_kernels itself is alive (sigma_x_bispinor.py:154-156); only the public wrapper is dead. Docstring is stale and should be deleted with the function.

item: src/gw/cohsex_sigma.py:80 _inv_sqrt_nk = -1.0/sqrt(Nk) (sign folded into normalization constant)
evidence: Per-element math verified correct against the module docstring convention (lines 6-7): _convolve(G,V,pref) = pref * FFT[G_R*V_R*(-1/sqrt(Nk))] = -pref/sqrt(Nk)*FFT[G_R*V_R]. sigma_sx (line 98, pref=+1.0) yields -1/sqrt(Nk)*FFT[G_occ*W] matching 'Sigma_SX = -project[FFT[G_occ(R)*W(R)/sqrt(Nk)]]' and the physically negative Fock exchange when called with V_q; sigma_coh (line 106, pref=-0.5) yields +1/(2 sqrt(Nk))*FFT[G_RI*(W-V)] matching 'Sigma_COH = +project[.../(2 sqrt(Nk))]'. So NOT a bug. However (a) the name _inv_sqrt_nk is wrong (it is MINUS 1/sqrt(Nk)) and the per-kernel prefactors (+1.0, -0.5) read as sign-flipped without it — a genuine refactor tripwire exactly as the reader claimed; (b) the same folded constant is triplicated at ppm_sigma.py:524 and aot_memory_model/kernels/sigma_kij.py:124, so it is a de-facto sigma-kernel convention but violates single-source-of-truth. Refactor should rename (e.g. _neg_inv_sqrt_nk) or move the -1 into explicit per-kernel prefactors (-1.0 SX, +0.5 COH) consistently at all three sites.

item: src/gw/cohsex_sigma.py:71 kernel cache keyed on id(mesh_xy), never evicted
evidence: Pervasive repo-wide convention, not local cruft: id(mesh)/id(mesh_xy) cache keys appear at 20+ sites (ppm_sigma.py:513,569; w_isdf.py:53,209,285; v_q_tile.py:509-844; v_q_g_flat.py:71; common/isdf_fitting.py:196,266,382,1144,1379; common/symmetry_maps.py:360,583; common/wfn_transforms.py:571; file_io/zeta_reader.py:384; file_io/wfn_loader.py:491; bandstructure/htransform.py:135). The rationale is documented at wfn_loader.py:488-490: 'id(mesh) is fine because the mesh outlives the loader in every production driver (the mesh is built once at top-of-main and threaded through every kernel)' — confirmed: gw_jax.py:124 builds mesh_xy once per process. The theoretical id-reuse-after-GC staleness is real but unreachable in production (singleton mesh, process-lifetime cache); fixing it here alone would be inconsistent with 20 sibling caches. Leave as-is in the refactor, or change all sites together (e.g. weakref keying) as a separate initiative.

item: src/gw/cohsex_sigma.py:136-139 _add_static_head do_screened 'double gate'
evidence: NOT belt-and-braces — the reader's hypothesis is wrong in mechanism; the two gates control different things and both are load-bearing. Gate 1: static_head_terms_to_kij(do_screened=...) selects only the FIRST return value — head_correction.py:621 'sx_diag = head.sigma_sx_diag if do_screened else head.sigma_x_diag' — the COH head (head.sigma_coh_diag) is ALWAYS returned nonzero (line 624). Gate 2: 'if not do_screened: coh_h = zeros_like(coh_h)' (cohsex_sigma.py:138-139) is therefore the ONLY suppression of the COH head in unscreened runs. It is required for consistency: when do_screened=False the caller passes W_q=V_q (gw_jax.py:291-292) so the kernel-level sig_coh = convolve(G_RI, W-V) is identically zero, and gw_jax consumes sig_coh unconditionally downstream (gw_jax.py:712 sigma_total = sig_sx + sig_coh + sig_h; line 876 coh_0 column) — without the zeroing, a spurious COH head would leak into sigma_total. The gw_jax comment at lines 345-350 about the 'Bare Sigma_X missing q->0 head' bug concerns head-vs-PPM gating, not this. Optional cleanup: make static_head_terms_to_kij zero its coh output when do_screened=False so the invariant lives in one place, but current code is correct.

item: src/gw/cohsex_sigma.py:40-46 build_Gij DO-NOT-REVERT host-numpy note
evidence: Comment's provenance verified against git history: commit 7781b80 ('gw_jax + wavefunction_bundle: _build_Gij and _build_occ on host (numpy)', 2026-04-18) exists and its message matches the comment exactly — each jnp zeros/eye/at[].set/asarray fired a standalone pjit at trace time (~8 cache misses), numpy conversion cut wavefunction_setup 1.79s -> 0.09s (20x). The array is tiny host bookkeeping ((nk, nb_sigma, nb_sigma) complex128, <1 MiB) with a single device_put at return (line 51). Keep as-is in the refactor, per the note.

item: src/gw/cohsex_sigma.py:170-173 unenforced contract: pass V_q as W_q when do_screened=False
evidence: The contract is real and is honored by both current callers, but only by caller-side discipline: gw_jax.py:291-292 does 'if not config.do_screened: W_q = V_q  # unscreened: W = V' before calling compute_cohsex_sigma (gw_jax.py:359-361 passes do_screened=config.do_screened); sigma_dispatch.py:163-171 only ever calls compute_cohsex_sigma with do_screened=True (COHSEX mode, W_static = W_by_role.get('static', V_q)) and routes everything else to compute_v_h_sigma_x which takes no W. Violation consequence if a future caller passes do_screened=False with a real W: sig_sx would silently be screened (kernel ignores the flag, cohsex_sigma.py:211) while its head uses the bare-X head (line 137 + head_correction.py:621), and sig_coh would be nonzero with its head zeroed — an inconsistent hybrid. Trivial to enforce inside compute_cohsex_sigma ('if not do_screened: W_q = V_q' or an assert); moving the substitution into the callee also lets the redundant copy at gw_jax.py:291 be deleted. Exactly the enforcement candidate the reader hypothesized.

item: LorraxConfig.use_ffi_io property (src/gw/gw_config.py:812-821)
evidence: Enumerated all mechanisms: (1) attribute reads — grep '\.use_ffi_io' minus 'use_ffi_io=' across src/tests/tools/scripts/docs/misc/config yields only src/file_io/slab_io.py:148,275, which is SlabIO's OWN instance attribute set in __init__ from its backend enum, not the config property; (2) string dispatch — grep getattr(...'use_ffi_io') and quoted-string dispatch: zero hits outside gw_config.py itself; (3) reflection — no dir()/vars()/asdict on LorraxConfig anywhere (asdict hits are aot_memory_model dataclasses only, and asdict would not touch properties anyway); (4) CLI entries (pyproject.toml:32-35: gw.gw_jax:main, centroid.kmeans_cli:main) reach it only via config.backend.slab_io routing built at gw_config.py:984-1018 from the INPUT KEY, which is alive and distinct from the property; (5) sandbox skills/runs — all hits are 'use_ffi_io = true/false' lines in cohsex.in input files (the key, not the property); (6) sole textual reference to 'config.use_ffi_io' is docs/dev/plans/unified_slab_io.md:179, a pre-refactor design-plan code snippet, not live code. Control check: the sibling back-compat property gspace_mode (gw_config.py:824-826) IS read at gw_init.py:720,842, proving the search method detects live aliases. Verdict: property has zero live readers.

item: LorraxConfig.isdf_memory_mode property (src/gw/gw_config.py:828-831)
evidence: grep '\.isdf_memory_mode' across src/tests/tools/scripts/docs/misc: only hit is the property definition itself (gw_config.py:829). _SCREENING_SOLVER_TO_LEGACY (gw_config.py:142) is used nowhere else. The input KEY 'isdf_memory_mode' is alive: parsed/validated at gw_config.py:852-858 and mapped to the enum at 1036 via _LEGACY_ISDF_MEMORY_MODE; all downstream consumers read the enum config.backend.screening_solver (src/gw/screening.py:164, src/gw/gw_jax.py:262,265) through w_isdf._normalize_screening_solver (w_isdf.py:314-329), never the legacy-string property. No getattr/string dispatch, no test/tool/skill references, no reflective iteration. Zero live readers of the property.

item: LorraxConfig.omega_grid_ry / omega_grid_ev properties (src/gw/gw_config.py:787-803)
evidence: grep '\.omega_grid_(ry|ev)\b' across src/tests/tools/scripts/docs/misc: every read is on (a) ppm_options — PPMSigmaRuntimeOptions, whose omega_grid_ev/ry are plain dataclass FIELDS (gw_driver_helpers.py:22-23) populated by an independent construction at gw_driver_helpers.py:252-258 (n_omega = floor((max-min)/step + 0.5)+1; grid = min + step*arange(n)); or (b) _sigma_result — SigmaResult fields (sigma_dispatch.py:78-79). Zero reads on any LorraxConfig object under any variable name (grep matches any receiver). No getattr dispatch, no test/tool/skill/doc-example reads, CLI paths go through build_ppm_sigma_runtime_options. Numeric equivalence check for safe deletion: config property uses arange(min, max+0.5*step, step) → count = ceil((max−min)/step + 0.5); helpers use floor((max−min)/step + 0.5)+1 — for x=(max−min)/step = n±ε (ε<0.5) both give n+1 points with identical values min + i*step, so the dead property is also redundant, not a divergent spec.

item: w_isdf.py:320 cross-module import of private _LEGACY_ISDF_MEMORY_MODE
evidence: The first-pass hypothesis is factually inverted: the dict is OWNED by gw_config (defined gw_config.py:137-141, used by the parser at 854/1036); w_isdf merely imports the private name inside _normalize_screening_solver (w_isdf.py:314-329), whose docstring explicitly states 'Kept narrow so the only place legacy strings cross over is here' — a documented, deliberate shim. However, the legacy-string acceptance path is now vestigial: every in-tree production caller passes the enum (screening.py:164, gw_jax.py:262,265 pass config.backend.screening_solver), and grep finds NO caller passing 'high_mem'/'low_mem'/'auto' strings or the memory_mode= kwarg (solve_w/precompile_solve_w signatures at w_isdf.py:658 accept it; nobody supplies it). Refactor: retire the string branch + memory_mode kwarg and the private import disappears; consistent with the project's no-parallel-paths rule.

item: gw_config.py:781-784 sigma_quadrature_config hardcoded crossing_max_nodes=max(500,...), crossing_eps_q=1.0e-3
evidence: crossing_eps_q=1.0e-3 exactly restates the SigmaQuadratureConfig dataclass default (src/gw/minimax_config.py:29), so no hidden divergence; crossing_max_nodes=max(500, self.ppm.sigma_max_nodes) restates the 500 default (minimax_config.py:28) while coupling it upward to the user's sigma_max_nodes so the crossing quadrature never gets fewer nodes than regular windows. These feed solve_phase_minimax_bandwidth for the 'core' crossing window (ppm_sigma.py:850-858: max_nodes=crossing_max_nodes, eps_q=crossing_eps_q, target_kind='hgl') — math-internal minimax-solver caps for a quadrature that intrinsically needs more nodes, consumed via ppm_pipeline.py:355 (sigma_window_quad=config.sigma_quadrature_config). Not physics knobs a user should sweep; sigma_target_error/sigma_max_nodes ARE exposed (ppm_sigma_target_error/ppm_sigma_max_nodes keys). The only cruft is the redundant restatement of the eps_q default. No bug.

item: gw_config.py:869-897 from_input_file env-var parsing: silent clamps and bare 'except Exception: pass'
evidence: Confirmed as described: ISDF_CHUNK_TARGET_UTILIZATION parse failure silently resets to 0.97 (871-874) then clamps to [0.85,1.0] (875) with no message; ISDF_ZCT_STAGE_CAP_GB parse failure hits 'except Exception: pass' (886-887) so a typo'd value (e.g. '8gb') silently disables the cap; ISDF_ZCT_STAGE_CAP_FRAC likewise (893-897), plus silent clamp to [0.10,0.95]. Behavior is defined and safe (never crashes a run; falls back to defaults), so not suspected-bug, but the failure scenario is real: an operator sets ISDF_ZCT_STAGE_CAP_GB=8gb expecting a ZCT cap, gets zct_stage_cap_gb=None, and the memory planner behaves as if uncapped with zero diagnostic — exactly the invisible-divergence class that made past planner debugging expensive in this project. from_input_file already threads print_fn (used at 865-867, 1003-1017); one warning line per swallowed/clamped value is the fix.

item: gw_config.py:152-157 _DEFAULTS physics defaults nval=5, ncond=5, nband=100, sys_dim=2
evidence: Confirmed footgun, no mitigating validation found: read_lorrax_input fills any absent key from _DEFAULTS with no warning (gw_config.py:441-443), there is no required-key list (grep 'required|must be set|missing' in gw_config.py: zero hits), and sys_dim flows straight to physics — gw_jax.py:150 sets meta.sys_dim = config.sys_dim, which selects the 2D slab-truncated Coulomb branch in compute_vcoul.py:362+ (f2d = 1 − exp(−zc·kxy)·cos(kz·zc)) versus the 3D 1/q² branch. Omitting sys_dim on bulk Si therefore silently runs 2D-truncated V(q): wrong physics, no error, plausible-looking output. Severity is amplified by the read_lorrax_input all-defaults fallback (separate finding). Defaults-by-design, so not suspected-bug in the code itself, but sys_dim (and arguably nval/ncond) should be required or warn-on-default; the sandbox template (templates/cohsex.in:9-12) always sets them explicitly, suggesting nobody relies on the defaults.

item: gw_config.py:270-276, 539 bare_coulomb_cutoff default None
evidence: The hypothesis (downstream default 4·ecutwfc mismatching BGW) is STALE for this checkout. The driver resolves None → wfn.ecutwfc in two synced places: gw_init.py:681-693 (_resolve_cutoff: 'if val is None: return float(wfn.ecutwfc)', with comment 'Both default to wfn.ecutwfc (matches BGW's screened_coulomb_cutoff default)') and gw_init.py:912-915, and passes the RESOLVED value to all kernels (gw_init.py:1023, 1107). It is also documented at the key definition: the zeta_cutoff comment at gw_config.py:272-273 says 'Defaults to ecutwfc (mirrors the bare-Coulomb default)', and compute_vcoul.py:213 says 'match BGW's bare_coulomb_cutoff (default: ecutwfc)'. The None-in-config is deferred resolution by necessity (ecutwfc lives in the WFN file, unavailable at parse time). Residual sharp edge: the low-level factories (compute_vcoul.py:859 make/compute entry defaults, v_q_g_flat.py:482) interpret a literal None as NO mask (compute_vcoul.py:351-353 'if vcoul_cutoff_ry is not None'), i.e. full FFT box ≈ ecutrho ≈ 4·ecutwfc — the likely origin of the memory note — but no in-tree caller passes config None through; only direct API users could trip it.

item: gw_config.py:383-386 + 456-457 read_lorrax_input silent header fallback / all-defaults path
evidence: Two distinct behaviors. (a) Typo'd header, lines 383-386: if no line starts with '[cohsex]', the first '[...]' line is used as section start and values are read via parser[parser.sections()[0]] (line ~417) — this is benign leniency; a file headed '[choesex]' parses all keys correctly. (b) NO section header at all, lines ~456-457: start stays None, the entire INI-parsing branch is skipped, and 'params = dict(_DEFAULTS)' — every user key=value line is silently DISCARDED, no warning, no error (configparser is never invoked so MissingSectionHeaderError never fires). Concrete failure: a cohsex.in written as bare key=value lines (no [cohsex]) in a directory where the default wfn_file='WFN.h5' exists runs to completion with nval=5, ncond=5, nband=100, sys_dim=2, use_ffi_io=True — a default-everything calculation masquerading as the requested one; only the K_POINTS block survives (parsed independently at lines ~460+). Unrelated but same class: typo'd KEYS inside a valid section are silently dropped too (params built only over _DEFAULTS keys, lines 439-454; only 3 legacy keys get explicit checks at 419-437). Minimum fix: raise or loudly warn when start is None and the file contains 'x = y' lines, and warn on unknown keys.

item: gw_config.py:878 unconditional 'import jax' vs 987-991 guarded 'import jax as _jax' in from_input_file
evidence: Module top imports are numpy-only (gw_config.py:31-40) — jax is deliberately kept out of import time (delays backend init); both jax imports are function-local inside from_input_file. Line 878 'import jax' is unconditional but its only use (jax.default_backend(), line 889) is gated on ISDF_ZCT_STAGE_CAP_FRAC being set — and that call is UNGUARDED. Lines 987-991 then re-import inside try/except with fallback _is_cpu_backend=False. Since line 878 already ran, the import in the try cannot fail; the except can only catch a default_backend() runtime failure — but the same failure would have raised unguarded at line 889 if the env var were set, and jax is a hard dependency of every driver module reached moments later. So the guard protects nothing real and the discipline is inconsistent, but no input/state produces wrong behavior: at worst identical crash timing. Cleanup: hoist one local 'import jax' and drop the try/except, or guard both call sites uniformly.

item: gw_config.py:791-803 omega grid np.arange with float step and +0.5*step endpoint fudge
evidence: Explicit index math: np.arange(min, max+0.5*step, step) has count = ceil((max−min+0.5·step)/step) = ceil((max−min)/step + 0.5). For the intended commensurate case (max−min) = n·step up to fp error ±ε with ε ≪ 0.5·step: ceil(n ± ε/step + 0.5) = n+1 points, elements min + i·step for i=0..n, last ≈ max; the point max+step is excluded because inclusion would require n+1 < n+0.5+ε/step, i.e. ε > 0.5·step. So endpoint inclusion flips only if fp error exceeds half a step — the standard robust half-step idiom, strictly MORE deterministic than bare arange(min, max, step) whose endpoint genuinely races fp rounding. The suspect's linspace concern is unfounded. Additionally moot: these properties are confirmed-dead (see omega_grid finding); the live construction at gw_driver_helpers.py:252-258 uses the equivalent floor((max−min)/step + 0.5)+1 count and produces identical element values (both min + i·step), so there is no divergent duplicate spec at runtime — just a dead copy to delete.

item: src/gw/w_isdf.py: resolve_minimax_energy_reference(reference_fn=...) parameter path (lines 387, 395-396)
evidence: Repo-wide grep (src/tests/tools/scripts/docs) plus sandbox skills/runs/tools/templates: 'reference_fn' appears ONLY at w_isdf.py:387/395/396. Sole caller of the function is build_static_quadrature (w_isdf.py:455-456), which passes reference= only. Config chain cannot inject a callable: gw_config.py:934 coerces minimax_energy_reference via str(...).strip().lower(); minimax_config.py:15 types energy_reference as str|float|int|None. No getattr/dict/string dispatch of the name, no CLI/console_scripts path, no test references. Function itself is alive; the reference_fn kwarg and its branch are unreachable.

item: src/gw/w_isdf.py:638-639 precompile_chi0 zero-node guard (if len(tau) == 0: return)
evidence: Two independent proofs. (1) Stale comment: at guard-introduction commit 36471a0 the kernel really had 'if len(tau_i)==0: return jnp.zeros(...)'; commit baa524b ('chi0: use build_G_tau; drop empty-tau wrapper') deleted it, and the current factory comment (w_isdf.py:193) states the compiled scan handles n>=1 with no short-circuit — the referenced static-zeros path in compute_chi0 no longer exists. (2) Unreachable: sole caller gw_jax.py:239 feeds a quad from build_static_minimax_window_pair (minimax_screening.py:715-772, raises on empty energies) -> solve_laplace_minimax_interval, whose exact solver calls noncrossing_grids(R, target, N_start=2, N_max=max_nodes) (minimax_screening.py:477, >=2 nodes) and whose shipped-table path loads concrete non-empty tau arrays (rejected only if node_count > max_nodes, minimax_screening.py:138). len(tau)==0 cannot occur; no test/tool calls precompile_chi0.

item: src/gw/w_isdf.py:437-441 flatten_V_qmunu 8-D/6-D legacy branches
evidence: Sole caller is gw_jax.py:198 (grep: only other hits are docs/architecture/codebase.md:325, PERFORMANCE.md:143 — prose, not calls; no tests/tools/scripts/sandbox references). Both producers of V_qmunu deliver 3-D: fresh path compute_V_q returns flat-q (nq, mu, mu) (gw_init.py:1130-1145), and the restart path load_restart_state_from_h5 performs the IDENTICAL 8-D/6-D -> 3-D normalization itself before returning (src/file_io/tagged_arrays.py:226-230, gw_init.py:1310-1312 passes rs.V_qmunu through unchanged). So arr.ndim==8/6 at w_isdf.py:437/440 is unreachable. BSE has its own independent shim (src/bse/bse_io.py:844-868) and does not import this one. Note: the branches also duplicate tagged_arrays.py logic — a single-source-of-truth violation; the function survives only as a pass-through.

item: src/gw/w_isdf.py:53/209/285 kernel caches keyed on id(mesh_xy)
evidence: The hypothesized id-recycling hazard is structurally impossible: every cached value closes over the mesh — _chi kernel via NamedSharding(mesh_xy,...) (lines 98-99, 101-111), _solve_w via mesh_xy.devices.size and shard_map(mesh=mesh_xy) (lines 239, 257), low-mem via batched_fused_w_solve(..., mesh=mesh_xy) (line 298). The module-level dicts (lines 32-33) are never evicted, so a cached mesh is pinned alive and its id() can never be reused by a new Mesh. Cost is only a leak-by-design identical to jit caches, plus duplicate compilation if an equal-but-distinct Mesh is built. Using the (hashable) Mesh itself as key would be marginally cleaner but changes nothing observable.

item: src/gw/w_isdf.py:59-79,124 Hermitian-swap conj trick (both Gv and Gc via fftn + call-site conj)
evidence: Core identity verified per-element: build_G_tau gives G_k(mu,nu)=Sum_n psi_n(mu) e^{-t(e_n-e_ref)} psi_n*(nu) (greens_function_kernel.py:35,54), Hermitian for real t. Then ifftn(G)_R(nu,mu)=Sum_k e^{+ikR} conj(G_k(mu,nu))=conj(fftn(G)_R(mu,nu)), so the historical 'Rambn,Rbnam->Rmn' (Gc via fftn, Gv via ifftn) equals Sum_ab fftn(Gc)(am,bn)*conj(fftn(Gv)(am,bn)) — the new same-index-order einsum at lines 181-182. This is exactly what eliminates the reshard (both operands keep P(_,_,'x',_,'y') from psi_xn/psi_yr, line 77-80). The absolute conj convention (line 124 conjugates BOTH G's — the conj moved out of the old builder per the line 122-123 comment) I could not re-derive from first principles read-only; it is pinned by the stated machine-precision A/B against the original formulation and the MoS2 regression. Deliberate performance refactor, not a bug.

item: src/gw/w_isdf.py:168-173 complex->float64 tau cast pinned to 'locked MoS2 3x3 regression hash'
evidence: The cast is load-bearing physics, not numerics-freezing cruft: the Hermitian-swap identity at lines 59-79/124/181 is valid ONLY if G_k is Hermitian, which requires real exp weights, i.e. real tau in build_G_tau (greens_function_kernel.py:54: phases=exp(-t*(enk-e_ref)) — complex t breaks G_k(mu,nu)=G_k(nu,mu)*). Nodes arrive complex128 only because MinimaxNodes is a shared pytree with the sigma path (lines 150-156); chi0's Laplace quad has Im(t)=0 by construction (compute_chi0 line 597-604 builds t from float64 tau). The comment justifies by regression hash where it should cite the Hermiticity requirement — a documentation weakness, but the code is correct and necessary.

item: src/gw/w_isdf.py:215-223 reshard_mid P('x',None,'y') two-hop reshard with hard-coded measured GB numbers
evidence: Deliberate, measured XLA-SPMD workaround: double with_sharding_constraint at lines 243-246 routes P(None,'x','y') -> P('x',None,'y') -> P(('x','y'),None,None) so SPMD plans two single-axis all_to_alls instead of full rematerialization; comment records the A/B measurement (2.95 -> 1.11 GB/dev at Si 4x4x4). The GB numbers are documentation, not code. Fragility across XLA versions is real but correctness-neutral — if XLA's planner changes, the worst case is a memory regression, never wrong numbers, and the comment gives the exact benchmark to re-run. Should be revalidated on XLA upgrades; not a defect.

item: src/gw/w_isdf.py:561 build_real_quadrature +branch R' diagnostic print
evidence: Genuine typo, print-only. The +branch minimax interval is [Omega+x_min, Omega+x_max] (lines 521-524), so R'=(Omega+x_max)/(Omega+x_min); line 561 prints Omega/quad.x_min + quad.x_max/quad.x_min = (Omega+x_max)/x_min. Example: Omega=200 Ry, x in [0.5, 5] Ry -> true R'=205/200.5=1.022, printed 410 — contradicting the docstring's own 'R' of either shifted interval ~1.03' (line 502). The -branch print at line 563, (Omega-x_min)/(Omega-x_max), is correct for its interval [Omega-x_max, Omega-x_min]. No numerical effect: tau/alpha come from solve_laplace_minimax_interval on the correct endpoints (lines 521-524, 532-535). Fix: (Omega+quad.x_max)/(Omega+quad.x_min).

item: src/gw/w_isdf.py:226-263 donate_argnums=(1,) on the chi0 argument of _solve_w
evidence: Deliberate, documented on BOTH sides of the module boundary: w_isdf.py:226-229 explains the donation and that V is NOT donated (reused by COHSEX/PPM); gw_jax.py W.exec block explicitly does 'del chi0_q_solve' right after solve_w with the comment 'chi0 is donated inside solve_w — the reference is now invalid. Do NOT touch chi0_q_solve after this', and the preceding block_until_ready choreography (gw_jax.py ~229-236) exists specifically to enable donation. The cross-module ownership coupling is real (any new caller must not reuse its chi0 buffer, and solve_w's public docstring at lines 355-372 does NOT mention donation — worth adding a line there), but this is a designed memory optimization, not cruft or a bug.

item: src/gw/w_isdf.py:539-543 (Omega-x) branch sign handling in build_real_quadrature
evidence: Verified term-by-term: x/(x^2-Omega^2) = -1/2 * 1/(Omega-x) + 1/2 * 1/(Omega+x). The -branch fits 1/y on y=Omega-x in [Omega-x_max, Omega-x_min] (lines 532-535): -1/2 * Sum alpha_i e^{-tau_i(Omega-x)} = Sum [-0.5*alpha_i*e^{-tau_i*Omega}] e^{+tau_i*x}, i.e. kernel form e^{-tau'*x} with tau'=-tau_raw (line 539), weight -0.5*alpha*exp(-tau_minus_raw*Omega) (lines 540-543) — exp correctly uses the RAW (positive) tau. +branch (lines 525-529) analogously uses +0.5 and tau_plus unchanged. Interval coverage and the compute_chi0 prefold stability with negative tau' are addressed in the docstring (lines 500-504, |tau'|~1/Omega). Pinned by tests/test_real_axis_quadrature.py (build_real_quadrature exercised at lines 71/91/127, plus the Omega<=x_max ValueError at 144). Correct as written.

item: src/gw/w_isdf.py:143 static_argnums=() on minimax_tau_integrate_chi jit
evidence: An empty static_argnums tuple is semantically identical to omitting the argument entirely — pure no-op. Present since at least commit 36471a0 (old line 132) and survived the MinimaxNodes refactor (6d2e5bc). The sibling jit _build_Gv_Gc (lines 101-111) and the W-solve jit (line 230) omit it. Safe to delete on the next touch; zero behavioral risk.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: fit_head_gn (lines 348-360)
evidence: Exhaustive reference hunt: (1) grep across all of lorrax_D (src, tests, tools, scripts, *.md/*.sh/*.toml/*.yaml/*.in) finds only the def at head_correction.py:348, a docstring cross-reference at head_correction.py:643 ('extracted in :func:`fit_head_gn`'), and a prose mention in docs/theory/physics.md:540 — none are calls. (2) Not in any __all__ (src/gw/__init__.py:11 exports only get_bandranges/read_lorrax_input/read_cohsex_input). (3) Not a console_script (pyproject.toml [project.scripts] lines 32-35: only gw.gw_jax:main x2, centroid.kmeans_cli:main). (4) No string dispatch: grep for quoted/f-string 'fit_head_' and getattr(...head...) across src/tests/tools/scripts finds nothing. (5) tests/test_head_correction.py imports only compute_static_head_terms, resolve_head_override, static_head_terms_to_kij. (6) Sandbox: grep of /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/skills and all *.sh/*.in/*.py under runs/ — zero hits. The live path is ppm_pipeline.py:_fit_head_correction (lines 68-107), which dispatches to fit_head_with_fixed_omega_from_sample / fit_head_hl_analytic_from_sample / fit_head_ppm_from_samples and constructs the GN imaginary probe itself (ppm_pipeline.py:336-337: 1j*config.ppm.omega_p), bypassing the fit_head_gn convenience wrapper entirely. The head_correction.py:643 docstring and physics.md:540 should be updated to point at fit_head_ppm when deleting.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: fit_head_gn_from_samples (lines 489-494)
evidence: Same exhaustive enumeration as fit_head_gn: repo-wide grep (code + docs + configs), __all__, pyproject console_scripts, string/getattr dispatch, tests/, tools/, scripts/, and sandbox skills/ + runs/*.sh all return zero references outside the definition itself — not even a docstring mention. The functionally-equivalent live call is ppm_pipeline.py:102-103, which calls fit_head_ppm_from_samples(head_static, head_probe, probe_omega=probe_omega) directly with probe_omega = 1j*float(config.ppm.omega_p) built at ppm_pipeline.py:336-337; fit_head_gn_from_samples is a trivial wrapper (probe_omega=1j*omega_p_ry) that no code path ever selects.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: fit_head_ppm:320-332 negative Omega_h^2 branch coerces omega_h=|Omega_h^2|^0.5
evidence: Explicit algebra shows the coerced branch flips the sign of the head in the static limit. Physical branch: pole model W^c(w)=B_h/(w^2-Omega_h^2) with B_h=-w1*Omega_h^2 (line 318), R_h=B_h/(2*Omega_h) (line 335); static-limit occupied-band head = R_h/omega_h * pref = B_h/(2*Omega_h^2) * pref = -w1/2 * pref, matching the exact COHSEX head Sigma^{SX-X}+Sigma^{COH} = (-wc + wc/2)*pref = -w1/2*pref (compute_static_head_terms lines 528-529). Coerced branch: keeps omega_h_sq<0 in B_h=-w1*omega_h_sq but sets omega_h=|omega_h_sq|^0.5 (line 321), so R_h/omega_h = -w1*omega_h_sq/(2*|omega_h_sq|) = +w1/2 — OPPOSITE sign to the COHSEX static head, contradicting compute_ppm_head_sigma_kij's own docstring claim of static-limit consistency (lines 652-657). compute_ppm_head_sigma_kij then places real poles at delta=+/-omega_h (lines 697-698) for what the fit says is an imaginary-pole (evanescent) mode. A sign-consistent coercion would rebuild B_h from the coerced positive omega_h^2 (BGW's HL-GPP instead effectively discards unphysical modes). Mitigating: branch only triggers when the 2-point fit is unphysical (normal GN data w1<w2<0 with imaginary probe gives Omega_h^2 = w2*wp^2/(w1-w2) > 0), so it is dormant on healthy runs — but when it fires it silently injects a wrong-signed head.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: fit_head_ppm:305-315 and fit_head_hl_analytic:402-407 degenerate fallback returns placeholder Omega_h^2=1.0, Omega_h=1.0
evidence: Verified nil physical effect: both placeholders set B_h=0.0 and R_h=0.0, and the only consumer of HeadGNParams for physics is compute_ppm_head_sigma_kij, which early-returns an all-zeros array when abs(head.R_h)<1e-30 (head_correction.py:690-691). The 1.0 Ry values exist purely to keep downstream divisions (e.g. format_head_diagnostics line 724: R_h/(omega_h*vol)) finite. Confirmed cosmetic harm only: format_head_diagnostics (lines 717-718) prints 'Omega_h = 1.000000 Ry (13.6 eV)' with no 'degenerate fit' marker, so a log reader sees a fake fitted pole. A sentinel (nan or explicit degenerate flag in the printout) would be cleaner, but no computed number is affected.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: fit_head_hl_analytic:411-412 I_eps_head<=0 silently set to 1.0
evidence: Branch fires when w1 = W(0)-v > 0 (anti-screened head; w1==0 already caught by the |w1|<1e-30 guard at line 402), i.e. when the resolved static head is outside the HL model's validity. Fallback sets Omega_h^2 = omega_p_sq_ry/1.0 = bare plasmon (line 414) with NO print_fn/warning anywhere in the function (contrast: every other anomaly path in this module prints, e.g. resolve_head_sample line 114, 127, 148, 153). Unlike the fit_head_ppm negative branch, this one stays sign-consistent — B_h=-w1*Omega_h^2 with Omega_h^2>0 gives static limit R_h/omega_h = -w1/2, matching COHSEX — so magnitude is preserved and only the pole frequency is silently replaced. Classification: physics-changing silent fallback that should at minimum print; fix is a one-line warning, hence refactor-target rather than suspected-bug.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: resolve_head_sample:191-195 source_order silently falls through to non-requested head source
evidence: Deliberate fallback-chain design: want_source is validated (lines 112-115 with an explicit print on unknown values), source_order = [want] + [others] is constructed explicitly (line 191), and the resolved HeadSample carries the true source string ('epshead' vs 's_tensor', lines 140/183) which IS printed on every run — gw_jax.py:83 calls format_head_sample_diagnostics, which emits 'Head source: <source>' (head_correction.py:263). One asymmetric gap verified: when want='epshead' and eps0mat.h5 is missing, from_epshead returns None with NO message (line 123-124), whereas the s_tensor missing-file path does print (line 153) and the epshead exception path prints (line 148); so the epshead-file-missing fall-through is discoverable only via the final 'Head source:' line, not flagged as a downgrade. Working as designed, with a one-line print worth adding at the missing-file early return.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: resolve_head_sample.from_epshead:147 bare 'except Exception' with print, pragma: no cover
evidence: head_correction.py:147-149: 'except Exception as exc: print_fn(f"epshead wcoul0 failed: {exc}"); return None'. The message is printed (not fully silent) and the design intent is resilience — a corrupt/incompatible eps0mat.h5 degrades to the s_tensor source rather than killing the run. But the net covers EPSReader construction, compute_q0_averages, and even programming errors (TypeError/AttributeError from refactors of those APIs), converting any of them into a silent source switch whose only trace is one log line plus 'Head source: s_tensor'. Narrowing to (OSError, KeyError, ValueError) — the actual file-format failure modes — would let real bugs propagate. Small, contained cleanup; no evidence it has masked a bug in the current tree.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: resolve_head_sample.from_s_tensor:162-163 occ[:, :min(nelec,nb)]=1.0 regardless of spin degeneracy
evidence: False alarm, verified by tracing both quantities. (1) wfn.nelec is NOT the electron count: wfn_loader.py:195-198 sets self.nelec = int(np.max(self.ifmax)) (1-based index of highest occupied band), falling back to np.sum(occs>0.5) — i.e. it is the occupied-BAND count under whatever spin convention the WFN uses (N_e/2 bands for nspinor=1 spin-degenerate, N_e bands for nspinor=2). So occ[:, :nelec]=1.0 marks exactly the occupied bands in both cases. (2) The spin-degeneracy factor of 2 lives in compute_S_omega: chi_from_dipole.py:153 pref = 4.0/(V*nk*max(nspin,1)*max(nspinor,1)) — nspinor=1,nspin=1 gives 4/(V*nk) (includes the spin-2 relative to Ry-units 2), nspinor=2 gives 2/(V*nk) (each band = 1 electron). Per-element: f_v - f_c = 1 for every (v<nelec, c>=nelec) transition in both conventions, and compute_S_omega internally re-derives its nelec as sum(f_nk[0]>0.5) (line 151), consistent with the mask it was handed. Only defect is the misleading field name 'nelec'.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: HeadResolver cache (202-252) has no invalidation hook; stale S(omega) in eqp self-consistency
evidence: Confirmed as a documented, deliberately-deferred limitation, not an oversight. HeadResolver (head_correction.py:202-252) memoizes per-omega with no clear/invalidate method; the SC-loop consequence is spelled out in a ~50-line roadmap comment in common/chi_from_dipole.py (the block ending '...This file is intentionally NOT modified to do any of (1)-(4)'): 'HeadResolver.at(omega) caches S(omega) computed at iteration 1's basis ... never refreshed. For an insulator with QP shifts << gap this is a small error (~few percent per head value, ~10s of meV per band); for systems near gap closure it is wrong', followed by the exact 4-step fix (rotate dipoles, recompute deltaE from E_QP, refresh f_nk, invalidate the cache). One-shot G0W0 (the dominant use) is unaffected since it genuinely wants iteration-0 DFT inputs. Known TODO with a written fix plan = refactor-target.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: apply_q0_head_rank1:790 vs apply_q0_head_rank1_sharded:831 — q=0 hardcoded at index 0, two different k-axis orders
evidence: Both halves check out against their actual callers. (a) q=0 at grid index (0,0,0): this is the module-wide flat-k convention — unshifted MP grid in FFT order puts Gamma at index 0; v_q_tile.py:1576 documents 'the only downstream use of g0 is the Gamma slot (q=0)' with identity-symmetry reasoning, and both call sites comment the layout explicitly (head_correction.py:789 'q=0 is index 0 on each k axis'; 830 'W_q layout: (n_mu,n_nu,nkx,nky,nkz). Add to the q=0 slice only'). (b) Divergent axis orders match the two real callers exactly: dense caller bse_io.py:816-829 passes legacy V_qmunu shaped (1,npol,npol,nkx,nky,nkz,mu,mu) (shape shim documented right below at bse_io.py ~838-843), consistent with the dense variant's (...,nkx,nky,nkz,mu,nu) contract; sharded caller bse_io.py:502-506 passes the BSE sharded-loader tensors V_q0 (mu,nu) / W_q (mu,nu,nkx,nky,nkz) with P('x','y') sharding on (mu,nu), consistent with the sharded variant's docstring (817-818). Two documented contracts serving two different storage layouts — unification would be nice (per the no-parallel-paths house rule) but nothing is wrong, and each docstring states its contract.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: HeadGNParams.omega_p field semantics drift (lines 298-299, 406, 426, 472)
evidence: Verified the field is diagnostic-only, so the drift cannot affect physics. Assignments: fit_head_ppm sets omega_p=probe_mag=abs(z) with an explicit comment acknowledging the drift (lines 297-299: 'omega_p was historically the imaginary-axis magnitude; for HL it's the real frequency itself'); fit_head_hl_analytic sets sqrt(omega_p_sq) = literal bulk plasmon (line 426, plus 406 in its degenerate branch); fit_head_with_fixed_omega sets omega_p=omega_h itself (line 472). Consumers: repo-wide grep for uses of the HeadGNParams field (excluding config.ppm.omega_p, a different object) finds exactly one — format_head_diagnostics line 716, the '[wp=...]' annotation on the W^c(q->0, i*wp) diagnostic line, which is itself mislabeled in the fixed-omega case (prints Omega_h as wp with wc_head_iwp=0). No arithmetic consumer exists. Rename/split (probe_omega field or per-model label) is a cosmetic cleanup.

item: src/gw/head_correction.py: compute_ppm_head_sigma_kij:637 eta=1e-6 Ry default; module-wide 1e-30/1e-14 magic thresholds
evidence: Confirmed no config plumbing: the sole call site, ppm_pipeline.py:139-148 (_inject_analytic_head), omits the eta kwarg, so 1e-6 Ry (~13.6 microeV pole width) is always used; config.head.wcoul0_eta (plumbed at head_correction.py:118 for S(omega)) is a different regularizer and does not reach this function. The 1e-6 broadening only matters within ~1e-5 Ry of an exact pole crossing omega - eps_n = +/-Omega_h on the Sigma^c(omega) grid — negligible for QP evaluation away from the pole. The 1e-30 guards (lines 305, 322, 402, 463, 690, 722) are pure degenerate-division sentinels and the 1e-14 checks (lines 92, 96, 126, 183, 267) are 'is omega zero' tests; none encode physics. Ad-hoc but internally consistent and harmless; worth consolidating into named module constants during the refactor, nothing more.

item: src/gw/sigma_dispatch.py:163 — W_static = W_by_role.get('static', V_q) silent bare-Coulomb fallback vs KeyError for 'probe'
