item: src/gw/kin_ion_io.py — get_kin_ion_k g_mask parameter
evidence: Enumerated every call mechanism: (1) direct/from-import: repo-wide grep for 'get_kin_ion_k' hits only the def (kin_ion_io.py:43) and one call (kin_ion_io.py:198), which omits g_mask; (2) string dispatch: no getattr/dict/config reference to the name anywhere in src/; (3) CLI: pyproject.toml [project.scripts] exposes only gw.gw_jax:main and centroid.kmeans_cli:main — kin_ion_io is reached only via `python -m gw.kin_ion_io` → main(), which never passes g_mask; (4) tests/tools/scripts: zero hits (src/psp/tests/test_dft_hamiltonian.py only quotes the CLI); (5) sandbox skills/execute_workflow/SKILL.md:198,314 and all runs/**/*.sh invoke `python -m gw.kin_ion_io[_chunked] -i cohsex.in` — module CLI only. The underlying g_mask support in compute_kinetic_k/compute_local_V_k (psp/get_DFT_mtxels.py:321,364) is separately defined but also has no live non-None caller outside psp/archive/. The parameter is pure unexercised plumbing.

item: src/gw/kin_ion_io.py:174 — `if pseudos:` guard / vnl_setup=None branch
evidence: validate_operator_inputs is called at kin_ion_io.py:129 BEFORE the guard at :174, and operator_checks.py:72-78 raises RuntimeError('No pseudopotentials loaded...') when `not pseudos`. So at line 174 pseudos is provably non-empty, the guard always evaluates True, and vnl_setup is always constructed. Consequently the vnl_setup-is-None skip path inside get_kin_ion_k (kin_ion_io.py:62-63, V_NL_k = 0.0) is unreachable too, because main() at :198 is get_kin_ion_k's only caller anywhere (see g_mask enumeration — same grep set). Always-true guard + unreachable else-branch = dead.

item: src/gw/kin_ion_io.py:146 — species grouping keyed on id(ap.pseudo)
evidence: Correct today by an implicit invariant: load_pseudopotentials (psp/pseudos.py:59-66) parses each *.upf once and stores exactly one object per element symbol in a dict (a second file for the same element overwrites, still one object); build_atom_pp_assignments (psp/pseudos.py:91-105) builds z_to_pseudo from that same dict and hands out references — no copy/deepcopy/pickle anywhere on the path — so id() grouping exactly reproduces per-species grouping. But the invariant is undocumented and identity-based, AtomPP already carries a robust key (ap.element, pseudos.py:80,101-102) that could replace id(), and the identical id()-grouping block is DUPLICATED verbatim in psp/get_DFT_mtxels.py get_kin_ion (~lines 643-657). Two copies of a fragile identity-coupled pattern, with a string key available for free: consolidate and key on element.

item: src/gw/kin_ion_io.py:121-122 — hard-coded ../qe/scf and ../qe/nscf pseudo-dir fallbacks
evidence: This sandbox-layout probing is an established codebase-wide convention, not a one-off leak: centroid/charge_density.py:155-164 documents and implements the same 'qe/scf', 'qe/nscf' search ('nearest ancestor's qe/scf and qe/nscf'), and centroid/kmeans_cli.py:76 advertises auto-detection 'from cwd via qe/scf/*.save'. kin_ion_io only uses the fallback when the primary dir has no *.upf (guarded by `if not pseudos`, :119), prints which fallback fired (:125), and is fully overridable via --pseudo_dir (:81-82,117). Deliberate ergonomics for the runs/SYSTEM/NN_run/{qe,NN_lorrax}/ layout; it does bake run-directory structure into library source, but that is the repo's chosen convention.

item: src/gw/kin_ion_io.py:14-18 — os.environ.setdefault at import time (incl. XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_ALLOCATOR=platform)
evidence: Deliberate low-peak-memory allocator posture for a chunked one-k-at-a-time preprocessing tool (module docstring lines 5-8: 'processes k-points one at a time to keep peak GPU memory bounded'), mirrored exactly in the sibling preprocessing module psp/get_DFT_mtxels.py:26-27 (PREALLOCATE=false + ALLOCATOR=platform). It intentionally differs from the GW driver: gw_jax.py:2 calls runtime.set_default_env (runtime/__init__.py:47-62), which sets only JAX_ENABLE_X64 and JAX_PLATFORMS and leaves the default preallocating BFC allocator for throughput. setdefault means any user/launcher-provided env wins, so it is an override-able default, not a hijack. Minor wart worth noting: it hand-duplicates the X64/JAX_PLATFORMS lines instead of calling set_default_env and adding only the allocator knobs, but the allocator divergence itself is the point.

item: src/gw/kin_ion_io.py:62 + attrs 205-212 — V_NL_k=0.0 scalar with no skipped-V_NL flag in output
evidence: The silent-skip cannot manifest: per the line-174 dead-guard verdict, vnl_setup is always non-None when main() (the sole caller, kin_ion_io.py:198) invokes get_kin_ion_k, so V_NL is always computed and the missing provenance flag in the h5 attrs (:205-212) is moot. The attrs do record the pseudopotential list (:212), which would expose an empty-PP run if validation were ever bypassed. Correct cleanup is to delete the None branch together with the dead guard, not to add a skip flag.

item: src/gw/kin_ion_io.py:143-148 — atoms with ap.pseudo is None silently skipped via continue
evidence: The guard is provably dead in this driver, so it masks nothing here. Per-element reasoning: validate_operator_inputs (called at :129, before the grouping loop at :142-148) computes covered_z = {symbol_to_Z(elem) for elem in pseudos if symbol_to_Z(elem) is not None} and raises unless every atom's Z is in covered_z (operator_checks.py:83-100). build_atom_pp_assignments builds z_to_pseudo with the IDENTICAL mapping — symbol_to_Z over the identical pseudos dict (pseudos.py:91-95) — so for every atom Z that passed validation, z_to_pseudo.get(Z) is non-None, hence ap.pseudo is never None post-validation. The same `continue` pattern duplicated in psp/get_DFT_mtxels.py:~646 sits behind the same validate_operator_inputs call (get_DFT_mtxels.py:628-631), so it is equally dead there. It could mask partial assignment only in a hypothetical caller that skips validation; none exists in kin_ion_io.

item: src/gw/kin_ion_io.py:103-105 — magic defaults nval=5, ncond=5, nband=100 from legacy parser
evidence: Hypothesis confirmed and the drift risk is already REALIZED in the same parser, just for a different key. (a) nval/ncond only feed Meta.from_system (kin_ion_io.py:110), which uses them to set band-window edges b_id_1..b_id_3 (common/meta.py:96-99); the kin_ion computation ignores those windows — output is the full (nk, nb_eff, nb_eff) matrix over bands 0..nb_eff, with nb_eff from nband/--nb only (:108-109,186) — so nval/ncond cannot affect the matrix elements. (b) The 5/5/100 values currently match gw_config._DEFAULTS (gw_config.py:154-156), but they are TRIPLICATED: kin_ion_io:103-105 re-applies params.get() defaults on top of read_cohsex_input's own fallbacks (psp/get_DFT_mtxels.py:143-167), independently of gw_config. (c) The realized drift: read_cohsex_input and kin_ion_io default sys_dim=3 (kin_ion_io.py:96, get_DFT_mtxels.py:156,167) while gw_config defaults sys_dim=2 (gw_config.py:157) — and sys_dim DOES change the physics here (truncation_2d → V_loc via build_local_ionic_potential_on_G_total, kin_ion_io.py:158-170). A cohsex.in omitting sys_dim yields a 3D-bulk kin_ion.h5 feeding a gw_jax run that defaults to 2D truncation. Consolidate all defaults to a single source (gw_config) and delete the legacy parser's independent fallbacks.

item: src/gw/v_q_g_flat.py: _make_read_all_ibz / _pick_g_chunk exported in __all__ (line 556-557)
evidence: The FUNCTIONS are alive: _pick_g_chunk is called at v_q_g_flat.py:349 (auto g_chunk on every G-flat V_q run — the production default, since gw_config.py 'vq_g_chunk_size': 0 routes here via compute_vcoul.py:911 g_chunk=None); _make_read_all_ibz is called at v_q_g_flat.py:390-392 on every tile. item: src/gw/v_q_g_flat.py:16-20 module docstring claims async worker-thread prefetch 'kept from the legacy driver'
evidence: Confirmed stale: the module imports no threading machinery (imports are os/functools/typing/numpy/jax, lines 31-44), and the actual orchestration is one batched pre-read of ALL IBZ zeta slabs (lines 394-420, read_all_ibz) followed by a synchronous per-q kernel loop (lines 423-436). The comment block at 394-406 explicitly records that the per-q read + prefetcher was removed after the NCCL/PHDF5 interleave deadlock and replaced on 2026-05-12 by the single batched call. Docstring paragraph 16-20 describes the pre-2026-05-12 design and is false; pure documentation rot, no runtime effect. Same rot family as the async_prefetch kwarg above — fix together.

item: src/gw/v_q_g_flat.py:51,71 _PER_Q_KERNEL_CACHE keyed on id(mesh_xy), unbounded
evidence: The suspected id()-reuse hazard is actually impossible: the cached fn closes over blk_xy_sh/V_sh/g0_sh/etc. NamedSharding objects (lines 77-84) which hold strong references to mesh_xy, and fn is stored in the module-level dict (line 143) — so the mesh keyed by id(mesh_xy) is pinned alive for the cache entry's lifetime, and CPython cannot recycle that id for a different Mesh while the entry exists. A key hit therefore always refers to the same live mesh. Remaining defect is only unbounded growth (no eviction anywhere; grep shows no .clear()/pop), which in practice is a handful of entries (one per (mesh, tile-shape, write_g0, same_zeta) combo per run) each pinning one compiled executable + one mesh. Matches the established codebase pattern (common/wfn_transforms.py:69-78 _KERNEL_CACHE, also unbounded). jax Mesh is hashable so keying on the mesh directly would be marginally cleaner, but behavior is equivalent.

item: src/gw/v_q_g_flat.py:432 jax.block_until_ready(V_acc) inside per-q loop regardless of verbose
evidence: Lines 423-436: per-q loop computes _t1/_t_k timing around the kernel call and blocks on V_acc every iteration; the ONLY consumer of the timing is the verbose-gated print at 434-436, but the block executes unconditionally. Correctness does not need it: the accumulators are donated (donate_argnums=(0,1), line 88) and JAX async dispatch handles donated-buffer chaining without host sync; the historical deadlock rationale (comment 394-406) applied to per-q PHDF5 reads interleaving with NCCL, and those reads are now all completed before the loop (lines 409-416). Effect: serializes dispatch to one in-flight kernel per q — negligible when the per-q GEMM is seconds (production) but pure overhead at small systems, and it is undocumented instrumentation in the hot path. Fix: gate the block+timing on verbose, or drop it and time the whole loop.

item: src/gw/v_q_g_flat.py:540 np.where(v_at_sphere != 0.0, v_at_sphere, v[qi]) exact-0.0 sentinel in bgw_v_grid_fn overlay
evidence: The sentinel is the documented producer contract: file_io/read_bgw_vcoul.py:160-161 ('Entries with no BGW value are zero. G=(0,0,0) is forced to zero (the q=0, G=0 head is handled separately via head correction)') — fill_v_grid_for_q zero-initializes the full FFT grid (line 164) and scatters only BGW-present G's, so 0.0 = 'no BGW value' by construction, and the forced-zero G=0 slot deliberately keeps LORRAX's own head (miniBZ-avg 3D / f2d-regularized 2D). The truncated-Coulomb edge case is self-consistent: BGW cell-slab v = (8π/|q+G|²)(1−e^{−k_xy z_c}cos(k_z z_c)) hits mathematical zero only at k_xy=0 with G_z even (k_z·z_c = G_z·π, cos=+1), and LORRAX's fallback v[qi] uses the identical formula (compute_vcoul.py:826-830, f2d with zc=π/bvec[2,2] ⇒ z_c=L_z/2), so both sides give the same (near-)zero there — in float64 cos(G_z·π) is not exactly 1 anyway, so genuine truncation values are tiny-nonzero and pass the sentinel test, preserving byte-level BGW substitution. Same convention duplicated at v_q_bispinor.py:629 and compute_vcoul.py:1019-1030 (three copies — a mild DRY smell, but the convention itself is sound).

item: src/gw/v_q_g_flat.py:224 _pick_g_chunk magic target=4096
evidence: target=4096 is an empirical GEMM/scan working-set bound with no written derivation anywhere (grepped src/gw/*.py, PERFORMANCE.md, docs/). It IS surfaced as the documented auto behavior of a user-tunable: gw_config.py:239-243 ('V_q inner G-axis GEMM chunk size... 0 (default) = auto (_pick_g_chunk(ngkmax) -> largest divisor of ngkmax <= 4096)') and vq_g_chunk_size flows through compute_vcoul.py:868,911 so any value can override it. Working-set math: g_chunk bounds the lax.scan slice at g_chunk x (n_rmu/p) per operand (lines 112-121), so 4096 x ~640 x 16B ~ 42 MB per slice — plausible sweet spot, but undocumented. Harmless; worth a one-line comment stating it is empirical.

item: src/gw/gflat_memory_model.py:4 docstring reference to gw.v_q_g_flat.compute_V_q
evidence: Confirmed stale cross-reference: grep 'def compute_V_q' shows no such function in v_q_g_flat.py — the module's entry points are compute_all_V_q_g_flat (line 472) and _compute_V_q_g_flat_one_tile (line 271). A compute_V_q does exist but in a different module (gw/gw_init.py:872, the driver-level dispatcher). The same memory-model file uses the correct name further down (gflat_memory_model.py:525 'Per-tile peak inside _compute_V_q_g_flat_one_tile'), so only the line-4 summary rotted. Doc-only; no runtime effect since :func: refs are not resolved at import.

item: src/gw/v_q_g_flat.py:379-382 g0_acc always allocated even with write_g0=False (donate target)
evidence: Deliberate and documented in place: lines 377-378 comment 'g0_acc is also the donate-target when write_g0=False — the per-q kernel still needs the buffer; the contents are simply unread.' The kernel signature fn(V_acc, g0_acc, ...) with donate_argnums=(0,1) (line 88) is uniform across write_g0 True/False so the caller loop (line 430-431) never branches; note write_g0 IS part of the compile-cache key (line 71-72) and the False branch returns g0_acc untouched (lines 138-139), so XLA sees a pass-through alias. Cost: (n_q_ibz, n_rmu_L_padded) c128 sharded P(None,'x') — e.g. CrI3-scale 36 x 768 x 16 B ~ 0.44 MB total — negligible vs the (n_q, n_rmu^2) V_acc. Wasted-buffer hypothesis is technically true but the trade is documented and the waste is sub-MB.

item: src/gw/v_q_g_flat.py:216 BGW half-grid wrap uses strict q > kg/2 (even-grid boundary stays +kg/2)
evidence: Consumer matches writer exactly: the zeta writer builds its per-q phases/gvec_components with the identical strict wrap at common/isdf_fitting.py:2198-2201 (np.where(q > kg / 2, q - kg, q)), whose comment explicitly cites 'BGW Brillouin-zone wrap used by the V_q kernel (_qvec_wrap at gw/v_q_tile.py:1204)'. The same strict-> convention appears at gw/v_q_tile.py:1204, gw/vcoul.py:164, gw/compute_vcoul.py:716 and 1090, and common/coulomb_sphere.py:67, whose _q_max_cart docstring (lines 49-61) documents the even-grid consequence on purpose: 'even kgrid: q_int = K/2 does not wrap, leaving q_frac = 1/2' — and derives the G-sphere bound from it. Per-element check for K=4: q=2 -> 2 > 2 is False -> stays +2 -> q_frac=+1/2 (positive boundary), matching BGW. The differing >= (n+1)//2 wrap at bandstructure/htransform.py:339,485 is FFT-index folding, a separate context. Consistent 6-site convention; agreeing it belongs in the planned unified sym layer, but nothing is wrong.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:19 _maybe_init_jax_distributed
evidence: 12 live sandbox scripts import it: grep of /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/scripts finds `from gw.gw_jax import _maybe_init_jax_distributed` in debug_zct_ws.py:12, debug_mem_stages.py:10, dump_fit_bufasgn.py:19, dump_fit_hlo.py:17, t6_hlo_si10_8gb.py:19, t6b_hlo_si10_4gb.py:14, test_iocallback_sharded.py:10, test_remat_effect.py:8, validate_aot_chooser.py:12, validate_fft_workspaces.py:21, validate_si10_4gb_dump.py:12, bisect_fit_rchunk_compile.py:26. item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:237 quad/e_ref defined only under do_screened but consumed by SC and dynamic branches
evidence: CONFIRMED latent NameError with two trigger paths. `quad, e_ref = build_static_quadrature(...)` at gw_jax.py:237 is inside `if config.do_screened:` (L199). Consumers outside that block: L421-422 (`compute_screening(..., quad=quad, e_ref=e_ref)` in the `mode.is_dynamic and not config.self_consistent` branch) and L522 (`SCInputs(... quad=quad, e_ref=e_ref ...)` in the `config.self_consistent` branch). Validation gaps verified in gw_config.py: the only guard is L746-748, which raises 'use_ppm_sigma=true requires do_screened=true' ONLY in the compute_mode='auto' inference path. Path 1: explicit `compute_mode = gn_ppm` + `do_screened = false` bypasses the guard entirely (L756-757 `return ComputeMode(raw)` with no do_screened cross-check) → NameError at L421. Path 2: `self_consistent = true` + `do_screened = false` (i.e., self-consistent HF/X_ONLY, which the config docstring calls an orthogonal, any-mode flag at gw_config.py:50 and :187) → NameError at L522; grep confirms self_consistent is only parsed/stored (L193, L708, L1072), never validated against do_screened. Failure is a raw NameError deep in the driver instead of a clean config error.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:276-277 _n_sym_spatial = sym_perm.shape[0] // 2 before unfold_v_q
evidence: This is the TRS-aware convention introduced BY the fix for the TRS-blind unfold bug, not a residue of it. Per-element contract: sym_perm is always built via centroid/orbit_syms.compute_centroid_sym_perm(..., extend_trs=True) — v_q_g_flat.py:180-186 comment states extend_trs=True is used precisely so sym_perm has shape (2*ntran, n_rmu); the docstring at symmetry_maps.py:196-209 specifies rows [ntran:] duplicate [:ntran] (centroid permutation is unchanged under TRS since r is fixed; only conj differs), sym_idx values live in [0, 2*n_sym_spatial), and TRS membership is decided by `sym_idx >= n_sym_spatial` (L237, L322). Runtime guard: symmetry_maps.py:265-270 computes trs_used = max_sym >= n_sym_spatial and RAISES if trs_used and 2*n_sym_spatial != sym_perm.shape[0], so a mis-halved value cannot silently clip out-of-bounds the way the historical bug did. The identical //2 idiom appears at v_q_g_flat.py:450 with the same justifying comment. So the halving is the documented inverse of extend_trs=True; correct by construction and validated.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:1-19 module-level side effects (set_default_env before import jax; distributed init at import time)
evidence: JAX architecturally requires env config before `import jax`, and the code documents this at both ends: gw_jax.py:2 comment 'BEFORE `import jax` — JAX reads env at import time', and runtime/__init__.py:47-56 docstring 'Must be called BEFORE ``import jax``... Uses ``setdefault`` so any caller-provided override wins.' Import-time init_jax_distributed()+fallback_to_cpu_if_no_gpu_backend() (L14-15) is load-bearing for the console entry points lorrax-gw / gw_jax (pyproject.toml:33-34) and for `python -m gw.gw_jax` in every run script (skills/execute_workflow/SKILL.md:211,238,315), where no other hook exists before main(). It is genuinely import-order-load-bearing (an isort/reorder would break it — a real refactor hazard worth a lint guard), but it is the standard, deliberate LORRAX pattern with setdefault-based override safety, not a bug.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:224-269 block_until_ready + del chi0 donation choreography
evidence: The donation contract is real and documented on BOTH sides of the seam. Consumer side: w_isdf.py:226-230 — '``chi_flat`` is donated (position 1): the caller releases χ₀ right [before]... ``V_flat`` is NOT donated — V is reused', enforced by `@partial(jax.jit, donate_argnums=(1,))` at w_isdf.py:230. Driver side: gw_jax.py:228-236 explains the two purposes of the explicit block_until_ready (accurate exec-section timing AND dropping the last Python reference so XLA can donate), the .watch() ban (a bound block_until_ready method on the timing-section object would pin the buffer past solve_w), plus L255 `del chi0_q` after the IBZ slice and L266-268 'χ₀ is donated inside solve_w — the reference is now invalid. Do NOT touch chi0_q_solve after this' followed by `del chi0_q_solve`. This is the standard JAX buffer-donation pattern for a peak-memory-critical array; fragile by nature but deliberately engineered and thoroughly commented. Any refactor must preserve exact reference lifetimes — the hypothesis is right about that — but it is a convention, not cruft or a bug.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:534-540 SC hyperparameters from env vars (LORRAX_SC_MAX_ITER/TOL_EV/ACCEL/DEPTH/MIXING)
evidence: Self-admitted scaffolding: gw_jax.py:534 'TODO: plumb max_iter / tol_ev through config; env vars for now so the first end-to-end QSGW test can vary them without rebuild.' Verified nothing was ever promoted: grep of gw_config.py finds no SC-iteration keys (no max_iter/mixing/accelerator anywhere in the config schema), and grep for LORRAX_SC_ across src/, tests/, repo docs/, and sandbox docs/+skills/ hits ONLY these five lines — the knobs are undocumented in COHSEX_INPUT.md, invisible to run manifests, and unreproducible from a cohsex.in file. Since SC runs are archived by their input files, hyperparameters controlling convergence live outside the provenance record. Matches the hypothesis exactly; should become cohsex.in keys.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:600 cross-module import of sc_iteration._rotate_to_dft_basis
evidence: The seam is acknowledged but half-public: sc_iteration.py:755 lists '_rotate_to_dft_basis' in __all__ with the comment 'used by main() to rotate SC SigmaResult fields', so it is a deliberate export wearing a private name — the underscore is the defect, not the dependency. Index math verified: sc_iteration.py:242-245 computes O_DFT[m,n] = Σ_pq U[m,p]·O_QP[p,q]·conj(U[n,q]) via einsum('kmp,kpq,knq->kmn'); with U from eigh (columns = QP eigenvectors in DFT basis, U[m,p] = <dft_m|qp_p>), O_QP = U† O_DFT U inverts to exactly this U O_QP U† — correct back-rotation. It is correctness-critical per gw_jax.py:590-599 (kin_ion is DFT-basis; feeding QP-basis sigma_xc to the L745 eigh gives eqp0 off by tens of eV on MoS2), and common/chi_from_dipole.py:37 already cross-references it as a convention anchor. Rename public (rotate_to_dft_basis) and keep one definition; no behavior change needed.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:767-771 try/except Exception: pass around multihost_utils.sync_global_devices
evidence: The blanket-swallow is demonstrably unnecessary and inconsistent: gw_init.py:888 (zeta_flush) and gw_init.py:1128 (g0_write) call sync_global_devices UNGUARDED in the same production runs, proving the platform/JAX-version compat concern the shim implies does not exist here — if bare calls were unsafe, every ISDF run would already crash. The identical guarded copy-paste exists at sc_iteration.py:735-739. Practical blast radius is small (the sync sits after the rank-0 WFN_qp.h5 write; nothing later in the same run reads that file, so a swallowed coordinator failure mainly risks a rank exiting before the file is durable and losing a real error's traceback), but `except Exception: pass` can hide genuine multihost sync failures and diverges from the module's own unguarded convention. Consolidate: drop the guards or make one shared documented helper.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:400-405 in-comment history note (analytic q->0 PPM head removed in 1542342, re-added 2026-04-25)
evidence: Verified half-true and decaying: commit 1542342 exists in this repo ('Unify head resolution and remove G0 injection' — matches the removal claim), and the described head injection is real code (ppm_pipeline.py:109 _inject_analytic_head, module header line 8 'analytic q→0 head injection'). But the comment's citation `reports/mos2_kgrid_gnppm_head_convergence_2026-4-10/` does NOT exist — ls of /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/reports/ has no such dir (closest candidates: mos2_3x3_nosym_cohsex_barecut_headnk_2026-04-10, mos2_kgrid_convergence_2026-04-05). The comment itself declares it is 'not yet captured anywhere else', i.e., a ±1.24 eV/band physics decision whose sole record is a driver comment with a broken evidence pointer. Move the note next to the code it describes (ppm_pipeline._inject_analytic_head docstring) or into docs, and fix/confirm the report path.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:649-651 hard-coded diagonal fixed-point constants (max_iter=120, tol_ev=1e-7/RYD_TO_EV, mixing=0.6)
evidence: The callee has proper keyword defaults (qsgw_utils.py:83-85: max_iter=80, tol_ev=1.0e-6, mixing=0.6); the call site deliberately tightens them for the Ry-unit invocation — inputs at L649-650 are in Ry (h0_diag_ry, omega_grid_ry), so tol_ev=1.0e-7/RYD_TO_EV correctly expresses a 1e-7 eV tolerance in Ry (the param name 'tol_ev' is units-of-input generic; the conversion is right, not a units bug). This solves a cheap vectorized DIAGONAL Σ(E) fixed point (searchsorted + gathers per iteration, qsgw_utils.py:89-90); 1e-7 eV is ~3 orders tighter than any physical accuracy in play and mixing=0.6 matches the callee default, so there is no realistic tuning need that would justify a config key. Not config-exposed, but not scaffolding either — just conservative numerics at a call site. Distinct from the L536-540 SC env vars, which DO need promoting.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:830 float(efermi_dft_ev or 0.0) in freq-debug Edft-Ef column
evidence: The classic `x or default` footgun requires the falsy-but-valid value to map to something DIFFERENT from itself; here the fallback IS 0.0, so E_F==0.0 → 0.0 and None → 0.0 produce bit-identical output. Enumerated cases: (a) static modes: efermi_dft_ev=None (L446, ppm_outputs None) → column degenerates to raw E_dft, the intended fallback; (b) dynamic one-shot: set from ppm_outputs.efermi_dft_ev (a float); (c) SC: set at L615 from float(wfn.efermi)*RYD_TO_EV; (d) hypothetical E_F exactly 0.0 eV: `0.0 or 0.0` → 0.0, numerically identical to the correct subtraction. No input/state produces a wrong number. Scope is a rank-0 debug table only (L805 gate config.debug.sigma_freq_debug_output). Also checked the sharper hazard nearby: L648 `float(efermi_dft_ev)` would crash on None, but that branch requires sigma_c_omega is not None (L628), which implies ppm_outputs existed and set it. Stylistically should be `0.0 if efermi_dft_ev is None else float(efermi_dft_ev)`, but there is no failure scenario.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:187 getattr(isdf, 'wf_bundle_transverse', None)
evidence: The getattr default is dead defensiveness: prepare_isdf_and_wavefunctions has exactly one return (gw_init.py:1324-1328) and it ALWAYS sets wf_bundle_transverse — initialized to None at gw_init.py:1283 (non-bispinor / no transverse centroids), populated at L1285 in the bispinor branch, and explicitly reset to None at L1322 on the restart path with a fail-loud comment ('Restart path doesn't yet round-trip the transverse Wfns... Mark as not-yet-supported'). Plain attribute access would behave identically on every path. The 'duck-typed optional attribute' hypothesis is wrong in its premise: the field is unconditionally present; the SimpleNamespace return (L1186 'Returns SimpleNamespace(V_qmunu, wf_bundle)' — docstring is one field stale, another micro-cruft) is deliberate repo style (procedural, no new API layers). Fix is a one-character-class cleanup: use isdf.wf_bundle_transverse directly and update the stale docstring.

item: src/gw/gw_jax.py:96 argparse default input filename 'cohsex_test.in'
evidence: Not a leak into one driver — it is the repo-wide conventional test-input filename used as the default in multiple CLIs: bandstructure/htransform.py:868 has the identical default, psp/get_DFT_mtxels.py:794 defaults to tests/cohsex_debug/cohsex_test.in, tests/test_gw_jax_regression.py:14 pins INPUT_FILE = CASE_DIR/'cohsex_test.in', and archive tests invoke main(['--input','cohsex_test.in']). Every production invocation passes -i explicitly (skills/execute_workflow/SKILL.md:211, :238, :315 all use `-m gw.gw_jax -i .../cohsex.in`; sampled runs/*/run*.sh likewise). The console scripts lorrax-gw/gw_jax (pyproject.toml:33-34) invoked bare would look for ./cohsex_test.in and fail fast in LorraxConfig.from_input_file with a missing-file error — no silent wrong-input hazard. A more honest default might be 'cohsex.in' or required=True, but current behavior is harmless dev convenience consistent across the codebase.

item: src/gw/qsgw_utils.py:306 plot_qp_energy_comparison
evidence: Zero live references under every mechanism: only def (qsgw_utils.py:306) + __all__ entry (line 351) in all of lorrax_D (*.py, *.md, *.sh, *.toml, *.ipynb); gw_jax.py:48-52 from-import lists only the other 3 names; no `import *` anywhere in src/ so __all__ is inert; no getattr/string dispatch on 'plot_qp'; pyproject.toml:32-35 console_scripts are only gw.gw_jax:main and centroid.kmeans_cli:main; tests/, tools/, scripts/, misc/, docs/ empty; sandbox skills/, runs/ (*.sh,*.py), templates/ empty — sole external mention is the audit's own notes reports/gw_refactor_map_2026-07-01/files/qsgw_utils.md. Git provenance: commit 8c50c21 'Remove diagnostic plot, sigma_munu streaming, dead imports' (2026-04-11) deleted the only caller + import from gw_jax.py (22 lines) but left the definition and __all__ entry orphaned. item: src/gw/qsgw_utils.py:143-171 extract_sigma_diag_replicated forced allgather
evidence: Verified: lines 162-169 force with_sharding_constraint to P(None,None,None,None) on the full (nω,nk,nb,nb) tensor before einsum('...ii->...i'). Deliberate, documented tradeoff — docstring lines 156-160 quantify per-device cost (nω·nk·nb²·16B, ~270 MB for MoS2 4x4x1/80b/41ω) and explicitly defer 'a shard_map specialisation ... for follow-up'. Three call sites: gw_jax.py:639, gw_jax.py:781, ppm_pipeline.py:205. One correction to the docstring's rationale: the 'per-shard block-diagonal is silently wrong' scenario (lines 149-154) only applies to a shard_map-local einsum; under GSPMD jit a plain einsum on the P(None,None,'x','y') array would be partitioned correctly with implicit collectives. So the allgather is conservative-correct, not a correctness necessity. The memory scaling is a real ceiling: at CrI3-scale (nω=41, nk=36, nb=600) the replicated buffer is ~8.5 GB/device against the 28 GB budget. Matches first-pass hypothesis: refactor target, not a bug.

item: src/gw/qsgw_utils.py:136 solve_diagonal_sigma_fixed_point non-converged return path
evidence: Confirmed by explicit math. In-loop (lines 131-134): diff = |E_next − E_prev| = mix·|E_new − E_prev| since E_next = (1−mix)E_prev + mix·E_new. Post-loop (line 136): flag = |E_new − E| where E is the final E_next, and E_new − E_next = (1−mix)(E_new − E_prev), so the flag tests (1−mix)·|E_new − E_prev| < tol. With the production mixing=0.6 the post-loop metric is (1−mix)/mix = 2/3 of the in-loop metric — bands with residual up to 1.5× tol (by the loop criterion) get flagged converged=True on max_iter exhaustion. Second defect: max_iter=0 skips the loop and line 136 raises NameError on undefined E_new. Impact today is nil: the only caller, gw_jax.py:649-652, passes max_iter=120 and discards the flag ('E_sc_rel_ry, _, n_iter = ...'). Latent copy-slip bug, benign at current call sites — matches first-pass hypothesis.

item: src/gw/qsgw_utils.py:78-136 + gw_jax.py:649/700-705 _ev-suffixed params fed Rydberg values
evidence: Confirmed naming/comment debt with no numerical error. (1) solve_diagonal_sigma_fixed_point is unit-agnostic (searchsorted + lerp + threshold compare only, lines 115-136); gw_jax.py:649-651 passes h0, Σ(ω), ω-grid all in Ry with tol_ev=1.0e-7/RYD_TO_EV — internally self-consistent, correct. (2) build_qsgw_sigma_xc is called (gw_jax.py:703-705; also sigma_dispatch.py:226-234) with ω-grid and E in eV while Σ_c/Σ_x values stay Ry; the interp weights w_hi=(E−ω_lo)/(ω_hi−ω_lo) (lines 249-251) are dimensionless ratios, invariant as long as abscissae share a unit, so the Ry-valued result is correct. (3) The gw_jax.py:700 parenthetical '(kernel internals convert; result is Ry)' is false — no unit conversion exists anywhere in _kernel (lines 262-285); values are never converted, only interpolated. gw_jax.py:634-636 itself declares Ry/eV mixing 'a footgun', and the *RYD_TO_EV at line 705 is a mathematical no-op (passing Ry for both abscissae would give identical weights). Rename params unit-neutral and fix the comment; classified refactor-target per first-pass hypothesis.

item: src/gw/qsgw_utils.py:259-260 real interp weights cast to complex128
evidence: Confirmed harmless. w_lo/w_hi are real float64 (lines 250-251); the kernel multiplies them into complex128 gathers at lines 273/279. LORRAX runs x64 (complex128 throughout), where float64·complex128 → complex128 anyway, so the cast is not needed for correctness — it only pins the jit argument dtype and moves the ConvertElementType off the device kernel. Cost is doubling a tiny (nk,nb) buffer. Deliberate dtype-promotion convenience exactly as the first-pass hypothesized; safe to keep or drop.

item: src/gw/qsgw_utils.py:124 fixed-point init at argmin|omega| assumes Fermi-centered grid
evidence: The ω-grid is Fermi-relative by construction throughout the codebase: gw_config.py:788-802 builds it from sigma_omega_min_ev/max_ev whose defaults are −5.0/+5.0 (gw_config.py:314-315, symmetric about 0), and gw_jax.py:502-504 converts to an absolute window by adding E_F; the fixed point is solved in Fermi-relative coordinates (h0_diag_ry − efermi_ry at gw_jax.py:650). So at defaults ω≈0 ↔ E_F and the grid brackets 0, exactly the convention the build_qsgw_sigma_xc docstring states (qsgw_utils.py:196-200: 'ω-grid is centered on E_F by construction in ppm_pipeline'). If a user configures an asymmetric window excluding 0, argmin|ω| picks the nearest grid point — the init degrades to a valid but worse starting guess; interp edge-clamping (line 59) keeps every iterate defined, and out-of-grid bands are patched by the scissor path anyway (gw_jax.py:654-695). No crash mode, no silent corruption of a converged answer; an assertion would be belt-and-braces only. Documented convention, not a bug.

item: src/gw/eqp_bgw.py: make_eqp_bgw / main / _build_parser (CLI path, lines 308-467)
evidence: Enumerated all call mechanisms: (1) imports — repo-wide grep hits only gw_output.py:235,324 (write_eqp_bgw_in_memory), gw_jax.py:807-808 (compute_eqp_diag/compute_z_factor_from_omega_grid for the debug table), provenance.py:3 (docstring), tests/active/test_eqp_bgw.py:14 (write_bgw_eqp only); none touch make_eqp_bgw/main/_build_parser. (2) console_scripts — pyproject.toml:32-36 lists only lorrax-gw, gw_jax, lorrax-centroids. (3) string dispatch — no getattr/dict/config-name references. (4) CLI invocation — zero 'gw.eqp_bgw' hits in sandbox skills/, runs/ (*.sh/*.py/*.md/*.yaml), lorrax_D docs//tools//scripts//misc//config/, AGENTS.md, README.md; reports mention it only in audit notes (reports/gw_refactor_map_2026-07-01/files/eqp_bgw.md), never as an executed command. Caveat for the deleter: it is the module's documented post-hoc recovery CLI (eqp_bgw.py:55-57) and the ONLY way to regenerate eqp{0,1}.dat from a finished run's h5 files; the live path (gw_output.py write_results) always writes them during the run, which is why it has never been invoked.

item: src/gw/eqp_bgw.py:271-286: write_eqp_bgw_in_memory silently overwrites required arg sigma_c_at_dft_diag_ev in dynamic mode
evidence: Intent is documented twice: eqp_bgw.py:279-280 ('Re-derive σ_c at E_DFT from the full ω-grid for self-consistency with the Z-factor central difference') and gw_jax.py:841-845 ('Compute Σ_c(E_DFT) + Z via the SAME recipe the eqp{0,1}.dat writer uses ... PPM pipeline's own interp produces a numerically slightly different value (~10 meV) due to a different vectorisation path'). Traced both values: the caller-passed value is ppm_pipeline.py:221-223 interp_along_omega(sig_c_diag, omega_ev, omega_dft_rel_ev) after an eV→Ry→eV round-trip (gw_jax.py:788-791 divides by RYD_TO_EV, gw_output.py:298-300 multiplies back), while the recompute at eqp_bgw.py:163-164 is the same interp_along_omega on the same diag grid with e_dft_rel from the same canonical efermi (gw_output.py:325 passes e_dft_ev_irr - results.efermi_ev). So today the overwrite changes at most ULP-level round-trip noise, and guarantees sigma_c(E_DFT), sigma_c(E_DFT±dE) and Z all come from one interpolation recipe. The trap is real but small: the arg is required-yet-ignored whenever sigma_c_omega_diag_ev is not None; a minor refactor (make it optional / assert None in dynamic mode) would remove the trap. Note the ~10 meV figure in gw_jax.py:845 could not be reproduced from current code paths (both sides call the identical qsgw_utils.interp_along_omega); it likely describes an older vectorisation and is stale as a magnitude claim.

item: src/gw/eqp_bgw.py:367-378: make_eqp_bgw local mid-gap E_F recompute, parallel to canonical results.efermi_ev
evidence: Canonical E_F is computed once at WFN load (wfn_loader.py:194-207: nelec = max(ifmax); vbm = max energies[:,:,nelec-1]; cbm = min energies[:,:,nelec]; efermi = 0.5*(vbm+cbm), metal fallback efermi=vbm) and ppm_pipeline.py:190-195 explicitly says 'Single source of truth ... Don't recompute here'. make_eqp_bgw duplicates the formula from ifmax + the sigma-window slice: for the only supported case (nspin=1, sorted eigenvalues, window bracketing VBM/CBM — enforced by the ValueError at eqp_bgw.py:370-374) max over occupied window bands == max of band nelec-1 and min over unoccupied window bands == min of band nelec, so the two E_F values are numerically identical today. Divergence cases: metals/no-unoccupied-band (loader falls back to efermi=vbm, eqp_bgw.py:376 would crash on empty slice or the 370-374 check raises first). The danger is structural: sigma_mnk.h5 stores omega_ev relative to the run's E_F but does NOT store E_F itself (ppm_pipeline.py:261-276 writes no efermi dataset), so any future divergence silently shifts the interpolation abscissa — exactly the failure class documented at gw_output.py:304-311 ('the writer used to recompute it from band_stop - band_start which silently treats every sigma-window band as occupied'). Fix: write efermi_ev into sigma_mnk.h5 and have the CLI read it; delete the local recompute (matches the user's single-source-of-truth discipline).

item: src/gw/eqp_bgw.py:342: NotImplementedError for nspin != 1 (and gw_output.py:337 hardcoded nspin=1)
evidence: The first-pass 'collision course with bispinor work' hypothesis is wrong. In the BGW WFN.h5 convention, fully-relativistic/spinor states are nspin=1 with nspinor=2 (docs_bgw/wfn.h5.spec:29-37: 'nspinor ... 2 for fully relativistic calculations'), and LORRAX reads nspinor as a separate header field (wfn_loader.py:157). So bispinor runs pass the nspin==1 check unchanged, and BGW's own eqp writer (sigma_main.f90:3179-3196, loop do ispin = 1, sig%nspin) also emits a single ispin=1 block for spinor calculations — the hardcoded nspin=1 reproduces BGW's spinor output exactly. The guard only excludes collinear spin-polarized (nspin=2/LSDA), which LORRAX does not support anywhere (cf. the known spin-blind V_xc limitation). A correct, explicit guard on an unsupported input class, in the right convention.

item: src/gw/eqp_bgw.py:388-408: make_eqp_bgw unconditionally reads omega_ev/sigma_c_kij_ev (no static/COHSEX fallback)
evidence: The suspected failure mode (KeyError on a static sigma_mnk.h5) cannot occur because static runs never produce sigma_mnk.h5 at all: sigma_dispatch.py:182-197 early-returns for ComputeMode.X_ONLY and ComputeMode.COHSEX before reaching compute_ppm_sigma_pipeline (line 210), and the only writers of the sigma_mnk.h5 layout (omega_ev/sigma_c_kij_ev/sigma_sx_kij_ev/hartree_kij_ev) are ppm_pipeline._write_sigma_omega_h5 (ppm_pipeline.py:247-276) and the SC-convergence copy (sc_iteration.py:649), both dynamic-only. A CLI run against a static run_dir fails at h5py.File open with FileNotFoundError — an accurate, if unfriendly, error. The asymmetry (in-memory path supports static via sigma_c_omega_diag_ev=None ⇒ Z=1, eqp_bgw.py:271-273 and compute_eqp_diag docstring 193-199; CLI is dynamic-only) is consistent with the on-disk reality, so this is dead-code-shaped consistency, not a bug. If the CLI path is deleted per the dead verdict, this becomes moot.

item: src/gw/eqp_bgw.py:125-127: comment that BGW format advertises 3f15.9 but sigma_main.f90 passes only 4 args
evidence: Verified against the actual BGW source at sources/BerkeleyGW/Sigma/sigma_main.f90:3176-3195: header is write(30,'(3(f13.9),i8)') kp%rk(:,ikn), sig%nspin*sig%ndiag — matching LORRAX's f'{kx:13.9f}{ky:13.9f}{kz:13.9f}{nspin*nb:8d}' at eqp_bgw.py:121 — and each body line is write(30,'(2i8,3f15.9)') ispin, sig%diag(i), elda, efsto+achcor: the edit descriptor list allows 3 floats but only 2 are passed (4 args total), and Fortran formatted output stops when the I/O list is exhausted, so BGW's actual bytes are 2i8 + 2f15.9 per line. LORRAX's f'{ispin:8d}{iband:8d}{e_dft:15.9f}{e_qp:15.9f}' (eqp_bgw.py:128-131) reproduces this byte-for-byte. The comment is accurate and load-bearing for the module's stated byte-identical-to-BGW guarantee (docstring lines 3-6, 50-53); do not 'fix' the formatter to emit a third float.

Full-repo grep: only the definition plus prose/docstring mentions (src/gw/ppm_sigma.py:428,938; src/bse/bse_simple.py:27; src/gw/aot_memory_model/kernels/sigma_kij.py:24; AGENTS.md:25; docs/architecture/codebase.md; docs/theory/physics.md:587). item: tests/archive/test_freqint_stage23.py import of gw_isdf.wavefunction_bundle / WavefunctionBundle
evidence: Doubly dead. (1) Never collected: pyproject.toml:60-61 sets testpaths=['tests'] and norecursedirs=['archive'], so pytest skips tests/archive entirely. (2) Would ImportError if run: src/ contains package `gw`, not `gw_isdf` (verified by ls src/), and no class WavefunctionBundle exists anywhere in src/ — the current object is the `Wavefunctions` dataclass (wavefunction_bundle.py:133). All other tests/archive files (test_cohsex_jax_driver.py:8, test_hgl_quadrature.py, test_frequency_integration_toy.py:17-18, test_input_file.py:9) import the same defunct gw_isdf package, confirming the whole directory is a pre-rename graveyard. Related live-tree rot worth flagging to the parent: tools/profile_gw_xprof.py:66 does `from gw_isdf import gw_jax` and is equally broken.

item: src/gw/wavefunction_bundle.py:181-198 _build_occ host-numpy NOTE
evidence: The cited commit exists and matches: git show 7781b80 (2026-04-18, 'gw_jax + wavefunction_bundle: _build_Gij and _build_occ on host (numpy)') — message documents ~8 standalone pjit cache misses per function from jnp.zeros/.at[].set at trace time and wavefunction_setup dropping 1.79s → 0.09s. The array is tiny ((nk, nb_full) replicated), returned via a single jnp.asarray H2D at line 198, and both call sites (build_wavefunctions:238, rotate_wavefunctions:361) immediately re-apply a replicated sharding constraint, so host residency is safe. This is a deliberately documented perf-regression trap; any refactor must keep the occ build on host numpy.

item: src/gw/wavefunction_bundle.py:230 single jnp.conj on psi_rmuT_X
evidence: Verified per-element. Producer common/load_wfns.py:507: `psi_rmuT = jnp.conj(psi_rmu.transpose(0, 3, 1, 2))`, so psi_rmuT_X[k,μ,n,s] = ψ*_{nk}(μ,s). This ψ* storage is load-bearing for the ISDF zeta fit: common/isdf_fitting.py pair_density (lines 176-209) computes P_k,ab(μ,col) = Σ_n ψ*_{n,k,a}(μ) ψ_{n,k,b}(col) via einsum 'kmna,knbr->kabmr' with NO conj inside the jit — the conj lives in the stored array. The bundle contract is the opposite ('All four copies store the un-conjugated ψ; consumers apply jnp.conj themselves', lines 13-14), so exactly one conj at line 230 is required: conj(psi_rmuT_X)[k,μ,n,s] = ψ; transpose(0,3,1,2) → psi_xn[k,s,μ,n] = ψ (μ stays on axis sharded 'x', matching PSI_XN_SPEC line 91); transpose(0,2,3,1) → psi_xr[k,n,s,μ] = ψ (PSI_XR_SPEC line 92). Downstream, project() (line 380) applies its own conj(psi_xr), consistent with the contract. The convention is coherent and documented at both endpoints (load_wfns.py:494-507 comment, build_wavefunctions docstring lines 211-215, module docstring lines 13-14), but the hypothesis is right that it is a docstring-only seam: a refactor of the loader that drops or doubles the conj would produce ψ instead of ψ* in the pair density (wrong zeta) or ψ* in the bundle (wrong Σ projection) with no runtime check.

item: src/gw/wavefunction_bundle.py:44-67 BandSlices occ==val duplication and b1
evidence: Half-confirmed, half-refuted. Confirmed: occ (line 66) and val (line 62) are byte-identical slice(0, b2-b0); occ is consumed only at line 195 (_build_occ insulator fill) — the duplication is semantic aliasing under the codebase-wide insulator assumption (b2 = LUMO = nelec, meta.py:98). REFUTED: b1 is NOT vestigial. b1 = nelec - nval (meta.py:97, start of the sigma valence window) and is load-bearing at: gw_init.py:551 `band_range_right = (band_slices.b1, band_slices.b4)` — the ISDF pair-density RIGHT band window for fit_zeta; gw_init.py:1202 `n_b_right=band_slices.b4 - band_slices.b1` — chunk-plan sizing in compute_optimal_chunks; gw_init.py:1161 and :1262 sigma bandrange (b1, b3) passed to get_enk_bandrange; plus meta.band_ranges (meta.py:64-72: valence=(b1,b2), sigma=(b1,b3), cond_plus_sigma=(b1,b4)). Shrinking BandSlices by dropping b1 would break the ISDF fit windows. One genuine wrinkle observed: BandSlices.sigma = slice(0, b3-b0) starts at b0 (line 64) while meta.band_ranges.sigma = (b1, b3) starts at b1 — these differ whenever nval < nelec; the BandSlices window is the wider/projection one, meta's is the enk-loader one.

item: src/gw/wavefunction_bundle.py:192-198 efermi=None insulator occ fill at construction
evidence: Confirmed behavior: gw_init.py:1162-1164 calls build_wavefunctions without efermi, so construction-time occ = band-count fill (occ[:, slices.occ]=1.0); efermi only enters via rotate_wavefunctions in the QSGW loop (sc_iteration.py:266-274, midgap E_F). And that occ IS consumed: ppm_sigma.py:1503 `occ_full = wfns.occ[:, s.full]` feeds the GN-PPM Σ^c(ω) driver. However this is not a latent metal bug in any supported configuration — the entire code is insulator-only by construction: meta.py:98 sets b_id_2 = int(wfn.nelec) (integer filling defines LUMO), chi0/G use the band-count val/cond split (slices.val/cond), sc_iteration.py:43-44 explicitly documents that downstream consumers assume strict val/cond ordering, and sc_iteration.py:237 computes E_F as 0.5*(vbm+cbm) which presupposes a gap. A metal would be wrong at every one of those points, not just here. The efermi branch at line 197 exists only for the QSGW rebuild where levels reorder within an insulating gap. Classify intentional-convention; a metal-support effort would be a codebase-wide feature, and this function is where the check `if efermi is None assume insulator` is at least explicit.

item: src/gw/wavefunction_bundle.py:148-162 jit accessors with Python slice as static arg
evidence: Both concerns check out as deliberate and declared, not implicit. (1) Version floor: slice hashability landed in CPython 3.12, and pyproject.toml declares requires-python = ">=3.12", so the trick's interpreter floor is the package's stated floor — no hidden constraint. (2) Perf motivation is independently documented: src/gw/PERFORMANCE.md:112 records '~17 misses → ~4 (one per unique slice in chi/W/Σ)' for exactly these accessors, and the comment at lines 144-147 matches. The accessors are heavily used (e.g. rotate_wavefunctions lines 340-343, ppm_sigma.py:1501 wfns.xr(s.sigma)). Only refactor hazard: anyone replacing the static slice with a traced dynamic_slice or a non-hashable window object reintroduces the eager-pjit gather tail; keep bands as a plain Python slice.

item: src/gw/experimental/head_wing_schur.py — entire module (all six public functions)
evidence: Enumerated every call mechanism: (1) direct/from-imports: repo-wide grep for head_wing_schur|extract_V_body_sharded|solve_W_body0_sharded|schur_reductions_sharded|assemble_W_sharded|reconstruct_W_via_schur_sharded|W_head_scalar_per_q hits ONLY tests/test_head_wing_schur.py (imports at lines 64-74, calls at 205, 271, 295, 318) and the module itself; (2) string dispatch: all getattr/importlib sites in src/ enumerated — none touch gw.experimental (postprocess/__init__.py getattr forwards only to rotate_wfn_to_qp); (3) CLI: pyproject.toml [project.scripts] defines only lorrax-gw/gw_jax -> gw.gw_jax:main and lorrax-centroids -> centroid.kmeans_cli:main; test file has its own __main__ smoke entry (tests/test_head_wing_schur.py:345) — still test-only; (4) sandbox: grep over /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/{skills,runs,docs,CHANGELOG.md} and lorrax_D/{tools,scripts,docs}: zero hits (grep exit 1); reports/ hits are the audit's own map files, not consumers; (5) gw/experimental/__init__.py re-exports nothing (docstring only) and gw_jax.py/w_isdf.py/compute_vcoul.py contain no 'schur' string. Not confirmed-dead because the test is a real live consumer and src/gw/experimental/__init__.py explicitly charters the package as staged-for-promotion ('real, tested, and intended for promotion — not abandoned code'); module docstring lines 14-18 say a driver patch will plumb it into static COHSEX later.

item: head_wing_schur.py:126-129 — pref default 1.0+0j and docstring claim 'gw_jax passes 2.0+0j for the spinor factor'
evidence: The docstring's factual claim is wrong, and was already wrong when written. Production never passes a bare 2.0: gw_jax.py:264 calls w_isdf.solve_w(V_q_solve, chi0_q_solve, meta, mesh_xy, ...) which computes pref via _w_solve_pref_scalar(meta) = 2/(√N_k · n_spin · n_spinor) (w_isdf.py:304-311). Verified at the module's creation commit 1134822 too: git show 1134822:src/gw/w_isdf.py line 316 already had pref_scalar = 2.0/(nq**0.5 * nspin * nspinor). The pref=1.0 default itself is harmless for the synthetic test (test passes explicit data with pref left at default), and the kernel scales chi correctly for any pref — the bug is documentary: the promotion patch author following this docstring would hardcode 2.0+0j and silently get W wrong by a factor of √N_k·n_spin·n_spinor. Fix at promotion: delete the default, require pref, and cite _w_solve_pref_scalar as the source of truth.

item: head_wing_schur.py:153,156 — chi_wing_x and chi_wingp_y parameters accepted but never referenced
evidence: Index math checked per contraction rather than pattern-matching einsum strings: A_wing[q,μ]=Σ_ν W_body0[q,μ,ν]·χ_wing[q,ν] (line 180, 'qmn,qn->qm') contracts the ν axis, which is y-sharded under V_FLATQ_SPEC=P(None,'x','y'), so it needs the y-replica chi_wing_y and outputs on x — correct. A_wingp[q,ν]=Σ_μ χ_wingp[q,μ]·W_body0[q,μ,ν] (line 185, 'qm,qmn->qn') contracts the x-sharded μ axis, needing chi_wingp_x, output on y — correct. χ_head_eff correction (line 190, 'qm,qmn,qn->q') needs chi_wingp_x and chi_wing_y — correct. assemble_W_sharded (lines 232-234) uses only g0_x, g0_y, A_wing_x, A_wingp_y. So the x-replica of χ_wing and y-replica of χ_wingp are dead at EVERY level: schur_reductions_sharded ignores them, and reconstruct_W_via_schur_sharded (lines 249, 252) threads them through unused. The first-pass hypothesis (symmetry with the 'four pre-replicated copies of every vector' API in docstring lines 22-25) is right for g0 (both replicas genuinely used in extract+assemble) but not for the wings. Not a bug — outputs unaffected — but the dead params force the promoted caller to device_put two never-consumed sharded arrays (real H2D + memory cost at production nq×μ). Drop them before promotion.

item: head_wing_schur.py:56-81 — _RESHARD_MID_SPEC P('x',None,'y') two-stage reshard duplicating w_isdf._get_w_solve_fn
evidence: Duplication confirmed: w_isdf.py:214-223 defines the identical trick (reshard_mid = NamedSharding(mesh_xy, P('x', None, 'y'))) with measured justification (Si 4x4x4: 2.95 GB/dev via replicated intermediate vs 1.11 GB/dev via mid-spec, 62% reduction) for the ENTRY direction P(None,'x','y')→q_shard; head_wing_schur.py:68-81 re-implements it for the EXIT direction (q_shard-ish output of _get_w_solve_fn → V_FLATQ_SPEC), applied at solve_W_body0_sharded:143. Both exist because _get_w_solve_fn's final with_sharding_constraint(W, rep_3d) (w_isdf.py:263) 'may or may not' be honoured by XLA (head_wing_schur.py:136-138) — i.e. the pattern is GSPMD-planner-behavior-sensitive and could silently regress on a JAX/XLA upgrade in one copy but not the other. Per the sandbox no-redundancy rule, hoist one two-stage-reshard helper (e.g. into w_isdf or a shared sharding util) taking (array, mesh, final_spec) and use it at both sites when this module is promoted.

item: head_wing_schur.py:171-177, 227-231 — schur_reductions_sharded/assemble_W_sharded skip defensive resharding
evidence: Both docstrings state the caller contract explicitly and give the cost rationale: 'This function does NOT defensively reshard, because doing so charges a 16-all-to-all reshard cost on every call even when the input is already correctly sharded' (lines 174-177) and 'trusts the sharding label and does no defensive resharding' (lines 229-231). Correctness is not at risk: both functions end in with_sharding_constraint to the declared output spec (lines 181-194, 236-237), so a wrong-sharded input makes XLA insert the implicit reshard/remat (the perf cliff) but produces numerically identical results. The sanctioned producer path (solve_W_body0_sharded → _reshard_W_to_flatq, line 143) delivers the contract, and the escape hatch (_reshard_W_to_flatq) is documented for foreign inputs. A debug-mode sharding assert would be a nice-to-have at promotion, but as written this is a deliberate, documented zero-defensive-cost convention consistent with the module's zero-communication design goal.

item: head_wing_schur.py:41 — 'from jax.experimental.shard_map import shard_map' unused
evidence: grep of the module body: 'shard_map' appears only at line 41. All sharding in this module goes through jax.lax.with_sharding_constraint (lines 77-80, 105, 181, 186, 193, 236); the one shard_map actually executed lives inside the delegate w_isdf._get_w_solve_fn, which does its own local 'from jax.experimental.shard_map import shard_map' (w_isdf.py:207). Consistent with the docstring's stated origin as a mirror of lorrax_A's finite_q_head_interp recast to with_sharding_constraint style (lines 3-9) — a leftover import from the draft. Zero runtime effect; delete at promotion (flake8 F401).

item: tests/test_head_wing_schur.py:1 — docstring names 'gw.head_wing_schur' but module lives at gw.experimental.head_wing_schur
evidence: Git history confirms the first-pass hypothesis exactly: commit 1134822 (2026-05-03) created the module at src/gw/head_wing_schur.py; housekeeping commit 62b0e94 moved it ('Move gw/head_wing_schur.py (274 lines) to gw/experimental/head_wing_schur.py', diffstat 'src/gw/{ => experimental}/head_wing_schur.py | 2 +-') and touched only 4 lines of the test — the functional import at line 64 and the sys.path comment at line 23 were updated to gw.experimental.head_wing_schur, but the line-1 docstring kept the pre-move path. Purely cosmetic; the test imports and runs against the correct module. One-word docstring fix.

item: use_query_fft_peak_bytes kwarg + AOT branch in _peak_D_accumulate (src/gw/gflat_memory_model.py:433, 483-499) and plan_gflat_chunks kwarg L658
evidence: Default False; zero call sites pass it: gw_init.py:606-635 omits it, all 10 test calls omit it, and the one sandbox driver (runs/CrI3/6x6_80Ry_gnppm_maxbands_2026-06-15/planner_predict.py) omits it. fft_helpers.query_fft_peak_bytes itself is alive via gw_init.py:343/351 and v_q_tile.py — but the gflat planner's AOT branch L483-499 is unreachable. item: use_ibz_T kwarg / _peak_E_v_q_bispinor_buffer 9+6-tile branch (src/gw/gflat_memory_model.py:610-631, 657)
evidence: REFUTES first-pass claim of confirmed-dead. Production gw_init.py:606-635 never passes it (so the branch never fires in a real run), but /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/runs/CrI3/6x6_80Ry_gnppm_maxbands_2026-06-15/planner_predict.py:36-60 passes use_ibz_T=True (with n_q_ibz=8) into the REAL plan_gflat_chunks — a 2026-06-15 offline analysis harness, i.e. bench/analysis-only. Two extra facts: (1) the docstring L696 claims 'use_ibz_T <- derived from sym + centroid orbit closure' but that wiring does not exist in gw_init — meanwhile the runtime cascade is real (v_q_bispinor.py:576 _use_ibz_T), so when the runtime cascade activates the planner silently omits the 9+6-tile lorentz-mix buffer (latent HWM under-prediction, not just cruft). (2) Per the TT in-plane unfold bug (transverse IBZ-unfold gives wrong in-plane Sigma^B), transverse IBZ may be retired anyway — decide the kwarg's fate together with that fix.

item: plan_gflat_chunks kwarg n_q_ibz (src/gw/gflat_memory_model.py:659, 728-733)
evidence: REFUTES confirmed-dead: planner_predict.py:45 passes n_q_ibz=(8 if use_ibz_T else None). No production caller passes it (gw_init.py:606-635), and the default None->nq full-BZ fallback (L731-733) is load-bearing for the conservative Peak E estimate, so it cannot be deleted without replacing Peak E's n_q_ibz argument with nq.

item: plan_gflat_chunks kwargs pair_density_slots_charge/transverse, fft_box_factor_A, fft_box_factor_D (src/gw/gflat_memory_model.py:648-651)
evidence: Zero call sites pass any of them: gw_init.py:620 uses only the legacy alias fft_box_factor=4.0 (which overwrites fft_box_factor_A at L705-706); no test or sandbox script passes them (manifest.yaml hits in runs/Si/NONBISPINOR_CPU_2026-05-20 and runs/CrI3/M_6x6_80Ry.../lorrax_D_bispinor_hlo_2026-05-17 are prose documentation of the calibration, not calls). Nuance: dead as caller-settable knobs only — their DEFAULTS are load-bearing (pair_density_slots resolves via _default_pair_density_slots() backend branch L759-765, HLO-calibrated GPU=3/CPU=4; fft_box_factor_D=2.0 feeds Peak D L835/929). Removal means inlining defaults, not deleting the logic.

item: _peak_C_fit_one_rchunk params band_chunk, n_bc, n_rtot, p_x, p_y, fft_box_factor, is_charge_channel (src/gw/gflat_memory_model.py:364-368)
evidence: Body (L396-427) references only nk, ns, nq, nq_disk, mu, ngkmax, r_chunk, nb_total, p, p_xy, pair_density_slots, fft_grid, n_sphere_buffers — verified term-by-term against every dict entry. item: no-op branch L885-888 (band_chunk_pre / if...pass)
evidence: L885-888: band_chunk_pre = band_chunk; ... if band_chunk != band_chunk_pre and band_chunk_pre < p_xy: pass. The body is literally `pass` and band_chunk_pre has no other use in the function (the override-path warning at L890-898 uses bc_pre = int(band_chunk_override), a different variable).
