The only consumer of the re-export path 'from common.load_wfns import make_sharded_ifftn_3d' is tests/archive/test_chunked_wfn_loading.py:419, and pyproject.toml sets norecursedirs=["archive"], so pytest never collects it — effectively dead. NOTE: the function itself is very alive via its home module common/fft_helpers.py:304 (used by src/bse/bse_simple.py, bse_lanczos.py, davidson_absorption.py, absorption_haydock.py, bse_ring_comm.py, common/wfn_transforms.py:284); only the load_wfns re-export is removable.

item: src/common/load_wfns.py: use_phdf5 kwarg of load_centroids_band_chunked
evidence: First-pass claim 'no caller passes it' is FALSE: src/centroid/pivoted_cholesky.py:961 and :977 pass use_phdf5=use_phdf5 to load_centroids_band_chunked, wired from the live CLI flag --use-phdf5 (src/centroid/kmeans_cli.py:108 add_argument, :366 _prune_kwargs) through pivoted_cholesky params at :295 and :799. The kwarg IS functionally inert (del use_phdf5 at load_wfns.py:357; WfnLoader backend='auto' decides), and pivoted_cholesky.py:953 prints a now-misleading backend={'phdf5'|'WFNReader'} label — but removal is a multi-file signature change (CLI flag + 2 signatures + 2 call sites), not a local deletion.

item: src/common/async_io.py: AsyncDispatcher (whole module)
evidence: Sole consumer is AsyncWfnReader (src/file_io/wfn_loader.py:1214 imports, :1218 instantiates), and AsyncWfnReader is never instantiated anywhere: repo-wide grep over src/tests/tools/scripts/docs hits only its class definition (wfn_loader.py:1179), the __all__ export (:1260), its own docstring usage example (:1193), and a comment at src/common/psi_G_store.py:195 saying it 'is available'. No test references async_io/AsyncWfnReader; no getattr/dict dispatch on these names; no console_scripts; no hits in sandbox skills/ or runs/ run scripts. SlabIO does NOT use it — file_io/_slab_io_ffi.py keeps its own _dispatch_loop (async_io docstring line 3-4 says 'generalises' it, but the write side was never migrated). Dead designed-ahead API: async_io.py + wfn_loader.py:1176-1257 are removable together.

item: src/common/load_wfns.py:87-91: sentinel band-energy pad = max(energies)+1.0 Ry
evidence: Comment at load_wfns.py:79-82 states the intent verbatim: sentinel-fill past-file bands so f_n=step(E_F-e)=0, finite 'max(real e)+1 Ry' instead of inf keeps PPM resolvent 1/(w-e+i.eta) safe under fp warnings. Verified interactions: sentinel > all file energies >= E_F, so np.where(enk<=efermi,...) at :108 selects the cond branch for sentinel bands -> weight 1/sqrt(sentinel-E_min), small and finite (no 1e-12 floor blow-up); sentinel cannot enter E_max/E_min since those come from energies_full[:, sigma_lo:sigma_hi] read directly from file (:100-101), not from padded enk. Hypothesis confirmed.

item: src/common/load_wfns.py:60-72: numpy-not-jnp guard block
evidence: git log confirms the cited commit exists and matches: 31b5961 'load_wfns: keep get_enk_bandrange on host (numpy), emit JAX only at return'. Arrays are host-side nk x nb doubles; each jnp reduction/where/repeat would dispatch as its own pjit at trace time (~16 standalone compiles per docstring). jnp.asarray cast only at return (:115) preserves the caller pytree contract. Deliberate compile-cache trim; do not revert.

item: src/common/load_wfns.py:106-113: 1/sqrt band-weight heuristic
evidence: Per-element math: for band energy e, valence branch (e<=E_F) gets w=1/sqrt(max(E_max-e,1e-12)) with E_max=top of sigma window (:101-103); conduction branch gets w=1/sqrt(max(e-E_min,1e-12)) with E_min=bottom of window — i.e. monotone 1/sqrt decay measured from the FAR window edge, sane zeta-fit least-squares weighting. Sub-case check of the 1e-12 floor: it can only fire if a valence band (e<=E_F) sits at E_max or a conduction band at E_min — impossible since E_max >= window conduction energies > E_F >= valence e (and symmetrically for E_min) — so wmax stays O(1/sqrt(gap)) and the :109-111 normalization never degenerates. In-window bands forced to 1.0 (:112) after normalization; np.repeat by nspinor (:113) matches the documented (nk, nb*nspinor) contract (:58). One real doc defect: the inline comment at :94-95 has the branch labels SWAPPED vs code ('1/sqrt(Ec-E) for conduction, 1/sqrt(E-Ev) for valence' — code does the mirror). Code behavior intentional; fix the comment. Exponent -1/2 undocumented but bounded.

item: src/common/load_wfns.py:387-399: peak_copies=4/9, gpu_mem_bytes=36e9
evidence: Comment at :383-386 documents provenance: 'same conservative XLA scratch multiplier used historically by the old k_chunk_size autodetect (4 on single-rank, 9 on multi-rank — covers the IFFT scratch + IFFT output)'. 36e9 is only a fallback — overridden whenever meta.memory_per_device_gb>0 (:390-391), which the sandbox always sets (28 Perlmutter / 6 local per CLAUDE.md). Load-bearing for cs_budget (:398-399) hence for OOM avoidance; empirically-fit multipliers go silently stale if XLA IFFT scratch behavior changes, and the tuned successor already exists (gw/gflat_memory_model.py, referenced at :371-374 and refit per reports/memory_model_refit_2026-05-17). Not a bug today; should be unified with the planner model.

item: src/common/load_wfns.py:245,433: loader._ensure_sym() private reach-in
evidence: Confirmed: WfnLoader exposes no public SymMaps/kvecs accessor (grep wfn_loader.py: _ensure_sym defined at :294, kvecs only appear internally at :939-950,:1050-1091; no @property for either). Three external private-attr reach-ins exist: load_wfns.py:245, :433 (both to get sym_loader.kvecs_asints for kvecs_frac), and common/psi_G_store.py:295. All three want the same thing — a kvecs_frac/SymMaps accessor on WfnLoader — consistent with the project's 'augment SymMaps with accessors, one canonical IBZ table' direction. Works correctly today (SymMaps is built lazily and cached in loader._sym), just an encapsulation smell.

item: src/common/load_wfns.py:26,144,210,357-358: del sym x3 + del use_phdf5 + stale WFNReader docstrings
evidence: Confirmed back-compat ballast from the WFNReader->WfnLoader migration. del sym at :26,:144,:210 (each docstring explains: loader builds its own SymMaps, param kept 'so existing callers don't have to change'); del use_phdf5 at :357. Stale docs: :319 'wfn: WFNReader' while the body requires WfnLoader (loader.load/:462, loader.box_index_dev/:432, loader.nbands/:459); :50 same in get_enk_bandrange; comment at :455 cites 'file_io/wfn_loader.py:678' for the b_hi<=nbands validation but it actually lives at wfn_loader.py:829; pivoted_cholesky.py:953 prints 'backend=WFNReader' which no longer exists as a path. Cleanup is safe but non-local: use_phdf5 has live callers (kmeans_cli.py:366, pivoted_cholesky.py:961,977) and sym is passed positionally at every call site (gw_init.py:796,:1221; htransform.py:87; pivoted_cholesky.py:959,:975; kin_ion_io.py:194; scf_potential.py:151; run_sternheimer.py:820; dft_operators.py:1113), so signature changes must sweep all of them.

item: src/common/load_wfns.py: load_kpoint_fftbox k_idx = unfolded full-BZ index
evidence: Documented trap, already consistent across the loader API. src/psp/dev_status.md:107-113 spells it out (IBZ vs unfolded coincide for k=0..2, diverge at k>=3, ~1-5 mRy errors if misused); src/psp/scf_potential.py:149 comment repeats it; psp/tests/test_dft_hamiltonian.py:133 works around it explicitly. Verified the 'unify with read_Gvecs_to_devices' hypothesis is already satisfied: both functions feed the same WfnLoader.load(k=...) parameter (load_kpoint_fftbox k=[int(k_idx)] at :30; read_Gvecs_to_devices k=list(range(k_range)) at :152), so both use the identical full-BZ index space resolved by loader._resolve_k with internal unfold. The remaining hazard is purely caller-side IBZ/full-BZ confusion, which is documented; no code change needed beyond possibly renaming the arg to k_full_idx.

item: src/common/load_wfns.py:447-478: past-mnband cap + zero-pad
evidence: This is the loader's SANCTIONED pattern, not a hack around it: WfnLoader.load's validation error at wfn_loader.py:829-832 reads 'band range out of [0, nbands); use bands_pad_to-style external padding for over-file requests' — i.e. the loader deliberately delegates over-file padding to callers. The cap (b_end_in_file=min(b_end, loader.nbands) at :460) + zero-pad back to nb_total (:467-477, sharding-preserving concat) implements exactly that for the Meta band-rounding contract (b_id_4=round_up(b_id_4_user, world_size), CrI3 mnband=86/world=16 => 96, per :447-458 comment citing common/meta.py:100-117). Pad rows are physically zero and the user-band-pad block at :526-533 re-zeros the (b_id_4_user, b_id_4) slots consistently. One nit: the comment's line ref ':678' is stale (validation moved to :829).

item: src/common/async_io.py:118-121: first-error-only stash in _loop
evidence: Deliberate and documented: docstring :49-55 'Worker exceptions are stashed in self._error and re-raised on the next caller-side submit/drain/close. This matches SlabIO's existing behaviour'. Verified mechanics: _loop catches BaseException (:118), keeps only the first (:120-121 'if self._error is None'), decrements _pending in finally (:123-125) so drain() never hangs; _raise_if_error (:104-109) clears and re-raises at the next coordination point. Subsequent errors ARE silently dropped (the caveat is real — cascading failures after the first are invisible), but that is the stated FIFO-coordination-point contract, and the entire module is currently dead code (see AsyncDispatcher verdict), so there is no production exposure.

item: src/common/async_io.py:89-95: close() error path leaves worker alive
evidence: Confirmed by trace: close() (:89) calls self.drain() (:92) BEFORE setting _closed=True (:93) or sending the poison pill (:94); drain() calls _raise_if_error() (:82) which raises any stashed worker error. On that path close() unwinds with _closed still False, no poison pill sent, and the daemon worker thread still parked on queue.get() (:113). __exit__ (:100-102) propagates the error, so a with-block whose last task failed exits leaving a live thread. Mitigations: daemon=True (:66) means it dies at process exit, and _raise_if_error clears _error so a SECOND close() would succeed — but nothing retries close. Correct fix is drain-then-mark-closed-then-pill in a try/finally. Severity near-zero today because AsyncDispatcher has no live consumers (module is confirmed-dead), but the defect is real if the module is ever wired in.

item: src/common/psi_G_store.py — imports partial/io_callback/shard_map (l.44,50,51)
evidence: grep of psi_G_store.py: 'partial' appears only at l.44; 'io_callback' and 'shard_map' appear only at import lines l.50-51 and inside comments/docstrings (l.17,23,115,149,154,172,311,314,334,347). The kernel that used them moved to isdf_fitting.py in commit 5cadd4b (shard_map decorator at isdf_fitting.py:657, _io_callback at l.733). Only _PSI_G_FLAT_SPEC (isdf_fitting.py:535) and build_psi_G_store (isdf_fitting.py:2433) are imported from this module anywhere; no getattr/string dispatch, no console_scripts entry (pyproject.toml [project.scripts] = gw.gw_jax:main, centroid.kmeans_cli:main), zero hits in lorrax_sandbox skills/ and runs/. Safe to delete the three imports.

item: src/common/psi_G_store.py — sym parameter of build_psi_G_store (l.443, del at l.461)
evidence: Explicit `del sym` at psi_G_store.py:461 with docstring 'kept for caller-API back-compat but is ignored' (l.457-459). Exhaustive caller search: the ONLY call site in src/, tests/, docs/, skills/, runs/ is isdf_fitting.py:2434 (`build_psi_G_store(wfn=wfn, sym=sym, ...)`). Tests use _FakePsiGStore (test_psi_g_store.py:63) and mock stores (test_zq_from_psi_sm_bit_identity.py:97), never the factory. The 'back-compat' justification protects nothing external — removing the kwarg requires touching exactly one in-repo call site.

item: src/common/psi_G_store.py — begin_rchunk(r_start, r_end) arguments (l.391, l.433)
evidence: All four implementations ignore the values: base PsiGStore.begin_rchunk l.391-394 (empty body), RereadPsiGStore.begin_rchunk l.433-434 calls _populate_from_loader() which reloads ALL band chunks with no r-window (l.180-300 has no r_start/r_end reference), AOT stub fit_one_rchunk.py:108 signature is `def begin_rchunk(self, *_): pass`, and the test mock (test_psi_g_store.py:91) overrides only end_rchunk. Sole caller isdf_fitting.py:2561 passes (r_start, r_end) into the void. An r-window would be structurally meaningless: host tiles are (nk, nb_local, ns, ngkmax) — the r axis exists only on device inside the jit. Arguments are interface noise; the reader's claim that the window param was never implemented is correct.

item: src/common/psi_G_store.py l.150-157, 305-325 — archaeology comments citing commits cdd0fba/5cadd4b and psi_G_device_full
evidence: The hashes are real and the history is accurate: git log confirms cdd0fba 'Path D scaffolding: to_rchunk_inner + _slice_local_tile_bc' (2026-05-13) and 5cadd4b 'isdf_fitting + psi_G_store: integrate gflat_to_rchunk; drop bc-loop + psig_k_chunk plumbing'. psi_G_device_full indeed no longer exists in-file — its absence is actively ENFORCED by test_psi_g_store.py:159-166 (test_psi_G_device_full_property_removed asserts not hasattr(store, 'psi_G_device_full')). The comments also carry live technical content (the io_callback static out_sds contract, l.154-157 and l.314-319) that must survive any pruning. Trim the hash-level archaeology if desired, but keep the static-shape contract paragraphs — they explain why _bpd_max exists.

item: src/common/psi_G_store.py l.322-325 + l.362 — np.zeros (not np.empty) on the pad buffer claimed load-bearing for IFFT precision
evidence: Per-element check of the sole production consumer (isdf_fitting.py:733-771): the io_callback slab goes to_rchunk_inner (batched spatial IFFT — no cross-band mixing, garbage in pad band rows stays in pad band rows) → all_gather along band axis → jnp.where(l_mask, psi, 0) where l_mask includes bc_valid = g_axis < b_hi_global[bc_idx] (l.765-771). Because the mask is a where-SELECT, not a multiply, even NaN/Inf garbage from np.empty would be replaced by exact 0 — so the comment's specific claim ('could still pollute IFFT precision') is not substantiated for the current consumer. However np.zeros IS the documented API contract (_slice_local_tile_bc docstring l.341-344 promises 'remaining rows are zero... math-neutral'), tests exercise it, and correctness under np.empty would silently depend on every future consumer using select-masks. Keep np.zeros; the cost is negligible next to the tile copy. Classification: intentional defensive contract with a slightly overstated rationale.

item: src/common/psi_G_store.py l.229-246 — whole-bc-past-EOF branch: comment claims b_hi-b_lo==0 and 'pre-zero via np.empty', yet code guards b_hi-b_lo>0 and zero-fills
evidence:
Comment rot confirmed, code correct. (1) 'host_tile entries already pre-zero via np.empty' (l.235-236) is factually false — np.empty (l.192) does not zero. (2) 'bpd_per_bc[bc]==0 since nb_total

0, and the guard+zero-fill at l.244-246 handles exactly that. (3) The zero-fill is genuinely load-bearing: band_range_right=(meta.b_id_0, meta.b_id_4) at isdf_fitting.py:1965 uses the PADDED b_id_4, so the einsum L/R masks (isdf_fitting.py:766-767) do NOT exclude bands in [b_id_4_user, b_id_4) — host-tile content for those slots must be physically zero (normally via _zero_user_band_pad_in_shard l.273-277, for all-pad bcs via this branch). Fix the comment; do not touch the guard.

item: src/common/psi_G_store.py l.277 — getattr(self.meta, 'b_id_4_user', self.meta.b_id_4) soft fallback
evidence: The fallback is unreachable AND masks the real hazard. Meta declares b_id_4_user as a dataclass field with default 0 (meta.py:46), so every real Meta HAS the attribute and getattr never falls back to b_id_4. The classmethod path sets it correctly (meta.py:107,159: b_id_4_user=int(nband)), but a directly-constructed Meta leaves it at 0 — then getattr returns 0, user_band_stop=0, and _zero_user_band_pad_in_shard's pad_mask = local_global_bands >= 0 (l.86) zeros EVERY band, silently corrupting the whole ψ cache. No live path triggers this today (the only direct Meta(...) constructions are fit_one_rchunk.py:435, which uses _AotStubPsiGStore and never calls _populate_from_loader, and tests/archive/). Replace the getattr with self.meta.b_id_4_user plus an assert b_id_4_user > 0 (or fall back on ==0, not on missing attribute).

item: src/common/psi_G_store.py l.295 — sym = self.loader._ensure_sym() private-API reach
evidence: _ensure_sym is WfnLoader's private lazy SymMaps initializer (wfn_loader.py:294-298); no public accessor exists. The cross-module private call is already established practice — load_wfns.py:245 and :433 do the same — so psi_G_store.py:295 is consistent with the codebase, not an outlier. Aligns with the standing 'unified sym action' direction (one canonical sym accessor): promote to a public property/accessor on WfnLoader and update the three call sites in one pass; do not add a parallel helper.

item: src/common/psi_G_store.py l.401-403 — close() docstring promises to drop the loader reference but only clears tiles
evidence: Doc/code mismatch confirmed: close() (l.401-403) calls only _clear_tiles(). But dropping self.loader would free nothing: build_psi_G_store deliberately aliases the caller's top-level WfnLoader (l.462-463: 'reuse top-level WfnLoader; opening a second one would re-slurp wfns/coeffs into host RAM'), and the sole close() caller documents the survival as intended (isdf_fitting.py:2717-2719: 'The phdf5 reader itself is cached at module level and survives'). The loader's host coeff cache is caller-owned, not store-owned, so there is no lingering-RAM bug attributable to close(). Fix: reword the docstring (or add self.loader = None for hygiene); no behavioral change needed.

item: src/common/psi_G_store.py l.195-201 — dead-experiment note re AsyncWfnReader depth-2 prefetch, overlap_frac 0.000
evidence: The note is a deliberate, evidence-backed decision record (measured xprof overlap_frac 0.000 at MoS2 3x3, explicit revisit condition 'until scale grows (CrI3)') — exactly the kind of comment that prevents re-running a failed experiment. The hypothesis is confirmed: AsyncWfnReader still exists at wfn_loader.py:1179, is exported in __all__ (wfn_loader.py:1260), and has ZERO callers anywhere in src/, tests/, tools/, scripts/ (only its own docstring, common/async_io.py's module docstring, and this note reference it; AsyncDispatcher in async_io.py is used only by AsyncWfnReader itself, wfn_loader.py:1214). Keep the note; separately, AsyncWfnReader+AsyncDispatcher are a dormant orphan pair — candidates for deletion in a future sweep if the CrI3-scale revisit never materializes.

item: src/common/psi_G_store.py l.165,193,422-424 — hard-wired complex128 and rank-0 bare print banner
evidence: complex128 is the uniform precision of the entire zeta-fit pipeline, not a local choice: isdf_fitting.py hard-codes jnp.complex128 at l.302,307,457,462,642 and the carries at l.723-726; the slicer out_sds (isdf_fitting.py:641-642) and _per_rank_shape_bytes '*16' (psi_G_store.py:427) assume it; the AOT memory model's stub also uses np.complex128 (fit_one_rchunk.py:106). Mixed precision would be a cross-cutting pipeline project, not a psi_G_store fix. The rank-0 print (l.422-424, gated on jax.process_index()==0) matches house style — isdf_fitting.py:2724 emits its summary the same way, while common.timing sections (used at l.202,248,268) handle perf; the banner is informational, not timing. No action warranted in this file alone.

item: SymMaps.get_qpt_id_from_kkp (symmetry_maps.py:981)
evidence: Only reference anywhere is the definition (src/common/symmetry_maps.py:981-984). Zero hits across repo *.py/*.sh/*.md/*.toml/*.yaml/*.in, misc/ (incl. archived_tests/*.ipynb), docs/, tools/, scripts/, tmp/, config/, profile/, and sandbox skills/, runs/, tools/. item: SymMaps.get_kminusqfull_map + kqfull_map attr (symmetry_maps.py:1351, :939, :809-814)
evidence: get_kminusqfull_map is called once, at :939, storing self.kqfull_map which is never read afterward. In the trivial no-symmetry branch kqfull_map (:809) is read only at :814 as an immediate self-copy into kq_map (removable alias; kq_map could be assigned directly from the lookup). kq_map IS alive (kq_mapping.py:51, projectors.py, run_sternheimer.py, psp/tests/test_sternheimer_jvp.py:110) but kqfull_map has zero external readers in repo, sandbox skills/runs/tools, docs, or notebooks. Bonus rot: the :1351 method body is a line-for-line duplicate of get_kminusq_map (:1330-1349) with the q loop widened to nk_full — pure O(nk_full^2) dead work at :939.

item: all_unfolded_qpts / all_unfolded_qpt_ids attrs (symmetry_maps.py:818-826, :954-961)
evidence: Zero external references (same exhaustive grep set as above). all_unfolded_qpt_ids has NO consumer at all — built at :823 and :957-961 (an O(n_q_unique * nk^2) python loop wasted on every SymMaps init) and never read. all_unfolded_qpts' sole reader is the confirmed-dead get_qpt_id_from_kkp (:984). The whole cluster (both attrs + method + the qpt_vecs build at :944-961 that exists only to feed them) is removable together; note :944-948 also assigns self.kvecs_asints which IS alive (used by find_irreducible_bz_points call at :968-969 and orbit code), so keep the kvecs_asints construction.

item: local R_dev in unfold_v_q_bispinor_lorentz (symmetry_maps.py:548)
evidence: R_dev = jnp.asarray(R_per_q) at :548 with comment '# closure constant', but the actual closure constant is created independently inside _get_unfold_v_q_lorentz_jit at :589 (R_per_q_j = jnp.asarray(R_per_q_arr)). R_dev is never referenced between :548 and the function's return at :566. Leftover from before the jit-cache extraction; also forces a pointless host->device transfer of the (n_q_full,3,3) table on every call.

item: SymMaps.validate_kgrid_unfolding (symmetry_maps.py:1125)
evidence: Sole caller is src/common/symmetry_test.py:22 inside run_symmetry_test, a standalone argparse debug CLI (raise SystemExit(...) at :58, python -m invocation only). symmetry_test itself has zero references in pyproject.toml scripts, docs/, skills/, tools/, or any run script under /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/runs (checked *.sh at depths 1-2; deep-tree grep of runs/ for validate_kgrid_unfolding via background grep returned empty). Not confirmed-dead because the diagnostic CLI is a legitimate (if unreferenced) smoke-test entry; docs/theory/symmetry.md's gate list references validate_atomic_symmetries but not this one.

item: no-break ikbar loops: highest matching IBZ index wins (symmetry_maps.py:77-83, :1077-1084)
evidence: find_symmetry_ops_simple (:1077-1084) iterates all ikbar without break, so the LAST (highest) matching ikbar overwrites, with matches[0] (lowest sym) for that ikbar; find_irreducible_bz_points' k-side branch replicates this deliberately with an explicit comment (:70-72 'outer iter over ikbar without break => HIGHEST ikbar with any match wins... Preserves bit-equality with prior code'). Correctness-neutral: for a proper IBZ each full k has exactly one matching ikbar, so which-wins only matters if wfn.kpoints contained sym-equivalent duplicates, and downstream consumers use the recorded (irr_idx, sym_idx) pair consistently (e.g. validate_kgrid_unfolding recomputes S*k_irr+kg0 from the stored pair, :1144-1149). Cost is wasted O(n_irr) inner work and a frozen quirk any refactor must reproduce bit-for-bit — a documented hazard, not a bug.

item: trivial-IBZ short-circuit in unfold_v_q (symmetry_maps.py:244-255)
evidence: Guard requires irr_idx == arange AND sym_idx all-zero (:252-254), which exactly matches the no-symmetry SymMaps branch (irr_idx_q=arange :830, sym_idx_q=0 :831); under those conditions the general path is mathematically identity (sym_perm[0]=identity perm, L_table[0]=0 => phase=1), so returning V_q_ibz unchanged is sound. The comment (:246-249) documents the motivation: sharded take_along_axis codegen 'observed to trip an XLA HLO verifier dtype mismatch (s64 broadcast vs s32 operand on a 2x2 mesh)'. Deliberate, correct workaround; the reader's 'retest on newer jax and drop' suggestion is valid housekeeping but nothing here is wrong.

item: R_proper is transpose of offline derivation fixture; compensated by 'qai,qbj,abqmn->ijqmn' einsum (symmetry_maps.py:904-937, :593-608)
evidence: Index math checks out: einsum gives out[i,j,q] = sum_{a,b} R[q,a,i]*R[q,b,j]*V_in[a,b,q] = sum R_LORRAX^{ai} R_LORRAX^{bj} V^{ab}; with R_deriv = R_LORRAX.T (orthogonal mtrx, per :598-601) this is exactly the derivation SS-A5 formula V^{ij} = sum R_deriv^{i,alpha} R_deriv^{j,beta} V^{alpha,beta}. Both conventions are documented at both sites (:918-925 NOTE block; :595-603) and cross-referenced to reports/bispinor_ibz_2026-05-16/derivation.md. Crucially the suspicion that this transpose hides the known CrI3 in-plane TT failure was already adjudicated: reports/bispinor_tt_conditioning_2026-06-16/report.md line 17 (R_proper orthogonal for C3, |RR^T-I|=1.1e-7), line 22 (the IBZ input data itself is non-C3-covariant), line 59 ('the unfold itself is correct; a separate R^T M R vs R M R^T transpose question is moot'). Unifying the fixture to one convention remains nice-to-have documentation work, not a correctness issue.

item: translations /(2*pi) in validate_atomic_symmetries vs raw in unfold_psi (symmetry_maps.py:1100 vs :700-704)
evidence: wfn.translations stores BGW's raw tnp = 2*pi*tau_frac. docs/theory/symmetry.md:48 states 'LORRAX divides by 2pi at every consumer... unfold_psi is the one exception — see SS3.1', and SS3.1 (symmetry.md:220): 'tau is the raw BGW value (= 2pi tau_frac). The exponent is therefore -i*(S G_kbar)*2pi*tau_frac, exactly BGW's cmplx(0,-1)*tpi*dot_product(...) in Common/gmap.f90.' Per-element check: unfold_psi's phase (:703-704) uses integer G, which needs the 2pi factor to form the physical exp(-2pi*i G.tau_frac); validate_atomic_symmetries (:1100-1104) transforms fractional atom coordinates rot@pos+tau, which needs tau_frac. Same convention as compute_centroid_sym_perm and build_real_space_syms (orbit_syms.py:344, :57). Deliberate, documented, and unit-consistent at every consumer.

item: zone-wrap snap thresholds 0.99999 vs 0.9999 (symmetry_maps.py:992 vs :1394)
evidence: _wrap_to_bz (:991-993) snaps wrapped>0.99999 (tol 1e-5); _get_umklapp_vector's TRS branch (:1393-1394) snaps q_inzone>0.9999 (tol 1e-4). Copy-paste drift, but harmless for any realistic mesh: the nearest legitimate nonzero grid fraction is 1 - 1/max(kgrid) (0.9167 at 12x12x12), orders of magnitude below either threshold, while float noise on q_full = S@kpoints is ~1e-15, orders below either tolerance. Both branches therefore classify every point identically. Worth harmonizing to one named constant when touched. (Adjacent sharper hazard noticed while verifying, outside this finding's scope: :1395 casts kg0 via .astype(np.int32) truncation rather than np.rint — safe today only because the diff is exactly-integer-valued up to ~1e-15 noise.)

item: nearest-IBZ / identity silent fallbacks on unmapped k-points (symmetry_maps.py:1050-1063, :874-881)
evidence: create_kpoint_symmetry_map (:1050-1057): if no stored sym maps a full-grid k to any irreducible k, it silently assigns the NEAREST irreducible point with only a warnings.warn (:1059-1063) — downstream this yields psi at the wrong k and silently wrong Sigma, exactly the eV-scale silent-corruption class this codebase has been burned by (the TRS-blind sym_perm OOB-clip bug produced max 13.85 eV V_q errors with no error raised). kirr_fullids (:874-881) has an even quieter identity fallback with NO warning. This directly contradicts the file's own newer policy: unfold_v_q raises 'rather than relying on JAX's silent OOB clamp' (:222-223, :259-271) and find_irreducible_bz_points raises RuntimeError on unmapped points (:84-89, :100-103). Incomplete WFN symmetry data should be a hard error (or at minimum an opt-in flag); a warning in a 10k-line log stream is not a safety mechanism. Note I found no evidence the fallback currently fires on any production system — it is a latent wrong-physics path, not a demonstrated live miscomputation.

item: jit caches keyed on tobytes() of tables + id(mesh_xy) (symmetry_maps.py:351-361, :580-584)
evidence: _get_unfold_v_q_jit key (:351-361) and _get_unfold_v_q_lorentz_jit key (:580-584) include id(mesh_xy). Two consequences verified from the code: (a) an equal-but-recreated Mesh gets a new id => silent recompile (perf only; matches the reader's hypothesis); (b) sharper latent hazard: the module-level dicts (:336, :569) never evict, and the key stores only the integer id — if the original mesh is GC'd and a DIFFERENT mesh later reuses that id with identical table bytes/shapes, the cache returns a jit closure whose NamedShardings (:590-591) reference the dead mesh. In practice LORRAX builds one process-lifetime mesh so neither fires today. Fix is cheap: key on (mesh.axis_names, tuple(mesh.devices.flat ids), mesh.shape) instead of id(). The tobytes() content-keying of the sym tables themselves is sound and deliberately documented (:312-318, sharing one HLO between V_q and W_q).

item: find_qpoint_index uses jnp for host scalar search (symmetry_maps.py:1408-1428)
evidence: ALIVE — called from src/gw/vcoul.py:166 (iq = sym.find_qpoint_index(qvec_wrapped, tol=1e-6)) in host-side setup. Body (:1420-1428) does jnp.abs/jnp.sum/jnp.min/jnp.argmin over self.unfolded_kpts, a small host numpy array: each call transfers the table to device, launches four trivial kernels, then blocks on min_diff>tol (:1425) and returns a device scalar the caller uses as a host index. Every sibling helper in the class (_wrap_to_bz, _periodic_delta, get_kminusq_map) is numpy. Pure leftover from the device-side era; s/jnp/np/ is a strict improvement (avoids per-q device sync and works before jax device init). No correctness issue — mod, abs-sum metric, and tol check are fine (though the plain q_frac = q_ext % 1.0 without a boundary snap at :1419 could mis-measure a q within 1e-6 below an integer; unfolded_kpts are snapped by _wrap_to_bz, and the sum-abs metric is not periodic — same latent-edge-case class as the snap-threshold finding, never triggered on grid-commensurate q).

item: unfold_psi docstring documents n_sym_spatial parameter absent from signature (symmetry_maps.py:660-663 vs :618-626, :688)
evidence: Confirmed: the Parameters block at :660-663 lists 'n_sym_spatial : int — Count of spatial-only sym ops (= wfn.ntran)', but the signature (:618-626) takes only (cnk_kbar, *, sym_idx, g_kbar, sym_mats_k, translations, U_spinor_spatial); the value is derived internally at :688 as sym_mats_k.shape[0] // 2 (valid because sym_mats_k is always TRS-augmented to 2*ntran, :852-856). Stale docstring from the PR3 TRS refactor (docs/theory/symmetry.md:251 describes exactly this consolidation). The docstring text is even self-inconsistent with the module-level unfold_v_q docs (:207-209) which still tell callers to 'pass n_sym_spatial=ntran' to that OTHER function, where the param does exist. One-line doc fix; the code is right.

evidence: Confirmed: top imports end at :19 (gamma_matrices), then the mem-probe block (:21-127, three defs: _mem_report, mem_probe, _nvsmi_used_mb_local_gpu), then stranded imports :128-141 — consistent with the block being inserted above the original import section. item: fit_zeta_to_h5 comments 2022-2025 and 2131-2135: claim SVD pseudoinverse with rcond cutoff
evidence: Comments at :2022-2025 ('robust solver in solve_zeta uses an SVD pseudoinverse with rcond cutoff') and :2131-2135 (same) are FALSE about the code: grep for svd|pinv|rcond hits only these two comments; solve_zeta's actual transverse path is ridge LU_RIDGE=1e-12 · |tr|/n on the diagonal + pivoted LU (jnp.linalg.solve at :1393, _ridge_indef_solve :1382-1393, cusolvermp_lu branch :1335-1341), with an accurate comment at :1363-1374 explicitly saying LU-with-ridge and that Bunch-Kaufman/SVD alternatives are not used. Suspected-bug in the DOCUMENTATION sense only (code is self-consistent): these stale comments predate the LU redesign and will actively mislead a refactoring agent about null-mode handling (ridge lifts null modes; it does NOT drop them like an rcond cutoff would — different physics for the near-null transverse-current modes).

item: z_q_from_psi_sm 599-636 / 683-706: front+back zero-padding of psi_X band axis
evidence: Index math verified per-element. Y-side gathered slot j of band-chunk bc carries global band g=bc.lo+j (g_axis at :763-764); masks key on g (:766-767). X-side padded array layout: [front_pad zeros][window L_lo_g..L_hi_g)[back_pad zeros], so padded index p ↔ global band p−front_pad+L_lo_g. Slice offset off=bc.lo−L_lo_g+front_pad (:620-625) ⇒ slice element j = global band (off+j)−front_pad+L_lo_g = bc.lo+j — exact alignment with Y-side slot j. Bounds: front_pad=max_bc(L_lo_g−bc.lo)⁺ guarantees off≥0; back_pad=max_end−(front_pad+nb) (:629-636) guarantees off+bpd_max_global ≤ padded length, so XLA dynamic_slice never clamps. WITHOUT the back-pad, clamp shifts the slice start by Δ>0, delivering band bc.lo+j−Δ at slot j while the mask still assumes bc.lo+j → silently wrong pair density (round6_discussion.md:506 BLOCKER, cited at :611, :694). Masked-in slots never land in pad rows (window bands map into the real block), pad rows only under masked-out slots (zeroed anyway) — math-neutral. Load-bearing; must survive any refactor byte-for-byte in intent.

item: z_q_from_psi_sm 594-598, 678-681: per-bc offset tables as numpy, lifted to jnp inside Manual-mode body
evidence: Confirmed at :588-594 (tables built as np arrays 'NOT jnp.asarray' with explicit rationale: closure-captured Auto-sharded jax.Arrays inside a Manual-mode shard_map body trigger a mesh-context mismatch, 'same issue as kvecs_frac in the earlier debug') and :674-681 (jnp.asarray lift inside _local). Corroborated by the parallel workaround at :649-654: g_index/kvecs_frac are threaded through in_specs 'NOT closure' for the same reason. This is a real JAX shard_map constraint, not cruft — a refactor that hoists these to device arrays outside the body reintroduces the mesh-context crash. Keep as-is (or thread through in_specs, never closure-capture device arrays).

item: solve_zeta 1511-1521 and z_q scan 800-806: Python q-batch DUS+donation loop / scan unroll=1
evidence: Both confirmed verbatim. :1514-1518: 'scan(unroll=8) was attempted but OOMs — XLA pipelines adjacent unrolled iterations (18.9 GB); scan without unroll triggers SPMD replication of the sharded accumulator (88 GB OOM); fori_loop has the same WhileOp issue. The Python loop is the only approach that gives constant DUS offsets AND sequential memory reuse' — implemented at :1519-1521 with donate_argnums=(2,) (:1425-1431) so output N aliases input N+1. :800-803: 'DO NOT unroll — FFT-box and psi_G_bc aliasing depends on per-iter sequential lifetime; unroll=1 keeps the WhileOp atomic and lets XLA's scan-internal allocator reuse the slot', with per-iter slabs documented as aliased at :736-742. Matches the known JAX SPMD slot-pile-up failure mode; memory-model load-bearing control flow, not cleanup fodder.

item: fit_zeta_to_h5 2638-2645: LORRAX_MAX_RCHUNKS early break falls through to zeta_q_G write + mark_zeta_done
evidence: Confirmed: the break at :2639-2645 ('for profiling/sweeping') exits the r-chunk loop with gflat_acc containing only chunks [0, N)·chunk_r of n_rtot, then execution falls through to the full G-flat write (:2658-2697) AND mark_zeta_done(output_file) at :2713-2715, whose own comment (:2709-2712) says 'Restart paths key off this flag to decide whether the on-disk ζ is trustable'. A truncated profiling run therefore produces a zeta_q.h5 with partial coefficients flagged zeta_is_done=True; any later restart/reader trusts it silently. Env-var-gated debug path only, but the failure is silent data corruption from the restart path's perspective — should skip the final write or skip mark_zeta_done when the break fires.

item: magic constants: ridge 1e-14·|tr| (:1119), LU_RIDGE=1e-12 defined twice (:1335, :1374), Lustre stripe 16/'4M' (:2335-2338), G-pad sentinel Miller (-nx/2,-ny/2,-nz/2) (:2231, :2661)
evidence: All four confirmed. (a) :1119 ridge 1e-14·|tr(C_q)|·I is the 1×1-mesh dense-Cholesky branch (JAX 0.9 shard_map+scan carry-type failure documented :1110-1113) — intentional, documented. (b) LU_RIDGE=1e-12 defined at :1335 (cusolvermp_lu branch) AND :1374 (legacy branch) — currently equal but two independent local constants in the same function; a one-sided tweak silently forks the transverse conditioning between solver kinds. Hoist to one module-level constant. (c) Stripe defaults :2335-2338 are env-overridable (LORRAX_PHDF5_STRIPE_COUNT/SIZE_FS) — benign. (d) Sentinel Miller pad slot (:2230-2232) is deliberately masked to zero post-accumulate at :2659-2673 because sentinel reads are physical-nonzero — intentional and self-documented, but the sentinel/mask pairing is an invariant a refactor must keep together. Net: the dual LU_RIDGE is the actionable divergence risk.

item: fit_zeta_to_h5 2034-2077: orbit-closure gate runs BEFORE the C_q IBZ slice; transverse loud-fails
evidence: Confirmed at :2034-2043: comment records the exact prior bug — the fallback previously ran after factor_c_q, so charge sliced L_q to IBZ then flipped write_ibz_only=False, leaving L_q at IBZ while Z_q stayed full-BZ → 'B.shape[0]=nq_full != Nq=nq_ibz distributed-potrs crash'. Ordering invariant: write_ibz_only must be FINAL before both (a) the C_q slice at :2124-2127 (slice_q_full_to_ibz before factor_c_q) and (b) the in-kernel Z_q IBZ gather (q_irr_full_idx baked into the jit closure at :1612-1616, applied at :1660-1661) — the two slices MUST agree. Transverse loud-fail (:2063-2073) is also deliberate: the V_q orchestrator assumes IBZ ζ̃_T so no fallback exists; error message points to regenerating orbit-closed centroids or LORRAX_FORCE_FULL_BZ=1. Correctness invariant of the IBZ cascade; do not reorder.

item: 2235-2241 q_irr_frac holds full-BZ vectors when write_ibz_only=False; 1457-1459 reshard-skip via spec equality without mesh comparison
evidence: Both confirmed. (a) :2215-2217 sets q_irr_frac=None in full-BZ mode, then :2235-2241 fills it with wrapped FULL-BZ q-vectors — behavior is correct (downstream sphere builder :2259-2265 and gflat accumulator :2617 just want the per-q-on-disk vectors) but the '_irr_' name lies in full-BZ mode; rename to q_disk_frac on any touch. (b) :1457-1459 skips the donated two-step reshard when Z_q.sharding.spec == z_col_shard.spec, comparing PartitionSpec ONLY — a same-spec array on a different Mesh would incorrectly skip. Currently unreachable-in-practice (single global mesh per run; caches key on id(mesh_xy)), so benign today, but the guard should compare NamedSharding (mesh+spec) to be robust. Low-severity naming/robustness debt, correctly diagnosed.

item: fft_helpers.py:85-100 — AOT-fallback comment says 'Logged so the caller notices' but nothing logs
evidence: Confirmed comment/code mismatch. The except block (fft_helpers.py:85-100) contains only arithmetic and a cache store — no logging call, no print, no warnings.warn; the file's imports are math/typing/jax only (lines 1-8), so no logging facility even exists in the module. Consequence is real: gw_init.py:337-360 sizes the production in-loop band-FFT via query_fft_peak_bytes, and the module's own header (lines 16-19) states that nominal N×data fudge factors 'under-predict badly for mixed-radix boxes ... at small batch sizes' — precisely the regime the silent 3×data/n_dev fallback (lines 89-98) reverts to. A bare `except Exception` swallowing all compile failures (not just the documented no-backend case) makes the planner silently run on the crude estimate with no operator-visible signal. Fix: add a warning at line 88 or narrow the except.

item: fft_helpers.py:84 — compiler option xla_gpu_memory_limit_slop_factor=10000
evidence: The option is applied only to the AOT probe compile (jit_fft.lower(spec).compile(...) at lines 83-84) whose sole purpose is to read compiled.memory_analysis() (line 102) — the executable is never run. The slop factor is a percent multiplier on XLA's device-memory limit used during compilation-time checks/autotuning; 10000 = 100× effectively disables memory-limit-driven compile failure. That is exactly what a memory *probe* needs: the planner must be able to measure shapes at or beyond device HBM in order to reject them (gw_init.py:362-366 halves band_chunk in a loop based on the returned number), so a compile that refuses over-limit shapes would defeat the measurement. Runtime safety is unaffected since nothing is executed. Deliberate, correctly scoped.

item: fft_helpers.py:68-79 vs v_q_tile.py:268-271 — planner measures jittable (custom_partitioning) FFT family while the ψ production kernel uses the shard_map family
evidence: Hypothesis confirmed by in-repo evidence. (1) Production in-loop ψ FFT: load_wfns.iter_psi_rchunk_bandwise → wfn_transforms.to_rchunk (load_wfns.py:211,256,274), whose kernel is plain jnp.fft.ifftn inside a shard_map region (wfn_transforms.py:520 via to_rchunk_inner, wrapped at :579-611) — a single per-rank 3D cuFFT plan. (2) gw_init._fft_inloop_bytes (gw_init.py:343-360) sizes that stage with query_fft_peak_bytes, which compiles make_jittable_local_fftn_3d (fft_helpers.py:76-79) — three sequential per-axis 1D custom_partitioning FFTs (fft_helpers.py:255-260). (3) v_q_tile._aot_fft_model explicitly refuses this substitution for its own model: 'Compiles ... make_sharded_fftn_3d — the SAME primitive the production _make_V_q_tile_kernel uses (NOT make_jittable_local_fftn_3d, which compiles a different HLO with 3 sequential 1D-FFT plans and a different memory profile)' (v_q_tile.py:268-271). So the ψ-stage planner measurement is structurally mismatched to its production kernel by the codebase's own documented standard; cuFFT workspace for 3×1D batched plans vs one 3D mixed-radix plan differs. Aggravating staleness: gw_init.py:341 and load_psi_rchunk.py:4 still cite `get_sharded_wfns_rchunk_slice`, which no longer exists in load_wfns.py (wfn_transforms.py:320 calls it 'the old get_sharded_wfns_rchunk_slice'); load_wfns.py:12-13 also carries a dead import of make_sharded_ifftn_3d (only occurrence in the file). Magnitude is bounded in practice (planner calibrated faithful within ~14% per memory-model reports) but the mismatch can drift with cuFFT/XLA versions and is silent.

item: fft_helpers.py:210-229 — IFFT implemented as conj(fft(conj(x)))/N with manual norm scaling
evidence: Per-element verification, all three norm sub-cases enumerated. Identity: [F x]_k = Σ_n x_n e^{-2πi kn/N}, so conj(F(conj x))_k = Σ_n x_n e^{+2πi kn/N} = N·ifft_backward(x)_k. Code (fft_helpers.py:213-220): raw = conj(fft(conj(x))); norm None/'backward' → raw/N = ifft ✓; 'ortho' → raw/√N = (1/√N)Σ e^{+} = ortho-ifft ✓; 'forward' → raw unscaled = forward-norm ifft (the 1/N lives on the forward transform under 'forward') ✓, and the forward branch (lines 222-228) applies the complementary scalings (backward: raw; ortho: /√N; forward: /N) ✓. So the math is exact in every case. Motive: inside the per-axis custom_partitioning wrapper both directions then lower to the same forward-FFT HLO/cuFFT plan kind, keeping one plan family per axis; the paired dispatch on fft_kind at line 207-208 shows the two directions were deliberately built on a single primitive. Convention-sensitive but correct; only defect is that the conj-trick rationale is uncommented — minor doc gap, not a bug.

item: fft_helpers.py:205 — `del mesh`; mesh param of jittable family unused
evidence: Not a leftover: the del is accompanied by an explanatory comment ('The active mesh is supplied to the partition callback during tracing', line 205) and the mesh genuinely arrives via the _partition(mesh_arg, ...) callback (fft_helpers.py:235-242). Keeping the parameter makes the jittable factory signature identical to the shard_map factory (make_sharded_ifftn_3d/make_sharded_fftn_3d take mesh as arg 1), which is load-bearing: call sites deliberately swap between the two families with no signature change — bse_lanczos.py:175-178 documents replacing 'the older make_jittable_local_ifftn_3d' with make_sharded_ifftn_3d, and make_flat_k_fft (fft_helpers.py:382-396) did the same swap citing a measured ~1 s Lanczos saving. API symmetry is the point, not cruft.

item: fft_helpers.py:305-310, 330-335 — TAB-indented signature lines; norm defaults differ across families; aot kernels mix norm='forward' and 'ortho'
evidence: Tabs confirmed: grep -P '^\t' hits exactly lines 305-310 and 330-335 (signature continuation lines of make_sharded_ifftn_3d/make_sharded_fftn_3d); legal Python (continuation inside parens) but style-divergent; src/common/isdf_fitting.py is tab-indented too (lines 421-439), consistent with copy-paste from the same origin. Norm mixing decomposed: (a) the per-call-site norms are INTENTIONAL and production-faithful — aot kernels zct_lr.py:56-57 / cct_lr.py:52-53 use norm='forward' exactly matching production isdf_fitting.py:421,426,439,811,816,827; sigma_kij.py:121-123 / chi0_tau_step.py:151-152 use 'ortho' matching production w_isdf.py:89-91 and ppm_sigma.py:521-523; htransform.py:388 'backward' is its own Wannier convention; v_q_tile.py:299,646 use the family default None(backward) consistently in both its model and production kernel — no measured/production norm mismatch found in any pair I checked. (b) The residual hazard is the differing FAMILY defaults: flat-k wrappers default norm='ortho' (fft_helpers.py:365,414,427) while the 3D jittable/sharded families default None(backward) (270,290,309,334) — a caller migrating between families and dropping the explicit norm silently changes normalization by (N)^{±1/2}. Unify the defaults (or make norm required) and convert tabs; no live bug today.

item: gvec_fft_box.py:131 — check_rep=False on the shard_map
evidence: Two-part ruling. (1) The enclosing kernel make_fft_box_kernel is confirmed-dead (see dead verdict), so the flag has zero runtime effect today. (2) On its own terms it is the codebase-standard idiom, not a silencer of a real hazard: the body (gvec_fft_box.py:101-122) is pure per-rank gather/transpose with no collectives — cnk_slab band-sharded P(('x','y'),None,...), g_index replicated, output P(None,('x','y'),...) claims replication only on axes derived from the replicated g_index, which shard_map's rep-checker cannot prove through take/transpose, hence the flag. The live siblings use it identically: wfn_transforms.py to_rchunk shard_maps at :584/:601 and gflat_to_rmu's kernel all pass check_rep=False. Delete it together with the dead kernel.

item: gvec_fft_box.py:39,55,106 — sentinel-index==ngkmax + append-zero-slot convention shared by wfn_transforms._box_kernel and wfn_loader.box_index
evidence: The coupling is real but the producer side is single-sourced, which caps the lockstep risk. Producer: build_g_index_for_fft_box writes sentinel=ngkmax (gvec_fft_box.py:55 np.full(..., ngkmax)); wfn_loader.box_index does NOT duplicate it — it imports and delegates (wfn_loader.py:421-428 'from common.gvec_fft_box import build_g_index_for_fft_box'), so its docs describing the sentinel are documentation of the same single implementation, not a third copy. Consumers that must agree with the producer: wfn_transforms._box_kernel (live; wfn_transforms.py:203-210 appends the zero slot so index ngkmax gathers zero, and :218-224 relies on flat_index being in-range BY this construction to justify mode='clip') and gvec_fft_box.make_fft_box_kernel (dead; :104-108 same construction). Each site explicitly cross-references the convention (wfn_transforms.py:186-193 names gvec_fft_box; gvec_fft_box.py:38-39 states the sentinel contract). After the dead kernel is deleted, this is one producer + one consumer with mutual doc pointers — a documented convention, though a refactor touching ngkmax padding must still update both plus the mode='clip' safety argument.

item: kq_mapping.py:77 — sign: float = +1.0 traced into jitted umklapp_phase_box_batched
evidence: Per-element math: arg = 2π·sign·(G_x n_x/N_x + G_y n_y/N_y + G_z n_z/N_z), output e^{i·arg} (kq_mapping.py:97-102); sign=+1 gives e^{+2πi G·r} (naive→wrapped gauge push), sign=-1 its complex conjugate (unwrap) — a unitary conjugate pair, documented in both module header (:26-29) and docstring (:86). Tracing sign (only fft_grid is static, :73) is deliberate and beneficial: the sole caller computes BOTH phases back-to-back — run_sternheimer.py:960-961 `phase_wrap_stack = umklapp_phase_box_batched(G_wrap_int, fft_grid_static, +1.0)` then `... -1.0` — and sharing one compiled executable for both values halves compiles; making sign static would double them for zero benefit. The 'silent inversion' risk is the generic risk of any signed convention, mitigated here by explicit literals at the call site and the +1/-1 semantics stated at three places in the file. No BGW-vs-physical subtlety involved (this is the umklapp gauge phase, unrelated to the p±vNL velocity convention).

item: kq_mapping.py:8-9 — header claims SOS mtxels 'consumed by common.chi_sos' but chi_sos.py never imports kq_mapping
evidence: The docstring is accurate; the first-pass reader misparsed 'consumed by' as 'imports'. Full sentence (kq_mapping.py:7-9): 'the sum-over-states finite-q matrix elements written by the augmented get_dipole_mtxels / consumed by common.chi_sos' — the subject consumed by chi_sos is the MATRIX ELEMENTS (data), not the kq_mapping module. Verified dataflow: get_dipole_mtxels imports kq_mapping (get_dipole_mtxels.py:310, kminq_idx_for_iq + umklapp_G_wrap) and writes the finite-q mtxels; chi_sos.py documents consuming exactly those outputs — line 7: inputs from 'psp.get_dipole_mtxels --with-finite-q (rho_cvkq, v_cvkq, ...)'; line 64: 'kminq_idx_kq : (nk_full, nq) int — output of get_dipole_mtxels --with-finite-q'. run_sternheimer.py:954-957 restates the same pipeline pairing. No import of kq_mapping by chi_sos is expected, so nothing is stale.

item: core.py SysDims.nspinor
evidence: Zero readers: grep nspinor across src/tests/tools/scripts/docs/skills/runs — kernels read meta.nspinor (a Meta namedtuple) built from sys.n_s at kernels/fit_one_rchunk.py:443 (nspinor=sys.n_s), never sys.nspinor; no PRIMITIVE touches it. Production gw_init.py:456 maps meta.nspinor into SysDims.n_s and leaves .nspinor at default 1. Only writer is predict_cli.py:43 (itself a no-op). Caveat: as_dict() serialized it into artifacts JSON (fit_one_rchunk__current__samples.json has "nspinor": 1), and samples_to_fit_input does SysDims(**sys_kw), so deleting the field requires popping the key for old artifacts (mirror the existing sys_kw.pop('n_k') at core.py:372).

evidence: Def chooser.py:109, __init__.py:39 export, docs/architecture/memory-model.md:736,828 prose, and a stale comment gw_config.py:221 — no caller. gw_init.py:471-479 dispatches chunk_chooser_mode by literal branch to choose_chunks_analytic/choose_chunks_heuristic only; no getattr/f-string constructs the name; no tests, tools/, scripts/, pyproject console_scripts, or sandbox skills//runs/ invocation (runs/ hits are HLO-dump file paths only). Deleting it removes the only regeneration path for a live artifact; either keep it or fold cost-fitting into sweep.py's fit mode.

The FUNCTION itself is alive — presets.py:96 and presets.py:404 call it — so only the sweep.py import line is dead, not doe.build_doe_axes.

Not in __init__ exports, not in docs/architecture/memory-model.md (grep matched only build_doe_axes there), no tests/tools/scripts/skills/runs usage, no string dispatch (sweep dispatches only points_* factories).
