item: predict_cli.py --nspinor flag
evidence: predict_cli.py:31,43 feeds SysDims.nspinor, which no kernel primitive or any other code reads (see SysDims.nspinor verdict). Passing --nspinor 2 produces byte-identical predictions to --nspinor 1 for every registered kernel; the spin lever that actually works is --n_s (gw_init.py:456 puts meta.nspinor into n_s). Remove the flag together with the field.

item: __init__.py:77 _PREDICT_CACHE module-global memo
evidence: Documented design: docstring __init__.py:96-98 says kernel+fit are 'memoised by (kernel_name, tag) so repeat calls are pure numpy dot-products'; gw_init.py:490 calls predict_kernel_peak once per chunk-planning pass, so the cache saves repeated JSON reads within one process. Staleness requires editing the artifact JSON mid-process, which the offline sweep→fit→run workflow never does (fits are regenerated between runs, not during). Harmless; keep.

item: core.py:191-198 disable_remat docstring vs slop_factor code
evidence: The CODE is intentional, the DOCSTRING is confirmed stale: commit d0b191f's message states explicitly "xla_disable_hlo_passes can't go through jax compiler_options (it's a repeated proto field); using xla_gpu_memory_limit_slop_factor=10000 instead to push XLA out of the rematerialization regime" — and the same commit's diff shows the docstring promising xla_disable_hlo_passes while adding the slop_factor code (core.py:212-215). Fix the docstring (and arguably rename disable_remat) in the refactor; do NOT change the compiler option back, the old one is known not to work.

item: core.py:250 Fit.gamma has no writer
evidence: Hand-calibrated artifact field by design: core.py:16-18 docstring says each kernel 'may carry a scalar gamma correction = actual_runtime_peak / aot_peak calibrated against one production-scale point'; gw_init.py:421-424/488-495 runs a predict-only path that logs the AOT peak precisely 'used for γ calibration against the runtime nvidia-smi sample taken inside fit_zeta'. Proof it was exercised: artifacts/fit_one_rchunk__prev__fit.json carries "gamma": 0.51 (non-default, no code writes it → artifact JSON was edited by hand), all __current__ fits carry 1.0. Consumers: predict_peak core.py:316, _group_alpha chooser.py:238-243, predict_cli.py:62. Keep the field; a small set-gamma helper would make the loop less fragile but is optional.

item: core.py:206-208 no-double-jit comment
evidence: Hard-won fix, keep verbatim: commit d0b191f item 1 documents the exact symptom — 'donate_argnums on already-jitted kernels — symptom was β[Gbuf]=4.489 (0.5 extra from chi_R_acc counted twice as arg+output with alias=0). Removing the double-jit gives β[Gbuf]=4.000 exactly.' aot_measure honors it (core.py:210 lowers fn directly, no jax.jit wrap). One caveat for the refactor: kernels/fit_one_rchunk.py:488 returns jax.jit(_apply) around the production factory's kernel — a nested-jit layer the comment's protection doesn't cover; worth auditing whether the inner kernel's donation survives there.

item: chooser.py:104-105 bc gate lets non-p-divisible bc >= p through
evidence:
Case enumeration shows the condition reduces EXACTLY to 'skip bc < p': for 1<=bc=p the second clause fails (pass); bc

=1. So the effective gate is bc>=p, matching both the inline comment ('band-sharding requires bc >= p in most kernels') and the analytic chooser's plain 'if bc < p: continue' at chooser.py:357, and it deliberately admits the non-divisible bc=n_b endpoint added at line 98. The divisibility clause is vacuous decoration — and the whole function is dead code (see choose_chunks_aot), so delete rather than clarify.

item: chooser.py:522-524 unreachable band_chunk<=0 fallback
evidence: Confirmed unreachable: _largest_even_divisor_leq (chooser.py:253-278) clamps cap = max(p, int(cap)) at line 264; the divisor branch only accepts cand % p == 0 with cand > 0 (hence cand >= p), and the no-divisor fallback returns (cap//p)*p >= p since cap >= p. So the return is always >= p >= 1 and 'if band_chunk <= 0' at 521-524 can never fire (same holds for the chunk_r<=0 raise at 563-567). Defensive leftover; safe to delete in the refactor.

item: chooser.py:349-354,555 magic constants (0.8 slack, wfn_frac=0.20, pair_temp_count=4, fft_multiplier=3, 16-byte literals, /p_x vs /P)
evidence: Each traces to a measured or structural fact: pair_temp_count=4 = zct_lr fit β[PrBr]=4 (commit d0b191f fit table; fit_one_rchunk.py:26-27 'ZCT stage — 4 concurrent pair-density-sized temps'); fft_workspace_multiplier=3 = load_psi_rchunk_fft β[psi_G]=3 (chooser.py:446-449 comment); 16 = sizeof(complex128); base_centroid/p_x matches the centroid sharding P(None,None,'x',None) (_T_centroid fit_one_rchunk.py:222-232 divides by mesh.p_x; specs line 377) while base_psiG and base_Lq /P match _T_psiG_cache and _T_Lq_sharded (fit_one_rchunk.py:193-200, 235-241, both /(p_x*p_y)); the 0.8 allgather slack encodes the documented '20% slack for concurrent live arrays during the gather' (chooser.py:352-353). Only wart: the 0.8*budget/(16*q_gather_min*mu) formula is duplicated verbatim at 354 and 555 — hoist to one helper when the dead grid chooser is removed (after which only one copy survives anyway).

item: chooser.py:542 cr_per_byte includes n_s**2
evidence: Verified correct against production, per-element: common/isdf_fitting.py:190 declares the pair carrier 'P_k_ab: (nk, ns, ns, n_rmu, n_col)', :250-253 P_v_k/P_c_k '(nk, ns, ns, n_rmu, n_rmu)', :668 the scan carries 'P_l_acc: (nk, ns, r_loc, mu_loc, ns)' — two open spin axes for ALL channels (charge gamma^0=I included), so each (mu, cr) element holds ns^2 complex128 entries → 16*n_k*ns^2*mu*cr/P per temp, x4 temps. Charge-only non-spinor (n_s=1) reduces to the pre-bispinor formula, so no regression. Adversarial finding in the OTHER direction: the regressed model's _T_pair primitive (fit_one_rchunk.py:184-190) has NO n_s factor, so the AOT fit — not the heuristic — is the one that under-scales pair temps for bispinor (n_s=2); the default heuristic path is right.

item: presets.py preset arg ignored in 9 of 11 factories
evidence: Uniform signature required by string dispatch: sweep.py:115-119 resolves getattr(presets, f'points_{args.kernel}') and always calls points_fn(args.preset). Confirmed count: only points_chi0_tau_step (presets.py:32-76) and points_fit_one_rchunk (presets.py:292-388) branch on preset (and raise ValueError on unknown names); the other 9 factories take it and ignore it. Minor wart: passing a bogus --preset to those 9 silently succeeds; acceptable for an offline calibration tool.

item: presets.py:174,225 SysDims.kgrid repurposed as FFT box
evidence: Deliberate, documented back-compat awaiting migration: presets.py:222-229 says outright 'we repurpose SysDims.kgrid as the real-space FFT grid... sys.n_k derived from kgrid=(24,24,24) → 13824... We override via k_chunk knob instead', and core.py:66-68 codifies the fallback ('back-compat with kernels that repurpose kgrid as the FFT box'). Since the fft_grid field now exists and the fit_one_rchunk presets already use it properly (presets.py:305-307,373-375), migrating vq_mu_chunk (:174, kgrid=(48,48,48)) and load_psi_rchunk_fft/reshard (:225,:259) to fft_grid + explicit kgrid, then deleting the fft_shape fallback, is the clean-up — note it changes the meaning of saved sample JSONs for those kernels, so re-tag or migrate artifacts.

item: presets.py:296-299 comment on non-integer beta dumps (1.65/5.02)
evidence: Accurate historical record, not cruft: the mos2_ortho preset it motivates IS the current fit's DoE (artifacts fit_one_rchunk__ortho__* exist; __current__ samples match its baseline: kgrid=(3,3,1), n_rmu=320, n_b=40, fft_grid=(80,72,8) per artifacts/fit_one_rchunk__current__samples.json), and the comment enumerates per-primitive identifiability axes (presets.py:298-304) that document WHY each DoE point exists. Matches the known planner-refit/collinearity history (cf. sigma_kij comment presets.py:194-199 and fit_one_rchunk.py:177-181 on Lq collinearity). Keep.

item: cost.py:97-99 rows with flops None silently skipped
evidence: Deliberate tolerance for older sample files, with a hard backstop: if ALL rows lack flops, fit_cost_nnls raises ValueError('No samples with flops data... Re-run the sweep with a version that captures cost_analysis') at cost.py:106-109, and the underdetermined check at :112-115 catches near-empty survivors. flops=None originates in aot_measure (core.py:233) when compiled.cost_analysis() isn't a dict with 'flops'. Silent PARTIAL skip could quietly shrink n_samples on a mixed-vintage file, but n_samples is recorded in the CostFit artifact (cost.py:129) so it's auditable. Acceptable; a one-line skipped-row count print would be a nicety.

item: sweep.py:134-147 _init_jax_for_sweep bare try/except pass around jax.distributed.initialize
evidence: Offline calibration convenience with loud downstream failure: if init raises under real multi-rank SLURM, each rank sees only local GPUs and _make_mesh raises RuntimeError('Need N devices... have n') (sweep.py:33-35) for meshes bigger than one node's GPUs; for meshes that DO fit locally, aot_measure results are deterministic compiler estimates (identical on every rank), and the worst outcome is multiple ranks each passing the _is_rank0 fallback (sweep.py:41-47 returns True on exception) and writing identical samples JSON to the same path. No wrong numbers are produced; a warning print inside the except would be an improvement but this never touches production gw_jax (which does its own distributed init).

item: predict_cli.py:41-45 cannot set fft_grid or n_b_sum
evidence: Confirmed gap: gw_init.py:452-460 builds production SysDims with kgrid=meta.kgrid, fft_grid=meta.fft_grid, n_b_sum=nb_L+nb_R, n_r=meta.n_rtot; predict_cli exposes neither --fft_grid nor --n_b_sum. Partial mitigation exists — --n_r (predict_cli.py:35) short-circuits the fft_shape fallback in every primitive that matters ('sys.n_r or fft_shape product', e.g. fit_one_rchunk.py:198,208) — so the irreproducible piece is n_b_sum: SysDims.nb_sum defaults to 2*n_b (core.py:86-88), which is wrong for asymmetric L/R band windows (pseudobands/sub-valence), inflating/deflating _T_centroid (fit_one_rchunk.py:231) and _F_pair_density (:292). n_b_sum was added in the most recent core.py commit ac539ce ('asymmetric L/R band ranges via n_b_sum') without a CLI update. Add --fft_grid and --n_b_sum flags; two-line fix.

item: src/common/chi_sos.py — entire module (5 __all__ functions)
evidence: Zero imports in src/, tests/, tools/, scripts/; no star-imports, no getattr/string dispatch, no pyproject console script, no run.sh `python -m` invocation. BUT one live from-import exists outside the repo: /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/runs/MoS2/02_mos2_3x3_nosym/00_lorrax_cohsex/test_sos_consistency.py:16 `from common.chi_sos import compute_S_tensor_sos` (a one-off consistency-check script in an archived run dir). The other 4 functions (deltaE_cv_at_q, compute_chi_head_at_q, compute_chi_wing_at_q, compute_w_tensor_sos) have zero references anywhere including sandbox skills/ and runs/. Docstring-only mentions at src/common/kq_mapping.py:9 and src/psp/run_sternheimer.py:956. Caveat for deletion: chi_sos is the documented consumer of `psp.get_dipole_mtxels --with-finite-q` output (chi_sos.py:6-8), so deleting it orphans that CLI flag; the runs/ scripts named *chi_sos* elsewhere only read npz variables, not the module.

item: src/common/chi_sos.py:96-100,134 — prefactor 2.0 vs chi_from_dipole 4.0
evidence: NOT a mismatch — the first-pass reader compared bare prefactors without folding in F_t's internal factor 2. Per-transition algebra: chi_sos S-tensor (chi_sos.py:266,236-241) = pref·(f_v−f_c)·conj(d^a)d^b·F with pref=2/(V·Nk·s), F=2Δ/(ω²−Δ²), d=v/Δ (line 272) ⇒ coefficient = 2·2Δ/(ω²−Δ²)·|v|²/Δ² = 4·|v|²/[Δ(ω²−Δ²)·V·Nk·s]. compute_S_omega (chi_from_dipole.py:128-131,153) = pref·(f_v−f_c)/denom·|v|² with pref=4/(V·Nk·s), denom=Δ·(ω²−Δ²) ⇒ identical coefficient 4·|v|²/[Δ(ω²−Δ²)·V·Nk·s]. The 2×2=4×1 factorization differs; the physics is equal. The docstring at chi_sos.py:96-100 explicitly flags the ambiguity and the driver resolved it correctly.

item: src/common/chi_sos.py:108 vs 271 — cutoffs 1e-16 vs 1e-12
evidence: The two guards protect different quantities with different units: 1e-16 guards denom=ω²−Δ² (Ha², lines 108,167,235,301); 1e-12 guards Δ itself (Ha, lines 271,338) before forming d=v/Δ. Both only fire for degenerate/zero-gap transitions where f_v−f_c=0 zeroes the term anyway, so no numerical consequence for insulators. Real cruft found: the docstring at chi_sos.py:264 claims compute_S_omega 'sets the term to 0 when |Δ| < 1e-12' — compute_S_omega actually guards the full denom at 1e-16 (chi_from_dipole.py:129); stale claim. Module is effectively dead (see above), lowering the stakes further.

item: src/common/chi_from_dipole.py:149-151 — nelec inferred from f_nk[0] with >0.5 threshold
evidence: Both production callers build f_nk as uniform k-independent 0/1 from wfn.nelec (src/gw/head_correction.py:160-164 `occ[:, :min(nelec, nb)] = 1.0`; scripts/checks/sigma_direct_check.py:186-189 same), so the k=0 slice is exact today. Latent hazard confirmed by index enumeration: c_idx/v_idx (lines 117-118) are k-independent band-index blocks; for a metal where band n is occupied at k1 but empty at k0, n lands in the c-block, and true transitions n→m (both in c-block at k1) are never enumerated — the fv−fc factor (line 124) only zeroes pairs that ARE enumerated, it cannot recover un-enumerated ones. Silent physics loss for metals/smearing; today correct by construction. Fix: assert integer uniform occupations, or enumerate all band pairs and let fv−fc select.

item: src/common/chi_from_dipole.py:13-88 — 75-line SC-GW WARNING/TODO block
evidence: Deliberate roadmap, and its factual claims check out: HeadResolver really memoizes head samples in _cache built once at top of main() (src/gw/head_correction.py:202-227, __slots__ includes '_cache'), so SC iterations reuse iteration-1-basis S(ω) as the block says; sc_iteration._rotate_to_dft_basis exists at src/gw/sc_iteration.py:242 doing the U·O·U† direction the block contrasts against (block line 37-40 correctly notes the dipole rotation needs the opposite dagger placement, einsum 'kpm,akpq,kqn->akmn'). Block line 84 states the file is 'intentionally NOT modified'. This is accurate documentation of a real SC-GW systematic (O(QP-shift/gap) head error); the refactor should assign an owner (SCInputs carry of the GW-level dipole per lines 79-82) rather than delete it.

item: src/common/chi_from_dipole.py:1-10 — __future__ import precedes docstring
evidence: Confirmed: line 1 is `from __future__ import annotations`, the triple-quoted string at lines 3-10 is therefore an ordinary expression statement, module __doc__ is None. Python permits the docstring BEFORE a __future__ import, so the correct order is simply swapped. No runtime effect beyond help()/introspection; trivial one-line fix.

item: src/common/gamma_matrices.py — gamma5
evidence: Repo-wide grep: only the definition (gamma_matrices.py:40-43) and __all__ (line 318). No star-imports of gamma_matrices anywhere (grep 'import *' + gamma: empty), no getattr/globals() dispatch in the module, tests import only gamma0..3/gamma_perm_phase/gamma_apply/gamma_double_contract (tests/test_sigma_x_bispinor.py:23,40,57; tests/test_zq_from_psi_sm_bit_identity.py:36), nothing in tools/, scripts/, sandbox skills/ or runs/. Bonus trap if ever revived: gamma5=[[0,I],[I,0]] is the TRUE Dirac γ⁵, breaking the module's γ̃^μ=γ⁰γ^μ tilde convention (ψ†γ⁵ψ ≠ ψ̄γ⁵ψ); its line-39 comment formula is written in un-tilded gammas.

item: src/common/gamma_matrices.py:45-48,79 — _to_sparse / gammas_sparse
evidence: No code reads the (rows, cols, vals) triples: grep 'gammas_sparse' hits only the definition (gamma_matrices.py:79) and the comment at src/common/isdf_fitting.py:2026-2032. gammas_sparse is NOT in __all__ (lines 316-321). The isdf_fitting warm import (`from . import gamma_matrices as _gm  # noqa: F401`, line 2032) exists solely to force line 79's module-level jnp.nonzero to execute outside a jit trace (documented ConcretizationTypeError otherwise). So the construction is a live import-time HAZARD, not a live value: deleting gammas_sparse and _to_sparse is safe (nothing consumes them) and also removes the reason the warm-import workaround exists — delete both together and simplify/annotate the isdf_fitting.py:2026-2032 block.

item: src/common/gamma_matrices.py:17 — gamma0..3 are γ⁰γ^μ, not standard Dirac gammas
evidence: Verified per element against standard Dirac rep (γ⁰=diag(I,−I), γ^i=[[0,σᵢ],[−σᵢ,0]]): γ⁰γ¹=[[0,σx],[σx,0]] matches gamma1 (lines 24-27); γ⁰γ²=[[0,σy],[σy,0]] matches gamma2 rows [0,0,0,−i]/[0,0,i,0]/[0,−i,0,0]/[i,0,0,0] (lines 29-32); γ⁰γ³=[[0,σz],[σz,0]] matches gamma3 (lines 34-37); γ⁰γ⁰=I matches gamma0. These are the Dirac α-matrices, so ψ†γ̃^μψ = ψ̄γ^μψ — exactly the psidag rationale in the line-17 comment, and consistent with downstream _to_perm_phase (all four are monomial). Two documentation traps to fix in the refactor: the module docstring (lines 5-9) still says 'standard Dirac representation' (false for the exports), and gamma5 (line 40) does NOT follow the tilde convention (it is plain γ⁵, not γ⁰γ⁵=[[0,I],[−I,0]]).

item: src/common/gamma_matrices.py:296-313 — _GAMMA_CONTRACT_MODE global defined after its readers
evidence: Define-after-use is legal Python: the line-299 assignment executes at import time, gamma_double_contract (lines 286-293) resolves the name at call time. It is set exactly once at config-build time by src/gw/gw_init.py:542-547 (`set_gamma_contract_mode(cfg.backend.gamma_contract_mode)`) before kernel tracing, with an explicit rationale comment there: 'the γ̃ contract sits inside shard_map bodies so threading a kwarg through every call would be churn'. The mutate-after-trace footgun is documented in the setter docstring (lines 305-306) and the setter validates values (lines 310-312). Matches the project's minimal-signatures preference; leave as is.

item: src/common/gamma_matrices.py:172-178,201-203 — einsum/scan silently fall back to take when spin_axes != (1,2)
evidence: Results are correct in all modes (take path is generic over axes; the three impls are mathematically identical), but the fallback makes the cohsex.in gamma_contract_mode knob silently inert on the DOMINANT kernels: of the three production call sites, two use spin_axes=(3,6) on the rank-7 flat-k FFT layout — isdf_fitting.py:430 (C_R, chi-metric path) and isdf_fitting.py:818 (Z_R, the per-r-chunk ζ-fit peak-memory kernel) — and only the small q=0 Gram at isdf_fitting.py:284 uses (1,2). The comment at gamma_matrices.py:174-176 ('the one rare path that uses spin_axes ≠ (1,2)') is stale post-flat-k-refactor: the 'rare' path is now the main pipeline. Consequence: setting gamma_contract_mode=scan/einsum to reduce rank-5/rank-7 slot pile-up does nothing where it matters most, with no warning. Refactor should either generalize einsum/scan to arbitrary spin_axes or emit a loud once-per-run notice on fallback.

item: src/common/gamma_matrices.py:2-9 — import jax precedes module docstring
evidence: Confirmed: lines 2-3 import jax/jnp, the triple-quoted string at lines 5-9 is an expression statement, __doc__ is None. Cosmetic; same one-line reorder fix as chi_from_dipole. (The docstring's content is also wrong — see the γ⁰γ^μ convention item — so fix both together.)

item: src/common/bispinor_init.py — get_small_psi_component
evidence: Only live references: tests/test_wfn_loader_eager.py:170 (import) and :194 (call), where it serves as the legacy reference implementation validating wfn_loader's vectorized small-component kernel — wfn_loader.py:926 and :1030 docstrings promise byte-for-byte agreement with it. No production import, no tools/scripts/skills/runs usage, no string dispatch. Not deletable for free: removing it orphans the bit-identity test; the refactor should either keep it as the test oracle or inline an independent reference into the test.

item: src/common/bispinor_init.py:30 + src/file_io/wfn_loader.py:1035 — duplicated magic constant 0.00364867628215
evidence: Both hardcode the same 12-digit literal: bispinor_init.py:30 `halfalpha = jnp.complex128(0.00364867628215)` and wfn_loader.py:1035 `_HALFALPHA = 0.00364867628215` (used at :1051). Value = α/2 with 1/α ≈ 137.0359992, agreeing with CODATA α/2 = 0.0036486762661 to ~7e-9 relative — physically fine but uncited. The duplicates are verbatim-identical today, which the eager-loader bit-identity test implicitly depends on (wfn_loader.py:926 'byte-for-byte'); any future edit to one copy silently breaks that. Hoist to a single shared constant (e.g. in bispinor_init or a constants module) and import it in wfn_loader.

item: src/common/bispinor_init.py:27-28 — TODO: σ·v with v = p + [r, V_NL + Σ], DKH4
evidence: The current lift (bispinor_init.py:30-41) is textbook kinetic balance: ψ_S = (α/2)(σ·(k+G))ψ_L with bare momentum only, and the docstring honestly labels the V_NL/Σ commutator and DKH4 pieces as 'possible improvements'. This is consistent with the project's bispinor roadmap state (bare DHF+Breit exchange only; screened-W and higher-order relativistic corrections unbuilt) and with the known p+vNL physical-velocity convention — a documented, known physics approximation, not a bug. No action needed beyond keeping the note when the shared constant is hoisted.

item: src/file_io/slab_io.py:311-374 free functions write_slab/read_slab/accumulate_slab
evidence: Enumerated all mechanisms: (1) every import of slab_io across src/tests/tools/scripts pulls only SlabIO (gw_init.py:919, v_q_bispinor.py:319/528/775, isdf_fitting.py:2291, tagged_arrays.py:46/108, zeta_loader.py:65, kin_ion.py:14, sigma_output.py:282, zeta_reader.py:58, tests/test_slab_io_ffi_contract.py:9); (2) no 'import file_io.slab_io as' module-attribute calls anywhere; (3) file_io/__init__.py does not re-export them; (4) no star-imports exist in src/tests/tools/scripts so __all__ is inert; (5) the op="write_slab" strings in tests/_slab_io_ffi are error-message labels, not dispatch; (6) pyproject console_scripts only expose gw.gw_jax:main and centroid.kmeans_cli:main; (7) lorrax_sandbox skills/ and runs/ hits are only tracebacks through the SlabIO.write_slab METHOD (slab_io.py line 197 frames). Only remaining references are the design doc docs/dev/plans/unified_slab_io.md:73 (aspirational API spec, not code).

item: src/file_io/slab_io.py:290 SlabIO.accumulate_slab method
evidence: grep '\.accumulate_slab(' over the whole repo finds only slab_io.py:305 (the method's own delegation) and slab_io.py:374 (the dead free function). The docstring's claimed consumer (slab_io.py:299 'Used by the Σ_c(ω) stream-mode accumulator in ppm_sigma') is false today: ppm_sigma.py:1633 _accumulate_kij_stream does direct h5py dset RMW, and ppm_sigma.py:1606-1609 explicitly says 'The accumulator pattern itself is unchanged from pre-SlabIO (rank-0 h5py)'. docs/dev/plans/unified_slab_io.md:238 ('_accumulate_kij_stream rewritten on top of accumulate_slab') is an unexecuted plan item. No string/getattr dispatch, no test coverage.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_ffi.py:708 _FfiBackend.accumulate_slab
evidence: Only reachable through SlabIO.accumulate_slab (slab_io.py:305), itself dead. No direct _FfiBackend instantiation outside slab_io.py (grep finds only two docstring mentions: v_q_g_flat.py:239 and aot_memory_model/kernels/slab_write.py:4, both referencing read_slab/write_slab, not accumulate). Not exercised by tests/test_slab_io_ffi_contract.py (covers _normalize_* helpers + one allgather write/read roundtrip only).

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_mpi_host.py:380 _MpiHostBackend.accumulate_slab
evidence: Only caller path is the facade SlabIO.accumulate_slab (slab_io.py:305), which has zero live callers (see above). No direct _MpiHostBackend construction outside slab_io.py:161. Zero references to _slab_io_mpi_host or PHDF5_HOST anywhere in tests/.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_allgather.py:273 _AllgatherBackend.accumulate_slab
evidence: Same dead facade chain: slab_io.py:305 is its only caller and grep '\.accumulate_slab(' across src/tests/tools/scripts/skills/runs finds no external site. Its docstring at _slab_io_allgather.py:282 ('Used by the ppm_sigma Σ_c(ω) stream mode') is falsified by ppm_sigma.py:1633's in-function h5py accumulator. The whole three-backend accumulate_slab surface is planned-future code from docs/dev/plans/unified_slab_io.md:121,189,238 that was shipped ahead of its consumer.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_mpi_host.py whole-file test coverage gap
evidence: The module is production-reachable, not dead: slab_io.py:156-161 instantiates _MpiHostBackend for SlabIOBackend.PHDF5_HOST, and the gw_config auto-router selects PHDF5_HOST when use_ffi_io=true on the CPU backend with mpi4py+h5py-parallel present (gw_config.py:1006-1009; module docstring _slab_io_mpi_host.py:35-37). The coverage claim is however verified: grep '_slab_io_mpi_host|_MpiHostBackend|PHDF5_HOST' in tests/ returns nothing; test_slab_io_ffi_contract.py exercises only _slab_io_ffi._normalize_* helpers and the allgather backend. Verdict alive (production path, zero direct tests).

item: src/file_io/slab_io.py:72,133 in-function import of SlabIOBackend from gw.gw_config
evidence: Confirmed at slab_io.py:72 and :133 ('avoid circular import at module load'). Layering inversion is real: the file_io layer's own backend selector enum lives in gw.gw_config:83 (SlabIOBackend). Cycle mechanics: 'from gw.gw_config import ...' executes gw/__init__.py, which imports gw_init + gw_config at package load; today neither has a module-level file_io import (gw_config.py:845 is function-level), so the deferred import is defensive rather than strictly required — but file_io/__init__ → zeta_loader.py:65 → slab_io means any future module-level file_io import inside the gw package would close the cycle instantly. Moving the enum into file_io (gw re-exporting for config parsing) removes both the inversion and the fragility.

item: src/file_io/slab_io.py:148-149 derived bool self.use_ffi_io alongside backend enum
evidence: Confirmed: use_ffi_io = backend in (PHDF5_FFI, PHDF5_HOST) at slab_io.py:148-149, consumed once at slab_io.py:275 to pick the read_slab kwarg set (FFI/HOST branch omits as_numpy and defaults mesh to self.mesh; allgather branch passes as_numpy). No external readers (grep '\.use_ffi_io' outside file_io/ is empty), and the comment at 143-147 explicitly disclaims source-of-truth status, so it is not a live two-sources bug. But the name lies (True for PHDF5_HOST, which is not FFI), it shadows the constructor kwarg of the same name with a different meaning, and the single use site could test the enum directly. Trivial deletion in the refactor.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_ffi.py:525-538,688 read_slab skips _drain_pending when ds_id cached
evidence: Confirmed latent read-after-write hazard by case enumeration of _ds_id (lines 525-538): (a) name NOT in _ds_ids → _drain_pending() at :531 before opening readonly → safe; (b) name IN _ds_ids (registered by a prior write_slab at :576-581 or create_dataset at :441) → returns cached id with NO drain. write_slab enqueues async tasks on the worker thread (:617-623) while read_slab executes its shard_map synchronously on the main thread (:703-704), so in case (b) a read can run while writes to the same dataset are still queued → stale/partial data. accumulate_slab (:722-727) inherits it: two back-to-back accumulates on one region lose the first update. Currently masked exactly as hypothesized: v_q_bispinor writes via per-tile open/close SlabIO handles (:428-429, :663-664, :719-720) and reads via a separate mode='r' handle (:779), and the code itself acknowledges the ordering hazard class at v_q_bispinor.py:351-355 ('defense-in-depth... create_dataset now drains pending writes'). No live caller trips it today (accumulate_slab is dead), but the facade contract permits it. Fix: drain in _ds_id unconditionally, or at least on the cached-hit path before reads.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_ffi.py:280,304 lru_cache(maxsize=None) on compiled shard_map factories
evidence: Confirmed. _get_read_sm (:280-301) and _get_write_sm (:304+) are module-level lru_cache(maxsize=None) keyed on (mesh, spec, ds_id, ctx_handle, mesh_shape, axis layout, out_struct). ctx_handle/ds_id are per-file-open ints, so after a file closes its entries can never hit again yet retain compiled jitted shard_maps forever — unbounded growth over long multi-file runs (v_q_bispinor opens a fresh SlabIO per tile). The design is deliberate and documented (:264-278: identity-stable closures for JAX trace-cache hits; coalescing needs FFI attrs to become runtime args on the C++ side, declared out of scope), and v_q_g_flat.py:238-243 shows the closure-identity problem it solves is real. Acknowledged limitation, not a bug; an eviction hook on backend close would bound it.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_ffi.py:451-455 create_dataset silently no-ops chunks/attrs
evidence: Confirmed at :442-455: chunks/attrs are dropped with a warnings.warn (not fully silent), with a documented reason (no collective set-chunks ctypes entry yet; FAPL alignment set at ctx init; 'pre-create with h5py if you need explicit chunking'). Divergence is real: _slab_io_mpi_host.py:214-222 honors both, _slab_io_allgather.py:116-122 honors both. Live consequence: sigma_output.py:314-315,319-320 always passes chunks=, so every FFI-backend sigma write emits the warning and produces contiguous layout — a documented perf/format divergence behind one facade, not a correctness bug. Worth closing in the refactor but clearly intentional v1 scoping.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_ffi.py:399-408 dispatch queue maxsize=2 with measurement table
evidence: Confirmed at :388-408. The bound exists because each queued _task closure pins its input jax.Array on GPU until the write completes (:389-395); the comment derives in-flight bound (K queued + 1 processing + 1 main-thread view) and cites measurements (Si 4x4x4/2400c/mem16: K=0 unbounded OOM 12.91→22.48+GB; K=2 flat 16.47GB/97s; K=4 18.50GB/92s — K=2 matches K=4 throughput since the writer saturates). Empirically tuned, correctness-neutral, thoroughly documented at the point of use. The only critique in the suspect ('undocumented outside comment') is cosmetic.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_ffi.py:123-135 _replicated_i64_vector helper
evidence: Confirmed at :123-135: explicitly replicates tiny int64 control buffers (offset, valid_shape) via device_put with NamedSharding(mesh, P()) because an implicitly-placed offset buffer once arrived in C++ with permuted dims in the real CrI3 driver. Used at :614-615 (write path) and :701-702 (read path). Since offsets are RUNTIME args to the cached shard_maps (:600-601), deterministic replicated placement is both the intended semantics and the stable JIT cache key. Real past bug correctly fossilized as a helper; keep.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_ffi.py:464-465 duplicated '# ---' separator comment
evidence: Confirmed: lines 464-465 are two consecutive identical '# ------' separator lines between write_attr and _dispatch_loop. Pure copy-paste residue; delete one line.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_ffi.py:511-514 _introspect_cache created lazily via getattr
evidence: Confirmed at :511-514: cache dict is conjured via getattr(self, '_introspect_cache', None) inside _introspect_dataset instead of __init__ (where _ds_ids, _dispatch_queue etc. are initialized, :408-417). Today read_slab is the only caller (:649) and runs exclusively on the main thread (the worker thread only executes write _task closures), so no live race; the check-then-set is a foot-gun only if introspection is ever moved onto the worker. Two-line move to __init__; cosmetic inconsistency, not a bug.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_mpi_host.py:388-391 accumulate_slab docstring claims 'Drains before/after'
evidence: Confirmed stale copy-paste: docstring at :390-391 says 'Drains before/after to keep the read + write rounds non-interleaved', but this backend has no queue, no worker thread, and no drain — the module docstring (:7-19) explicitly explains writes are synchronous on the main thread (Cray MPICH MPI_THREAD_SINGLE deadlock rationale). The synchronous design makes the claimed property vacuously true, so it misleads rather than breaks. It also sits on a confirmed-dead method; fix or delete alongside the accumulate_slab decision.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_mpi_host.py:337-359 read_slab full-global proto + discarded shape-carrier + third-way keep_shape
evidence: Confirmed, all three counts: (1) :337-340 builds proto = device_put(jnp.zeros(read_shape), sharding) — on the CPU backend jnp.zeros materializes the FULL global array per process before resharding, i.e. O(global) memory to answer 'what slab does this rank own?'; (2) :350-353 passes np.zeros(local_shape) into _clip_shard_to_valid purely as a shape carrier — the returned clipped array is discarded (`_, ds_offset = clipped` at :356), only ds_offset survives; (3) :357-359 re-derives the clip extent as keep_shape = min(ls, ve−so). Index math check: _clip_shard_to_valid gives keep = min(shard_off+local, valid_end) − shard_off = min(local, valid_end−shard_off) per axis — identical to keep_shape, so the three derivations are mutually consistent (no bug) but redundant. Pure index arithmetic on shard_offset/local_shape/vshape (NamedSharding shard-shape computation, no proto) plus one host_local buffer replaces all of it.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_mpi_host.py:207-209 create_dataset idempotent-return vs allgather delete+recreate
evidence: Confirmed divergence behind one facade call: _slab_io_mpi_host.py:207-209 returns silently if name exists ('idempotent on a mode: respect the existing dataset'), while _slab_io_allgather.py:113-115 does `del self._file[name]` then recreates; the FFI path uses phdf5_ensure_dataset (:437-440, open-or-create, i.e. mpi_host-like). Failure scenario requiring a caller: recreating a same-named dataset with different shape/dtype in a reused ('a'-mode) file — allgather gives a fresh correctly-shaped dataset, mpi_host keeps the stale one and the subsequent hyperslab write either fails bounds checks or leaves a stale tail. No current caller does this (writers open mode='w' or write disjoint names), so it is a divergence to unify, not an active bug. Note also that HDF5 delete does not reclaim space in the allgather variant — pick one semantic (probably raise-on-shape-mismatch ensure) for all three.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_allgather.py:188-204 k_chunk_size hard-coded axis-1 streaming
evidence: Confirmed and index-checked: for i≠1 dst slice(off[i], off[i]+vshape[i]) ↔ src slice(0, vshape[i]); for i==1 dst slice(off[1]+k0, off[1]+k1) ↔ src slice(k0, k1) — element-for-element identical to the unchunked path (:186-187,206), so the chunked write is correct for ANY layout, and when vshape[1] ≤ k_chunk_size the plain write is the intended fast path, not a silent fallback. Axis 1 is documented API (slab_io.py:233-236: 'streams the rank-0 write along axis 1... matches the legacy sigma_output k_chunk pattern'). Sole caller is sigma_output.py:316-336: for (n_omega,nk,nb,nb) axis 1 = k as designed; for the 3-D (nk,nb,nb) datasets (:327-329,:334-336) axis 1 is nb, so the 'k' chunking actually streams over bands — still a correct write, still memory-bounded, just misnamed. Also note the chunking only bounds the h5py write; the _to_host gather at :175 already materialized the full array. Legacy-compatible convention with a naming wart.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_allgather.py:42-75 _to_host Path-(B)/(D) process_allgather dispatch
evidence: Confirmed at :42-75. Dispatch enumerates four cases on stable metadata: numpy passthrough (:68-69), single-process device_get (:70-71), multi-process fully-replicated → addressable_data(0) to dodge both the identity-jit reshard and process_allgather(tiled=True)'s Path-(D) (world*N0, *rest) stacking for fully-addressable inputs (:72-73), and multi-process sharded → Path-(B) allgather returning exactly A.shape (:74-75). The logic is JAX-internals-coupled (behavior of _handle_array_process_allgather) and cites an external design review (:65-66), but it is the single source of truth for the gather (used by write_slab :175 and accumulate_slab :285) — exactly where fragile version-coupled logic should live. Keep single-sourced; add a pinned-behavior test if JAX is upgraded.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_allgather.py:114-115 create_dataset delete-then-recreate
evidence: Confirmed at :113-115 — the mirror image of the mpi_host divergence above (same facade, opposite overwrite semantics; FFI's phdf5_ensure_dataset sides with mpi_host). Additionally the `del` on 'w'-mode files is unreachable dead-weight (fresh file, name can't exist) and on 'a'-mode files leaks HDF5 space (delete doesn't reclaim). Unify the three backends on one documented create_dataset overwrite semantic during the refactor; counted as one divergence with two sides.

item: src/file_io/_slab_io_allgather.py:35-39 _barrier swallows all exceptions
evidence: Confirmed: bare `except Exception: pass` around multihost_utils.sync_global_devices at :36-39, duplicated verbatim in _slab_io_mpi_host.py:62-69 (there with an explanatory docstring: fallback when the distributed runtime isn't initialised — single-process/test mode). The intent is legitimate, but the net is too wide: a REAL multi-process sync failure (timeout, dead peer) is swallowed, after which rank 0 proceeds to open/write/close the file unsynchronized with other ranks — e.g. a non-rank-0 process could reopen the file for read before rank 0's h5py handle is closed. Barriers gate every op (:99,123,143,207,295,305), so the blast radius of a masked failure is the whole backend. Guard on jax.process_count() > 1 (matching _to_host's own dispatch at :70) and let multi-process exceptions propagate; also dedupe the two copies.

item: src/centroid/orbit_syms.py: orbit_images
evidence: Called at orbit_syms.py:120 by canonicalize_orbit, which is live in the production kmeans path: kmeans_isdf._canonicalize_rep (kmeans_isdf.py:721-722) is invoked at kmeans_isdf.py:434 (vmap'd rep canonicalisation in Lloyd update), :758 and :770 (kmeans++ init); kmeans_cli drives kmeans_isdf. Also referenced in docs/theory/symmetry.md:61,573. Grep of src/, tests/, tools/, the sandbox skills/ and runs/ trees, plus string-dispatch patterns (getattr, import *) found no other call mechanism — but the internal production-path call chain alone refutes 'dead'. At most a rename-to-private candidate; the first-pass 'zero external callers' framing conflated 'not exported' with 'dead'.

item: src/centroid/orbit_syms.py:312-326 snap-to-int before floor in compute_centroid_sym_perm
evidence: Load-bearing fp fix; per-element math verified: centroids at integer FFT indices, tau*N integer (BGW commensurability), S integer, so images_raw*N is exactly integer up to ~1e-17 einsum noise; np.rint (line 322) then int64 np.floor_divide (line 324) gives the exact Euclidean L including negatives (-1//24=-1, mod 23). Naive float np.floor on a true-0 value carrying -1e-17 noise flips L by -1, entering unfold_v_q's umklapp phase exp(2*pi*i q.(L_mu-L_nu)); for q-components of 1/4 that is exactly the documented spurious exp(+-i pi/2) (comment lines 313-321, 14/64 q's on Si Fd-3m 24^3). The plain float floor in compute_rgrid_sym_perm:470 is NOT a latent copy of the bug: that function discards L entirely (returns only the permutation), and the rint + % fg at lines 472-473 absorbs the floor discontinuity in the mod-1 residue. Must survive any refactor; do not 'harmonise' the two functions by removing the snap.

item: src/centroid/orbit_syms.py:88 vs :149 two canonicalisation resolutions (_CANON_INV=10^12 jax vs inv=round(1/tol)=10^6 numpy)
evidence: Not the same concept, so 'unify to one constant' is wrong. _CANON_INV=10^12 (line 88, docstring 89-92) absorbs single-op fp64 drift (~1e-15) in the jit lex-winner without collapsing distinct orbit members. tol=1e-6 (line 130 default, line 149) on the numpy path is a DEDUP tolerance for kmeans reps that physically drifted into the same orbit during Lloyd (docstring 143-146) — at 1e-12 those duplicates would not merge and the orbit-aware pivoted Cholesky would see one physical orbit as two. The 'int! avoid fp64-precision loss at 1e18' comment at :149 documents a real past fix (float key overflow), not an unresolved scar. One genuine asymmetry worth a comment: numpy path wraps keys % inv (line 155) so 1-eps == 0, jax path (line 104) does not — consequence is measure-zero for continuous kmeans reps and zero for grid-commensurate data (spacing 1/N >> both tolerances).

item: src/centroid/orbit_syms.py:375-388 extend_trs row duplication in compute_centroid_sym_perm
evidence: Correctness is intentional and verified: TRS fixes r, so duplicating spatial rows to (2*n_sym,...) makes tables index-compatible with SymMaps.irr_idx_q/sym_idx_q in [0, 2*ntran), closing the silent promise_in_bounds OOB clip (comment 376-385). Division of labor confirmed: TRS conjugation of scalar V_q is applied in common/symmetry_maps.unfold_v_q:463-467 (conj where trs_mask), one-leg zeta conj in gw/v_q_tile._unfold_g0_ibz_to_full:1655-1659; both consumers enforce the 2*n_sym_spatial contract (symmetry_maps.py:263-271, v_q_tile.py:1610-1619). Caveat: the suspect's cite 'gw.v_q_tile._unfold_v_q_ibz_to_full' is a stale function name surviving only in comments — the scalar unfold lives in common/symmetry_maps.unfold_v_q. Confirmed minor cruft: .copy() inside np.concatenate (lines 386-387) is redundant (concatenate always copies). int8 L_table is safe (|L| <= ~3 for crystallographic S with entries in {-1,0,1}, far under 127) but the bound is undocumented. This is the known Phase-1 TRS patch; per the unified-sym-action plan, Phase-2 should route psi/zeta/V_q through one canonical IBZ table + one sym-action helper rather than keep this duplication convention — refactor-target, with the conj-placement contract preserved.

item: src/centroid/orbit_syms.py:458-470 compute_rgrid_sym_perm full-grid fp64 intermediates
evidence: Claim confirmed by reading 445-487: images fp64 (n_sym, n_rtot, 3) = 48 x 1e6 x 3 x 8 B ~= 1.15 GB at 100^3 x 48 syms, plus img_idx int64 same shape (~1.15 GB) and img_flat/sym_perm int64 (n_sym, n_rtot) (~0.4 GB each) => ~2.5-3 GB transient HOST peak, one-time setup, plain numpy (never a device buffer). Sole caller is file_io/zeta_loader.py:446 (q='full_bz' zeta unfold path; test coverage at tests/test_zeta_loader.py:191-213). Benign on Perlmutter (hundreds of GB host RAM) and current grid sizes; also note the validate loop (489-500) does per-sym np.unique over n_rtot — same one-time O(n_sym * n_rtot log n_rtot) class of cost. Not a defect today; if very large cells arrive, chunk over syms.

evidence: Exhaustive caller enumeration: (1) direct/from-import: repo-wide grep for 'AsyncWfnReader' hits only its own definition/docstring (wfn_loader.py:1176,1179,1193,1260), a prose mention in common/async_io.py:6, and the tried-and-rejected note at common/psi_G_store.py:195-201 ('xprof shows H2D/compute overlap_frac = 0.000 ... Keep the synchronous path'). item: Stale hardcoded line refs 'wfn_loader.py:678' for load() bounds check
evidence: Confirmed stale at all four sites. The actual bounds check 'if b_lo < 0 or b_hi > int(self.nbands): raise ValueError(band range ... out of [0, nbands))' lives at src/file_io/wfn_loader.py:829-832. Referencing comments: wfn_loader.py:739-740 ('WfnLoader.load rejects upstream at lines 678-681'), common/load_wfns.py:453 ('validates b_hi <= self.nbands at file_io/wfn_loader.py:678'), common/psi_G_store.py:~213 and ~243 (two more ':678' anchors, verified in the 180-260 window). All four describe the check's SEMANTICS correctly — only the line anchors drifted after edits. item: _HALFALPHA = 0.00364867628215 duplicated (wfn_loader.py:1035 and common/bispinor_init.py:30)
evidence: Confirmed verbatim duplication: src/file_io/wfn_loader.py:1035 module constant _HALFALPHA and src/common/bispinor_init.py:30 inline literal ('halfalpha = jnp.complex128(0.00364867628215)'). Repo-wide grep for the digit string finds exactly these two sites — no shared constants module. Values are bit-identical today so no divergence bug; the value implies 1/alpha = 137.0359992 (agrees with CODATA alpha/2 = 0.0036486762846 to ~4e-10 relative — physically irrelevant). The surrounding kernels are also duplicated math: wfn_loader's _get_bispinor_lift_jit (lines 1049-1072, k-vectorised sigma-dot-p einsum 'kgij,kbjg->kbig') mirrors bispinor_init.get_small_psi_component's 'ijG,bjG->biG' per-k version, and wfn_loader.py:925-927 explicitly claims byte-for-byte agreement. Two sources of truth for both the constant and the lift; consolidation target per the no-redundancy rule.

item: box_index_dev id(mesh) cache key (wfn_loader.py:491) + unbounded lru_caches keyed on Mesh/NamedSharding (lines 1038, 1106)
evidence: The suspected id-reuse-after-GC failure CANNOT fire, by a stronger argument than the comment's: the cached value at wfn_loader.py:504-505 is dev = jax.device_put(g_idx_np, sharding) where sharding = NamedSharding(mesh, ...) (line 496/498); a jax.Array holds its NamedSharding, and NamedSharding holds a strong reference to the Mesh. So as long as the _gvecs_dev_cache entry exists, the keyed mesh is reachable and its id() cannot be recycled — a stale-id lookup returning a wrong-mesh buffer is structurally impossible. The two lru_caches (_get_bispinor_lift_jit line 1038, _phdf5_unfold_kernel line 1106) key on hash/eq of Mesh/NamedSharding (not id), so an equal-but-distinct mesh hits correctly; maxsize=None growth is bounded in practice by the handful of distinct (mesh, shape-signature, sharding) tuples per run, and each entry pins one compiled executable — the intended behavior (the docstrings at 1040-1048 and 1099-1104 explain the trace-cache-miss problem they solve). Acknowledged design at lines 488-490. Only residual nit: module-level lru_caches keep meshes alive for interpreter lifetime, which matters for pytest suites creating many meshes — not production.

item: load() ns_out unused + 'elif bispinor: pass' no-op branch (wfn_loader.py:852-860)
evidence: Confirmed: ns_out = 4 if bispinor else int(self.nspinor) at line 852 is never referenced again anywhere in load() (the phdf5 branch goes straight to _phdf5_build at 861 and _apply_bispinor_lift at 865; grep confirms no later use of ns_out). The 'elif bispinor: pass' (856-860) is a genuine no-op, BUT its justifying comment is factually correct: a PartitionSpec/NamedSharding constrains axis partitioning by rank, not by size — the spec P(None,('x','y'),None,None) applies equally to the pre-lift (n_k, nb, 2, ngkmax) and post-lift (n_k, nb, 4, ngkmax) arrays since the spinor axis is replicated (None). So no bug; the branch is scaffolding left from when a post-lift sharding rebuild looked necessary and was found not to be. Safe to delete both lines; keep one comment line if the size-agnostic fact is worth preserving.

item: TRS tau-phase spatial-formula 'sign conspiracy' (_ensure_phdf5_static wfn_loader.py:596-620 + _phdf5_unfold_kernel wfn_loader.py:1140-1145)
evidence: Verified per-element, not pattern-matched. Setup: sym_mats_k TRS rows are -S_spatial by construction (common/symmetry_maps.py:855-856: time_reversal_syms = -self.sym_mats_k, concatenated). Phase table (wfn_loader.py:618-620): rotated = sym_mats_k[s] @ g_bar; phase = exp(-i rotated.tau). For a TRS row: rotated = -S@g, so phase[nk,g] = exp(+i (S g).tau) = conj(phase_spatial). Kernel (1140-1145), in order: cnk -> conj (where tr_mask), then * phase, then einsum 'kac,bckg->bakg' i.e. psi_out[b,a,k,g] = sum_c U[k,a,c] psi[b,c,k,g], with U_per for TRS rows = i*sigma_y @ conj(U_spatial) (lines 586-594). Composite TRS element: psi_full = (i sigma_y conj U) . conj(psi_kbar) . exp(+i(S g).tau). Target rule: psi_full = i sigma_y . conj(U psi_kbar exp(-i(S g).tau)) = (i sigma_y conj U) conj(psi_kbar) exp(+i(S g).tau). Match, including the order-sensitivity (conj BEFORE phase — conj after would flip the phase sign). This is byte-identical to the eager path's unfold_psi (symmetry_maps.py:696-724), whose docstring documents exactly this convention ('sym_mats_k[sym_idx] already encodes the +-S sign ... no separate sign branch'), and is covered by tests/test_unfold_psi_trs.py plus the eager<->phdf5 bit-equality contract (wfn_loader.py:26-27). The two-site coupling concern is real but the sign source is single (sym_mats_k) and documented at both sites. Residual refactor itch (not filed as the verdict): the tau-phase construction is duplicated between _ensure_phdf5_static (608-620) and unfold_psi (703-711) — a unified-sym-action consolidation candidate.

item: gvecs() cross-module call to private SymMaps._get_umklapp_vector passing loader as wfn (wfn_loader.py:377)
evidence: Verified functionally correct: symmetry_maps.py:1383-1406 shows _get_umklapp_vector reads only wfn.kpoints (lines 1392 and 1398) plus SymMaps' own sym_matrices/unfolded_kpts; WfnLoader exposes .kpoints (wfn_loader.py:168), so passing the loader as 'wfn' works and matches BGW's kg0 convention (k_full = S k_irred + kg0, per docstring / Common/gmap.f90). Exactly two call sites: symmetry_maps.py:1147 (internal) and wfn_loader.py:377 (the cross-module private access, left over from inlining the former sym.get_gvecs_kfull body — see comment at 364-368). No correctness risk today, but a private-method dependency across modules with a misleadingly wide 'wfn' parameter that actually needs one array; fix per no-new-API-layers style: make it take kpoints (or kbar's k-vector) directly, or promote to a public SymMaps accessor.

item: __init__ eager slurp of full wfns/coeffs into host RAM (wfn_loader.py:211)
evidence: Worse than the first-pass claim. The slurp self._coeffs_raw = self._file['wfns/coeffs'][:] at wfn_loader.py:211 is UNCONDITIONAL — there is no backend guard between backend selection (lines 136-143) and line 211, despite the comment at 209 labeling it 'Eager-backend state'. So it fires not only for the ~20 metadata-only WFNReader-alias consumers (WFNReader = WfnLoader shim at file_io/__init__.py:22, with the acknowledged 'a follow-up commit will sweep them' note at lines 17-21; construction sites include bse/bse_io.py:403,496,641,714,820,860, centroid/kmeans_cli.py:213, bandstructure/htransform.py:429, psp/run_sternheimer.py:1094, ...), but ALSO on the backend='phdf5' path used by the production driver (gw/gw_jax.py:136: WFNReader(config.paths.wfn_file, mesh=mesh_xy) -> auto -> phdf5 when multi-process): every rank host-slurps the entire (mnband, nspinor, ngktot, 2) f64 dataset that the phdf5 FFI then reads again collectively — _coeffs_raw is consumed only by the eager _eager_build (wfn_loader.py:992,1009) and get_gvec_nk; _ensure_phdf5_static needs only the small _gvecs_raw/_kpt_starts. At CrI3 6x6 600-band scale that is O(10 GB)/rank of pure host-RAM waste, and callers already tiptoe around it (common/load_wfns.py:29: 'do NOT re-open (would re-slurp coeffs)'). Comment/code mismatch + double-read on the production path = defect, not just deferred cleanup; fix is to gate line 211 on backend=='eager' (or lazy-load on first _eager_build/get_gvec_nk use) while keeping the cheap gvecs/kpt_starts reads.

item: _resolve_k: explicit k-list always means full-BZ indices with unfold=True (wfn_loader.py:321-336)
evidence: Documented at three places: _resolve_k docstring line 327 ('explicit list: interpreted as **full-BZ indices**, unfold=True'), load()'s padding contract (wfn_loader.py:810-812: 'For k='full_bz' (or explicit list): symmetry unfold ... applied internally'), and the KSpec type alias (line 68). It exists to carry the legacy full-zone semantics: common/load_wfns.py:17-30 (load_kpoint_fftbox, the migrated legacy get_cnk_fullzone caller) passes k=[int(k_idx)] with a full-BZ k_idx and its comment says 'the loader's full-BZ unfold is internal to load(k=[k_idx])'. Grep found no caller passing IBZ indices as a list; whole-IBZ access goes through k='ibz' (used by the 'ibz' string mode, e.g. tests/test_wfn_loader_eager.py:157). The footgun is real in principle — an IBZ index list would be silently reinterpreted through sym.unfolded_kpts ordering (irr_idx_k/sym_idx_k lookup at 370-371) and return sym-rotated data — but it is a documented convention with zero misusing callers today. A k_kind='ibz' escape hatch would be cheap hardening; not a bug.

item: close()/__del__ broad 'except Exception: pass' around h5py close and phdf5 FFI close_file (wfn_loader.py:238-262)
evidence: Read in full: close() (238-253) wraps both self._file.close() and ffi.phdf5.close_file(ctx) in bare except Exception: pass; __del__ (261-262) just calls close(). The __del__-time suppression is defensible — at interpreter shutdown the 'from ffi.phdf5 import close_file' can fail on torn-down module globals, and phdf5_close is a COLLECTIVE MPI operation (ffi/phdf5/context.py:80-94: 'Collective close', calls ffi_loader.phdf5_close) that can legitimately fail/hang if peer ranks already exited. The defect is that the SAME suppression applies on the deliberate paths: explicit close() and the context-manager __exit__ (258-259), so a real collective-close failure (e.g. one rank's H5Fclose erroring under the phdf5-mpich indep-write stack) is silently eaten and the run reports success with a possibly-unflushed file handle — for a read-only 'r' handle the data-loss risk is nil, which keeps this out of suspected-bug territory, but it masks MPI/FFI health signals. Fix: raise (or at least warn) on the explicit close()/__exit__ path, suppress only when invoked from __del__ (e.g. close(self, *, suppress=False) with __del__ passing suppress=True).

item: L452-472 comment: 'sharded pivoted-Cholesky SELECT path is not in this commit ... Coming in a follow-up'
evidence: Factually stale: make_sharded_pivoted_cholesky_select is defined at pivoted_cholesky.py:601 in the same file, implements exactly the psum/pmax pattern the comment promises (pmax pivot value L672, tie-break L674-677, masked-psum L[p,:] broadcast L687-690 — matching the comment's own collective list at L584-591), and is the mandatory production select (L412).
