item: L870-882 build_gram_q0_via_loadwfns legacy (n_val,n_cond) mode maps to right=(0, n_val+n_cond), contradicting docstring L806-808 and kmeans_cli v_x_c banner
evidence: Verified per-range: legacy branch sets left_range=(0,n_val), right_range=(0, n_val+n_cond) (L881-882), i.e. v x (v+c). Three pieces of its own doc trail claim v x c literal: (a) the same function's docstring L806-808 'right window = (n_val, n_val + n_cond). This is the literal valence x conduction pair-product Gram'; (b) prune wrapper comment L318 'right = (n_val, n_val + n_cond)'; (c) kmeans_cli.py:378-382 --prune-window v_x_c branch passes n_val/n_cond and prints 'prune window: v x c ... right=(nval,max) [legacy]'. Consequence: v_x_c and the default v_x_vc (kmeans_cli.py:368-370, explicit ranges (0,nval)/(0,max)) produce identical Grams, so the documented legacy option is unreachable via CLI and its banner lies. The underlying window change is deliberate and empirically motivated (in-code comment L873-880: ~3x V_H CBM error cut on MoS2 4x4; escape hatch = explicit band ranges), but the unfixed doc/CLI contract is a real defect. Latent today: no skills/ or runs/ script passes --prune-window (grep empty), so all production runs use the default. Fix = update docstring L806-808 + wrapper comment L318, and make kmeans_cli v_x_c pass band_range_right=(nval, max) explicitly (or delete the option).

item: L674-677 tie-break sentinel jnp.int32(2**30) in -pmax(-winner_p)
evidence: Standard pmin-via-negated-pmax idiom: devices whose local max misses global_pv contribute the sentinel (L674-676), winners contribute their int32 global index; -lax.pmax(-x) (L677) selects the smallest, i.e. lowest global index among tied winners (within-device ties already resolved to lowest local index by jnp.argmax L670). Correctness requires every real index < 2^30 and safe negation: |−2^30| < 2^31−1, OK. M >= 2^30 is physically unreachable — the row-sharded Gram is (M/n_dev, M) complex128, so M=2^30 needs ~2^64 bytes; actual M ~ 10^3-10^5 candidates. A named INT_SENTINEL constant would be nicer but there is nothing to fix.

item: L51 + L98 + L114: 'from file_io import WfnLoader as WFNReader' at module top; dead gather_wfn_at_candidates re-imports WfnLoader and reads wfn._filename
evidence: Confirmed naming shear from the WFNReader->WfnLoader migration: L51 aliases the new class to the legacy name purely for type annotations used throughout the file (L61, L280, L786); the dead function then re-imports the same class under its new name at L98 and opens WfnLoader(wfn._filename) at L114. The private attribute is deliberately maintained for exactly this compat: file_io/wfn_loader.py:133 'self._filename = self._path  # legacy WFNReader compat'. Since gather_wfn_at_candidates is confirmed-dead (above), deleting it removes the re-import and the private-attr read; the L51 alias could then be renamed in a cosmetic pass. No behavioral risk.

item: L3-4 and L816 docstrings cite pivoted_cholesky.md (sandbox root) and isdf_fitting.py:838-847 for the norm clamp
evidence: Both citations verified stale. (1) /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/pivoted_cholesky.md does not exist; the only file of that name anywhere is reports/gw_refactor_map_2026-07-01/files/pivoted_cholesky.md — the first-pass auditor's artifact from today, not the cited design doc. The module docstring leans on it repeatedly ('per the md's guidance' L22, 'following the md' L26, section refs L145). (2) isdf_fitting.py:838-847 is now a jit-cache insertion + gamma-perm block (_pair_pipeline_sm_cache[cache_key] = fn at 838); the psi /= max(norm,1.0) clamp actually lives in _band_norms_slice at isdf_fitting.py:1804-1824 (jnp.maximum(jnp.asarray(sliced), 1.0) at 1824). Doc-only rot; update or drop the references.

item: L920-926 bare 'except Exception' -> memory_per_device_gb = 0.0 relying on a downstream 36 GB default
evidence: The mechanism is real and the comment accurate: common/load_wfns.py:389-391 sets gpu_mem_bytes = 36e9 unless meta.memory_per_device_gb > 0, so the 0.0 sentinel deliberately routes to a 36 GB chunking budget. Risk confirmed by environment facts: the sandbox's local GPU is 8 GB (CLAUDE.md memory table, budget 6.0) — if common.gpu_utils.get_device_memory_gb (gpu_utils.py:77, normally returns 0.9*bytes_limit) raises for any reason, the band-+k-chunker silently sizes FFT boxes for 36 GB and OOMs with no hint of the misdetection; the bare except emits no log line and catches everything including programming errors in gpu_utils. Only reached when the caller passes no explicit budget (kmeans_cli path does not pin one). Fix: narrow the except, print the fallback, and/or default to a conservative value with a warning.

item: L557-570 make_sharded_gram_q0 enforce_hermitian all-gathers full (M,M) G to every device then reshards
evidence: Explicitly acknowledged tradeoff, twice: docstring L497-501 ('requires an all-gather ... caller can defer this step') and inline comment L558-562 ('acceptable while G still fits post-gather on one device ... the select step can tolerate small non-Hermiticity via its own clamp' — the clamp being jnp.maximum(real(diag),0) at L217/L649-651 plus pivot_val eps floor L235/L678). Exposure is nil: make_sharded_gram_q0 is test-only (see verdict above); the production Gram builder is build_gram_q0_via_loadwfns which never calls it. A scalability cliff in reference/test code with a written deferral plan is a documented convention, not a defect.

item: L243 / L697-699 pivot entry of L force-set to exactly sqrt(d[p])
evidence: Deliberate and the two kernels ARE in sync, verified element-wise: single-device L241-243 computes newcol=(G[:,p]-corr)/sqrt(d[p]) then newcol.at[p].set(denom); sharded L696-699 computes the same slab column then jnp.where(fix_row_mask, denom, newcol) with fix_row_mask = my_has_p & (arange(M_slab)==local_p_rel) (L698) — exactly one element globally (only the owner shard has global_p//M_slab==my_idx, L684), the same element p. Purpose: guarantees the Schur update d_new = max(d - |newcol|^2, 0) (L250/L706) drives d[p] to exactly 0 instead of fp residue, so the subsequent minus_inf kill (L254/L722) and the trR/trG diagnostics are clean. Parity is regression-guarded: test_pivoted_cholesky.py:302-334 compares sharded vs single-device pivot order and residuals. Keep, but the 'must stay in sync' coupling is worth a cross-reference comment.

item: L179 Gram accumulator shaped (M, M2) mixing phi's M and psi's M2 with no M==M2 assertion
evidence: Not a silent footgun — enumerated the shape cases. P_v = phi_k.T @ conj(phi_k) is (M,M) (L171); P_c_tilde = conj(psi_k).T @ psi_k is (M2,M2) (L175); their elementwise product (L177): if M != M2 and both > 1, broadcasting fails at jit-trace with a shape error; if one is 1 (e.g. M=1, M2=5), the product broadcasts to (5,5) but the fori_loop carry was initialized (M,M2)=(1,5) (L179-180), and lax.fori_loop enforces carry-shape invariance, so tracing raises a carry-structure TypeError; the (G+conj(G.T))/2 symmetrization (L183) likewise requires square. Every mismatch dies loudly at trace time before producing numbers. Function is also test-only. The (M, M2) init is just odd style inviting confusion; an explicit 'if M != M2: raise' would only improve the error message. Cosmetic cleanup, no correctness risk.

item: src/centroid/kmeans_isdf.py: kmeans_update_step
evidence: Defined kmeans_isdf.py:442. Repo-wide grep (src, tests, tools, scripts, docs, pyproject, sandbox skills/ and runs/ *.sh): only tests/test_kmeans_sharded.py:86,137,385 call it, where it is the single-device reference oracle against the sharded loop. Production driver weighted_kmeans_jax (kmeans_isdf.py:868-877) uses make_sharded_lloyd_loop/make_sharded_orbit_lloyd_loop, whose body _kmeans_step_shardmap_body (line 472) re-composes the same helpers rather than calling kmeans_update_step. No getattr/dict dispatch in src/centroid; console scripts (pyproject.toml:32-35) expose only gw_jax and kmeans_cli mains; skills invoke only `python3 -m centroid.kmeans_cli`. The hits under runs/Si_pseudobands/.../30_profile_kmeans_isdf/ are stale 2026-04-16 XLA-dump logs whose source lines (166,199) match a pre-refactor file layout, not live callers. Note: deleting it would remove the tests' ground-truth reference.

item: src/centroid/kmeans_isdf.py: make_sharded_kmeans_update
evidence: Defined kmeans_isdf.py:510; section header line 469 says 'kept for direct tests / reuse'. Same exhaustive mechanism enumeration as above: only tests/test_kmeans_sharded.py:147,394 reference it. The production sharded path is make_sharded_lloyd_loop (kmeans_isdf.py:560, called at 874) which inlines _kmeans_step_shardmap_body in a lax.while_loop instead of reusing this per-step wrapper. Zero non-test references in entry points, string dispatch, docs, tools, scripts, sandbox skills/runs.

item: src/centroid/kmeans_plot.py (whole file)
evidence: NOT dead: kmeans_cli.py:419-422 imports plot_density_and_centroids + interpolate_density when --plot is passed, and kmeans_cli.main is a wired console script (pyproject.toml:35 lorrax-centroids) plus the documented `python3 -m centroid.kmeans_cli` path in skills/execute_workflow/SKILL.md:190,312. --plot is default-off (kmeans_cli.py:49-51) and no test, skill, or run.sh passes it, so it is cold but reachable production code with an advertised CLI flag. Per the rubric (confirmed-dead requires zero live references), verdict is alive; 'near-dead' is a fair qualitative description but not a removal-safe finding without also removing the CLI flag.

item: src/centroid/centroid_io.py: read_centroids / CentroidFile / DensityLabel (entire file)
evidence: Repo-wide grep for read_centroids|centroid_io|CentroidFile|DensityLabel across src, tests, tools, scripts, docs, pyproject, plus sandbox skills/ and runs/: the only reference outside the file itself is prose in a docstring, src/file_io/isdf_header.py:15 (':class:`centroid.centroid_io.CentroidFile`'), which is documentation text, not an import. The file's own stated consumer ('gw_jax in bispinor mode', centroid_io.py:38-39) actually uses file_io.centroids.load_centroids (gw_init.py:767,772 and 950,963). No string dispatch, no entry point, no test. The density-header dispatch capability it provides is instead handled by requiring cfg.paths.centroids_file_current explicitly (gw_init.py:757-763). Zero live references.

item: kmeans_isdf.py:36,791 — BOHR_TO_ANG / Å-denominated tolerance=5e-3
evidence: weighted_kmeans_jax is deliberately unit-agnostic: its docstring (kmeans_isdf.py:822) states max_movement_sq is 'in avec units squared', i.e. tolerance is in whatever units the caller's avec carries. The only production caller (kmeans_cli.py:247 avec_ang = wfn.avec*alat*BOHR_TO_ANG) works in Å by design (prints 'Lattice lengths ... Å', line 249), making the default 5e-3 an Å tolerance. A hypothetical Bohr caller would converge ~1.9x tighter, but the effect is immaterial because centroids are grid-snapped afterwards (snap_centroids_to_grid, cli:310/329) and 5e-3 Å is already far below typical FFT spacing (~0.05-0.1 Å). Unit coupling is real but documented and harmless; the suspect's '~2x different convergence' failure mode has no in-tree caller.

item: kmeans_isdf.py:114 — _DEFAULT_C_BLOCK=32, '~30 MB at P=1e6 in fp32' comment
evidence: The comment is stale twice over. (1) Dtype: the module forces x64 (kmeans_isdf.py:33 config.update('jax_enable_x64', True)) and positions are built fp64 (line 836), so the scan buffers dx0/dy0/dz0 (each (P, c_block), lines 146-151) are fp64. (2) Arithmetic: even in fp32, 1e6 x 32 x 4 B = 128 MB per axis, not ~30 MB; in the actual fp64 it is 256 MB per axis — so the estimate is 4-8x off, worse than the suspect's '2x'. Chunking behavior itself is correct (memory scales with c_block not n_c); only the prose is wrong. Comment fix only.

item: kmeans_isdf.py:137,163,261,281 — NaN-as-sentinel centroid padding
evidence: Explicit per-case check: pad rows are NaN (lines 137,261); dx0 = pos - NaN = NaN; round(NaN)=NaN; _quadform_G(NaN,...)=NaN; XLA jnp.minimum propagates NaN through both the offset fori_loop (init inf, minimum(inf,NaN)=NaN) and the orbit sym fori_loop; a single isnan->inf mask (lines 163, 281) then runs before argmin. Padded columns cannot win argmin because padding count = n_c_padded-n_c <= c_block-1, so the (only) padded chunk always contains >=1 real centroid with finite d2; pass-2 winning_rep=rep_chunk[local_c] (line 287) therefore never indexes a NaN rep. The invariant is deliberate and commented at both sites ('NaN propagates through the offset loop; mask once at the end', 161-163; 'NaN sentinel for padded reps...', 279-280). Fragile-if-edited but correct as written.

item: kmeans_isdf.py:231 — tie_tol=1e-10 absolute orbit tie tolerance
evidence: Used at line 292: is_tied = d2_s <= local_d + tie_tol, comparing squared distances in the caller's avec units (Å² via kmeans_cli). At physical scales (d² ~ 1-100 Å²) fp64 roundoff between sym-equivalent images is ~1e-14-1e-13, so 1e-10 has 3-4 orders of margin; even a 100 Å cell (d² ~1e4) leaves margin. It is genuinely scale-dependent (a Bohr-metric or huge-supercell caller shifts the safety margin) and a relative tolerance would be cleaner, but no in-tree caller is near the failure regime. Magic constant, not a bug.

item: kmeans_isdf.py:987-995 — _warn_dense_grid_regime unused m_cand parameter
evidence: Confirmed: signature takes m_cand (line 987) but the body tests only n_c (line 990). The caller passes the oversampled count (kmeans_cli.py:220 _warn_dense_grid_regime(M_cand, N_c, n_rtot)), and M_cand = ceil(N_c*oversample) >= N_c is the count kmeans actually runs with (cli:205), so the intended dense-grid warning under-triggers by up to the oversample factor (default 1.5x). Advisory-print only, no numerical impact, but the vestigial parameter signals the gate was meant to use m_cand — either use it or drop it (repo convention: no dead parallel intent).

item: kmeans_isdf.py:878-882 — comment about NOT using the chi.compile/chi.exec split
evidence: Lines 878-882 explain why the compile/exec split idiom from gw_jax is 'moot here because only one call happens per process' while noting block_until_ready still pins timing to GPU work. It documents a considered-and-rejected alternative; no code depends on it. Harmless explanatory prose, at worst deletable.

item: kmeans_cli.py:101-107 vs gw/v_q_bispinor.py:571, docs/theory/symmetry.md:505 — '--orbit-aware' does not exist
evidence: Confirmed stale flag name. Parser defines only --orbit (kmeans_cli.py:101) and --no-orbit (:105); grep for 'orbit-aware' finds the runtime fallback message v_q_bispinor.py:571 ('Regenerate centroids with ``kmeans_cli --orbit-aware``'), a comment at :555, and docs/theory/symmetry.md:505 (twice). Following the advice verbatim gives an argparse 'unrecognized arguments: --orbit-aware' error (it is longer than --orbit so prefix-abbreviation cannot save it). Doubly stale: since orbit mode auto-enables when wfn.ntran>1 (cli:265), the correct remediation is usually 'regenerate WITHOUT --no-orbit', not adding any flag. Defect lives in message strings/docs, not in executable control flow.

item: kmeans_cli.py:265 — orbit mode auto-enables when wfn.ntran>1
evidence: orbit_aware = args.orbit or (int(wfn.ntran) > 1 and not args.no_orbit), preceded by an explicit design comment (lines 263-264: 'Default heuristic: orbit if --orbit explicit OR WFN has > 1 spatial sym op AND --no-orbit not set') and announced at runtime ('Orbit-aware mode: n_sym = ...', line 285-287). This default is what feeds the downstream IBZ cascade gating (v_q_bispinor closure checks); an escape hatch (--no-orbit) exists. Behavior does silently depend on WFN sym content, but that dependence is the documented design, not an accident.

item: kmeans_cli.py:369-382 — two kwarg protocols into prune_candidates_by_pivoted_cholesky
evidence: Confirmed parallel old/new protocols. prune_candidates_by_pivoted_cholesky accepts both (n_val, n_cond) and (band_range_left, band_range_right) (pivoted_cholesky.py:286-289) and internally rewrites the legacy pair as left=(0,n_val), right=(n_val,n_val+n_cond) (comment at :318). The call site branches three ways (cli:368-382), passing band ranges for v_x_vc/vc_x_vc but raw n_val/n_cond for the legacy v_x_c window — which is itself expressible as band ranges. Both paths are live (v_x_c reachable via --prune-window), so not dead code, but it is exactly the 'no parallel old/new paths, single source of truth' violation the repo conventions forbid: normalize to band ranges at the call site and drop the (n_val, n_cond) kwargs.

item: kmeans_cli.py:356-360 vs 229 — prune n_val default = wfn.nelec with no nspinor adjustment
evidence: Confirmed inconsistency between two occupied-count conventions in the same file. --density-mode current computes n_occ = nelec (nspinor=2) or nelec//2 (nspinor=1) with an explicit comment ('restricted Kohn-Sham, two electrons per band', cli:228-229). The prune default is _n_val_eff = int(wfn.nelec) (cli:356-357; same default inside pivoted_cholesky.py:319-320), used as a BAND-index bound (band_range_left=(0,n_val)). For nspinor=1 that is 2x the true valence band count. Consequences: in the default v_x_vc window the left range just over-includes ~nelec/2 conduction bands (conservative, mostly benign); in legacy v_x_c the right window becomes (nelec, nelec+n_cond), so pairs of valence with the LOWEST nelec/2 conduction bands land left-x-left and are absent from the left-x-right Gram — not the intended val-x-cond structure. Overridable via --prune-n-val; correct for nspinor=2. Latent nspinor=1 defect in the default, could not runtime-verify magnitude of the pruning-quality impact (login node).

item: kmeans_cli.py:140 — _P_PER_SHARD_MIN=100_000 NCCL-latency floor
evidence: Empirical constant with measurement provenance in its own docstring (lines 141-143: 'measured on Si 4x4x4: P=110k / 4 GPUs gave a slower run than single-device because allreduce dominated'). The multi-host never-fall-back exception is separately justified (lines 166-168: single-device fallback would leave other ranks hanging on collectives and stall the distributed shutdown barrier) and logged when taken (:175-177). Escape hatch --force-shard exists (:57-59). Documented, deliberate, benign.

item: kmeans_cli.py:304-320 — snap-reps-before-unfold, grid-commensurate tau assumed in prose only
evidence: The rationale comment (cli:305-309) is correct: snapping fp64 reps first, then unfolding with integer R and grid-commensurate tau, guarantees on-grid orbit closure. The commensurability assumption is not checked inside unfold_orbit_unique_with_id (orbit_syms.py:127-173, dedup at tol=1e-6 only), but it is (a) guaranteed for QE/BGW inputs ('mtrx and tau are commensurate (BGW guarantee)', orbit_syms.py:313-314; :425-428 'QE outputs satisfy this commensurability; if a future workflow...'), and (b) NOT silent downstream: compute_centroid_sym_perm raises when a rounded image is off-grid (orbit_syms.py:~370) and validate_atomic_symmetries errors on non-integer tau x fft_grid (:495), while the bispinor IBZ cascade detects closure failure and falls back to full-BZ with a loud log (v_q_bispinor.py:566-573). The Rinv=inv(mtrx) direction comment matches the validated BGW convention (orbit_syms.py:199-202, 96/96 Si atom mappings). The only silent window is the CLI emitting a non-closed centroid file; the pipeline catches it before it corrupts physics.

item: kmeans_cli.py:350 — n_orbit_keep = ceil(N_c*n_orbits/n_unique)
evidence: Approximate-by-design and documented at both ends: the call-site comment (cli:346-347 'Orbit mode: target ORBITS not points; final centroid count is Sum orbit_size of picked orbits (~= N_c by construction)') and the pruner docstring (pivoted_cholesky.py:304-309: with orbit_id, n_keep counts ORBITS and keep_idx is the union of picked orbits, guaranteed orbit-closed). Exact N_c is impossible while preserving orbit closure (orbit sizes vary, 1..n_sym at special positions), so approximation is forced by the closure requirement; the achieved count is printed (cli:390) and the output filename embeds the actual n_unique (cli:396).

item: kmeans_plot.py:61 — np.log(np.abs(rho_at_pts) - 0.9*threshold)
evidence: Safe by the mask: rho_at_pts = rho_np[mask] with mask = rho_np > threshold (lines 44,52), and threshold = 0.05*rho.max() > 0, so rho_at_pts > threshold > 0.9*threshold, making the log argument > 0.1*threshold > 0. The abs() is vestigial (values already positive under the mask) and 0.9 is an ad-hoc contrast stretch to spread the low end of the log color scale. Plot-only cosmetic hack behind the default-off --plot flag; no numerical consumer.

item: kmeans_plot.py:54,67 — cartesian via @ wfn.avec (alat units) vs CLI's avec*alat*BOHR_TO_ANG
evidence: Confirmed unit mismatch with the kmeans metric (kmeans_cli.py:247 uses avec*alat*BOHR_TO_ANG), but internally self-consistent: density points (:54), centroids (:67), and the cell box (:75) all use the same pts_frac @ wfn.avec, so the geometry is a uniform alat-scaled rendering — relative positions and shape are correct, only the axis units are unlabeled alat (axis labels are bare 'X'/'Y'/'Z', :82). Cosmetic only, behind default-off --plot; worst case a reader misreads axis magnitudes on a diagnostic PNG.

item: src/centroid/charge_density.py — rho_from_wfn_ibz / _load_wfn_k_fftbox_ibz (wfn_ibz fallback path)
evidence: Reachable via string-based dispatch on a live production CLI: kmeans_cli.py:60 argparse choice 'wfn_ibz' -> kmeans_cli.py:234-238 get_charge_density(source=args.rho_source) -> charge_density.py:230 rho_from_wfn_ibz; ALSO reachable via the DEFAULT source='auto' fallback (charge_density.py:218-220) whenever _autodetect_save_dir finds no QE .save — no flag needed. kmeans_cli:main is a console script 'lorrax-centroids' (pyproject.toml:35) and documented entry ('python -m centroid.kmeans_cli', docs/quickstart.md:42, docs/architecture/codebase.md:478). Mitigating: zero tests, zero run.sh/skill invocations pass wfn_ibz (grep over sandbox skills/ and runs/ finds none), and every production log shows 'auto -> reading .../charge-density.hdf5'. So it has never been exercised — but it is NOT confirmed-dead: it is a live, dispatchable path that crashes on first use (see NameError verdict).

item: src/centroid/charge_density.py:97 — jax.devices() with no 'import jax'
evidence: CONFIRMED NameError. Module-level imports: line 37 'import jax.numpy as jnp' (binds only jnp); function-local line 91 'from jax.sharding import Mesh' (binds only Mesh). The bare name 'jax' is never bound, so line 97 'np.asarray(jax.devices()[:1])' raises NameError the moment _load_wfn_k_fftbox_ibz executes. Since this path is reachable from the default 'auto' fallback of a production CLI (see alive verdict), any user launching lorrax-centroids from a directory without a findable QE .save gets a crash instead of the advertised fallback. Hypothesis consistent with evidence: the WfnLoader/to_box refactor (P5 comment at lines 87-90) was committed without exercising this branch — all logged runs took the qe_save path. One-line fix: add 'import jax' (or use a numpy-free Mesh construction).

item: src/centroid/charge_density.py:139 — n_val default = wfn.nelec allegedly 2x occupied bands for nspinor=1
evidence: CLAIM REFUTED by the actual nelec semantics: wfn_loader.py:194-198 sets self.nelec = int(np.max(self.ifmax)) — the 1-based index of the highest occupied BAND (fallback: count of bands with occ>0.5), NOT the electron count. For nspinor=1 restricted-KS this is already n_electrons/2, so n_val = wfn.nelec selects exactly the occupied bands in both spinor cases. The live pivoted-Cholesky prune path uses the same convention (kmeans_cli.py:356 n_val default = wfn.nelec as a band window) and works in production. The attribute is badly NAMED (qe_save_reader.py:316 even asserts QE's true electron count equals wfn.nelec with tol=0.5, which can only pass for nspinor=2), and the charge_density.py:121-122 docstring ('all valence electrons for spinor wfn') only describes the nspinor=2 case — but the VALUE is correct. COUNTER-FINDING uncovered during verification: kmeans_cli.py:229 'n_occ = int(wfn.nelec) if nspinor==2 else int(wfn.nelec)//2' assumes nelec=electron-count and therefore HALVES the occupied-band count for scalar current-mode runs — the bug the first-pass reader hypothesized exists, but in the opposite file and opposite direction.

item: src/centroid/charge_density.py:88-90,150-171 — P5 planned-refactor marker; _autodetect_save_dir hard-codes sandbox layout
evidence: Confirmed acknowledged-transitional. Line 87-89 docstring: 'P5 will switch the caller to pass a WfnLoader directly so this transient construction goes away' — an explicit TODO; the double-open (WFNReader alias already IS WfnLoader per file_io/__init__.py:22, then _load_wfn_k_fftbox_ibz opens a second WfnLoader on wfn._filename at line 95) is the transitional cost. _autodetect_save_dir (lines 150-171) hard-codes 'qe/scf'/'qe/nscf' glob patterns and stops walking at an ancestor literally named 'runs' (line 166) — the lorrax_sandbox directory convention baked into library code. This is intentional and documented user-facing behavior (kmeans_cli.py:74-78 --qe-save help: 'no need to pass when launching from a normal sandbox run dir'), so not a bug, but it couples the library to one deployment layout. Classify refactor-target, matching the code's own P5 marker.

item: src/centroid/orbit_syms.py:19 — docstring cites charge_density._symmetrise_density which does not exist
evidence: Confirmed stale pointer. grep '_symmetrise_density' over src/ + tests/: the only definition is psp/archive/charge_density.py:130 (called at :125 inside the archived module); centroid/charge_density.py has no such function. The function is documented BROKEN ('increases density', psp/dev_status.md:176) and psp/scf_potential.py:19-20,118 explicitly routes around it. So orbit_syms.py:19 cites, as the canonical example of a sign convention, a function that was archived and is known-wrong — a doc-only defect (no code path touches it), but actively misleading for anyone verifying the tau-sign convention. Fix: point at sym.validate_atomic_symmetries / symmetry_maps.py alone.

item: src/centroid/current_density.py:53-64 — Kc_x/y/z cast complex128, captured as jit closure constants
evidence: Confirmed as described: _Kc_axis (lines 59-62) builds three full (nx,ny,nz) grids and casts .astype(jnp.complex128); they are closed over by @jax.jit _kpt_contrib (used at lines 122-127), so they live as device-resident constants for the whole build. The complex cast is unnecessary: usage is '1j * (Kc_y*sz_G - Kc_z*sy_G)' where sz_G is already complex128 — JAX promotes float64*complex128 -> complex128 with no warning under x64, so real float64 grids halve the constant footprint (e.g. CrI3 75x75x200: 3x18MB instead of 3x36MB). Correctness unaffected (values are purely real integers-times-bvec either way; fftfreq(n, d=1/n) yields integer frequencies). Minor memory/hygiene cleanup only — the module's own budget note (lines 40-42) shows this is not the dominant term.

item: src/centroid/current_density.py:155-157 — scalar nspinor=1 psi zero-padded into 2-spinor slot -> spurious spin-curl weight
evidence: Index math confirms the physics claim: with psi in the up slot and down=0, s_x=2Re(conj(psi_up)psi_dn)=0, s_y=0, s_z=|psi|^2 (lines 104-109), so the Gordon weight gains (1/2)grad-cross(z-hat |psi|^2) != 0 — treating restricted-KS electrons as 100% spin-up polarized, whereas the physical spin density of a doubly-occupied band is zero and the spin-curl should vanish. The scalar case is NOT unreachable: kmeans_cli.py:227-231 explicitly computes n_occ for nspinor==1 before calling build_current_density, so scalar+current is an anticipated combination despite the module docstring and --density-mode help (kmeans_cli.py:119-122) advertising it as the bispinor-pipeline weight. The embedding is uncommented in current_density.py itself. Compounding: kmeans_cli.py:229 also halves n_occ for scalar (nelec//2 where wfn.nelec=max(ifmax) is already the band count — wfn_loader.py:194-198), so scalar-current mode would both drop half the valence bands AND add a spurious spin term. Bispinor (nspinor=2) mode is unaffected (both slots filled exactly). Consequence is bounded — this is only a k-means weight, not a physics output — but the scalar branch was clearly never validated.

item: src/centroid/current_density.py:174 — uniform /nk_full vs charge path's wfn.kweights
evidence: Not an inconsistency in the weights themselves, only in loop structure. current_density loops the FULL BZ (line 147 'for ik in range(nk_full)' over WfnLoader k='full_bz' unfolded points), where every k-point has equal weight 1/nk_full — line 174 is exactly right. The charge path loops the IBZ and compute_valence_density (psp/get_DFT_mtxels.py:211-218) applies wfn.kweights when the leading dim matches len(kweights), and itself falls back to uniform 1/sym.nk_tot for full-BZ input — i.e. the two conventions are the same measure expressed for different loop domains, and the charge helper already handles both. Sum of IBZ kweights = 1 = nk_full * (1/nk_full); no double counting, no normalization mismatch. Harmless asymmetry that would disappear if both used one loop domain, but nothing to fix for correctness.

item: src/centroid/current_density.py:144 — private-attr access wfn._filename
evidence: Confirmed transitional double-open cruft, same pattern as charge_density.py:95. wfn is already a WfnLoader (kmeans_cli.py:24 'from file_io import WfnLoader as WFNReader'; file_io/__init__.py:22 'WFNReader = WfnLoader'), and _filename is set purely as a compat alias (wfn_loader.py:133 'self._filename = self._path  # legacy WFNReader compat'). So line 144 re-opens the same HDF5 file in a second WfnLoader instead of using the instance it was handed. Functionally safe (read-only double open; the comment block at lines 140-143 even explains why the per-k loop lives here), and file_io/__init__.py:17-21 documents that a follow-up commit will sweep callers to WfnLoader directly — the P5 cleanup. Aligns with the no-redundancy directive but is not a defect today.

item: src/centroid/current_density.py:94-109 — einsums 'naxyz,naxyz->nxyz' (.imag) and 'naxyz,ab,nbxyz->nxyz' (.real)
evidence: Verified by explicit per-element index math, not pattern matching. (1) 'naxyz,naxyz->nxyz' on (conj(psi_r), grad_i): out[n,x,y,z] = SUM_a conj(psi[n,a,xyz]) * d_i psi[n,a,xyz]; .imag gives Im[psi^dag d_i psi], the paramagnetic current per band (hbar=m=1). The gradient (lines 86-90) multiplies psi(G) by i(k+G)_i, so the code computes Im[u^dag(grad + ik)u] which equals Im[psi^dag grad psi] for Bloch psi=e^{ikr}u — the k|u|^2 term is correctly included. (2) 'naxyz,ab,nbxyz->nxyz': out[n,xyz] = SUM_{a,b} conj(psi[n,a]) sigma_i[a,b] psi[n,b] = psi^dag sigma_i psi; taking .real is exact (not a truncation) because sigma_i are Hermitian (gamma_matrices.py:12-14). (3) Curl (lines 122-127): (curl s)_x = i(K_y s_z - K_z s_y), _y = i(K_z s_x - K_x s_z), _z = i(K_x s_y - K_y s_x) — all three match epsilon_ijk d_j s_k, and correctly use k-free FFT-grid momenta since s(r) is cell-periodic. (4) Combination j_para + 0.5*curl (lines 131-133) matches the Gordon docstring. No defect found in the bispinor path.

item: fit_one_rchunk.py:469 ngkmax_aot = n_r_total // 16
evidence: 1/16 is the standard wfc-G-sphere-to-FFT-box volume ratio: FFT box sides span the charge-density sphere diameter 4*kmax, wfc sphere radius kmax, ratio (4pi/3 kmax^3)/(4 kmax)^3 = pi/48 ~ 1/15.3, so //16 is the physically right order (~4% under the sphere count, so 'conservative' in the docstring is slightly optimistic, not load-bearing). Latent wart found while verifying: SysDims (core.py:37-88) defines NO n_g field, so getattr(sys,'n_g',0) is ALWAYS 0 and the fallback always fires — the 'when set' branch is unreachable dead code.

evidence: Both assignments read the same immutable sys.kgrid; the first feeds Meta(nkx=kx,...) at l.444, the second (identical values) feeds the MP-grid meshgrid at l.451-454. Pure edit leftover, zero behavioral effect.

item: fit_one_rchunk.py:437-446 synthetic Meta (cell_volume=1.0, zeros)
evidence: Fragility confirmed: the documented field subset (l.432-434: 'nk_tot, n_rmu, nspinor, fft_grid, kgrid, n_rtot') is ALREADY stale — _make_fit_one_rchunk_kernel reads meta.n_rmu_padded (isdf_fitting.py:1605), which the synthetic Meta leaves at dataclass default 0 (meta.py:40). Currently harmless only because that local is unused downstream in the factory body (checked isdf_fitting.py:1605-1716: no functional n_rmu consumer; z_q_from_psi_sm derives shapes from the psi args), but any future meta consumer silently breaks the AOT model. kgrid works via Meta.__post_init__ (meta.py:51). The implicit field-subset contract should be made explicit or the factory should take dims directly.

item: fit_one_rchunk.py:385-402 gamma_perm/gamma_phase always passed
evidence: Exact mirror of production: isdf_fitting.py:1670-1680 `_kernel(psi_l, psi_r, L_q, norms_l, norms_r, r_start_dyn, gamma_perm, gamma_phase, cct_trace_per_q)` — the same unified 9-arg signature for charge and bispinor channels; l.1636 `gamma_mu = None if is_charge else (gamma_perm, gamma_phase)` resolves at trace exactly as the spec comment says; shapes verified: gamma_matrices.py:87-101 gammas_perm (4,4) int32 / gammas_phase (4,4) c128, gamma_perm_phase returns one row each -> (4,) int32 + (4,) c128, matching the (4,) specs at l.400-401.

item: load_psi_rchunk.py:127-141 n_r = prod(sys.kgrid) in kernel 1
evidence: Deliberate and internally consistent at current presets — presets.py:222-226 documents 'we repurpose SysDims.kgrid as the real-space FFT grid; n_r = prod(kgrid)' and every point sets n_r=prod(kgrid) (e.g. 24^3 with kgrid=(24,24,24)), so specs and primitives agree. But core.py:63-67 added SysDims.fft_grid precisely to end this overload ('Falls back to kgrid when unset (back-compat with kernels that repurpose kgrid as the FFT box)'); the kernel should read sys.fft_shape. If a caller ever passes a real k-grid here the spec shapes and primitive bytes silently diverge — the trap is real, just not sprung by current presets.

The 2026-04-21 XLA note is now historical and this kernel pair models a retired production structure — exactly the drift the note warns about.

item: chi0_tau_step.py:126-135 'must update in lockstep' docstring
evidence: The warned drift HAS happened and lockstep was not maintained. Production _get_chi_minimax_kernel (w_isdf.py:47-200) was rewritten to the Hermitian-swap form: BOTH Gv and Gc via make_flat_k_fftn (w_isdf.py:90-92), both on the SAME sharding G_FLATK_SPEC=P(None,None,'x',None,'y') (wavefunction_bundle.py:121; w_isdf.py:99,116-123), einsum 'Rambn,Rambn->Rmn' with conj(Gv_R) (w_isdf.py:180-182), chi in CHI_R_SPEC=P(None,'x','y') (wavefunction_bundle.py:125), built via build_G_tau and a lax.scan over ALL nodes with alpha weights (w_isdf.py:174-196). The model still lowers the historical single-tau form (ifftn+fftn, opposite shardings l.142-149, swapped einsum l.171, donated per-step accumulator, build_G+phases). The old form forced mu-resharding collectives the new form was designed to eliminate, so the modeled peak/collective structure no longer tracks production; gamma calibration can mask the magnitude but not the structural drift.

item: chi0_tau_step.py:44-58 hardcoded allocation dump header
evidence: Comment-only calibration provenance (21.97 GiB = 4x Gbuf etc.) keyed to runs/Si/C_probe_si444_60Ry xla_dump — useful archaeology for why beta_Gbuf~4 was expected, but stale-prone and now doubly stale given the production rewrite (see lockstep item). No code reads it.

item: chi0_tau_step.py:160-167 Gv/Gc opposite shardings + both conjugated
evidence: Internally coherent mirror of the RETIRED production convention, verified by index math: einsum 'Rambn,Rbnam->Rmn' contracts a,b; operand 1 Gc_mR axes (R,a,m,b,n) with spec P(_,_,'y',_,'x') puts m on 'y', n on 'x'; operand 2 Gv_R read as (R,b,n,a,m) with spec P(_,_,'x',_,'y') puts n(axis2) on 'x', m(axis4) on 'y' — both operands agree, chi lands P(None,'y','x') matching l.149. But current production abolished this: same-sign FT + Hermitian property G_R(mu,nu)=G_{-R}(nu,mu)* makes both operands identically ordered/sharded ('Rambn,Rambn->Rmn', w_isdf.py:58-82,181) and chi lands P(None,'x','y'). The double conj (l.166-167) does still match production w_isdf.py:124, but the sharding/einsum layer models the pre-refactor kernel — stale, same root cause as the lockstep item.

item: sigma_kij.py:38 N_RS_STAGES placement between docstring and imports
evidence: Confirmed: module docstring ends l.37, N_RS_STAGES=4 at l.38, `from functools import partial` at l.39. Legal Python, lint-hostile, and the constant is dead (see dead verdict) — it exists purely to name the re/im x 2-psum_scatter structure described in the docstring l.22-36.

item: sigma_kij.py:124 inv_sqrt_nk = -1.0/sqrt(nk)
evidence: Bit-identical to production: gw/ppm_sigma.py:524 `inv_sqrt_nk = -1.0 / np.sqrt(float(nk_tot))` applied at the same site `sigma_k = _G_fftn(G_R * V_R * inv_sqrt_nk)` (ppm_sigma.py:554 vs model l.135). The minus is LORRAX's production Sigma-sign folded into the flat-k normalization (Sigma ~ -G.W in this real-space kernel), inherited name and all — a memory model must not change it since sign doesn't affect bytes but bit-for-bit mirroring is the kernel's stated contract. The misleading variable name originates in production, so fix it there first if at all.

item: sigma_kij.py:66-71 _T_rs_left byte-identical to _T_psi_Y
evidence: Declared collinearity resolved by the fit exactly as the docstring l.29-36 anticipates: sigma_kij__current__fit.json coefs psi_Y=2.0031, rs_left=0.0, rs_mn=0.0023 — NNLS lumped the pre-scatter partial into psi_Y's beta. Named primitive kept so a multi-node or n_b-asymmetric DoE could later attribute the RS live-set separately.

item: sigma_kij.py:18 'n_b represents m_coh == n_proj for this POC'
evidence: Documented POC simplification; SysDims carries only one n_b for this kernel (SYSTEM_DIMS l.84). Production _get_sigma_kij_kernel indeed has independent coherent/projection windows, and has ALSO drifted in signature since the model was written: it now takes (E_A, mask_A, t_node) with build_G_tau and donates W_q via donate_argnums=(8,) (ppm_sigma.py:530-556) vs the model's build_G(Gij=replicated) 6-arg form with no W_q donation (model l.127-136) — extrapolating this fit to asymmetric band counts or donation-sensitive peaks needs a refresh, but the equal-bands assumption itself is a stated, intentional scope limit.

item: vq_mu_chunk.py:63-68 sys.kgrid repurposed as 3-D FFT box
evidence: Triple inconsistency confirmed: (1) SYSTEM_DIMS l.48 lists 'fft_grid' but the code reads sys.kgrid l.64; (2) presets.py:173-185 set kgrid=(48,48,48) etc. with n_r=48**3 and never set fft_grid, relying on the overload; (3) the `if sys.kgrid else (1,1,1)` fallback is dead — SysDims.kgrid is a required non-empty tuple, always truthy. core.py:63-67 added SysDims.fft_grid/fft_shape explicitly to retire this pattern ('back-compat with kernels that repurpose kgrid as the FFT box'); this kernel should read sys.fft_shape. Note n_q plays no role here (single-q kernel), so unlike load_psi_rchunk there is no scenario needing both grids — a one-line fix.

item: slab_write.py:73-77 anti-DCE A_local + jnp.zeros_like
evidence: Currently effective: slab_write__current__fit.json beta[slab]=1.0000, intercept 0 — the slab is counted once, as intended. But add-broadcast-zero is squarely in XLA algebraic-simplifier territory (x+0 -> x), and with donate_argnums=(0,) a folded identity would alias input to output and could zero the measured live-set on a future XLA upgrade with no error raised. jax.lax.optimization_barrier is the supported idiom for exactly this 'keep the buffer live for accounting' purpose; swap it in.

item: slab_write.py:35-39 sys.n_k as q-count + 'sys.n_r or 1' fallback
evidence: The nq==nk Gamma-centered-MP assumption is uniform across the whole model, stated where it matters: fit_one_rchunk l.170 'kgrid axis -> varies n_k (and n_q, collinear at Gamma-centered q)' and _F_solve l.312-313 'q = k for Gamma-centered grids'; production flat-q arrays are likewise sized nk_tot (e.g. W_q (nq,mu,mu) with nq=prod(kgrid), ppm_sigma.py:512). LORRAX only runs Gamma-centered grids, so the shared implicit assumption is safe; the 'or 1' guard just protects the default when a preset passes chunk_r explicitly (all slab_write presets do). Could deserve one comment line, not a change.

item: zct_lr.py:59-78 two donating inner jits inside a donating outer jit
evidence: Deliberate and empirically validated. Production compute_ZCT_from_left_right_zchunk really executes two separate donating jits; the AOT model must lower ONE program, so it composes them under an outer jit with donate_argnums=(0,1) (inner donations are dropped on inlining, but within a single XLA program liveness reuse of the dead P_l/P_l_Rt buffers plus outer input donation reproduces the same live-set). Validation: zct_lr__current__fit.json beta[PrBr]=4.000000 exactly — the integer concurrency MEMORY_MODEL.md predicted (l.16-18), with residual ~2e-5. Drift hazard exists if production changes its split, but today the composite is calibrated and correct.

item: zct_lr.py:30-32 single primitive _T_PrBr absorbs all concurrency
evidence: All candidate buffers (P_l, P_r, both Rt intermediates, Z output) are byte-identical 16*nk*mu*Br/P shards — no DoE at fixed device count can separate them, so one primitive whose beta IS the concurrency count is the honest parameterization; fit returns beta=4.000 (integer) confirming the design. Same pattern as cct_lr.

item: solve_q.py:31-40 _T_Lrep vs _T_Lfull_rep near-duplicates
evidence: Deeper than the suspect's collinearity framing: the q_chunk knob feeds ONLY the _T_Lrep formula — build_callable/build_specs ignore it entirely (the callable is always the full-replication _solve_all_at_once, l.60-77), so the presets' q_chunk=4 point (presets.py:161) varies the feature while the measured peak is unchanged, and NNLS necessarily zeroes it: solve_q__current__fit.json coefs Lrep=0.0, Lfull_rep=2.381. _T_Lrep models production's q-chunked solve_zeta path (isdf_fitting.py:1429) that this kernel never builds — a dead feature in the current fit, harmless (coef pinned to 0) but misleading; either build the q-chunked variant or drop the primitive.

item: solve_q.py:64-71 L_q fully replicated on every device
evidence: Faithful to production: isdf_fitting.py:1434-1437 _solve_all_at_once does exactly `L_full_rep = with_sharding_constraint(L_q_sharded, L_batch_rep_shard)` then a replicated-panel shard_map cho_solve — same code shape as the model. Fit confirms the cost is real (beta[Lfull_rep]=2.38, i.e. ~2.4 full replicas live). Production already has the mitigations the suspect hypothesizes (q_chunk_size gather path isdf_fitting.py:1429, cusolvermp distributed solvers per solve_zeta docstring), so the model correctly flags a known hotspot rather than discovering a new one.

item: CarryT / ScanBody typing aliases (src/common/progress.py:35-36)
evidence: grep repo-wide: only the two definition lines. Not used in any annotation even within progress.py (scan_progress is annotated '-> Callable', body args untyped). The third alias PrintFn IS used (lines 97, 151), so these two are the leftovers.

item: TimingCollector.format (src/common/timing.py:177-179)
evidence: grep '(timing|collector|_timing|COLLECTOR)\.format(' across src/tests/tools/scripts: zero hits. The only ways to reach a collector instance are get_collector()/_GLOBAL_COLLECTOR, and the sole get_collector caller (tests/archive/test_chunked_wfn_loading.py:971) calls .report(), not .format(). The module-level convenience functions (timing.py:189-208) wrap reset/section/timed/report but not format.

item: timing.get_collector (src/common/timing.py:185-186)
evidence: Single external reference: tests/archive/test_chunked_wfn_loading.py:971 (an archived test; note tests/archive also contains test_kmeans.py which is un-importable, so the archive is stale). All production code uses the module-level wrappers timing.section/timed/report/reset instead.

item: _ensure_compilation_cache module alias (src/common/jax_compile_cache.py:120)
evidence: Zero imports of the alias: grep shows every caller of the NAME '_ensure_compilation_cache' (ppm_sigma.py:520/573/622, w_isdf.py:584/626/668) resolves to the separately defined wrapper at src/gw/w_isdf.py:38-40, which itself calls the public ensure_jax_compile_cache. All other modules (kmeans_cli.py:193, bse_feast.py:1160, gw_driver_helpers.py:171, run_sternheimer.py:1506, run_nscf.py:505) import the public name directly. src/ffi/phdf5/ARCHITECTURE.md:240 references the w_isdf one historically. The line-120 alias has no consumers.

item: gpu_utils.get_gpu_memory_nvidia_smi, get_cpu_memory_total (src/common/gpu_utils.py:31,56)
evidence: OVERTURNED: both have live internal callers inside gpu_utils.py — get_gpu_memory_nvidia_smi at lines 105 (get_device_memory_gb fallback) and 152 (get_device_memory_info fallback); get_cpu_memory_total at lines 112 and 169 (CPU-backend paths). Zero EXTERNAL callers (production imports are only get_device_memory_gb at pivoted_cholesky.py:923/gw_config.py:864 and get_device_memory_info at gw_config.py:891/gw_init.py:901), so the correct action is demoting them from __all__ (line 186-187) to _private, not deletion.

item: common/__init__.py get_enk_bandrange re-export (line 2)
evidence: All 8 call sites import from the submodule, never the facade: gw_jax.py:25, htransform.py:24-25, ppm_pipeline.py:26, sc_iteration.py:152, gw_init.py:1156+1192 — all 'from common.load_wfns import get_enk_bandrange'. grep 'from common import' repo-wide shows the facade is used only for Meta, RYD_TO_EV, and submodules (symmetry_maps, timing, jax_profile, minimax); no 'common.get_enk_bandrange' attribute access anywhere.

item: common/__init__.py cholesky_2d re-exports, all 5 (lines 6-12)
evidence: grep for 'from common import' with any of cholesky_2d_single/cholesky_2d_batched/cholesky_solve_2d/dense_to_tiles/tiles_to_dense and for 'common.<name>' attribute access: zero hits. The only production importer is src/common/isdf_fitting.py:129-130 via 'from .cholesky_2d import ...'; the only other reference is a docstring usage example inside cholesky_2d.py:16 that also uses the submodule path.

item: meta.py:94 rank_topo device==int comparison
evidence: np.asarray(jax.devices()) yields an object array of Device; '== rank' (an int from jax.process_index()) does elementwise Device.__eq__(int) which is False for every element, so np.where returns an empty index tuple — and even conceptually it conflates process index with device identity. The result is never read (see dead verdict above), so it is unreachable-wrong code, not a live bug. Delete the line.

item: meta.py:147 from_system hardwires nfreq=1 positionally
evidence: grep '\.nfreq' repo-wide: the only hits are file_io/epsreader.py's own unrelated attribute (lines 40-41,158). No code reads meta.nfreq, so the field (meta.py:26) is vestigial and the bare positional literal '1' at line 147 is opaque (a reader must count 12 positional args to identify it). Consistent with frequency handling living in minimax/quadrature code (build_static_quadrature in gw_jax.py:237). Drop the field or at minimum pass it as a keyword.

item: meta.py:36-39 n_rmu_jax legacy field + sys_dim monkey-patch (gw_init.py:786,793)
evidence: Verified both halves. (a) n_rmu_jax: meta.py:36-39 says 'kept for back-compat with callers that still reference it' but grep finds ZERO readers; gw_init.py:774-786 dutifully recomputes and replaces it for the bispinor transverse channel — maintenance of a write-only field. (b) sys_dim: set as a non-dataclass instance attr at gw_jax.py:150 (meta.sys_dim = config.sys_dim); dataclasses.replace() rebuilds via __init__ so non-field attrs are dropped, hence the manual re-copy at gw_init.py:793 with an explanatory comment. sys_dim is read widely (gw/coulomb/base.py:126, gw/coulomb/__init__.py:12, isdf_fitting.py:2255+2264, gw_init.py:1021/1041/1103) and vcoul.py:191 even uses defensive getattr(meta,'sys_dim',None). Promoting sys_dim to a real field with a default and deleting n_rmu_jax/nbnd_jax/n_rtot_jax is the right consolidation; suspect's hypothesis confirmed.

item: timing.py:141-153 min_percent skips a row but recurses children at unchanged depth
evidence: The feared orphan rows are mathematically unreachable. percent = node.inclusive/total (line 145) where total is the fixed sum over root children (line 133). A child's inclusive wall time is strictly contained in its parent's inclusive time (TimingSection.__exit__ adds the child's full inclusive to the parent's elapsed window, timing.py:55-62; each TimingNode lives under exactly one parent in per-parent OrderedDicts, timing.py:20-25). Therefore child% <= parent%, so every descendant of a suppressed node is also < min_percent and suppressed — the recursion after the skip can never print anything. The code shape is misleading but the output can never show a child of a hidden parent. Not the 'keep deep hot spots visible' feature the reader hypothesized; just an unreachable branch.

item: gw_jax.py:234 'Do NOT use _chi_sec.watch' — watch buffer-retention footgun
evidence: Confirmed and traced to a concrete defect in timing.py: TimingSection.__exit__ (lines 51-54) invokes watchers but never clears self._watchers, so the bound block_until_ready methods — each of which pins the underlying jax.Array — stay alive as long as the section object is referenced (e.g. via 'with ... as sec'), which at the gw_jax call site would block XLA buffer donation of chi0_q into the W-solve (comment at gw_jax.py:224-236 is accurate). watch IS legitimately used elsewhere (psp/get_DFT_mtxels.py:421,603,658,666,770,870; bse/test_bse.py:206,261,283). Fix: 'self._watchers = []' at the end of __exit__ plus a docstring note on the watch mechanism in timing.py; today the constraint is documented only at one call site.

item: jax_compile_cache.py:76-80 process-global warnings.filterwarnings
evidence: The filter is message-regex-targeted ('Error reading persistent compilation cache entry .*', category=UserWarning) so its blast radius is that one JAX warning; the docstring (lines 58-61) explicitly frames it as defense-in-depth for pre-existing shared caches from older versions of the helper, and the per-rank cache partitioning (lines 46-56, np{N}/rank{N} dirs) is the primary fix that makes the warning structurally rare. Global side effect is inherent to the warnings module; deliberate and documented.

item: jax_compile_cache.py:90-115 nested bare except-Exception ladders
evidence: Each ladder has a coherent fallback: process_count/index probe -> assume single-rank (94-96); mkdir failure -> run uncached (101-103); modern jax.config knob -> legacy set_cache_dir -> give up (109-115). The docstring (lines 37-44 and 17-18) shows the cache is a pure optimization worth ~3-4 s on warm runs, so silent no-op costs only compile time, never correctness. A one-line rank-0 log on the silent paths would be a nice-to-have, but the best-effort design is clearly deliberate.

item: jax_compile_cache ISDF_ env-var prefix
evidence: Explicitly documented at jax_compile_cache.py:20-22: 'The env-var naming is legacy — historically this was for ISDF kernels only, now it caches the whole run. Left as-is for backward compat with existing user shell aliases and run scripts.' The sibling knob ISDF_JAX_PROFILE_DIR (jax_profile.py:12) and gw_config's ISDF_CHUNK_TARGET_UTILIZATION / ISDF_ZCT_STAGE_CAP_* (gw_config.py:872,880-881) show ISDF_ is the de facto project-wide env prefix, not an isolated relic.

item: jax_profile.py:26-32 _warned_trace_failure gates all future messages
evidence: Verified: _log_once has exactly one call site (line 50, profiler.trace construction failure inside trace_section), so the over-broad flag name/scope has no observable consequence today. Would only become a bug if a second message type were added without renaming the flag. Marked '# pragma: no cover - extremely rare'.

item: gpu_utils.py:97 vs 149 budget policy: 0.9*bytes_limit vs 0.9*bytes_available
evidence: Confirmed inconsistency with a live failure path. get_device_memory_gb's docstring (lines 80-83) states the policy switched to bytes_limit precisely because bytes_available 'can vary by rank due to JIT timing'; get_device_memory_info:149 still computes budget_gb = 0.90*bytes_available. Consumer gw_init.py:899-904 clamps the V_q budget via min(budget_gb, info['budget_gb']) and feeds it as budget_bytes into compute_all_V_q -> _choose_v_q_chunks (v_q_tile.py:1140-1148), which runs independently on every rank with no broadcast/multihost sync of the choice — so rank-varying bytes_in_use can yield different q_chunk/mu_chunk per rank in an SPMD program with collectives (hang/shape-mismatch risk on multi-host runs). Corroborating stale doc: docs/architecture/memory-model.md:44 still documents get_device_memory_gb as 0.9*bytes_available, i.e. the info-dict side was never updated when the policy switched. Fix: make info['budget_gb'] mirror the bytes_limit policy (or expose both explicitly).

item: gpu_utils.py:41 jnp.zeros(1).block_until_ready() to force backend init
evidence: devices()[0].memory_stats() returns nothing useful before the backend (and, under XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_PREALLOCATE=true, the BFC pool) exists; bytes_limit — the very quantity the caller wants — is the pool size, so triggering pool creation is the point, not an accident. The 'hidden side effect' is real but benign in context: production callers (gw_config.py:864 during config resolution, gw_init.py:901 mid-pipeline) run after jax.distributed.initialize/mesh setup when the backend is initialized anyway. A comment on line 41 would help; behavior is correct.

item: gpu_utils magic constants (0.90 x6, 4.0 GB, 8.0 GB)
evidence: Verified sites: 0.90 safety factor at lines 97, 107, 149, 156, 165, 172 (six, not four); 4.0 GB fallback at lines 109, 117 and as default available/budget at 141-142; 8.0 GB default total at line 140. All express one policy ('budget 90% of detected memory, assume a small GPU when blind') and should be module constants (_SAFETY_FRAC, _FALLBACK_BUDGET_GB, _FALLBACK_TOTAL_GB). Pure cosmetics, no behavioral divergence found among the 0.90 sites beyond the bytes_limit/bytes_available issue reported separately.

item: tests/archive/test_kmeans.py:5 'from common.gpu_utils import cp'
evidence: Confirmed: gpu_utils.py contains no 'cp' or cupy symbol; repo-wide grep for 'cupy' in src/ finds nothing except the phantom weighted_kmeans_cupy referenced at tests/archive/test_kmeans.py:23 which also no longer exists. The test is un-importable, but it lives in tests/archive/ (not collected as a supported suite; sibling archive tests also construct legacy Meta signatures). Evidence of the excised cupy path; delete the archived file rather than fix it.

item: common/__init__.py:2,6 eager h5py/JAX-heavy imports for Meta-only callers
evidence: Confirmed weight and confirmed trim path. Line 2 pulls load_wfns (h5py + timing + FFI-adjacent loaders) and lines 6-12 pull cholesky_2d (JAX kernels) into every 'import common', including the ~20 modules that do 'from common import symmetry_maps' or 'from common import Meta' only. Both heavy re-exports are confirmed-dead facade paths (see dead verdicts), so they can be deleted outright. The facade cannot be removed entirely: 'from common import Meta, RYD_TO_EV' is live (gw_jax.py:60, vcoul.py:13, kin_ion_io.py:27, w_isdf.py:19, gw/coulomb/*.py, htransform.py:23, sigma_x_bispinor.py:186). Trim __init__.py to 'from .meta import Meta' + 'from .units import RYD_TO_EV, EV_TO_RYD' (or drop EV_TO_RYD too, per its dead verdict).

item: src/file_io/zeta_loader.py — ZetaLoader (production use)
evidence: Instantiated only in tests (tests/test_zeta_loader.py:61+, tests/test_compute_all_V_q_g_flat.py:142,183,217, tests/test_compute_V_q_bispinor_g_flat.py:235). Enumerated all call mechanisms: production gw_init.py:1004-1012 and 1092-1095 construct ZetaReader; console scripts (pyproject.toml:33-35) are gw.gw_jax:main and centroid.kmeans_cli:main, neither imports it; no hits in tools/, scripts/, or the sandbox skills/ and runs/ trees; the only string-ish dispatch is v_q_g_flat.py:242 getattr(zeta_loader,'load') which fires only on whatever object is passed in — production passes ZetaReader (which has no .load). v_q_g_flat.py:235 comment itself labels it 'ZetaLoader (test bench) / ZetaReader (production)'. Exported from src/file_io/__init__.py:15 but no non-test importer exists.

item: src/file_io/zeta_loader.py:221 — ZetaLoader.slab_io property
evidence: Repo-wide grep for '.slab_io' attribute access: only hits are ZetaReader's property in tests/test_zeta_reader.py:108-122 (a different class) and config.backend.slab_io (gw_config/gw_init/gw_jax, unrelated enum field). Not even tests/test_zeta_loader.py touches ld.slab_io. Zero references of any kind to ZetaLoader's property; and since ZetaLoader itself is test-only, no indirect path exists either.

item: src/file_io/zeta_loader.py:92-99,193 — backend kwarg + _auto_pick_backend
evidence: _auto_pick_backend does execute on every __init__ (line 93), but its result is functionally inert: stored in self.backend which is never read again anywhere (grep for reads of a loader's .backend in src/tests/tools/scripts: zero hits), and SlabIO is constructed at lines 189-190 WITHOUT the backend argument — the loader's own comment at 184-188 admits 'backend on ZetaLoader is currently advisory'. The only observable effect is the ValueError at 96-98 (backend='phdf5' with mesh=None), which no caller can reach since all instantiations are tests that pass mesh and use the default backend='auto'. Contrast ZetaReader, which does forward backend to SlabIO (zeta_reader.py:165-166).

item: src/file_io/zeta_reader.py:198 — read_zeta_r_slab (public API)
evidence: Zero direct external callers, but it is live legacy-compat code, not dead: read_zeta_G_slab calls it at zeta_reader.py:333 on the r_space branch, and that branch is production-reachable — compute_all_V_q (compute_vcoul.py:899 'if getattr(zeta_io, "zeta_layout", None) == "G_flat"') dispatches on the FILE's layout, and legacy files lacking the zeta_layout field default to 'r_space' (isdf_header.py:193-194), routing to v_q_tile → read_zeta_G_slab (v_q_tile.py:1303,1369) → read_zeta_r_slab. The first-pass reader's premise ('dead if the writer is G-flat-only') conflates writer behavior with reader reachability: the current writer indeed only emits G_flat (isdf_fitting.py:2317), but the dispatcher docstring (compute_vcoul.py:882-886) explicitly retains the path 'Reachable only for legacy r-space ζ files'. Delete-candidate only after a decision to drop legacy-file support.

item: src/file_io/zeta_reader.py:183 — ZetaReader.slab_io property
evidence: Only accesses are tests/test_zeta_reader.py:108 (assert zr.slab_io is not None), :110 (is None after close), :122 (hasattr read_slab). No src/, tools/, scripts/, skills/, or runs/ consumer; production code that needs raw slab reads constructs SlabIO directly (e.g. gw_init.py:1048-1054 legacy bispinor branch). The docstring's stated purpose ('callers still migrating to G-flat') has no remaining callers.

item: src/file_io/isdf_header.py — zeta_is_done as a restart guard
evidence: The flag is written in production (isdf_fitting.py:2709-2716 calls mark_zeta_done after the global sync) and surfaced as an attribute by both readers (zeta_reader.py:124, zeta_loader.py:159), but nothing outside tests ever branches on it: grep 'zeta_is_done' across src/ hits only file_io attribute plumbing + the writer flip. The one place a restart guard would live — gw_init.py:934-936 bispinor_ready — gates only on os.path.exists(zeta_T_paths), never on the flag. Only tests read/assert it (tests/test_zeta_loader.py:67-94, test fixtures in test_per_q_sphere.py:185 etc.). The docstring claims 'Restart paths check this flag' (isdf_header.py:29-30, echoed at zeta_loader.py:20) describe a consumer that does not exist yet; the guard is aspirational.

item: src/file_io/zeta_loader.py:291-292 — comment 'the current writer always produces G-flat on disk'
evidence: The suspected contradiction is refuted: the statement is factually correct — the sole ζ writer hardcodes zeta_layout='G_flat' (isdf_fitting.py:2317, with IsdfHeader.build validation requiring the sphere metadata at 2320-2328). The isdf_header 'r_space' default applies only to legacy files lacking the field (isdf_header.py:193-194), and the r-space branch below zeta_loader.py:318 is deliberate legacy-file support — the same design stated at compute_vcoul.py:884-886 ('Reachable only for legacy r-space ζ files; not exercised by the current writer'). Not a stale mid-migration comment; it accurately describes writer-vs-legacy-reader asymmetry.

item: src/file_io/zeta_loader.py:444 — hard-coded cross-ref 'isdf_fitting.py:1689'
evidence: Confirmed stale: isdf_fitting.py:1689 today is kernel-factory plumbing ('# Attach the un-fused phases...' / '_kernel.z_q_phase = z_q_phase'), nothing to do with on-disk row order. The actual writer row-order logic (IBZ slice Z_q[q_irr_idx_j] in sym.q_irr_full_idx order) now lives around isdf_fitting.py:1654-1668 (solve_phase) with the G-flat write path near 2240-2340/2501-2660. Comment-only defect; the invariant it asserts (rows stored in q_irr_full_idx order) still holds.

item: src/file_io/zeta_loader.py:433-440 — NotImplementedError when full-BZ q needs TR sym
evidence: This is the loud-guard pattern from the TRS-blind-sym-bug fix. SymMaps' q tables are TRS-augmented (sym_idx_q ∈ [0, 2·ntran), second half = -sym_mats_k — see compute_vcoul.py:944-951 'extend_trs=True' commentary and reports/trs_sym_audit_2026-05-14), while ZetaLoader's r/μ permutation unfold implements only the spatial action ζ_full[q,r,μ]=ζ_ibz[i(q), r_perm[s,r], inv_mu[s,μ]] (lines 469-517); TR additionally requires ζ→ζ* which is unwired. The historical bug was exactly a silent OOB clip when TRS-augmented indices hit spatial-only tables (max 13.85 eV V_q error on MoS2); raising loudly with a workaround message is the correct Phase-1 behavior, not a defect. Exposure is limited further by ZetaLoader being test-only.

item: src/file_io/zeta_loader.py:400-409 — _ensure_sym passes loader itself into SymMaps as fake WFNReader
evidence: Verified the duck-type contract holds today: SymMaps.__init__ (symmetry_maps.py:729-843+) consumes wfn.ntran, sym_matrices, translations, nkpts, kpoints, kgrid, shift (get_spinor_rotations uses no additional wfn attrs in its body), and ZetaLoader mirrors the complete MfHeader surface 1:1 at lines 117-151 — the mirror is the documented design ('same names ZetaReader exposes', zeta_reader.py docstring lines 3-8: drop-in wherever a WFNReader was used). ZetaReader carries the identical mirror (zeta_reader.py:85-119). The fragility claim is fair in principle but drift would require deleting a mirrored MfHeader field; note wfn_loader.py:297 uses a _sym_wfn_stub() for the same purpose, so there are already two idioms — unification per the no-parallel-routines rule would be the eventual cleanup.

item: src/file_io/zeta_reader.py:384 — _disk_to_G_cache keyed on id(mesh_xy)/id(sphere_idx)
evidence: The headline hazard (GC id-reuse serving a stale compiled fn with the wrong baked-in sphere) is REFUTED by closure analysis: the cached _f closes over sphere_idx (used at line 415 jnp.take(box, sphere_idx, ...)) and over mesh_xy via NamedSharding/make_sharded_fftn_3d (lines 389-396), so every live cache entry pins its keyed objects — CPython cannot recycle id(x) while x is referenced, hence a colliding id from a different object is impossible while the entry exists. Remaining true defects are minor: (a) entries are never evicted, each pinning device arrays + a compiled executable; (b) a caller passing a fresh numpy-derived sphere per call would miss every time and recompile. Production is safe on both counts: compute_vcoul.py:259-266 builds sphere_idx ONCE as a persistent jnp int32 array (jnp.asarray is then identity, id stable), and mesh_xy is process-lifetime.

item: src/file_io/zeta_reader.py:322 — G-flat branch validates caller sphere by size only
evidence: Confirmed latent wrong-data hazard by index enumeration: on-disk G-axis element j at row q is the j-th smallest flat-FFT index in the q-DEPENDENT sphere {G:|q+G|²≤zeta_cutoff} (coulomb_sphere.py:229-239, per-q ascending sort), whereas the caller's sphere_idx defines one q-INDEPENDENT flat-index list (compute_vcoul.py:258-266). If n_G_sph == n_G_sph_disk (e.g. shared-sphere size coincidentally equals ngkmax) the raw slab is returned with NO gather and no error — element j is then the wrong G for the caller at essentially every q. The guard should raise on 'sphere_jx is not None' rather than on size mismatch. Currently unreachable in production: the dispatcher routes G-flat files to v_q_g_flat which passes sphere_idx=None (v_q_g_flat.py:259), and v_q_tile (which passes non-None sphere_idx at 1303/1369) only receives r-space files via compute_all_V_q; but any direct compute_V_q_tile call on a G-flat reader with coincident sizes silently corrupts V_q.

item: src/file_io/zeta_reader.py:299-301 — qvec_batch_frac required but ignored on G-flat-disk branch
evidence: Documented API asymmetry, deliberately kept so one signature serves both on-disk layouts: on G-flat disk the per-q phase exp(-2πi q·r) was applied by the writer before the FFT (comment block zeta_reader.py:299-310; writer threads sphere/phase through accumulate_rchunk_to_gflat, isdf_fitting.py:2244-2247), so there is nothing to apply at read time; on r-space disk the same argument is load-bearing (fed to _do_disk_to_G/apply_bloch_phase, lines 339-343). The single production G-flat caller knowingly passes zeros (v_q_g_flat.py:258 qvec_batch_frac=jnp.zeros((n_q,3))). Making it Optional=None would be marginally cleaner but the current form is an acknowledged convention, not drift.

item: src/file_io/zeta_reader.py:41 — docstring cites isdf_fitting.py:1656 for open-handle motivation
evidence: Confirmed stale: isdf_fitting.py:1656 today is a comment inside solve_phase about IBZ-only slicing ('at IBZ rows (via symmetry_maps.slice_q_full_to_ibz ...') — nothing about FFI open/close amortization. The underlying claim (SlabIO handle held open across reads to amortize FFI open cost) remains true of the code (zeta_reader.py:165-171), only the line citation rotted. Same failure mode as the zeta_loader.py:444 citation; both argue for citing function names, not line numbers.

item: src/file_io/isdf_header.py:264 — ngk_per_q stored under HDF5 dataset name 'ngk'
evidence: Deliberate WFN.h5-format mirroring, not drift: the module docstring (isdf_header.py:34-39) states the G-flat metadata block 'mirror[s] the WFN.h5 wfns group layout', where the per-k sphere-size dataset is named ngk; here it is the per-q analog, stored in the separate 'isdf_header' HDF5 group so there is no actual name collision with mf_header's per-k ngk (different group paths). The reader immediately renames it to the unambiguous field ngk_per_q (read at line 198, dataclass field at 86), and both readers expose it as .ngk_per_q alongside the mf .ngk (zeta_reader.py:95/131, zeta_loader.py:127/164). The grep-confusion hazard is real but the on-disk name is a chosen convention with an in-memory disambiguation.

item: src/file_io/isdf_header.py:13-15 vs :56 — density enum docstring mismatch (mu_L=<int>)
evidence: The dataclass comment at line 56 ('scalar' | 'current' | 'mu_L=<int>' | 'unknown') is the stale one. Canonical enum is {scalar, current, unknown}: centroid_io.py:22 defines DensityLabel = Literal['scalar','current','unknown'] and coerces anything else to 'unknown' (line 50); the only ζ writer emits exactly _density_label = 'scalar' if vertex_mu_L==0 else 'current' (isdf_fitting.py:2312). Repo-wide grep finds no code writing or parsing a 'mu_L=<int>' density string (all mu_L= hits are unrelated kwargs like n_rmu_L=). Doc-only fix: delete 'mu_L=<int>' from line 56.

item: src/file_io/isdf_header.py:37/85 — pad sentinel (-nx/2,-ny/2,-nz/2) for gvec_components
evidence: Deliberate and self-consistent; consumers do NOT need to special-case it. Producer: coulomb_sphere.py:210-227 fills pad slots with flat-FFT index at the Nyquist corner (nx//2, ny//2, nz//2), whose fftfreq Miller triple is (-nx/2,-ny/2,-nz/2) for even grids — the isdf_header docstring quotes the Miller form, coulomb_sphere the flat-index form; same G by construction ('taken from the fftfreq tables so a downstream reader ... gets the same triple'). Safety enumeration: (a) pad COEFF slots are zero by writer construction (isdf_header.py:87-89 'zeta_q_G[q,:,ngk[q]:] is zero'), so in the V_q contraction v(q+G_sentinel)·0 = 0 — e.g. v_q_g_flat._bare_v_per_G computes v at pad Millers harmlessly (v_q_g_flat.py:521-540 area); (b) no false G=0 match: sphere_idx_padded[q,0]==0 is guaranteed (coulomb_sphere.py:231-235) and the Nyquist sentinel is never (0,0,0), plus compute_per_q_bare_coulomb_components hard-errors if G=0 is missing from any q-sphere (coulomb_sphere.py:199-204).

item: src/file_io/isdf_header.py — zeta_is_done dataclass default True vs build() default False
evidence: Both defaults are explicitly documented with their rationale: dataclass True 'for in-memory construction so synthetic test headers don't need to set it' (isdf_header.py:60-63); build() False 'for the writer path — the writer flips it to True via mark_zeta_done after the final chunk' (:123-125). The sole production writer goes through IsdfHeader.build (isdf_fitting.py:2328) and then mark_zeta_done (:2716), so the intended False→True lifecycle is followed. The footgun (a future writer constructing IsdfHeader(...) directly would stamp done=True before data lands) is real but currently consequence-free, because no production code branches on the flag at all (see the zeta_is_done dead-guard verdict). Worth revisiting only when a restart consumer is actually wired.

item: src/ffi/phdf5/__init__.py — advertised slab API (re-exported write_sharded_slab / read_sharded_slab)
evidence: Enumerated all mechanisms: repo-wide grep for write_sharded_slab|read_sharded_slab hits only src/ffi/phdf5/{__init__,write,read}.py plus prose in src/ffi/phdf5/ARCHITECTURE.md:363-366, src/ffi/AGENTS.md:62, docs/dev/plans/unified_slab_io.md, and two C++ comments (cpp/context.cc:344,396). No getattr/import_module dispatch to them; pyproject.toml [project.scripts] only exposes gw.gw_jax:main and centroid.kmeans_cli:main; nothing in tests/, tools/, scripts/, nor in /pscratch/sd/j/jackm/lorrax_sandbox/skills or runs/*.sh (those only set env paths / invoke gw_jax). Real consumers bypass __init__: src/file_io/_slab_io_ffi.py:286,311,337 imports ffi_read_call/ffi_write_call/open_file/close_file from submodules; src/file_io/wfn_loader.py:569,624,663 imports open_file and read_kchunk_union_sharded. The re-exports of open_file/close_file in __init__.py:24 ARE alive (wfn_loader.py:249,286 and _slab_io_ffi.py:337 import via the package); only the two slab re-exports are dead.

item: src/ffi/phdf5/__init__.py:3-17 — docstring documents write/read_sharded_slab as THE public API
evidence: Stale first-gen surface, confirmed superseded: write.py:1-11 docstring itself says 'Preferred public entry point ... is file_io.slab_io'; read.py:28-31 says the same; docs/dev/plans/unified_slab_io.md:228-229 explicitly plans that 'the existing 2-D write_sharded_slab / read_sharded_slab either get deprecated in favour of the N-D' path. The docstring, ARCHITECTURE.md:363-366 and src/ffi/AGENTS.md:62 all still advertise the dead names. Doc rot, not a behavior hazard.

item: src/ffi/phdf5/context.py — validate_mesh_2d (in __all__)
evidence: The FUNCTION is load-bearing on a hot live path: open_file calls it at context.py:63, and open_file is invoked from wfn_loader.py:624 and _slab_io_ffi.py:362 in every multi-process phdf5 run (production tracebacks in runs/.../gw_3264.out go through context.py:71, two lines below). It is also imported by write.py:25 and read.py:46 (used at write.py:92, read.py:204, though those two call sites are inside the dead slab wrappers). What has zero external consumers is only its advertisement in __all__ at context.py:23 — no file outside src/ffi/phdf5/ ever imports validate_mesh_2d. Removing the function would break open_file; removing it from __all__ alone is safe.

item: src/ffi/phdf5/context.py:68-70 — open_file cache hit ignores mode
evidence: Confirmed by reading context.py:68-77: `if path in _FILE_CTXS: return _FILE_CTXS[path]` runs before _MODE_FLAGS[mode] is ever consulted, so open_file(p,mode='r') after open_file(p,mode='w') silently returns the truncate/RDWR handle (and worse, 'r'-then-'w' returns a RDONLY handle whose subsequent H5Dwrite fails far from the cause; C++ mode semantics at cpp/context.cc:95-109). Current callers don't collide: wfn_loader.py:624 opens WFN files 'r' only; _slab_io_ffi.py:362 opens output files 'w'/'a' and _FfiBackend.close() removes the entry via close_file (context.py:87). So no live bug today, but the fix is one line (key cache on (path, mode) or raise on mode mismatch at a cache hit) and the failure mode is silent — classic latent trap for a write-then-read refactor.

item: src/ffi/phdf5/context.py:36 — _MODE_FLAGS magic ints across ctypes boundary
evidence: Verified the convention holds on all three layers, per value: Python context.py:36 {'w':0,'a':1,'r':2}; ffi_loader.py:284 docstring '0 = truncate (w), 1 = append-or-create (a), 2 = read-only (r)'; C++ cpp/context.cc:95-109 switch: case 0 → H5Fcreate(H5F_ACC_TRUNC), case 1 → H5Fopen(H5F_ACC_RDWR) with H5F_ACC_EXCL create fallback (context.cc:104-106), case 2 → H5Fopen(H5F_ACC_RDONLY). No shared header, but the mapping is documented identically at both ends of the ctypes call (lrx_phdf5_open argtypes at ffi_loader.py:137-145). Consistent-by-convention with duplicated comments — the normal pattern for this codebase's ctypes FFI; not a bug.

item: src/ffi/phdf5/context.py:97-110 — atexit close swallows all exceptions
evidence: Confirmed best-effort by design: _atexit_close_all docstring (context.py:98-99) says 'catches forgotten close_file calls', get_lib failure returns early (101-103), per-file close wrapped in except-pass (106-109). The MPI-finalized worry is mostly moot: the C++ close path deliberately never calls MPI_Finalize (cpp/context.cc:467-470), so MPI is still alive during Python atexit; note however the comment at cpp/context.cc:58-60 claims close_file DOES finalize via a ref count — that comment is stale and contradicts context.cc:467-470 (actual code frees the comm only, context.cc:463-465). The sharper unswallowable hazard is that H5Fclose on a parallel file is collective, so a rank whose peers died can hang in atexit rather than raise — but for a last-ditch leak catcher, swallow-and-continue is the right call. Only the contradictory C++ comment deserves cleanup.

item: src/ffi/phdf5/write.py — write_sharded_slab
evidence: Same exhaustive enumeration as the __init__ finding: zero callers anywhere (src, tests, tools, scripts, docs-as-code, pyproject scripts, sandbox skills/ and runs/). Its only referents are its own def (write.py:73), the __init__.py:25 re-export, and prose. The live write path is ffi_write_call (write.py:34), consumed by _slab_io_ffi._get_write_sm (src/file_io/_slab_io_ffi.py:311) — so deleting write_sharded_slab must keep ffi_write_call. Its own module docstring (write.py:3-7) already defers to file_io.slab_io.

item: src/ffi/phdf5/write.py:89-91 — NotImplementedError for ndim!=2 though C++ handler is N-D
evidence: Both halves of the claim check out: the guard at write.py:89-91 hard-limits the wrapper to 2-D, while the docstring at write.py:8-10 and the C++ WriteDispatch (cpp/write_ffi.cc:292-297, (ndim,)-shaped offset/valid_shape buffers; rank-mismatch check at 325-327) are genuinely N-D — the wrapper just hardcodes mesh_shape=(p,q), axis_flat=(0,1) (write.py:127-129). N-D generality is reached in production via file_io._slab_io_ffi (its own header, _slab_io_ffi.py:1). Since write_sharded_slab is confirmed-dead, this restriction constrains nothing; it dies with the function.

item: src/ffi/phdf5/write.py:46-58 — three-shape padding contract (physical shard / valid_shape / global_shape), comment-only
evidence: Read the full contract. Per-element: A_local is the equal-block physical shard (physical must divide mesh, write.py:102-104); valid_shape is the logical prefix, checked valid<=physical (105-108) and valid<=dataset global (109-112); global_shape sizes the dataset via phdf5_ensure_dataset (115-117). The C++ side enforces the clip it promises: hyperslab out-of-bounds check (cpp/write_ffi.cc:215), H5Sselect_none for empty intersections (238), and mem-space sub-selection of the valid region (259). Rationale for runtime buffers vs attrs is measured and documented (write.py:47-53: one compile per (ds, ndim, dtype, sharding), ~400 ms per avoided recompile, reports/zeta_offset_runtime_2026-04-19). This is deliberate pad-to-divisible sharding; 'comment-only' is fair but the checks that matter are runtime-enforced on both sides.

item: src/ffi/phdf5/write.py:60-61 — (1,) int32 token + has_side_effect=True; reads have no token
evidence: Confirmed asymmetry: write uses has_side_effect=True with a token_out result (write.py:60-61; cpp/write_ffi.cc:292-297 returns ffi::Future — async-write design documented at write_ffi.cc:22-36); ffi_read_call at read.py:117 passes no has_side_effect. Reads are data-producing, so consumers are ordered by data dependence and there is no XLA-level read-after-write ordering to lose WITHIN a program; across dispatches, ordering is engineered in Python: _FfiBackend's single FIFO dispatch worker with block_until_ready per task (_slab_io_ffi.py:370-385 comment block) plus queue drain before close. So read-after-write correctness genuinely rests on caller sequencing, exactly as the reader hypothesized — but that is the designed contract (writes and reads also target different files/phases in practice: WFN read vs zeta/eps write). Not a bug; worth one sentence in ARCHITECTURE.md.

item: src/ffi/phdf5/read.py — read_sharded_slab
evidence: Zero callers under every mechanism enumerated (imports, dynamic dispatch, entry points, tests/tools/scripts, sandbox skills/runs). Referents: def at read.py:190, __init__.py:26 re-export, __all__ read.py:53, prose. Live read paths are ffi_read_call (used by _slab_io_ffi.py:286 via _get_read_sm) and read_kchunk_union_sharded (wfn_loader.py:663). Note read.py:204 is one of only two live-looking call sites of validate_mesh_2d in this file — deleting read_sharded_slab removes it, which is fine since open_file retains the function.

item: src/ffi/phdf5/read.py — read_kchunk_sharded
evidence: grep for read_kchunk_sharded (the union variant's name read_kchunk_union_sharded does NOT contain this substring, so no false negatives) hits only read.py (def at :232, __all__ :54) and prose. The sole production kchunk consumer is the union variant: wfn_loader.py:663 'from ffi.phdf5.read import read_kchunk_union_sharded'. No tests, no scripts, no dynamic dispatch, nothing in sandbox skills/runs. Its documented niche (overlapping ngkmax windows, read.py:9-14 and cpp/read_ffi.cc:297-307) was made unnecessary by the clamped-counts approach the union caller uses (tests/test_wfn_loader_phdf5_clamp.py exercises _build_phdf5_clamped_counts).
