item: charge_density.py:334-355 — compute_grad_rho_sq core de-aliasing workaround
evidence: Stronger than 'correct code, misleading trail' — the workaround is an exact NO-OP. Proof: rho_core_gridded = real(ifftn(rhog_core)) (line 353); rhog_core is Hermitian-symmetric (real radial ρ_core, real F_l table values, conjugate-symmetric structure phases e^{-2πiG·τ}, built at lines 262-271), so ifftn(rhog_core) is real and fftn(rho_core_gridded) == rhog_core exactly (discrete fftn∘ifftn is always the identity — grid resolution affects the continuous↔discrete mapping, not the round trip, contrary to the comment at lines 343-345). Therefore rho_G = fftn(rho_r - rho_core_gridded) + rhog_core ≡ fftn(rho_r), identical to the naive else-branch (line 357). The intended QE-gradcorr de-aliasing never happens; the same dead plumbing is repeated in _build_V_xc_gga (483-486). Silent divergence between stated intent and effect → suspected-bug (in a dead module; its only caller is the broken manual test).

item: charge_density.py:242 — NLCC gate str(cc) != 'UpfLogical.T'
evidence: UpfLogical enum (psp/upf/upf_model_2_0_1/qe_pp_2_0_1.py:74-78) has FOUR members: TRUE='.true.', T='T', FALSE='.false.', F='F'. str() of an enum member is 'UpfLogical.T' only for the 'T' spelling; a UPF written with core_correction=".true." parses to UpfLogical.TRUE → str='UpfLogical.TRUE' → gate at line 242 silently skips NLCC → wrong V_xc with no warning. It happens to work for the project's PseudoDojo ONCVPSP files (which write 'T'), which is why it survived. Correct test is membership in {UpfLogical.T, UpfLogical.TRUE}. Not merely brittle-against-future-changes: wrong today for a valid UPF spelling. (Side observation, out of scope: live species.py:67 uses bool(getattr(hdr,'core_correction',False)), and bool(UpfLogical.F) is truthy — a separate live smell.)

item: charge_density.py:159-171 — _symmetrise_density star average lacks fractional-translation phase
evidence: Confirmed: the loop sums rho_G_flat[idx] for G'=R·G (lines 159-169) using only sym.R_grid — no e^{-2πiG·t_S} factor. SymMaps DOES carry the BGW non-symmorphic translations (symmetry_maps.py:844-850, self.translations = wfn.translations[:ntran]) and the live ψ-unfold path applies the phase (symmetry_maps.py:1100, tau = translations[sym]/2π); correct ρ(G) symmetrization (QE sym_rho) requires the phase for every non-symmorphic operation. Missing it makes the 'average' mix rho(SG) values with wrong relative phases for any structure with fractional translations (e.g. diamond-Si in the BGW origin convention), degrading rather than improving the density — quantitatively consistent with dev_status.md:176 ('increases the density error 4.5x. Not used in the current pipeline'). Strongly supported cause of the documented regression; cannot execute on the login node to prove the 4.5x number itself.

item: charge_density.py:102 — spin_factor = 2 if nspinor==1 else 1, no spin-polarized branch
evidence: Confirmed at lines 100-102: only nspinor∈{1,2} is handled (closed-shell factor 2, or FR bispinor factor 1 with the norm spanning both spinor components per the comment at 88-96); there is no nspin=2 collinear branch and no spin-resolved ρ↑/ρ↓. This is the same documented, project-wide 'V_xc is spin-blind' limitation (project memory: dft_operators.compute_V_H_and_V_xc uses spin-unpolarized V_xc, omits B_xc; CrI3 H-residual 1.4 eV vs MoS2 0.2 meV). The hypothesis 'origin of the known spin-blindness' is only partially right: the live spin-blind code is psp/dft_operators.py compute_V_H_and_V_xc (line 293) — this archive copy merely shares the same deliberate unpolarized-only design assumption; deleting it fixes nothing. Known limitation by construction, not a latent bug in this file.

item: build_projectors.py:11 — unguarded sph_harm_y import, guarded deprecated sph_harm (inverted shim)
evidence: Two layers. (a) The inversion claim: pyproject.toml:13 pins scipy>=1.16.0, and sph_harm_y exists since scipy 1.15, so the unguarded import at line 11 cannot fail in the declared environment — the 'dies at import on older scipy' failure mode is moot in-project. (b) The REAL bug is in the fallback body: _sphY (lines 15-18) calls _sph_harm_y(m, l, phi, theta) with the LEGACY argument order. Legacy sph_harm signature is (m_order, n_degree, theta_azimuthal, phi_polar); new sph_harm_y signature is (n_degree, m_order, theta_POLAR, phi_azimuthal). Correct migration is sph_harm_y(l, m, theta, phi); as written, degree/order are swapped AND polar/azimuth are swapped, so once scipy>=1.17 removes sph_harm and the fallback activates, _sphY silently returns garbage (or errors for |m|>l interpreted as degree) for every l>=1 harmonic. Prefers the deprecated function and mis-migrates the replacement — suspected-bug, currently latent because scipy 1.16 still ships sph_harm, and the module is confirmed-dead anyway.

item: build_projectors.py:60-81 — compute_spinor_omegas CG index gymnastics
evidence: Verified by per-element enumeration against the standard spin-angular functions Ω_{ljm_j}. j=l+1/2 (lines 57-68): two_mj=-(2l+1)+2·idx is odd, m=(two_mj-1)//2 = m_j-1/2 exactly (floor division on odd two_mj is exact, e.g. two_mj=-3 → -2 = -1.5-0.5); a=√((l+m+1)/(2l+1))=√((l+m_j+1/2)/(2l+1)) multiplying Y_{l,m_j-1/2} (up) and b=√((l-m)/(2l+1))=√((l-m_j+1/2)/(2l+1)) multiplying Y_{l,m_j+1/2} (down) — exactly the textbook Ω_{j=l+1/2}. Edge cases m_j=±(l+1/2): getY clamps out-of-range m to 0 AND the paired coefficient vanishes analytically (m_j=l+1/2 → b=√((l-l)/…)=0; m_j=-(l+1/2) → a=√(0)=0). j=l-1/2 (lines 71-82): m=(two_mj+1)//2=m_j+1/2; c=√((l-m_j+1/2)/(2l+1))·Y_{l,m_j-1/2} (up), -d with d=√((l+m_j+1/2)/(2l+1))·Y_{l,m_j+1/2} (down) — this is (-1)× the common convention (which puts the minus on the upper component), i.e. an overall per-row phase. That phase is physically immaterial here: expand_D_block (lines 150-168) couples rows only within identical (l,j,mj_slot) keys, so each Ω row enters V_NL bilinearly as Z E Z† and the global row sign cancels. No sign bug found; correct up to a harmless overall row phase.

item: build_projectors.py:144 — spline ext=1 zero-extrapolation beyond tabulated qmax
evidence: Cannot fire even in principle from this API: q_grid is built with qmax = max(K_norm) of the very K being evaluated (lines 129-132), the spline is evaluated immediately on that same K_norm (line 145), and only the evaluated ARRAY F_on_K is stored in the returned dict (line 146) — the spline object never escapes the function, so no later caller can hit the ext=1 region. Defensive flag with no reachable hazard; the 'reuse with larger K' scenario would require copying the code, not calling it. Dead module besides.

item: load_upf.py:34-95 nested try/except around preprocess + jjj/lll annotation

item: normalize.py:15 np.fromstring(text, sep=' ')
evidence: Claim overstated: only fromstring's BINARY mode (sep='') is deprecated in NumPy; text mode with sep=' ' remains supported through numpy 2.x. Real (minor) wart is leniency: malformed trailing tokens truncate the array with only a DeprecationWarning, and the `arr.size > 0` guard at line 16 keeps partial arrays. Works correctly on well-formed UPF numeric blocks; one-line fromiter swap if desired, not urgent.

item: normalize.py:46-56 re-attach non-field attrs after dataclasses.replace

item: species.py:88-89 proj_j defaults l+0.5 via 'total_angular_momentum'

item: solid_harmonics.py l=1..3 sign flips (-c*x, -c*y, ...)
evidence: Verified per-element against the in-repo QE ylmr2 replica (_ylmr2_qe, build_projectors_qe.py:102-156), which seeds arr[3]=-sinθ/√2: l=1 → cos-component = c·√2·(-sinθ/√2)·cosφ = -c·x̂, sin = -c·ŷ, m=0 = c·ẑ — matches solid_harmonics.py:32 [c z, -c x, -c y] exactly. l=2 m=1: recurrence gives √3·cosθ·(-sinθ/√2) → -c2√3·xz / -c2√3·yz (lines 39-40 match); l=2 m=2: -(√((2·2-1))/√4)·sinθ·(-sinθ/√2) → +c2√3/2·(x²-y²) and +c2√3·xy (lines 41-42 match). These are QE's ylmr2 real-harmonic phases (odd-m components carry the -sinθ Legendre sign); vnl_ops/dft_operators projectors and get_DFT_mtxels V_NL depend on this pairing with QE-generated D-matrices. Any 'textbook Y_lm' unification MUST preserve them (or transform E blocks consistently). Note both copies (solid_harmonics_jax and all_solid_harmonics, lines 84-108) must stay in sync.

item: build_projectors_qe.py:539 LR Coulomb tail exp(-0.25*G2) hardcoded
evidence: Verified the pairing analytically: FT of erf(r)/r is (4π/q²)·exp(-q²/4), so the unit-width scipy_erf(r) added to the SR integrand in make_local_sr_table (radial_jax.py:317-321) demands EXACTLY exp(-G²/4) on the re-added LR tail — this is QE upflib/vloc_mod's split verbatim (aux1 = r·vloc + z·e2·erf(r); tail -4πze2·exp(-q²/4)/q²). Correctness precondition: G2 from bdot must be in absolute bohr⁻² (it is — bdot includes blat²). The 0.25 and the erf width are one constant appearing in two files with no cross-reference comment; a one-line 'must match erf(r) width in make_local_sr_table' note at :539 would close the trap. Not a bug.

item: build_projectors_qe.py:501-526 alpha-Z G=0 in try/except Exception: pass
evidence: Hypothesis is correct in principle: if anything inside raised, V(G=0) silently keeps the SR-table q→0 interpolated value instead of the alpha-Z integral → uniform potential offset → all eigenvalues shifted with no warning. Mitigations found: the body is plain numpy on arrays already validated upstream (r, v_r, rab exist or the species was `continue`d at :481), so no realistic exception path exists — which is exactly why the wrapper should be deleted rather than kept: it can only ever hide a future bug. Also duplicates radial_tables.alpha_z with a lower-order quadrature (raw rab at :514 vs Simpson at radial_tables.py:107-111) — fold into one implementation (see radial_weights fork entry).

item: build_projectors_qe.py:551 2D truncation lz = pi/B[2,2] assumes z-aligned c-axis
evidence: Confirmed latent: lz=π/B[2,2] and the Gz used in cos(Gz·lz) (:546-553) are only the QE Coul_cut_2D formula when the third reciprocal vector is z-aligned; QE's cutoff_2D module VALIDATES this and errors out ('2D cutoff requires the third lattice vector along z'), LORRAX computes silently-wrong cutoffs for a tilted c-axis. All sandbox 2D systems (MoS2, CrI3 slabs) are z-aligned, so it has never fired. Minimal fix: assert |B[0,2]|,|B[1,2]| (and B[2,0],B[2,1]) ≈ 0 when truncation_2d, mirroring QE.

item: src/psp/run_sternheimer.py:1208-1209 prefactor_chi carries sqrt(N_grid), prefactor_st does not
evidence: Verified by tracing the two pipelines' FFT normalizations. Chi path: ifftn(norm='ortho') gives u_r = u_phys(r_j)/sqrt(N) (line 152/177); the bilinear density delta_n_r = sum_v u_r*conj(du_r) is therefore delta_n_phys/N (line 191); project_density_to_Gsphere applies another ortho fftn contributing 1/sqrt(N) (line 199), so the gathered coefficient = (1/sqrt(N))*(1/N)*Sum_j delta_n_phys*e^{-iG'r} = (1/sqrt(N))*(cell-average Fourier component). Multiplying by sqrt(N_grid) in prefactor_chi (1208) exactly cancels this. S-tensor path: ovlp is a pure G-sphere coefficient inner product einsum('ivsG,jvsG->ij') at lines 591-594 — Parseval on normalized coefficient lists, no FFT, hence no sqrt(N) in prefactor_st (1209). The convention IS documented at the prefactor param comment, line 690: 'spin_factor·2·√N/(V·N_k)'. Correct but fragile; worth a one-line comment at 1208-1209 pointing at the ortho-FFT cancellation.

item: src/psp/run_sternheimer.py:1184 output HDF5 opened before heavy compute, no try/finally

Partially mitigated design intent: attrs and per-q groups are written incrementally so a graceful failure preserves earlier q's if the file flushes. One-line fix: 'with h5py.File(...) as out_h5:' — no behavior change on the happy path.

item: src/psp/run_nscf.py:299-331 pseudobands-only WFN load
evidence: CONFIRMED broken for nk>1, by index math. WFNWriter writes wfns/coeffs with shape (nbands, nspinor, ngktot, 2) where ngktot = sum_k ngk, per-k data at offsets (src/file_io/wfn_writer.py:184-187 create_dataset, :203-206 write_k offset slicing; matches BGW spec docs/docs_bgw/wfn.h5.spec 'Dims(2): ngktot ... all G-vectors for k-point 1, all G-vectors for k-point 2'). The loader (run_nscf.py:314-321) takes coeffs[ib] = (nspinor, ngktot, 2) — the concatenated-over-k axis — and writes it ONLY into all_evecs[0, ib, :, :ngk_file]. For nk_file>1, ngk_file=ngktot>ngkmax, so the assignment raises a broadcast ValueError; were it ever to fit, k=0 would hold concatenated multi-k garbage and all_evecs[ik>0] would stay zero, yet the pseudobands stage consumes all_evecs[ik] for every ik (line 394). Corroborating nk=1-only provenance: even at nk=1 the loaded coefficients are QE-ordered (reorder_to_qe applied before writing, line 235) while the Davidson path stores H_k-internal-order evecs (line 241) — the two sphere-sort orders differ per the tie-breaking note in run_sternheimer.py:1132-1138 — so the loaded Phi_dav ordering does not match apply_H_flat's expected layout without an inverse reorder. Mode needs a per-k offset loop + inverse reorder, or a guard 'nk_file==1 required'.

item: src/psp/run_nscf.py:484 pb_wfn_input = inp.wfn_file if inp.rho_from_wfn else None
evidence: Misnomer confirmed: rho is unconditionally loaded from the QE .save via crystal.load_charge_density() at run_nscf.py:61; no code path in run_nscf reads a density from a WFN file. rho_from_wfn's ONLY effect is gating whether inp.wfn_file becomes pb_wfn_input, the pseudobands-only input WFN consumed at lines 288-294 (--no-davidson mode); the CLI --pb-wfn flag (lines 455-456, 498-499) sets the same variable honestly. The pair is alive but semantically mislabeled; rename to e.g. pb_wfn/pb_wfn_file in [nscf] input and drop the rho_ prefix.

item: src/psp/run_nscf.py:471-476 args.X or inp.X override pattern
evidence: Confirmed at lines 471-476 (save_dir, nbnd, kgrid via truthiness of args.nk, nosym, output, tol) plus the same idiom at 489-493 in the no-input branch. Failure cases are real but degenerate: '--tol 0' (nonsensical for an iterative eigensolver), '--nbnd 0', '-o ""'; also '--nosym' can only turn nosym ON, never override an input-file nosym=true to false (argparse store_true gives False default, which 'or' discards). No realistic invocation misbehaves, so cruft rather than bug; the clean fix is 'x = args.x if args.x is not None else inp.x'.

item: src/psp/run_nscf.py:249-261 allgather-as-reduce of full evec buffers
evidence: Confirmed: process_allgather of the full (nk, nbnd, nspinor, ngkmax) complex128 local_coeffs (and all_evecs when pseudobands on) produces an (n_proc, nk, nbnd, nspinor, ngkmax) array on every rank, then .sum(axis=0) — n_proc× transient host memory and O(n_proc × size) traffic to implement what is semantically a psum over owner-exclusive zero-padded buffers (design acknowledged in the comment at lines 211-213 and 247-248). Correct as written (each ik has exactly one owner, so summing zeros is exact); purely a memory/scale flag. At large nk·nbnd·ngkmax or high rank counts this becomes the NSCF host-memory HWM; replace with multihost psum or per-owner gather at the writer.

item: src/psp/run_nscf.py:201-203 Davidson JIT warmup loop with m_max=4*nbnd
evidence: The magic 4 is davidson's own documented default: solvers/davidson.py:275-276 sets m_max = 4*n_eig inside davidson(), and warmup_davidson_jit uses the identical default and loop shape (davidson.py:210-213: for m in range(n_eig, m_max+n_eig, n_eig): m_eff=min(m,m_max)). run_nscf.py:197 already calls warmup_davidson_jit for the solver-internal kernels; the 201-203 loop additionally pre-compiles the user-supplied apply_H0 at each block size (nbnd, 2nbnd, 3nbnd, 4nbnd) the expanding subspace will hit — sizes warmup_davidson_jit cannot warm because apply_H is caller-owned. Deliberate and correct; the residual smell is the constant 4 duplicated across two files (drift cost = mid-run recompiles, perf-only, never wrong numbers). A shared DEFAULT_M_MAX_FACTOR or letting warmup take apply_H would remove the coupling.

item: _EPS2=1e-60 (dft_operators.py:892,910,966) vs 1e-8 (vnl_ops.py:363,416) for the same sqrt(K^2) guard
evidence: Two genuinely divergent copies of the K=0 guard, and vnl_ops.py:361-362 has a stale doc pointer ('See _build_vnl_kdata_core for the full physics rationale' — that docstring at 381-392 only documents tail-G padding, no regularizer rationale). Numerics: 1e-8 inside q=sqrt(K²+ε) maps q_true=0 → 1e-4 and shifts q by ε/(2q)<5e-8 for q>0.1; value error ≈ G'_l(0)·1e-4 = 0 since Gp(0)=0 by construction (vnl_ops.py:182-190), so both are physically benign. Context differs: the 1e-60 copies live in the now-dead autodiff section (custom JVP divides by q at :924, needs only exact-0 protection); the 1e-8 copy is production and keeps sqrt smooth under jax.jvp near K=0. Unify (and delete the dead copies with the section above).

item: 1e10 padding sentinel for T_diag/h_diag in setup_H_k/setup_H_k_from_kvec (dft_operators.py:506,527,577,600)
evidence: The sentinel is a documented cross-module contract, not an accident: dft_precond.make_pw_init docstring requires 'padding entries ≥ 1e10' (dft_precond.py:50) so argsort puts padded G last, and the preconditioner denominator |h_diag−ε| becomes huge at padded entries → corrections ≈ 0 there. The snap-back comment at dft_operators.py:596-599 explicitly says it 'restores the old sentinel exactly for any preconditioner that tests against it'. The mask idiom IS copy-pasted between setup_H_k (504-513) and setup_H_k_from_kvec (575-584) — a small dedup opportunity, but the convention itself is deliberate and relied upon.

evidence: The except can never fire with the canonical sym object: SymMaps unconditionally sets irr_idx_k in both branches (common/symmetry_maps.py:766 no-sym identity, :867 sym path). If a non-SymMaps object lacked it, fallback k_red=ik indexes IBZ-shaped wfn.energies (nspin, nk_red, nb) with a full-BZ index → IndexError for ik>=nk_red (loud, not silent) or wrong-but-equal only when nk_red==nk_tot (nosym, where ik==irr_idx_k[ik] anyway). Latent-hazard shape is real but the enclosing function is confirmed-dead; delete with the section.

item: apply_H_k_from_G .add() scatter for padded-G collision (dft_operators.py:696-702)
evidence: Verified index math: under ngkmax padding all padded rows carry G=(0,0,0) (padded with constant 0 at :507-509/578-580), colliding with the real G=0 index. psi_G_m = psi_G*mask zeros the padded coefficients first (:690), so .at[...].add accumulates real+0+0... preserving the physical G=0 coefficient; .set() with duplicate indices has undefined write order in XLA and a padded zero could land last, wiping G=0. Correct and fully documented at this site (comment :696-700).

item: three stacked normalization factors in _compute_local_V_k_jit (get_DFT_mtxels.py:400-415)
evidence: NOT empirically tuned — the factors provably multiply to exactly 1.0: scale·(deltaV·fft_norm)·sqrt(1/vol) = sqrt(N/V)·(V/N)·sqrt(N)·(1/sqrt(V)) = [sqrt(N)·V·sqrt(N)] / [sqrt(V)·N·sqrt(V)] = V/V = 1 (N=ngrid, V=volume). The routine is algebraically identical to a plain ortho ifft → multiply V_r → ortho fft → gather → einsum with no prefactor. The decomposition is physical bookkeeping (ψ(r) normalization, ∫dr measure, bra 1/sqrt(V)) that fully cancels; collapse to factor-free ortho FFTs to remove the refactor hazard.

Mitigating context: both functions are reachable only from the dead main(); the production path (gw/kin_ion_io.py:129,169) threads ctx.truncation_2d correctly and never builds V_H. Fix or delete with the driver.

item: q=sqrt(K²+1e-8) regularizer in vnl_ops (lines 363,416)
evidence: Same finding as the _EPS2 divergence item (see per-element analysis there): shift is ε/(2q) ≤ 5e-8 for physical q, and at q=0 exactly the value error is G'_l(0)·1e-4 = 0 since all Gp tables have Gp(0)=0 (vnl_ops.py:182-190). So '1e-8 shifts small-q by ~1e-4' is true only for the q coordinate at K≡0, where the form factor is flat — numerically benign. The real defect is the undocumented 52-orders divergence from the (dead) dft_operators copies plus the stale rationale pointer at :361-362. Unify to one documented epsilon when the dead section is deleted.

item: q_max *= 1.01 in build_vnl_setup (vnl_ops.py:132) duplicated by scf_potential.py:95
evidence: Confirmed genuine double application: vnl_ops.py:132 applies *=1.01 UNCONDITIONALLY (outside the `if q_max is None` block, so even explicit caller-provided q_max gets it), and scf_potential.py:95 passes q_max=sqrt(ecutwfc)*1.01 → net 1.0201× table range. Consequence analysis: q_max only sets the radial-table span; for fixed n_q the grid spacing dq=q_max/n_q becomes ~1% coarser — interpolation is still well inside range (|k+G| ≤ sqrt(ecutwfc) by cutoff definition), no correctness effect. Sloppy layering, not a bug; drop the caller-side 1.01.

item: padded tail-G rows of Z hold finite garbage; callers must mask (vnl_ops _build_vnl_kdata_core docstring + call sites)
evidence: Deliberate for JIT shape-stability (one _assemble_Z_jit compile across k, dft_operators.py:569-574 explains) and the contract IS documented at vnl_ops.py:387-392. Verified every padding consumer handles it: setup_H_k masks Z post-call (dft_operators.py:521-523), setup_H_k_from_kvec likewise (:589-591), and the Sternheimer path masks the ψ side instead — apply_H_k_from_G computes psi_G_m=psi_G*mask before P=Z†ψ (:690,708), which zeroes exactly the contractions the garbage columns would enter, and masks H_G output (:705,710). Non-padding callers (get_DFT_mtxels :574/:758, get_dipole_mtxels, kin_ion_io) pass unpadded G-lists so nothing to mask. Correct at all sites; fragile only for future padded callers.

item: ChannelMeta.E documented (2,2,R,R) full-spin, sliced [:nspinor,:nspinor] (vnl_ops.py:43,233)
evidence: build_E_blocks_full returns the full Pauli-2-spinor D-matrix; :233 slices E[:nspinor,:nspinor] giving (1,1,..) for scalar and (2,2,..) for FR — the two physical cases for KB projectors. Bispinor (4-component ψ) is handled by the established caller convention of slicing ψ down to nspinor_E before contraction (dft_operators.py:406-410, 421-425; get_dipole_mtxels.py:67-70,88-91), i.e. V_NL acts on the upper 2 spinor components. A true 4-component Dirac V_NL would indeed require redesign (and nspinor=4 here would break: numpy clips the slice to (2,2,..) and the E_super block assignment at :236 would shape-error, a loud failure not a silent one). Max-2-spinor is the deliberate current physics scope.

item: h_dft.py:25 .add() scatter, rationale undocumented locally
evidence: Same verified-correct convention as apply_H_k_from_G: line 25 scatters psi_G*mask_f with .add, so padded (0,0,0) duplicates contribute zeros and the real G=0 coefficient survives XLA's undefined duplicate-write order. The rationale comment lives only at dft_operators.py:696-700. Correct as-is; worth a one-line cross-reference comment (or routing both through one scatter helper), but no behavior issue.

item: dft_precond precond_fn double denominator guard + magic 1e-2/1e-30 (dft_precond.py:28-34)
evidence: Enumerated the cases: (a) |denom|>=1e-2 → untouched; (b) 0<|denom|<1e-2 → sign(denom)*1e-2, sign preserved; (c) denom==0 exactly → sign(0)*1e-2 = 0, then line 31 catches ==0 → +1e-2. The second where is NOT redundant belt-and-suspenders — without it case (c) divides by zero, and denom==0 is reachable (h_diag ≈ eigenvalue during Davidson). 1e-2 Ry floor is the stated QE g_psi-style diagonal-preconditioner convention (docstring :22-24); 1e-30 norm floor guards 0/0 on a fully-preconditioned-to-zero residual row. Minimal and correct.

item: xc magic thresholds rho=max(rho,1e-10) and LDA-fallback mask (rho_raw>1e-6)&(sigma>1e-10) (xc.py:95,136)
evidence: These mirror Quantum ESPRESSO's XClib gradient-correction cutoffs almost literally: QE skips the gradient correction (falls back to LDA-only) below epsr=1e-6 on rho and epsg~1e-10 on |∇rho|², which is exactly the (rho_raw>1e-6)&(sigma>1e-10) mask at :136-138 (df_dsigma zeroed, df_drho snapped to the σ=0 baseline). Since LORRAX's V_scf reconstruction is validated against QE-generated wavefunctions, matching QE's low-density behavior is the correct convention, not a silent physics switch. The 1e-10 rho floor at :95 additionally guards autodiff of ε_xc(ρ) (ρ^(-2/3)-type kernels NaN at 0). Worth a QE-citing comment, nothing more.

item: xc _vxc_mgga tau term added as multiplicative potential (xc.py:166-169)
evidence: Confirmed incomplete placeholder: tau is jnp.zeros_like(rho) at the only dispatch site (:105), df_dtau is then the functional's ∂ε/∂τ evaluated at τ=0 added as a multiplicative term, and the comment admits the real non-multiplicative τ·(-½∇²) Hamiltonian channel is unwired. However the entire MGGA branch is unreachable (see _vxc_lda/_vxc_mgga dead verdict), so this is scaffolding for a feature that was never finished — dead placeholder, no live physics risk. Delete or finish.

item: xc.py whole module spin-blind (no spin-polarized branch, no B_xc)
evidence: Confirmed for this checkout: xc.py has only total-density signatures (compute_V_xc(rho_total,...) :71-77), no compute_V_xc_spin, no B_xc anywhere in src/psp. Matches the documented known blocker (memory: CrI3 H-residual 1.4 eV vs MoS2 0.2 meV; blocks Sternheimer + rebuilt-V_scf screening on magnetic systems; core gw.gw_jax unaffected since it never rebuilds V_xc). A spin version already exists on the lorrax_B_orbmag_wt worktree (its report script imports compute_V_xc_spin), so the refactor path is porting, not design.

item: scf_potential.build_rho_val_from_wfn insulator-only fixed n_occ, spin_factor 2/1 by nspinor, no nspin=2 (scf_potential.py:107-158)
evidence: The limitation is explicit and documented, not accidental: docstring says 'Number of occupied bands per k (insulator)' (:127) and spells out spin_factor=2 scalar /1 FR (:112,144); the verbose check prints the expected integral spin_factor*n_occ (:161-164) which would immediately flag a metal/fractional-occupation misuse. Wrong for metals (no occupation weighting) and for collinear nspin=2 (no per-spin channel) — the same known magnetic-systems blocker as xc.py — but within its declared insulator scope the full-BZ sum with uniform 1/N_k weights is correct. Extend when the spin work lands; don't treat as a latent bug.

item: scf_potential.py:95 q_max=sqrt(ecutwfc)*1.01 duplicating build_vnl_setup's internal margin
evidence: Same double-1.01 finding verified from the vnl_ops side (vnl_ops.py:132 applies *=1.01 unconditionally even to explicit q_max). Net 1.0201× radial-table span → dq 1% coarser at fixed n_q; all physical |k+G| ≤ sqrt(ecutwfc) remain deep in-range. No correctness effect; remove the caller-side factor (or make vnl_ops skip the margin for explicit q_max) when touched.

item: operator_checks sys_dim=0 (molecule) → truncation_2d=False (operator_checks.py:108)
evidence: Confirmed: `truncation_2d = sys_dim == 2` after validating sys_dim ∈ {0,2,3} (:103-108), so a molecule gets untruncated bulk Coulomb in V_loc/V_H — no 0D truncation exists anywhere in the psp potential path. Inconsistent with the GW side, which either implements 0D box truncation (gw/compute_vcoul.py:269-274, requires bdot) or refuses loudly (gw/v_q_g_flat.py:503-506 raises '0-D box per-q v(G) not wired'). Mitigations: it matches QE's own default (periodic Coulomb unless assume_isolated), kin_ion_io prints the resolved truncation (:134), and no current caller passes sys_dim=0. Should either raise NotImplementedError like v_q_g_flat or document the QE-periodic equivalence; silently validating 0 as 'supported' is the defect.

item: operator_checks bypassed by potential-building paths hardcoding truncation_2d
evidence: Partially stale as stated: the bypasses are real but ALL confined to the dead get_DFT_mtxels driver — get_H_matrix_elements hardcodes True (:533,:556) and get_kin_ion's Hartree branch hardcodes False (:738) while its V_loc uses ctx (:717); both reachable only via dead main(). Every live path is consistent: gw/kin_ion_io validates (:129) and threads ctx.truncation_2d into V_loc (:169); scf_potential.build_dft_potentials takes truncation_2d as a required keyword and threads it into both build_ionic_and_core (:82) and compute_V_H_and_V_xc (:91). The 'plumbing incomplete' residue dies with the driver; no live fix needed beyond deleting the dead code.
